IN THE CHAIR: ROBERTA METSOLA
President

3. European Council meetings and European security (joint debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Good morning, dear colleagues. The first item on the agenda is a joint debate concerning European Council meetings and European security:

芒聙聯 European Council and Commission statements on the conclusions of the special European Council meeting of 6脗聽March脗聽2025脗聽(2025/2589(RSP));

芒聙聯 Council and Commission statements on preparation of the European Council of 20-21脗聽March脗聽2025 (2025/2544(RSP));

芒聙聯 European Council and Commission statements on European security architecture: urgent decisive steps and unwavering support for Ukraine (2025/2590(RSP)).

The President of the European Council is on his way. He was delayed by fog in Brussels, so as soon as he lands he will come straight to the Chamber. So we can go first to the Council and the Minister for the European Union, Adam Sz脜聜apka.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adam Sz脜聜apka, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President Metsola, Madam脗聽President von der Leyen, honourable Members, allow me to focus on the preparation of the meeting of the European Council on 20脗聽March, which will, as always, start with an exchange with the President of the European Parliament.

The spring European Council meeting is traditionally focused on economic issues. However, in view of the rapidly evolving international context, leaders will also follow up on the special meeting of 6脗聽March. We are all aware of what is at stake, and the leaders will build on the important progress made at their meeting last week.

Allow me to add my voice here in stressing that only a comprehensive, just and lasting peace for Ukraine can put an end to the Russian war of aggression. A peace that is negotiated by Ukraine and its legitimated authorities led by President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. A peace that involves Europeans, whose security it will affect without a doubt. A peace based on strong security guarantees.

The European Council is also expected to revert to the situation in the Middle East, which has evolved substantially since the leaders last addressed the topic in December.

Let me now turn to competitiveness, which is a centrepiece of the agenda of the next European Council. 2024 was a year of assessment and reflection, with the Letta and Draghi reports the strategic agenda for the next five years. 2025 is the year for action and delivery.

Three priority areas have been identified for the leaders' debate on competitiveness: simplification, energy and the Saving and Investments Union. For the EU to remain competitive, we need to cut red tape and simplify EU rules for businesses, especially SMEs. Reducing administrative burdens and creating a simple and clear legislative framework remain a matter of urgency.

Europeans also require access to clean, secure and affordable energy, and we have to take steps to address the saving and investments mismatch in the EU in order to better meet Europe's investment needs, in particular those of young and innovative companies. This will also be important to ensure private financing to develop our defence industry for years ahead.

Leaders will also address the topics of the single market, industrial policy and skills, paying special attention to those sectors that are heavily impacted by the sustained rise in energy prices.

Finally, under this point, the European Council will also turn to its customary point on the European Semester. For a more long-term perspective, leaders will have a first exchange of views of views on the next multiannual financial framework and the new own resources, following the publication of the Commission Communication of 11脗聽February and ahead of the proposal expected in summer 2025.

Migration also remains high on the European Council agenda. Leaders will take stock of progress made in the implementation of previous conclusions and ensure a steady pace of work on returns.

Finally, other foreign policy issues will be addressed, notably multilateralism and oceans, which are expected to be on the table during the working lunch leaders will have with UN Secretary-General Ant脙鲁nio Guterres on Thursday. Depending on developments, Member States may also want to address issues in specific third countries or regions.

A Euro Summit is also scheduled to take place in the margins of the European Council, and will include the usual discussion on the economic situation with the President of the Eurogroup and the European Central Bank.

To conclude, we expect a substantial European Council meeting following up on the decision taken at last week's special European Council and advancing on essential work to ensure we are well prepared to meet the many challenges ahead. We are at a crucial juncture. We must rise to the occasion.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ursula von der Leyen, President of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Roberta, Minister, honourable Members, it's now 70 years ago that Alcide de Gasperi said: 'We do not just need peace among us, but to build a common defence. This is not to threaten or conquer, but to deter any attack from the outside, driven by hatred against a united Europe. This is the task of our generation.'

70 years have gone by, but our generation is faced with the very same task. Because peace in our Union can no longer be taken for granted. We are facing a crisis of European security, but we know that it is in crisis that Europe has always been built.

So this is the moment for peace through strength. This is the moment for a common defence effort. And at the European Council, I saw a level of consensus on European defence which is not just unprecedented but was completely unthinkable only a few weeks ago. There is a new understanding that we must think differently and act accordingly. We have started to mobilise Europe's massive resources. And in the coming weeks and months, more courage will be necessary and other difficult choices await us.

Honourable Members, the European security order is being shaken, and so many of our illusions are being shattered. After the end of the Cold War, some believed that Russia could be integrated in Europe's economic and security architecture. Others hoped that we could rely indefinitely on America's full protection. And so, we lowered our guard. We cut our defence spending from routinely averaging more than 3.5脗聽% to less than half of that. We thought we were enjoying a peace dividend, but in reality we were just running a security deficit.

The time of illusions is over now. Europe is called to take greater charge of its own defence, not in some distant future but already today, not with incremental steps but with the courage that the situation requires. We need a surge in European defence, and we need it now.

We need it first and foremost because of the situation in Ukraine. There is the urgent need to fill the gaps in Ukraine's military supplies and to provide Ukraine with solid security guarantees. But this moment of reckoning is not only about Ukraine. It is about all of Europe and our entire continent's security.

Putin has proven time and again that he is a hostile neighbour. He cannot be trusted, he can only be deterred. And we know that Russia's military complex is outproducing ours. If we look at the military expenditure in real terms, the Kremlin is spending more than all of Europe combined. Europe's production is still on a lower order of magnitude. And beyond traditional capabilities, the range of threats that we face is getting broader by the day. I know that the European Parliament argued for years that Europe needs to do more, and you were absolutely right. And this is more dangerous, today, the era we live in, so Europe really needs to step up.

This is the goal of the plan that I presented to leaders last week. Its logic is very simple: we want to pull every single financial lever we have, to strengthen and fast track our defence production. With the REARM Europe plan, we can mobilise up to EUR脗聽800脗聽billion. So, honourable Members, let me focus on some of the main features of this plan.

First, there is the so-called national escape clause. Let me start with why it is crucial to mobilise national budgets. If we look today, we spend just short of 2脗聽% on average of our GDP on defence. Every analysis today agrees that we need to move north of 3脗聽%. Now look at the entire European budget 芒聙聯 it only reaches 1脗聽% of the GDP. So it is obvious that the bulk of new investments can only come from Member States. This is why we are activating the national escape clause, foreseen by our new fiscal rules. This is a new tool that was created just last year, and we propose to trigger it in a controlled, time-bound and coordinated way for all Member States.

This can transform our defence budgets quickly and effectively. Member States could mobilise up to EUR脗聽650脗聽billion over the next four years, adding round about 1.5脗聽% of GDP to their defence budgets over four years. This is massive. And yet the European Council has tasked us to explore further measures, to facilitate significant defence spending at national level while ensuring debt sustainability.

The second point the European Council has agreed on our proposal for a new financial instrument. We call it SAFE (Security Action for Europe). We offer Member States up to EUR脗聽150脗聽billion in loans, to invest following a few basic principles 芒聙聯 they could focus on a few selected strategic capability domains, you are familiar with them, it's advanced air defence, drones, missiles and ammunition, strategic enablers, military mobility, cyber, artificial intelligence, to name just脗聽a few 芒聙聯 so that we maximise the impact of our investments.

These loans should finance purchases from European producers to help boost our own defence industry. The contract should be multiannual, to give the industry the predictability they need. And finally, there should be a focus on joint procurement, because we have seen how powerful this can be. Just think of the Czech initiative or the Danish initiative to provide weapons and ammunition for Ukraine. One nation took the lead. Others joined in, to place larger orders. Industry then scaled up, and prices went down. It was both very quick and very effective. And this is exactly what we need right now: scale and speed.

This is why we have chosen the emergency procedure under Article 122, which is designed precisely for times when 'severe difficulties arise in the supply of certain products'. In other words, Article 122 allows us to raise money, to lend it to Member States for them to invest in defence. This is the only possibility for emergency financial assistance, and that is what we need now. We will keep Parliament constantly updated on progress.

This leads me to my third point: the cohesion funds. This is a possibility that we are offering to Member States. Member States will have the possibility to redirect some of their uncommitted funds to defence-related projects. This could be infrastructure, it could be research and development. This would be voluntary 芒聙聯 voluntary for those who want to go the extra mile. It will be up to Parliament and Council to decide on this additional option.脗聽For the same reason, REARM Europe also includes measures to mobilise private investment, with the European Investment Bank and our upcoming Savings and Investment Union.

Honourable Members, all of this will also have positive spillovers for our economy and our competitiveness. It will include new factories and production lines that will be necessary, creating good jobs right here in Europe. The investment boost will be felt well beyond the defence sector. We know the spillover effects to both sides, be it from steel to space, large transport companies or the innovative AI start-ups.

Together, we have the size to deter any hostile country, we have the economic power and now, finally, we also have the political will.脗聽We all wish we could live in more peaceful times, without any question. But I'm confident that if we unleash our industrial power, we can restore deterrence against those who seek to harm us. It is time to build a European Defence Union that ensures peace on our continent, through unity and through strength. This is Europe's moment. And Europe will rise to it. Thank you very much, and long live Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Manfred Weber, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President of the European Parliament, European Commission, Council representatives, dear colleagues, we as the EPP know what we owe the Americans: as Supreme Commander Eisenhower and American soldiers brought freedom and democracy back to Europe; as脗聽Reagan's call to the Soviet Union 芒聙聯 'Tear down this wall!' 芒聙聯 in 1987 brought freedom and democracy also to Central and Eastern Europe.

On the other hand, what happened in the White House two weeks ago was simply a scandal. To say Ukraine is responsible for the war is simply a lie. And even with our long history, with our US friends in mind, we must call a lie a lie. No one can twist the truth. Putin doesn't want to end the war; he wants to end Ukraine. And as Europeans we will never allow this.

After the speech of Vice President Vance in Munich and what happened with Zelenskyy in the Oval Office, one reality is clear: dear friends, we are alone. The world is in turmoil. Europe has to wake up. We have to become responsible for ourselves. Our security is not in the hands of Washington or Moscow. It must be in our hands.

Since 1952, Madam脗聽President 芒聙聯 the Commission脗聽President referred to this 芒聙聯 more than 70 years ago, we had the first idea of building up a defence union. I have to say, we wasted a lot of time in the last 70 years for discussing it endlessly. Last Thursday, putting EUR脗聽800脗聽billion on the table to build up a European defence was an important step, showing that Europe finally takes its defence into its own hands. Money is in this regard a precondition, but is only the starting point of doing so. We need to go further.

The first point: we would save a lot of taxpayers' money if we act together. We have 17 tank脗聽models in the European Union. The Americans only have one. We need standardisation. We need an obligatory common procurement. And we need to build up a true European market for defence goods, with all the further mergers in the industry side. This is not yet fully foreseen in the plan of Thursday.

Secondly, we need joint European projects, like a missile or drones defence system, especially in the east side of Europe; a cyber-defence brigade where everybody understands that cyber is not any more a national issue; a satellite surveillance system where we are not any more depending on US data. And every European citizen would immediately understand it's better to do these kinds of investments together.

I would love to see, then, parts of the troops with the European flags on their uniform. This is not yet foreseen in the plans of last Thursday.

Europe has to answer the offer of France to define the French nuclear shield as a kind of a European shield. This is not yet part of the plan of last Thursday.

Imagine for a second that Trump finally would invite Europe to go to the negotiation table to Saudi Arabia. Who would represent Europe then? Commission President? Council President? High Representative? Merz? Macron? Who would speak on behalf of Europe and on which basis could he or she then speak and act? Unanimity as a precondition for having a voice, then, in Saudi Arabia? Dear colleagues, that shows clearly we are ridiculous. We are not up to the task in this moment of time to give a proper answer. Kissinger said years ago, decades ago, 'Whom shall I call when I want to speak with Europe?' Let's be honest, he didn't give a proper answer on this until now.

Europe stands at a self-defining moment. Personally, I think we should once in the future elect the president of the European Union directly by the citizens of Europe. This is naive. This is a dream. Without dreamers, as Adenauer, De脗聽Gasperi, Schuman, V脙隆clav Havel and Wa脜聜脛聶sa, no one of us would sit today in this Chamber of the European Parliament.

The truth is that the plan of 1952 was more ambitious on a European spirit than what is on the table today. We need dreams that create hope and the determination to implement them. This method brought 70脗聽years of peace and then finally the reunification of the European Union 35脗聽years ago.

Finally, dear colleagues, because the purpose of the European defence union is to defend our democracy, even more democratic legitimacy of the process is needed. The Parliament must be fully involved. Bypassing the Parliament with Article 122 is a mistake. Europe's democracy stands on two pillars: it's the citizens and it's the Member States. We need both for our security.

Last Thursday was a great starting point, and I truly congratulate Ursula von脗聽der脗聽Leyen and Ant脙鲁nio Costa for the strong message 芒聙聯 the will to invest into our defence and to our strength. But this can only be a starting point. The main task of our generation is to create a true European defence union. The European People's Party is ready to do so.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Iratxe Garc脙颅a P脙漏rez, en nombre del Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, hoy, una vez m脙隆s, podemos mirar hacia atr脙隆s y reconocer que nuestros fundadores ten脙颅an raz脙鲁n: Europa se forja en las crisis. Desde los escombros de la Segunda Guerra Mundial hasta la pandemia, pasando por la ca脙颅da del Muro de Berl脙颅n, cada desaf脙颅o nos ha hecho m脙隆s fuertes, m脙隆s unidos y m脙隆s conscientes de nuestro papel en el mundo.

La llegada al poder de Donald Trump y su vergonzoso acercamiento al criminal de guerra Vlad脙颅mir Putin han dejado claro que no podemos depender de terceros para garantizar nuestra seguridad. Esta crisis nos brinda una oportunidad sin precedentes: dar un salto adelante en la construcci脙鲁n de una verdadera Uni脙鲁n Europea de Defensa.

El plan de rearme para movilizar 800脗聽000 millones de euros es un paso positivo; pero no nos enga脙卤emos, es solo el primer paso. La suspensi脙鲁n de las reglas fiscales para liberar 650脗聽000 millones no garantiza que se alcance esa cifra, ya que no todos los pa脙颅ses tienen el mismo m脙潞sculo fiscal; y los 150脗聽000 millones en pr脙漏stamos respaldados por deuda comunitaria deben ser el embri脙鲁n de un nuevo instrumento, el sucesor del Next脗聽Generation脗聽EU.

No podemos permitir que las soluciones nacionales fragmenten nuestra defensa y aumenten los costes. Necesitamos m脙隆s deuda com脙潞n, m脙隆s solidaridad y m脙隆s visi脙鲁n estrat脙漏gica y, al mismo tiempo, garantizar que esta inversi脙鲁n en defensa no se haga a costa del gasto social y del estado de bienestar, que son la esencia del proyecto europeo. Porque, si sacrificamos nuestro modelo social, estaremos alimentando el caldo de cultivo de la extrema derecha y socavando los cimientos de nuestra democracia.

Madam President, colleagues, the construction of a genuine common defence and our support for Ukraine are two sides of the same coin. For three years now, a democratic nation has been suffering the brutal aggression of Vladimir Putin, an aggression driven by imperialist paranoia, justified with lies, aimed at stopping Ukraine's integration into our democratic and prosperous Europe.

We cannot and will not allow Ukraine's sacrifice to be in vain. A sacrifice that has already cost thousands of lives, with hundreds of thousands injured and millions displaced. The European people have a long memory. Neither Trump nor Putin will make us forget the bombing of civilians, the massacres in Bucha, Mariupol and Zaporizhzhia, or the bravery of families who have sent their loved ones to the frontlines only to lose everything.

But let me be clear, we cannot resign ourselves to helping Ukraine only to resist. We must help Ukraine to win this war. To achieve this, we need bold and decisive action. Mrs van der Leyen, we are waiting for your legal proposal to confiscate the EUR 200 billion in frozen Russian assets to rebuild and arm Ukraine.脗聽We cannot we cannot continue to tie Ukraine's hands while Russia bombs its people.

Colleagues, if we let Kiev fall to Putin, autocrats will have won a decisive battle against democracy, a battle that will define the 21st century.

As Churchill said to Chamberlain, "You were given the choice between war and dishonour. You chose dishonour and you will have war". Let us not repeat that mistake. Let us stand with Ukraine. Let us defend our values and let us ensure that democracy prevails.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jordan Bardella, au nom du groupe PfE. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame la Pr脙漏sidente von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, vous voil脙聽 rattrap脙漏e par l芒聙聶histoire et par la r脙漏alit脙漏 d芒聙聶un monde dont vous avez toujours rejet脙漏 l芒聙聶existence 芒聙聯脗聽un monde dans lequel vous avez cru bon et juste de d脙漏l脙漏guer notre s脙漏curit脙漏 aux 脙聣tats-Unis, nos industries 脙聽 la Chine et notre politique 脙漏nerg脙漏tique 脙聽 la Russie. La le脙搂on des dix derniers jours est celle de l芒聙聶humanit脙漏 tout enti脙篓re: une nation qui ne ma脙庐trise ni ses armes, ni son 脙漏conomie, ni son destin est une nation condamn脙漏e 脙聽 subir l芒聙聶histoire plut脙麓t qu芒聙聶脙聽 l芒聙聶脙漏crire. Nous voil脙聽 donc immerg脙漏s dans un monde d芒聙聶int脙漏r脙陋ts et d芒聙聶ambitions, un monde dur, dangereux et mouvant, o脙鹿 les app脙漏tits s芒聙聶aiguisent. Face 脙聽 l芒聙聶enlisement du conflit en Ukraine et au risque de prolonger une guerre sans perspective de victoire claire, face 脙聽 l芒聙聶horizon du d脙漏sengagement am脙漏ricain, nous devons avoir deux priorit脙漏s absolues: la paix et l芒聙聶ind脙漏pendance.

La premi脙篓re, la plus urgente, se construira par la diplomatie. L芒聙聶organisation d芒聙聶un sommet de la famille occidentale, id脙漏e formul脙漏e par la Premi脙篓re ministre italienne, Giorgia脗聽Meloni, devrait nous permettre de d脙漏finir clairement nos objectifs pour la souverainet脙漏 de l芒聙聶Ukraine, les exigences que nous devons mettre sur la table et les garanties de s脙漏curit脙漏 脙聽 offrir 脙聽 ce pays alli脙漏, afin qu芒聙聶il ne subisse plus jamais les assauts de l芒聙聶agresseur russe.

La deuxi脙篓me boussole, 脙聽 plus long terme, est de b脙垄tir l芒聙聶autonomie des nations d芒聙聶Europe, pour faire en sorte que personne d芒聙聶autre que nous ne d脙漏termine nos int脙漏r脙陋ts et les conditions de notre s脙漏curit脙漏. Si elle est capable de repenser une diplomatie libre et ind脙漏pendante, la France peut redevenir la force d芒聙聶entra脙庐nement du continent et l芒聙聶interlocutrice 脙漏vidente et naturelle de ce nouveau monde, parce que la France est dot脙漏e d芒聙聶une arm脙漏e de m脙漏tier reconnue, d芒聙聶une base industrielle d芒聙聶exception, d芒聙聶un si脙篓ge permanent au Conseil de s脙漏curit脙漏 de l芒聙聶ONU, de la dissuasion nucl脙漏aire et, bien s脙禄r, parce qu芒聙聶elle est l芒聙聶h脙漏riti脙篓re d芒聙聶un pacte vingt fois s脙漏culaire entre la grandeur d芒聙聶une nation et la libert脙漏 du monde.

Madame von der Leyen, permettez-moi de vous dire que tous vos choix politiques se sont, h脙漏las, fracass脙漏s sur le mur du r脙漏el. Avec le pacte vert, vous avez opt脙漏 pour la d脙漏croissance, alors qu芒聙聶il fallait produire sans entrave. Vous avez d脙漏savou脙漏 le nucl脙漏aire, opt脙漏 pour l芒聙聶inflation de normes et de taxes, et encourag脙漏 les achats de mat脙漏riel am脙漏ricain plut脙麓t que d芒聙聶appliquer la pr脙漏f脙漏rence europ脙漏enne en mati脙篓re de d脙漏fense. Ce n芒聙聶est qu芒聙聶au prix de changements d芒聙聶orientation profonds que l芒聙聶Europe restera une actrice de l芒聙聶histoire, dissuadera les menaces et imposera le respect aux empires.

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Now I give the floor to the President of the European Council.

Mr脗聽Procaccini, can I give the floor to the President of the European Council first, because he has just arrived?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ant脙鲁nio Costa, President of the European Council. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Madam脗聽President of the European Commission, Minister Sz脜聜apka, honourable Members, the European Union is living a defining moment. The sense of urgency is upon us. Geopolitical tensions have risen over the last weeks. Multilateralism is under enormous pressure, and so is the rules-based international order.

Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine entered its third year and the threat it represents has wider implications for Europe and international security. This is exactly why, last week, the European Council took fast and ambitious decisions on defence and Ukraine. We are putting our money where our mouth is. We are delivering on what we promised.

Three years ago precisely, in Versailles, we decided to boost Europe's defence to build the Europe of defence. Three years ago, we laid the foundation of what we decided last week. Over the last three years, Member States increased their defence spending by 30脗聽%. Over the last three years, on average, among the 23 European Union Member States that are in NATO, we reached the NATO target for 2024 of 2脗聽% of GDP.

Now we are going much further. In one month, working hand in hand with the European Commission, we have kick-started the Europe of Defence. On 3脗聽February, in the informal leaders' meeting with the special participation of the British Prime Minister and the Secretary General of NATO, we opened the way for decisions.

Last week, the Commission anticipated some of its proposals on defence financing, which were the basis for European Council decisions. In this special European Council, we decided to invest in the priority areas already defined by the European Defence Agency, reflecting the lessons learned from the war in Ukraine and in full coherence with NATO: air and missile defence, artillery systems, missiles and ammunition, drones and anti-drone systems, strategic enablers, military mobility, artificial intelligence, cyber and electronic warfare.

We have also decided to mobilise more public and private funds for our defence. We welcome the Commission's initiative to dedicate EUR脗聽150脗聽billion of additional defence spending to funding the capabilities we urgently need. In addition to the loan instrument proposed by the Commission, leaders were clear that we need flexibility. With the Stability and Growth Pact and in the use of the European funds, it will also be important to provide more public money for the defence sector where Member States decide to do so.

The European Investment Bank initiative to broaden its mandate for more loans to the defence sector will improve access to private financing.

Finally, we decided to spend better and more efficiently together, with joint procurement ensuring standardisation and simplifying permitting and reporting requirements.

Our focus is clear: next week, the Commission and the High Representative will present the white paper on defence, and the European Council will continue to take work forward to build our deterrence, to strengthen the security of our continent.

Competitiveness and defence must go hand in hand to boost Europe's industrial and technological competitiveness, to create jobs, reinforcing economic convergence and the resilience of our value-chain networks. It is the right way to make the European Union stronger. It is the right way to protect our citizens and our social model.

Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Mesdames et Messieurs, les efforts que nous d脙漏ployons pour d脙漏penser davantage en faveur de la d脙漏fense europ脙漏enne profiteront 脙漏galement 脙聽 l芒聙聶Ukraine. La s脙漏curit脙漏 de l芒聙聶Ukraine et celle de l芒聙聶Europe sont indissociables. Les meilleures garanties de s脙漏curit脙漏 que nous pouvons apporter sont le renforcement de la capacit脙漏 de l芒聙聶Ukraine 脙聽 assurer sa d脙漏fense et la construction d'une Europe de la d脙漏fense cr脙漏dible et solide.

La semaine derni脙篓re, nous avons 脙漏galement charg脙漏 le Conseil de travailler rapidement 脙聽 de nouvelles initiatives visant 脙聽 r脙漏pondre aux besoins urgents de l芒聙聶Ukraine en mati脙篓re militaire et de d脙漏fense. Dans le m脙陋me temps, nous nous pr脙漏parons 脙聽 soutenir l芒聙聶Ukraine lorsqu芒聙聶elle aura d脙漏cid脙漏 d芒聙聶entamer des n脙漏gociations, afin de l芒聙聶aider 脙聽 parvenir 脙聽 une paix globale, juste et durable. Nous nous tenons, ainsi que de nombreux partenaires, aux c脙麓t脙漏s de l芒聙聶Ukraine.

Vendredi, j芒聙聶ai organis脙漏 une vid脙漏oconf脙漏rence avec la pr脙漏sidente de la Commission europ脙漏enne, la haute repr脙漏sentante et des partenaires de l芒聙聶OTAN tels que les 脙聣tats-Unis, le Canada, la Turquie, la Norv脙篓ge et l芒聙聶Islande. La r脙漏union extraordinaire du Conseil europ脙漏en venait de se terminer, et nos amis ont eu des r脙漏actions tr脙篓s positives en ce qui concerne nos d脙漏cisions, notre rapidit脙漏, ainsi que l芒聙聶engagement et l芒聙聶int脙漏r脙陋t qui sont les n脙麓tres d脙篓s lors qu芒聙聶il s芒聙聶agit de renforcer, toujours plus, notre coop脙漏ration.

Nous devons garder le rythme. Lors du Conseil europ脙漏en qui se tiendra la semaine prochaine, nous poursuivrons les discussions et prendrons des d脙漏cisions suppl脙漏mentaires sur la comp脙漏titivit脙漏, la prosp脙漏rit脙漏 partag脙漏e de l芒聙聶Europe et l芒聙聶autonomie strat脙漏gique europ脙漏enne. Nous sommes tous conscients de l芒聙聶urgence qu芒聙聶il y a 脙聽 combler notre retard en mati脙篓re d芒聙聶innovation, de productivit脙漏 et d芒聙聶investissement. Le r脙漏tablissement de la comp脙漏titivit脙漏 de l芒聙聶Europe doit donc rester au premier rang de nos priorit脙漏s. Il s芒聙聶agira d芒聙聶une question essentielle lors de la prochaine r脙漏union du Conseil europ脙漏en, et je reviendrai devant ce Parlement pour rendre compte de nos discussions.

Senhora Presidente, Senhoras Deputadas e Senhores Deputados, hoje n脙拢o 脙漏 apenas o terceiro anivers脙隆rio da Declara脙搂脙拢o de Versalhes, 脙漏 tamb脙漏m o quinquag脙漏simo anivers脙隆rio da primeira reuni脙拢o do Conselho Europeu, em mar脙搂o de 1975 芒聙聰 e 脙漏 tamb脙漏m o meu cent脙漏simo dia nestas fun脙搂脙碌es.

Cem dias em que se tornou claro que a Europa da Defesa e a Europa da Prosperidade Partilhada s脙拢o as prioridades deste mandato e s脙拢o prioridades interdependentes.

Cem dias em que se tornou claro que refor脙搂ar o papel da Europa no mundo 脙漏 uma prioridade estrat脙漏gica. Porque o atual contexto nos impele a alargar a nossa rede de parceiros, uma rede que j脙隆 脙漏 vasta, mas uma rede que tem tudo para poder crescer: nos acordos de com脙漏rcio, na coopera脙搂脙拢o para o desenvolvimento e na lideran脙搂a das grandes causas globais, como o Pacto para o Futuro ou o combate 脙聽s altera脙搂脙碌es clim脙隆ticas. Porque a Uni脙拢o Europeia 脙漏 um parceiro fi脙隆vel, previs脙颅vel e atrativo e muitos pa脙颅ses t脙陋m muita vontade de colaborar connosco a todos os n脙颅veis.

Por 脙潞ltimo, cem dias num esp脙颅rito de excelente coopera脙搂脙拢o interinstitucional.脗聽Thank you, dear Roberta, danke sch脙露n, liebe Ursula.

As institui脙搂脙碌es europeias e os Estados-Membros t脙陋m de estar mais unidos do que nunca para refor脙搂ar a Uni脙拢o Europeia. Para avan脙搂ar, precisamos de ter mais confian脙搂a em n脙鲁s pr脙鲁prios, mais confian脙搂a no nosso poder coletivo, mais unidade para enfrentar uma nova era geopol脙颅tica.

N脙拢o h脙隆 d脙潞vida de que estamos a obter resultados mais rapidamente, de que a Uni脙拢o est脙隆 mais forte pela seguran脙搂a e pela paz e pela prosperidade na Ucr脙垄nia, pela seguran脙搂a e pela paz e pela prosperidade na Europa.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicola Procaccini, on behalf of the ECR Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, now I deserve an extra minute because of the discomfort you cause me.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 I understand, I understand!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicola Procaccini (ECR). 芒聙聯 So thank you, President. The European Council represents a step in the journey back to reality, which we welcome.

Yes, we would have liked greater clarity on the sources of financing for the plan, on long-term planning and on its integration within the overall framework of the Atlantic Alliance.

Considering rearmed EU retaliation against Trump and the USA is a tragic mistake, just as it would be to see it as a concession to the Trump administration. Investing in defence and security is an act of dignity for our nations, and the mark of respect for our international allies.

It was Obama and Biden before Trump who asked Europe not to rely solely on the sacrifices of the American working class. And what was Europe's response? The Green Deal, a massively expensive welfare system, tariffs on American cars, and some moral lessons towards those who ensured our security with their own money and even their own blood.

Macron is right when he says that imagining the European Union as the only herbivorous power in a world of carnivores is pure utopia. The problem is that we don't stop breeding the carnivores. Look at the European imports of liquefied natural gas from Russia. Do you know how much France alone has increased its LNG purchases in the past year? By 81脗聽%! Money that Putin uses for the missiles, with which he bombs Ukraine daily.

When Trump says that the European Union has given more money to Putin than to Zelenskyy, he is telling the truth. I hope this hypocrisy ends once and for all.

The Italian Prime Minister is right. Let us not call it ReArmEU, but DefendEU because the peace and freedom we enjoy must be defended. And defence is not only about weapons but also about strategic infrastructure, raw materials, communication systems and technological innovations.

Meloni is right again, and not only her, when she states that the European defence detached from American allies and NATO is unthinkable.

Politicians and legislatures come and go, but the West remains and must remain for a long time. We are bound by a common path and a common destiny. As they say in Latin, "Simul stabunt, simul cadent".

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Val脙漏rie Hayer, au nom du groupe Renew. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Madame la Pr脙漏sidente de la Commission, chers coll脙篓gues, Monsieur le Ministre, qui aurait pu penser, il y a quelques semaines, que les 脙聣tats-Unis soutiendraient 脙聽 l芒聙聶ONU, avec la Chine et la Cor脙漏e du Nord, une r脙漏solution russe contre l芒聙聶Ukraine? Qui aurait pu penser que son pr脙漏sident dirait un jour qu芒聙聶il r脙漏cup脙漏rerait le Groenland, territoire europ脙漏en, 脗芦d芒聙聶une mani脙篓re ou d芒聙聶une autre脗禄? Ce qui est en train de se passer outre-Atlantique, ce n芒聙聶est pas un simple d脙漏sengagement; c芒聙聶est un renversement complet d芒聙聶alliances. Je ne pensais pas dire cela un jour, mais aujourd芒聙聶hui les faits sont l脙聽: dans un monde o脙鹿, depuis vingt脗聽ans, la Russie et la Chine augmentent massivement leurs d脙漏penses de d脙漏fense, la Maison-Blanche, sous Donald脗聽Trump, n芒聙聶est plus notre alli脙漏e. Notre monde est de plus en plus hostile, et nous devons en tirer deux cons脙漏quences.

La premi脙篓re, c芒聙聶est que nous devons savoir nous d脙漏fendre tout seuls. Alors, Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, achetons europ脙漏en, investissons europ脙漏en et produisons europ脙漏en. Oui, cent fois oui, 脙聽 ces 800脗聽milliards d芒聙聶euros, 脙聽 sortir la d脙漏fense de nos r脙篓gles budg脙漏taires et m脙陋me 脙聽 un emprunt commun. Oui 脙聽 une pr脙漏f脙漏rence europ脙漏enne, faute de quoi notre argent ira encore alimenter d芒聙聶autres 脙漏conomies que la n脙麓tre. Sans pr脙漏f脙漏rence europ脙漏enne, pas d芒聙聶autonomie strat脙漏gique et donc pas de souverainet脙漏.

Toutefois, il faut aussi dire les choses: on aura besoin de plus. Il faudra donc travailler tr脙篓s vite, Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, aux obligations europ脙漏ennes propos脙漏s par le Parlement et par un certain nombre de gouvernements en Europe. Il faudra aussi travailler aux modalit脙漏s de mobilisation de l芒聙聶脙漏pargne des Europ脙漏ens. L芒聙聶enjeu strat脙漏gique, 脙漏videmment, c芒聙聶est l芒聙聶argent, mais pas uniquement. La coordination sera cl脙漏. Il faut que nos arm脙漏es et notre million de soldats europ脙漏ens soient capables de travailler ensemble. Il faut aussi que nous avancions sur le b脙漏n脙漏fice europ脙漏en de la dissuasion nucl脙漏aire, sans bien s脙禄r remettre en cause la d脙漏cision souveraine nationale de l芒聙聶activer. C芒聙聶est 脙聽 ces seules conditions que nous pr脙漏serverons la paix pour nos concitoyens.

Il est 脙漏galement urgent, pour assurer notre s脙漏curit脙漏, de continuer 脙聽 soutenir l芒聙聶Ukraine, parce que la s脙漏curit脙漏 des Ukrainiens, c芒聙聶est aussi notre s脙漏curit脙漏. La d脙漏cision de Donald脗聽Trump d芒聙聶arr脙陋ter les livraisons d芒聙聶armements et le partage de renseignements ne nous rappelle qu芒聙聶une chose: nous devons reprendre notre destin en main, continuer 脙聽 essayer de convaincre l芒聙聶administration am脙漏ricaine qu芒聙聶elle fait une erreur, 脙漏videmment, mais, pendant ce temps, acc脙漏l脙漏rer notre aide 脙聽 l芒聙聶Ukraine et 脙陋tre force de proposition pour offrir des garanties de s脙漏curit脙漏 脙聽 l芒聙聶Ukraine 芒聙聯脗聽et 脙聽 nous-m脙陋mes. Cela passe notamment par l芒聙聶envoi de troupes europ脙漏ennes pour le maintien de la paix, une fois un accord obtenu. Nous devons dissuader une fois pour toutes la menace russe.

La deuxi脙篓me cons脙漏quence 脙聽 tirer, c芒聙聶est que notre mod脙篓le d脙漏mocratique est attaqu脙漏: 脙聽 l芒聙聶est par les ing脙漏rences russes et 脙聽 l芒聙聶ouest par le camp Trump et les alli脙漏s de cette internationale r脙漏actionnaire, ici m脙陋me dans cet h脙漏micycle. Monsieur脗聽Procaccini, quand vous pr脙漏tendez que les 脙聣tats-Unis ont donn脙漏 davantage d芒聙聶argent et de soutien 脙聽 l芒聙聶Ukraine que l芒聙聶Europe, c芒聙聶est faux. Vous le savez parfaitement et vous reprenez en ce sens 脙聽 la fois la propagande de Trump et la propagande du Kremlin. Nous sommes aux c脙麓t脙漏s de l芒聙聶Ukraine, nous avons 脙漏t脙漏 aux c脙麓t脙漏s de l芒聙聶Ukraine depuis le d脙漏but et nous continuerons de l芒聙聶脙陋tre jusqu芒聙聶脙聽 l芒聙聶obtention de la paix 芒聙聯脗聽la paix n芒聙聶脙漏tant en aucun cas la capitulation.

Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, le bouclier d脙漏mocratique doit 脙陋tre une cl脙漏 de vo脙禄te de ce mandat, et nous attendons votre proposition avec impatience autant qu芒聙聶exigence; mais commen脙搂ons d脙漏j脙聽 par appliquer les lois qui existent d脙漏j脙聽. O脙鹿 en est l芒聙聶enqu脙陋te sur le DSA? Le principe fondamental du droit, c芒聙聶est que, quand on viole les r脙篓gles, il y a sanction, et c芒聙聶est votre responsabilit脙漏 d芒聙聶en assurer le respect.

Chers coll脙篓gues, l芒聙聶Europe est une puissance stabilisatrice. Notre objectif, c芒聙聶est la paix, l芒聙聶ordre international et la promotion de la d脙漏mocratie. Nous avons les atouts pour 脙陋tre une puissance politique et militaire. Il est temps de s芒聙聶assumer comme telle.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bas Eickhout, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, first of all, Mr Costa, I would like to thank you for the Council conclusions, and certainly on Ukraine, where you opted for a conclusion by 26 Member States and not 27. And I think that was a very good decision to do, and not every time try to weaken our conclusions in order to get all 27 on board.

However, this also means that the Council should act on this asset of Putin within the Council. Doing conclusions with only 26 is a first step, triggering Article 7 is really, really time to act on it.

Secondly, Madam President, I want to thank you for your ReArm proposal. And the Greens are fully on board and supporting you for a truly European defence and security Union. However, in your plan, we do have some questions and concerns.

First of all, triggering an escape clause. To be very honest, if you are triggering an escape clause in your fiscal rules continuously, maybe at a certain moment you need to wonder whether maybe the fiscal rules are not fit for purpose and you need a proper review instead of every time triggering an escape rule.

Secondly, and here I have to agree with Mr Weber, the European approach is not strong enough in your approach. Of the 800 billion, only 150 billion are European, the rest is national. Without any clear indication it will be a European approach. And even with the 150, only by doing it through a joint procurement, I'm not sure this will really have a proper European approach. We don't need more tanks on the Greek-Turkish border.

That brings me to the third point. Defence and security is much broader than arms and ammunition. This is about infrastructure. European infrastructure needs to be improved also to transport all the materials throughout Europe. This is about real infrastructure as well. This is about cyber security but this is also about energy independence. And Mr Procaccini, you said it already, looking at how much money we as Europe are spending on fossil dependency, EUR 400 billion each year, and we moved it from Russia to the United States. It's not helping anything. So the green transition is in the same agenda as the security agenda and should not be treated separately.

And that is why we are concerned that investments in green and social transition are being at the cost of investments in security, and therefore we need to increase both and we need to increase spending on both.

That brings me to the last point. The situation in Ukraine is very dire and very sincere, and we cannot not act and we cannot lack on action and have complacency. The next Council needs to act faster. We need to stand by Ukraine. We need peace in Europe. We need peace in Ukraine. We need peace on our own terms.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

VORSITZ: KATARINA BARLEY
Vizepr脙陇sidentin

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Martin Schirdewan, im Namen der Fraktion The Left. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Donald Trump ist bereit, die Ukraine Russland zum Fra脙聼 vorzuwerfen. Die Zerst脙露rung des V脙露lkerrechts und eine internationale Ordnung, in der nicht mehr die St脙陇rke des Rechts, sondern das Recht des St脙陇rkeren G脙录ltigkeit hat, sind dabei eingepreist. Umso mehr r脙陇cht sich jetzt, Frau von der Leyen, dass Sie die Diplomatie in den letzten Jahren leider so sch脙陇ndlich ignoriert haben und einzig und allein auf eine milit脙陇rische L脙露sung des Krieges in der Ukraine gesetzt haben.

Keine einzige diplomatische Initiative wurde durch die Kommission unterst脙录tzt, weder die Bem脙录hungen der T脙录rkei, Brasiliens, Chinas, Israels, der afrikanischen Staaten, nicht einmal die des Papstes. Und ja, es handelt sich um ein massives politisches Versagen europ脙陇ischer Politik. Und auch jetzt in Ihrer Rede脗聽芒聙聯 aber auch in Ihrer Rede, Herr Costa脗聽芒聙聯 kein einziges Wort dazu, dass die Kommission oder der Rat sich der Diplomatie zuwenden wollen, die doch vor allem und zuallererst der Ukraine dienen w脙录rde.

Die transatlantische Partnerschaft existiert nicht mehr. Trump 脙录berzieht die Welt mit einem massiven Handelskrieg und macht seinen Frieden mit Diktatoren 脙聽 la Putin, w脙陇hrend europ脙陇ische Politiker am Nasenring durch die Manege des Wei脙聼en Hauses gezogen werden. Doch hier bei Ihnen dominiert weiterhin ausschlie脙聼lich milit脙陇risches Denken. Pl脙露tzlich gibt es 800脗聽Milliarden mehr f脙录r Aufr脙录stung, keine Schuldenbremse mehr脗聽芒聙聯 Geld, das garantiere ich, das vor allem in amerikanische Waffen flie脙聼en wird.

Aber strategische Unabh脙陇ngigkeit bedeutet doch, f脙录r verantwortungsvolle Politik endlich Schluss mit der Schuldenbremse zu machen und massive Investitionen in Industrie und in digitale Infrastruktur vorzunehmen. Sie bedeutet Energiesicherheit, soziale Sicherheit und Forschung und Entwicklung. Jetzt ist die Gelegenheit, die kl脙录gsten K脙露pfe nach Europa zu locken. Der europ脙陇ische Digitalmarkt darf sich nicht l脙陇nger in den H脙陇nden von Elon Musk und anderen Tech-Faschos aus den USA befinden.

Die NATO ist doch jetzt schon Geschichte. Die EU braucht eine eigene Sicherheitsarchitektur, bei der Diplomatie immer Vorrang vor milit脙陇rischen L脙露sungen haben muss. Das widerspricht nicht der Idee notwendiger Verteidigungsf脙陇higkeit, das widerspricht aber fundamental den Profitinteressen der R脙录stungskonzerne und ihrer Gro脙聼aktion脙陇re: Damit niemand am Krieg verdient, muss die europ脙陇ische R脙录stungsindustrie verstaatlicht werden.

Und Sie sollten sich gerade jetzt f脙录r die St脙陇rkung internationaler Organisationen wie der UNO einsetzen und das V脙露lkerrecht verteidigen. Doch auch dazu kein einziges Wort, weder in Ihrer Rede, Frau von der Leyen, noch in Ihrer Rede, Herr Costa.

Ja, Selbstbestimmung und Souver脙陇nit脙陇t stehen der Ukraine zu. Doch nicht nur bei Russland, sondern auch bei der T脙录rkei und Israel m脙录ssen V脙露lkerrechtsbr脙录che klar kritisiert werden. Internationales Recht gilt auch f脙录r Freunde, nicht nur f脙录r erkl脙陇rte Gegner. Wer Glaubw脙录rdigkeit will, muss Doppelstandards 脙录berwinden.

Und strategische Unabh脙陇ngigkeit bedeutet nicht zuletzt auch ein besseres Verh脙陇ltnis zu China. Frau von der Leyen, die EU-Kommission muss endlich aufh脙露ren, Weltpolitik mit der strategischen Weitsicht eines Kindes zu betreiben.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Zsuzsanna Borvend脙漏g, a ESN k脙漏pvisel脜聭csoport nev脙漏ben. 芒聙聯 Eln脙露k Asszony! Tudj脙隆k, mit nevezett Arthur Koestler z脙隆rt rendszernek? Egy olyan gondolkod脙隆si s脙漏m脙隆t, amely kiz脙隆rja a realit脙隆st 脙漏s a logikai 脙露sszef脙录gg脙漏seket az 脙漏rvel脙漏sb脜聭l. 脙聣rzelmi alapon 脙隆ll, 脙漏s figyelmen k脙颅v脙录l hagyja a j脙鲁zan 脙漏szt 脙漏s a val脙鲁s脙隆got. Amikor 脙聳n脙露k a k脙露r脙录lm脙漏nyek v脙隆ltoz脙隆s脙隆t脙鲁l f脙录ggetlen脙录l Ukrajna tov脙隆bbi t脙露retlen t脙隆mogat脙隆s脙隆t er脜聭ltetik, ebben a z脙隆rt rendszerben gondolkodnak. A realit脙隆sok tagad脙隆s脙隆val el脙漏rt脙漏k azt, hogy 脙潞gy t脜卤nik, Eur脙鲁pa kimarad a b脙漏kerendez脙漏sb脜聭l 脙漏s ez脙隆ltal az 脙漏rdekszf脙漏r脙隆k 脙潞jrafeloszt脙隆s脙隆b脙鲁l is.

Ideje lenne szemben脙漏zni脙录k a val脙鲁s脙隆ggal. Magyarorsz脙隆got 脙漏vek 脙鲁ta sz脙漏gyenpadra 脙录ltetik jog脙隆llamis脙隆gi probl脙漏m脙隆k 脙漏s a transzparencia hi脙隆nya miatt. Ukrajna kapcs脙隆n ez mi脙漏rt nem zavarja 脙聳n脙露ket? Demokr脙隆ci脙隆r脙鲁l nem besz脙漏lhet脙录nk, illegitim az eln脙露ke, mindennaposak az emberi jogokat s脙漏rt脜聭, er脜聭szakos k脙漏nyszersoroz脙隆sok, a kisebbs脙漏gi jogok tipr脙隆sa lassan n脙漏pirt脙隆sba fordul, m脙漏gsem hallok jog脙隆llamis脙隆gi aggodalmakat.

Ahelyett, hogy 脙潞jabb csillag脙隆szati 脙露sszegeket szavaznak meg a h脙隆bor脙潞ra, ki kellene vizsg脙隆lni a transzparencia jegy脙漏ben, hogy az eddig Ukrajn脙隆ba k脙录ld脙露tt eur脙鲁milli脙隆rdokb脙鲁l mennyi ker脙录lt oligarch脙隆k kez脙漏be. Tiszt脙隆zni kell, hogy az oda 脙漏rkez脜聭 fegyverek egy r脙漏sze val脙鲁ban olyan terrorszervezetekn脙漏l k脙露t脙露tt-e ki, mint a Hamasz vagy az ISIS, esetleg a mexik脙鲁i drogkartell is profit脙隆lt-e bel脜聭le? L脙漏pjenek ki elm脙漏j脙录k z脙隆rt rendszer脙漏b脜聭l, 脙漏s m脙漏rj脙漏k fel a realit脙隆sokat, miel脜聭tt Ukrajna EU-s 脙漏s NATO tags脙隆g脙隆r脙鲁l fant脙隆zi脙隆lnak, mert Eur脙鲁pa v脙漏gzetes elgyeng脙录l脙漏s脙漏vel 脙漏s a harmadik vil脙隆gh脙隆bor脙潞val j脙隆tszanak!

脗聽脗聽Andrzej Halicki (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! To bardzo wa脜录ne dni i bardzo wa脜录ny moment. To moment, na kt脙鲁ry czekaj脛聟 te脜录 obywatele, 脜录eby przesta脛聡 ju脜录 rozmawia脛聡, tkwi脛聡 w nieustannych sporach politycznych. Trzeba dzia脜聜a脛聡. Bezpiecze脜聞stwo wymaga dzisiaj szybkiego dzia脜聜ania, bo na to czekaj脛聟 obywatele. I patriotyzm nie polega ju脜录 dzisiaj na gadaniu. Dzi脜聸 patriot脛聟 jest ten, kto szybko uruchamia 脜聸rodki, projekty, kt脙鲁re s脜聜u脜录脛聟 budowaniu bezpiecze脜聞stwa. Nie ma prawa nazywa脛聡 si脛聶 patriot脛聟 ten, kto proces ten b脛聶dzie spowalnia脜聜, blokowa脜聜 albo g脜聜osowa脜聜 przeciw. I to nie jest te脜录 moment, w kt脙鲁rym mo脜录emy prowadzi脛聡 proceduralne spory.

Apeluj脛聶 z tego miejsca do Parlamentu o podj脛聶cie bardzo szybkiej decyzji i uruchomienie 脜聸rodk脙鲁w z silnym mandatem dla Komisji Europejskiej i z silnym mandatem dla rz脛聟d脙鲁w i naszych lider脙鲁w. Tak, dzisiaj te projekty musimy uruchamia脛聡 jak najszybciej i tempo podejmowania decyzji jest kluczowe. Obywatele czekaj脛聟 nie tylko na uruchomienie miliard脙鲁w na przemys脜聜 zbrojeniowy, ale tak脜录e na wzmocnienie infrastruktury granicznej. Lata stracili脜聸my, 脜录eby przekaza脛聡 w tym kierunku pieni脛聟dze. Dzi脜聸 pierwsze euro pop脜聜yn脛聶脜聜y, ale to kropla w morzu potrzeb.

Potrzebny jest projekt Tarcza Wsch脙鲁d. To nie jest projekt polskiego rz脛聟du. To jest projekt wzmacniania bezpiecze脜聞stwa ca脜聜ej Europy, od Finlandii po Rumuni脛聶. On musi mie脛聡 europejskie wsparcie. Potrzebne s脛聟 脜聸rodki na projekty tak zwanego podw脙鲁jnego przeznaczenia, bo szko脜聜y, szpitale, parkingi mog脛聟 by脛聡 kluczowe tak脜录e dla bezpiecze脜聞stwa, i te projekty musz脛聟 mie脛聡 wsparcie europejskich 脜聸rodk脙鲁w, europejskich funduszy.

Obywatele czekaj脛聟 na nasz rozs脛聟dek i szybkie decyzje. Jeszcze raz apeluj脛聶: mniej spor脙鲁w, mniej gadania, mniej biurokracji, mniej debat i rezolucji. Szybsze wsp脙鲁lne decyzje, by mo脜录na by脜聜o uruchomi脛聡 proponowane miliardy przez Komisj脛聶 Europejsk脛聟 dla bezpiecze脜聞stwa naszych obywateli.

脗聽脗聽Tam脙隆s Deutsch (PfE). 芒聙聯 Eln脙露k Asszony! Eur脙鲁p脙隆ban az emberek b脙漏k脙漏t akarnak, Amerik脙隆ban az emberek b脙漏k脙漏t akarnak. Az USA patri脙鲁ta vezet脙漏se ez脙漏rt elk脙露telezetten b脙漏kep脙隆rti. Az eur脙鲁pai vezet脜聭k viszont tov脙隆bbra is a h脙隆bor脙潞t akarj脙隆k folytatni. Ez t脙露bb, mint b脜卤n. Ez hiba. Br脙录sszel eddigi Ukrajna strat脙漏gi脙隆ja megbukott. Az eur脙鲁pai vezet脜聭k h脙隆bor脙潞s usz脙颅t脙隆sa, a hangzatos sz脙鲁noklatok, a szankci脙鲁s politika biztos siker脙漏r脜聭l, az orosz agresszor katonai t脙漏rdre k脙漏nyszer脙颅t脙漏s脙漏r脜聭l eddig m脙隆r 134 milli脙隆rd eur脙鲁 elk脙露lt脙漏s脙漏t jelentett脙漏k a h脙隆bor脙潞ra, 脙漏s mindez csak gazdas脙隆gi bajokat, puszt脙颅t脙隆st 脙漏s szenved脙漏st hozott.

A h脙隆bor脙潞p脙隆rtiak r脙隆ad脙隆sul m脙漏g ford脙颅tva is 脙录lnek a d脙露gl脙露tt lovon. Donald Trump vil脙隆gosan megmondta, Amerika kisz脙隆ll a h脙隆bor脙潞b脙鲁l, 脙漏s b脙漏k脙漏t fog teremteni, pont. Teh脙隆t, amikor m脙隆r az amerikaiaknak sem kell a proxy h脙隆bor脙潞juk, akkor ezt a levetett amerikai h脙隆bor脙潞s hacik脙隆t akarj脙隆k a h脙隆bor脙潞p脙隆rti vezet脜聭k Eur脙鲁p脙隆ra k脙漏nyszer脙颅teni. Az eddig elk脙露lt脙露tt eur脙鲁milli脙隆rdok ut脙隆n, imm脙隆r b脙隆rmennyi p脙漏nzzel finansz脙颅rozni akarj脙隆k az ukrajnai 脙露ld脙露kl脙漏st. Amerika helyett is, s脜聭t az amerikai b脙漏ket脙露rekv脙漏seket megakad脙隆lyozand脙鲁 is. Br脙录sszel vezet脜聭i r脙隆ad脙隆sul n脙露velik a bajt, megfejelik ezt azzal, hogy m脙隆r Ukrajna gyors脙颅tott uni脙鲁s csatlakoz脙隆s脙隆t mantr脙隆zz脙隆k, ami egyenesen 脙露sszed脙露nten脙漏 az eur脙鲁pai gazdas脙隆got. Ezt a h脙隆bor脙潞t le kell z脙隆rni, 脙漏s lez脙隆rni csak t脙隆rgyal脙隆sokkal lehet.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Patryk Jaki (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Ministers, there is no doubt who is the aggressor and who is the victim. The aggressor is Russia and the victim is Ukraine. There is no doubt that no reset with Russia has ever succeeded and never will. The only thing that can stop Russia is credible force, and the general direction of strengthening European arms capabilities is good.

However, you will not escape from the fundamental questions. You are talking about the security of Europe. Great. I would like to ask you how the security of Europe is served by cancelling elections in Romania and not allowing the candidate with 50脗聽% of support to run? How does allowing Tusk to destroy the opposition, take away all public money from the opposition party, and imprison MEPs serve Europe's security? How do your immigration policy and the Migration Pact serve European security? And the key question is: do you want Europe to defend itself from external threats, or for Europeans to kill each other? Instead of integrating Europe in the face of threats, as usual you choose your particular interests.

If the strength of Ukraine grew from the number of your empty words, the Ukrainian army now would be preparing to parade in Moscow. Do you want to help? You have EUR脗聽300脗聽billion of frozen Russian assets. Give them to Ukraine. That will be enough for five years of war. But you won't do it. You keep it because you want them to use to negotiate further resets with Russia. That is why you keep attacking America, provoking it to leave Europe. And you yourselves do not have those military capabilities. Your behaviour will cause the EU countries that border Russia to be in great danger.脗聽President Zelenskyy found about this after the summit in London. He will not provide the army weapons with your slogans, and that's why we need to change it.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Billy Kelleher (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, the EU is a beacon of hope for millions of people across the globe. It's why Ukrainians broke down in tears when they crossed into our Union after Putin's immoral invasion of their country. And it's why migrants do everything to get here, to live and raise their families. And that's why Putin and other tinpot dictators despise us. We give people hope, while they only give people fear.脗聽It's why it must be defended.

'If something is worth building, it is worth defending' 芒聙聯 these are the words is Se脙隆n Lemass, former Irish Taoiseach and leader who submitted Ireland's application to join the European Economic Community. My country is militarily neutral. We won't join a military alliance, but that doesn't mean we don't take sides. We are on the side of freedom, democracy, multilateralism, and a global order based on international law.

Ireland is lucky. We are an island in the Atlantic, surrounded by non-threatening nations. But I know my friends and colleagues along the eastern border of our Union live in fear of the return of Russian imperialism. I see the worry in their eyes. I hear the pain in my friends' voices when they speak about living under Russian occupation before independence. That is why I will not stand in the way in terms of defending your homelands and your homes.

And Europe must collectively defend itself. Our unity is our strength. This is a big change, and I know I will be castigated by the extremists at home, on the left and on the right, but it is morally the right thing to do. Ireland will not join NATO, but I firmly believe we must ensure our fellow Member States have the resources needed to defend their sovereignty and their values.

We must have defence bonds. We must make it easier for the states to strengthen their militaries by relaxing Stability and Growth Pact rules. We must all work together on sharing intelligence and ensuring interoperability and defence systems and energy security. Ireland must use every possibility under our constitution to support our European friends: humanitarian aid training, non-lethal weapons, support for demining initiatives, financial support and increasing our own military defence capabilities.

I will say it again, if it is worth building, it is worth defending. There has never been a greater force for peace, prosperity and progress than the European Union and we must defend this.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Petras Gra脜戮ulis (ESN), pak脛聴lus m脛聴lyn脛聟j脛聟 kortel脛聶 pateiktas klausimas. 芒聙聯 Gerbiamas prane脜隆脛聴jau, daug skambi脜鲁 脜戮od脜戮i脜鲁, bet labai ma脜戮ai darb脜鲁 Ukrainos at脜戮vilgiu i脜隆 valdan脛聧iosios daugumos ir i脜隆 Europos. Kod脛聴l po 脜隆iai dienai Ukraina nepriimta 脛炉 Europos S脛聟jung脛聟? Kas, Amerika kalta? Ar Rusija kalta? Kod脛聴l j脜芦s nei脜隆girdot Zelenskio per tok脛炉 laikotarp脛炉? Gal i脜隆girsit ir priimsit?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Billy Kelleher (Renew), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 Well, we have been very supportive of Ukraine's application to join the European Union and it has happened over many, many years. In fact, in recent times we have supported them financially. They have been supported militarily. And of course, you are promoting the propaganda from Russia and elsewhere saying quite clearly that the European Union does not support. But if you look at it, and if you listen to President Zelenskyy himself, he says quite clearly and categorically that the European Union has been a friend to Ukraine and a support them both financially, militarily and in humanitarian assistance as well. So I reject your points out of hand, as they don't stack up with the evidence.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽M脛聛rti脜聠脜隆 Sta脛路is (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Europe is a giant in chains: wealthy, powerful, yet paralysed. Objective reality and data show that there is no need to despair. Quite the opposite. We have given Ukraine EUR脗聽134脗聽billion. That sounds like a lot, but it's just 0.2脗聽% of our GDP. If the US steps back, we can raise that to 0.4脗聽% without difficulty. The Baltics, Finland and Denmark already contributed nearly 1脗聽% 芒聙聯 a small price for lasting security 芒聙聯 and our economies have only become stronger as a result.

We can make Russia pay. Let's seize 200脗聽billion of frozen Russian assets and direct them to Ukraine. Madam脗聽President, the European Commission must make a decision on this right now.

Dear colleagues, the numbers don't lie. We are 450脗聽million Europeans. Russia has just 140脗聽million people. Our economy is 12 times larger. We have everything we need to defend ourselves and others. The only step left: removing the chains we have placed on ourselves.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Botenga (The Left). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, 800 miljard euro: niet om de salarissen of pensioenen te verhogen, niet om de wachtlijsten in de gezondheidszorg weg te werken, en ook niet om in elk dorp eindelijk stipte bussen en treinen te garanderen. Nee, voor wapens.

Laat me even wat cijfers voorleggen. We geven nu al 2 tot 3 keer meer uit aan oorlog dan Rusland. Deze week verklaarde de Duitse pers dat we over vier keer zo veel oorlogsschepen, drie keer zo veel gevechtstanks, drie keer zo veel artillerie en twee keer zo veel gevechtsvliegtuigen beschikken als Rusland. Ondertussen slaagt Rusland er gelukkig niet eens in om een kwart van Oekra脙炉ne te bezetten. De Russische tanks zullen dus niet meteen op de Grote Markt van Brussel staan.

Aan al wie vandaag zegt dat we meer wapens en meer soldaten nodig hebben, wil ik de vraag stellen 芒聙聯 en aan mevrouw Van der Leyen 芒聙聯 wie zal straks zijn zonen en kinderen naar het front sturen? Hou op met die oorlogskoorts en onzin. We hebben meer investeringen nodig in diplomatie. We moeten meer co脙露rdineren, niet meer wapens financieren.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ewa Zaj脛聟czkowska-Hernik (ESN). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Szanowni Pa脜聞stwo! Nowy militarny eurod脜聜ug b脛聶dzie narz脛聶dziem nacisku na pa脜聞stwa i zagro脜录eniem dla ich suwerenno脜聸ci. To b脛聶dzie dok脜聜adnie ten sam mechanizm jak w przypadku KPO. Je脜录eli spe脜聜nimy kamienie milowe, a rz脛聟d b脛聶dzie podoba脜聜 si脛聶 Komisji Europejskiej, to dostaniemy jakie脜聸 pieni脛聟dze. To nic innego jak polityczny szanta脜录. Co b脛聶dziemy musieli odda脛聡 w zamian za te pieni脛聟dze? Jakie b脛聶dzie oprocentowanie zaci脛聟gni脛聶tych po脜录yczek? Jedyne co potraficie, to zaci脛聟ga脛聡 po脜录yczki i prze脜录era脛聡 je na unijn脛聟 machin脛聶. Planeta ju脜录 nie p脜聜onie, 脜录e chcecie stawia脛聡 fabryki i produkowa脛聡 bro脜聞? Zmarnowali脜聸cie lata na Zielony 脜聛ad i obudzili脜聸cie si脛聶 z r脛聶k脛聟 w nocniku. Chomika bym wam nie da脜聜a pod opiek脛聶, a co dopiero armi脛聶, widz脛聟c, jak obalacie demokracj脛聶 w Rumunii. Potrzebujemy pokoju, a nie wojny. Bezpiecze脜聞stwo kraju i jego suwerenno脜聸脛聡 zale脜录y od jego potencja脜聜u militarnego, a nie wielkiej wsp脙鲁lnej unijnej armii. Ani skrawka wi脛聶cej naszej suwerenno脜聸ci dla Was. Za nasze pieni脛聟dze chcemy budowa脛聡 nasz脛聟 pot脛聶g脛聶 militarn脛聟, a nie 芒聙聯 Ukrainy.

脦卤脦陆脧聞脦卤脦鲁脧聣脦陆脦鹿脧聝脧聞脦颅脧聜 脧聞脦路脧聜. 脦聼脦戮脧聟脦鲁脧聦脦陆脦驴 脦碌脦炉脦陆脦卤脦鹿 脦路 脦麓脦鹿脦潞脦庐 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脦卤脧聟脧聞脦驴脧聞脦碌脦禄脦庐脧聜 脧聙脦卢脦禄脦路 脦鲁脦鹿脦卤 脧聞脦卤 脧聝脧聟脦录脧聠脦颅脧聛脦驴脦陆脧聞脦卤 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脧聞脦鹿脧聜 脦卤脦陆脦卢脦鲁脦潞脦碌脧聜 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脦碌脦陆脦卢脦陆脧聞脦鹿脦卤 脧聝脦碌 脦聲脧聟脧聛脧聣脧聙脦卤脧聤脦潞脦庐 脦聢脦陆脧聣脧聝脦路, 脦潞脧聟脦虏脦碌脧聛脦陆脦庐脧聝脦碌脦鹿脧聜 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦潞脦碌脧聠脦卢脦禄脦卤脦鹿脦驴.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Siegfried Mure脜聼an (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, President of the European Council, dear colleagues, unfortunately, our long standing partner the United States of America is disengaging from Europe. It is disengaging in terms of security and defence, it is disengaging in terms of trade and economy, and it is disengaging in terms of defending democracy, rule of law and human rights.

We do not want this to happen. We regret this. We will never turn our backs on the United States of America. But we need to recognise the new reality where the United States administration has other priorities.

When Russia is challenging us from the East, and the current Trump administration is questioning the defence of Europe from the West. The conclusion for us, Europe, is simple we have to defend ourselves. What matters most now is what we are going to do ourselves at European level. And I believe we have to do two things.

Firstly, continuing to support Ukraine. Stopping military support to Ukraine is not a way to peace, it is a way to help Russia. We should not do that. Keeping Ukraine safe means keeping Europe safe.

Secondly, we have to invest in defence. And I believe the EUR 150 billion common European project put forward by the European Commission is correct. But we have to do it right. When we spend European money, we have to support European projects. What projects we are going to finance matters a lot. We should use this European fund to do projects which none of the Member States can do alone, so that the citizens of Europe see the added value of Europe. We should do the Common European Air Defence Shield and other European projects, invest in European research, produce more oil in bigger quantities and at smaller unit prices. Same equipment for the militaries of all EU Member States. Europe can do a lot to make sure that our militaries are well equipped with this European project. And of course, if European money is spent, the European Parliament has to be involved.

Let me say, in the end, that investing in investing in our defence means keeping Europe safe. If we are weak, we are a target. If we are strong, we are safe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sven Mikser (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, colleagues, a wise person once said that tactics is the art of using personnel and arms to win battles, whereas strategy is the art of using battles to win wars. Much has been said about how we need to rebuild European defence and security in the medium- to long-term, and I agree with most of it. Meanwhile, the decisive battle for Europe's future is playing out on the front lines in Ukraine and needless to say, following the very dramatic reversal, of course, by our American allies, the outlook has changed to worse.

So we need to step out, and we need to fill the gap left by Americans. And we need to do it now. It's not a matter of months or years, it's a matter of hours, days, maximum weeks. If we fail in Ukraine, if Ukraine falls, then we will not merely be back to square one, we will be in a much, much darker place, starting to rebuild Europe's security from shambles. We must not let this happen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anna Bry脜聜ka (NI). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca, Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Unia Europejska pr脙鲁buje zaklina脛聡 rzeczywisto脜聸脛聡 kolejnymi prawami, regulacjami, a tak naprawd脛聶 znajduje si脛聶 w gigantycznym kryzysie przyw脙鲁dztwa, kryzysie gospodarczym i kryzysie bezpiecze脜聞stwa. Takie s脛聟 owoce Unii Europejskiej.

Komisja Europejska wykorzystuje wojn脛聶 na Ukrainie i kryzys w relacjach z administracj脛聟 ameryka脜聞sk脛聟 jako pretekst do przyspieszenia budowy europejskiego superpa脜聞stwa. Prawdziw脛聟 intencj脛聟 Komisji Europejskiej nie jest udzielenie pomocy ogarni脛聶tej wojn脛聟 Ukrainie czy rozwi脛聟zanie jakiegokolwiek europejskiego problemu. Kolejne problemy to tylko pretekst do zaw脜聜aszczania kolejnych kompetencji przez Uni脛聶. Mieli脜聸my ju脜录 propozycje zmiany unijnych traktat脙鲁w, reform脛聶 procesu decyzyjnego, w tym likwidacj脛聶 prawa weta, i po ostatnim posiedzeniu Rady Europejskiej dostajemy decyzj脛聶 o kolejnym eurod脜聜ugu, aby nast脛聶pnie w脜聜a脜聸nie tymi 脜聸rodkami szanta脜录owa脛聡 finansowo pa脜聞stwa narodowe, jak w przypadku krajowych plan脙鲁w odbudowy. Nie ma naszej zgody na przekazywanie nowych kompetencji Unii.

Teraz Unia rzekomo chce si脛聶 zajmowa脛聡 rozwojem przemys脜聜u zbrojeniowego w Europie. Najlepsza pomoc, jakiej mo脜录e udzieli脛聡 Europie ta Komisja i ta Izba, to powstrzymanie si脛聶 od jakiegokolwiek dzia脜聜ania. Europy nie da si脛聶 obroni脛聡 ani rezolucjami, ani scentralizowan脛聟 w脜聜adz脛聟 biurokracji. Dzisiaj potrzeba nam jak nigdy dot脛聟d silnych pa脜聞stw narodowych. To one maj脛聟 dzia脜聜a脛聡, to one maj脛聟 rozwija脛聡 sw脙鲁j przemys脜聜 militarny i budowa脛聡 narodow脛聟 armi脛聶, samodzielnie decydowa脛聡, czego im potrzeba i w jakich ilo脜聸ciach, a nie anonimowy urz脛聶dnik z Brukseli.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alexandr Vondra (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, European security is under immense strain. Panic and reactionary statements, especially on social media, only make matters worse. We must stay clear-eyed and strategically focused. European nations must recognise reality. The burden of defence now rests mostly on our own shoulders. We see clearly the signs of this shift. Governments are waking up. I am keeping my fingers crossed for Chancellor-elect Merz to find the financial resources for much-needed security enforcement.

We must act swiftly. Our defence industries need a framework that enables them to deliver, both through cooperation and national initiatives. That means cutting bureaucracy, simplifying the processes, providing real financial access and bringing energy prices down. We cannot have both an overambitious green transition and a fully capable military. Resources are finite. Europe must decide what it values most.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Dear colleague Gra脜戮ulis, I will explain it again. If I keep giving you blue cards now, with every speaker that comes, I cannot give them to other people. And there will be a point where I have to say no blue cards at all anymore.

This is why I decided to give every Member the opportunity to raise a blue card, but only once, because otherwise we cannot finish in time. We are limited in the end. If you do not agree, then please file a complaint to the President.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gerben-Jan Gerbrandy (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, Minister, Yes, we can!, Nous pouvons!, Wir schaffen das! After every major geopolitical shock in our young history, we have responded with the courage to share our sovereignties.

After 1945, we stopped the cycle of European civil war by merging our resources and our political destinies. And after 1989, we healed a divided nation, created a single citizenship, and proceeded to reunite a continent with parts that were caught behind the Iron Curtain.

And now, in 2025, Europeans are asking us to respond to the shock unfolding before our eyes. The rupture of the transatlantic alliance in the face of war on our continent. I believe we know what to do and how to do it. We can support Ukraine and its defence against Russian aggression. To replace the US, the EU would thus have to spend only another 0.12脗聽% of its GDP. We can build up a European defence industry worthy of the world's envy. We already have the technology. Now it's just a matter of investment and long-term contracts. We can jolt our economy into unprecedented growth and build up the euro as an alternative to the dollar as a global reserve currency.

It's a matter of breaking down jealously guarded national barriers, investing massively in our universities, issuing common European debt and opening up trade with all those countries looking for new leadership in the West.

And finally, we can build a European army in order to achieve lasting peace. We need a European Defence Force to secure and defend our continent, enabled by commonly procured European capabilities and led by a European commander. The only thing standing in our way is a giant Orb脙隆n shaped elephant in the room. But that too we can fix. We in the Parliament can provide ideas and political pressure. After that, Mr Costa and Mrs von der Leyen, the fate of Europe is in your capable hands. And remember. Yes, we can!

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bogdan Rzo脜聞ca (ECR), pytanie zadane przez podniesienie niebieskiej kartki. 芒聙聯 Chcia脜聜em Pana dopyta脛聡, poniewa脜录 bardzo uwa脜录nie s脜聜ucha脜聜em Pana wypowiedzi. Co Pan mia脜聜 na uwadze, m脙鲁wi脛聟c, 脜录e wi脛聶zi transatlantyckie zosta脜聜y zerwane? Kto zerwa脜聜 te wi脛聶zi? Kto zrywa? Ewentualnie kto jest winny zerwaniu tych wi脛聶zi transatlantyckich? I druga rzecz: gdy ju脜录 uzbieramy te pieni脛聟dze, za脜聜脙鲁脜录my, 脜录e one b脛聶d脛聟. Czy Pan dopuszcza tak脛聟 sytuacj脛聶, 脜录e b脛聶dziemy z tych pieni脛聶dzy kupowa脛聡 najnowsze technologie zbrojeniowe w Stanach Zjednoczonych?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gerben-Jan Gerbrandy (Renew), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 Well, for me, it's absolutely clear that Europe not at all wants to tear down the transatlantic alliance. It seems to be a unilateral decision now from the US to at least make it much weaker than it is.

If Article 5 is under pressure and it's not a total guarantee, then NATO is not as strong as it should be. It's a deterrence. It's not an aggressive force.

And yes, we have to rebuild our European defence industry. That's absolutely clear. We have to be able to build up our own military and not buying it somewhere else.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Diana Riba i Giner (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, la Uni脙鲁n Europea gasta un 1,9脗聽% de su PIB en defensa, casi el famoso 2脗聽%. 脗驴Y qu脙漏 significa esto? Voy a poner dos ejemplos para que se entienda mejor: en primer lugar, la Uni脙鲁n Europea gasta tres veces m脙隆s en defensa que Rusia y, en segundo lugar, la Uni脙鲁n Europea y el Reino Unido gastan en conjunto m脙隆s que potencias globales como China. De hecho, si Europa fuera un Estado, ser脙颅a la segunda potencia global en t脙漏rminos de gasto militar.

Se脙卤oras y se脙卤ores, el problema no es cu脙隆nto se gasta, sino c脙鲁mo se gasta. El dilema es que tenemos veintisiete sistemas defensivos diferentes sin una coordinaci脙鲁n efectiva. Somos ineficientes y esto no lo resolveremos con una lluvia de millones en los presupuestos nacionales; aunque esta lluvia de millones tal vez s脙颅 calme a Donald Trump y a la industria armament脙颅stica estadounidense.

Representantes de la Comisi脙鲁n, analicen c脙鲁mo podemos ser m脙隆s eficientes, coordinen desde la Uni脙鲁n Europea la estrategia y la inversi脙鲁n y hablemos sin tab脙潞s de c脙鲁mo financiar este esfuerzo, por ejemplo con impuestos europeos propios, impuestos a los m脙隆s ricos y a los que m脙隆s contaminan, porque, de lo contrario, sabemos que ser脙隆 la ciudadan脙颅a quien pagar脙隆 esta factura. Y esta es una l脙颅nea roja.

No podemos permitir que los presupuestos estatales o de la Uni脙鲁n Europea vean reducidas las partidas sociales y clim脙隆ticas, porque hace quince a脙卤os en la Uni脙鲁n Europea se recortaron derechos sociales en nombre de la austeridad. Hoy no lo har脙隆n en nombre de la guerra.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Danilo Della Valle (The Left). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, oggi tutto il Movimento cinque Stelle 脙篓 qui a Strasburgo con il suo leader e i suoi eletti nazionali ed europei: Le portiamo la voce del popolo, che 脙篓 stanco di questa guerra senza fine e vuole solo pace e prosperit脙聽.

Il vostro piano di riarmo 脙篓 una dichiarazione di guerra, non si sa a chi o a cosa, ma 脙篓 un attacco ai cittadini europei, perch脙漏 sposta miliardi di euro dal sociale alle armi, dalla sanit脙聽 alla militarizzazione.

Noi crediamo che sia indegno spendere 800 miliardi per il settore militare mentre le imprese fanno bancarotta, le famiglie non riescono a pagare le bollette e i giovani non fanno figli perch脙漏 non hanno i soldi per crescerli. Invece voi preferite compiacere le lobby delle armi.

Oggi, gli stessi leader che da tre anni a questa parte hanno sbagliato tutto, puntando sulla sconfitta militare della Russia e boicottando ogni iniziativa di pace, come quella in Turchia, ci dicono che dobbiamo continuare ad armarci inseguendo la follia bellicista.

Volete la Terza guerra mondiale per nascondere i vostri fallimenti: ma noi ve lo impediremo. Non un euro per le armi! Le armi non sono la soluzione ma l'ostacolo al raggiungimento della pace. L'Europa senza pace 脙篓 morta.

Per questo voglio lasciare la bandiera europea cucita con quella della pace: per non dimenticarlo mai. L'avrei lasciata alla presidente von der Leyen ma ancora una volta ha snobbato il dibattito parlamentare e questo luogo che, pi脙鹿 della Commissione, rappresenta la democrazia.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 There is a blue card.

(Mr脗聽Della Valle unfurled a flag)

Please, please come on. No blue card then.

I might remind you that this is probably against the rules. This is a parliament. It comes from parlare. Yes, I know it's the European flag and we love it.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Milan Uhr脙颅k (ESN). 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pani predsedaj脙潞ca, pani Leyenov脙隆, vy chcete nakupova脜楼 zbrane za 800 mili脙隆rd eur? Neskr脙陆vajte sa, pros脙颅m v脙隆s, tam v z脙隆kulis脙颅 a rad脜隆ej n脙隆m uk脙隆脜戮te, ako ste nakupovali vakc脙颅ny, preto脜戮e ja som presved脛聧en脙陆, 脜戮e pod va脜隆ou taktovkou bude n脙隆kup zbran脙颅 len 脛聫al脜隆脙颅m obrovsk脙陆m megapodvodom na Eur脙鲁panov.

A takisto by ma zauj脙颅malo, 脜戮e kto by mal s t脙陆mi v脜隆etk脙陆mi nak脙潞pen脙陆mi zbra脜聢ami 脙颅s脜楼 bojova脜楼, preto脜戮e pre Eur脙鲁panov to bude znamena脜楼 len 脛聫al脜隆iu militariz脙隆ciu, 脛聫al脜隆ie zadl脜戮ovanie, 脛聫al脜隆iu konsolid脙隆ciu a 脛聫al脜隆ie zdra脜戮ovanie. Mrz脙颅 ma, 脜戮e jedin脙陆, kto sa na samite postavil proti tomuto vojnov脙漏mu pl脙隆nu Leyenovej, bol op脙陇脜楼 iba ma脛聫arsk脙陆 premi脙漏r Viktor Orb脙隆n, 脜戮e v脜隆etci ostatn脙颅 芒聙聯 Macron, Scholz, Fiala, Fico a 脛聫al脜隆脙颅 芒聙聯 hlasovali za Leyenovej nav脙陆脜隆enie eur脙鲁pskej vojenskej a finan脛聧nej pomoci pre Zelensk脙漏ho namiesto toho, aby sa pridali k tomu Trumpovmu pl脙隆nu a zatla脛聧ili na Zelensk脙漏ho a dotla脛聧ili ho k mierov脙陆m rokovaniam. Zradili a sklamali t脙陆mto mnoh脙陆ch voli脛聧ov.

Ja viem garantova脜楼, 脜戮e my v hlasovan脙颅 Leyenovej vojensk脙陆 pl脙隆n nepodpor脙颅me a na脜隆ich voli脛聧ov nezrad脙颅me.
