脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脜陆eljana Zovko (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, the proposal endorsed by the European Council on 6 March is an encouraging sign towards the strengthening of European defence. A good sign in times of stormy weather.

However, the EU's much-needed wake-up should not make us forget that the American umbrella is vital for us. We still need the rainy-day friend, now more than ever. The strengthening of our defence must go hand in hand with the strengthening of our relations with our allies, including with the US.

Besides the fact that the current EU measures will only produce effects in at least four years, we have to bear in mind that the United States and Russia together account for 85脗聽% of the world's new nuclear arsenal. The European Union also also strongly needs American intelligence, with some Member States even relying entirely on NATO. And the relative autonomy from American intelligence would take years. However, time is running out now 芒聙聯 we all know in Ukraine what is going on.

For all these reasons, the EU must intensify its diplomatic efforts to find common grounds with our US ally. The EU defence and strengthening of a pillar of NATO can become robust within some years, but we don't have time to lose in not engaging in dialogue.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Thijs Reuten (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, colleagues, Council, Commission, Europe is at a critical juncture. You said it yourself, Minister. We are relevant now or never again to act or to be passive observers of the destruction of the rules-based order.

I commend the Member States for their clear understanding that Ukraine and Europe's future requires peace through strength 芒聙聯 strength for Ukraine to stand up against Putin and against Trump. The US is forcing Ukraine into an unacceptable surrender it could also achieve without their help. If the circumstances are unfavourable for Ukraine, for Europe, we will not accept it. We have to change these circumstances towards a just peace, or accept the ugly consequences.

Now President von脗聽der脗聽Leyen said that the Parliament was right, asking for more military support all along. Translate understanding then into real united action now, and for all the Member States, President Costa 芒聙聯 some a bit more than others 芒聙聯 that means sending Ukraine the weapons it needs now. It's irritating 芒聙聯 it's appalling even 芒聙聯 that there are still warehouses where there's critical air defence that Ukraine needs now. Do what is necessary now for Ukraine, for Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anders Vistisen (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, EUR 35 billion in loans for the Ukraine Recovery Mechanism. EUR 100 billion in Corona loans, EUR 750 billion for the recovery funds. And now the Commission proposes EUR 150 billion for rearmament.

But none of this would have been necessary if we had a Commission that was more preoccupied with creating growth instead of bureaucracy. If you had a Commission that was willing to prioritise within the EUR 1.2 trillion budget. Ursula von der Leyen, you are making the Europeans into welfare junkies and you're doing it from borrowed money.

So let me put this very simple. We want our money back!

N脙楼r vi ser p脙楼 det fra et dansk perspektiv, m脙楼 man sige, at EU har gjort meget lidt. Igen og igen har Danmark st脙赂ttet Ukraine milit脙娄rt og 脙赂konomisk, og igen og igen har vi set, hvordan de st脙赂rste EU medlemsstater; Tyskland, Frankrig, Italien og Spanien ikke har fulgt trop. S脙楼 det er let at give Trump skylden for den situation, Ukraine st脙楼r i. Men hvis man virkelig vil forst脙楼, hvorfor vi tre 脙楼r inde i krigen har et EU, der ikke er i stand til at forsvare sig selv, har et EU, der ikke er i stand til at forsvare Ukraine, s脙楼 skal vi se indad. Der har v脙娄ret alt for lidt handling og alt for meget snak, og det forts脙娄tter desv脙娄rre.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Assita Kanko (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, for energy we used to look at Russia; for trade we depend on China; for defence we rely on the United States. When we fail, we blame someone else. The truth is that we are weak because we neglected our potential power. Because by not investing enough, or soon enough, in defence and industry, we gave up the right to be respected. We abandoned our primary duty, which is to provide security and pride to our citizens.

They wonder when we will take responsibility. Right now, we are only excited because Trump woke us up. Basically, he just showed us that there is no such thing as a free lunch. And we are upset? The fact that we needed this wake-up call about defence is so disturbing for our citizens.

We need to remember that Europe was stronger, and can be strong again. We need to remember that still the end of the Cold War was not the end of history, that we cannot earn respect if we do not change our mindset.

We need to invest in our defence and values, and stand firm for our principles 芒聙聯 not only when it's comfortable, but also when it is not. Because to the question of whether our citizens can count on us, the answer must be 'yes'!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Cotrim De Figueiredo (Renew). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Presidente do Conselho, Senhores Comiss脙隆rios, Senhores Deputados, o mundo mudou muito desde o 脙潞ltimo plen脙隆rio aqui, em Estrasburgo.

No dia 13 de fevereiro, 脙潞ltimo dia desse plen脙隆rio, o secret脙隆rio da Defesa americano, Pete Hegseth, disse a seguinte frase: 脗芦Os Estados Unidos j脙隆 n脙拢o est脙拢o dispon脙颅veis para garantir a seguran脙搂a da Europa脗禄. Clarinho como 脙隆gua. Como disse a presidente da Comiss脙拢o h脙隆 pouco, o tempo da ilus脙拢o acabou.

E, por isso, fez bem o Conselho Europeu, o Conselho da passada quinta-feira, em flexibilizar as regras do Pacto de Estabilidade e Crescimento, em criar uma nova linha de financiamento e at脙漏 em alterar o mandato do Banco Europeu de Investimento para permitir cr脙漏ditos em projetos na 脙隆rea da defesa, o que, ali脙隆s, mostra bem a forma adormecida como esta institui脙搂脙拢o europeia estava a olhar para a ind脙潞stria da defesa.

Portanto, o Conselho fez bem. Fez bem, mas n脙拢o chega. Porque 脙漏 urgente definir uma arquitetura de defesa europeia, porque, sem isso, o plano industrial que 脙漏 longamente falado continuar脙隆 vago, as compras conjuntas continuar脙拢o descoordenadas e a defesa da Europa continuar脙隆 dependente de outros, inclusivamente na sua componente nuclear.

Portanto, o fim do tempo da ilus脙拢o exige estas respostas r脙隆pidas e decididas, mas j脙隆 sabemos que a velocidade tem riscos 芒聙聰 e vale a pena, hoje, falar aqui destes riscos.脗聽O risco de, sistematicamente, se ultrapassar e passar ao lado do Parlamento Europeu; o risco de a Comiss脙拢o e a sua presidente passarem a ser essencialmente uma figura decorativa face a iniciativas pol脙颅ticas de Macron, Starmer, Merz ou, quem sabe, outros; e o risco de destruir a NATO, para a qual ningu脙漏m tem hoje uma alternativa cred脙颅vel.

Portanto, 脙漏 esse,脗聽Senhores Deputados, o nosso desafio: salvar a Europa 芒聙聰 a come脙搂ar pela Ucr脙垄nia, que tanto merece a nossa ajuda 芒聙聰, sem destruir as suas institui脙搂脙碌es. E 脙漏 nossa responsabilidade estar 脙聽 altura deste desafio.

(O orador aceita responder a uma pergunta 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left), Pergunta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Deputado Cotrim de Figueiredo, o senhor deputado acha boa ideia aliviar o garrote or脙搂amental para gastos militares, o garrote do Pacto de Estabilidade para gastos militares, mas n脙拢o acha bem se for para gastos com a habita脙搂脙拢o, com a sa脙潞de, com as pens脙碌es.

O senhor deputado acha que os recursos p脙潞blicos n脙拢o podem servir para investir na habita脙搂脙拢o, para resolver os problemas do acesso 脙聽 habita脙搂脙拢o, porque s脙拢o recursos p脙潞blicos, mas desviar 800 mil milh脙碌es de euros para gastos militares, j脙隆, na perspetiva dos liberais, est脙隆 certo.脗聽E o senhor acha que 脙漏 com a corrida global aos armamentos, aumentando os riscos de confronta脙搂脙拢o, de guerra e de destrui脙搂脙拢o, que se alcan脙搂a a paz e a seguran脙搂a coletiva.

Explique-nos l脙隆, senhor deputado, essa ilus脙拢o liberal, porque n脙鲁s n脙拢o encontramos nisso nenhuma refer脙陋ncia que sirva a povo nenhum, incluindo ao povo portugu脙陋s.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Cotrim De Figueiredo (Renew), Resposta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Senhor Deputado Jo脙拢o Oliveira, eu acredito 脙漏 que, se n脙拢o pararmos Putin, n脙拢o h脙隆 habita脙搂脙拢o, n脙拢o h脙隆 educa脙搂脙拢o, n脙拢o h脙隆 sa脙潞de que valha a nenhum europeu.

E, portanto, quem tem de explicar 脙漏 o senhor deputado: porque 脙漏 que, h脙隆 tr脙陋s anos, desde a invas脙拢o da Ucr脙垄nia pela R脙潞ssia, defende sistematicamente uma pol脙颅tica de apaziguamento de tiranos como Vladimir Putin? Isso 脙漏 que o senhor deputado tem de explicar.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Villy S脙赂vndal (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Fru formand! Det smerter mig at konstatere, at vi ikke l脙娄ngere kan stole p脙楼 USA under Trumps ledelse. Jeg er netop kommet tilbage fra Ukraine. Her k脙娄mper landets befolkning for deres liv, deres frihed og deres fremtid. Vi ser den ukrainske befolkning blive svigtet af USA. USA har gjort det lettere for Rusland at bombe i Ukraine og dr脙娄be civile. Det ser vi hver dag nu p脙楼 tabstallene. Vores t脙娄tte allierede kan vi ikke l脙娄ngere kende. I Det Hvide Hus skal frihedsk脙娄mperen Zelenskyj, ydmyges og presses til at sige tak foran rullende kamera. Det er skammeligt! Jeg vil dog gerne sige tak. Tak til Zelenskyj og det ukrainske folk for at stille op mod autokraterne. Tak til de ukrainske soldater, der k脙娄mper p脙楼 slagmarken. Kolleger, lad mig g脙赂re det klart: Europa skal g脙赂re sig uafh脙娄ngigt af Trumps USA. Det betyder 脙赂get samarbejde med forsvarsmateriel, teknologi og efterretninger. Det betyder, at vi skal skabe de n脙赂dvendige alliancer med de lande, der stadig st脙赂tter en verden formet af den internationale retsorden. Det betyder, at vi skal tro p脙楼 os selv, p脙楼 Europa og p脙楼 Ukraine.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Li Andersson (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, as a leftist, I think that Putin and Trump form one of the most dangerous ideological alliances that the world has seen in a long time.

Therefore, it is essential that the EU strengthens its support for Ukraine. In addition, the EU should set a strategic goal of reducing our dependencies on the US. This means: creating a European security architecture, redirecting all financing currently going to the US arms industry to Europe; reducing energy dependencies; and limiting the powers of the digital oligarchs.

Changes to the fiscal rules must enable all of these investments. I do understand that the goal might also require more investment in European defence. But it would be a historic mistake to finance this by cutting welfare, because that would only cement the rise to power of Putin's and Trump's allies in Europe: the extreme right. And for once, the EU needs to be smarter than this.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脨聲脨虏脩聙脨戮脨驴脨碌脨鹿脩聛脨潞脨赂脩聫脩聜 脩聛脩聤脩聨脨路 脩聜脩聙脩聫脨卤脨虏脨掳 脨麓脨掳 脩聜脩聤脩聙脩聛脨赂 脩聛脩聤脩聜脩聙脩聝脨麓脨陆脨赂脩聡脨碌脩聛脩聜脨虏脨戮 脩聛脩聤脩聛 脩聛脨虏脨戮脨赂脩聜脨碌 脩聛脩聤脩聛脨碌脨麓脨赂 脨赂 脩聛 脨虏脩聛脨赂脩聡脨潞脨赂 脨鲁脨禄脨戮脨卤脨掳脨禄脨陆脨赂 脩聛脨赂脨禄脨赂, 脨麓脨掳 脩聛脨赂 脨戮脩聛脨赂脨鲁脩聝脩聙脨赂 脨麓脨戮脩聛脩聜脩聤脨驴 脨麓脨戮 脨碌脨虏脩聜脨赂脨陆脨赂 脩聙脨碌脩聛脩聝脩聙脩聛脨赂 脨赂 脨麓脨掳 脨赂脨路脨鲁脩聙脨掳脨麓脨赂 脩聛脩聜脩聙脨掳脩聜脨碌脨鲁脨赂脩聡脨碌脩聛脨潞脨赂 脨驴脨掳脩聙脩聜脨陆脩聦脨戮脩聙脩聛脩聜脨虏脨掳. 脨聺脨掳脨鹿-脨禄脨碌脩聛脨陆脨赂脩聫脩聜 脨陆脨掳脩聡脨赂脨陆 脨麓脨掳 脩聛脨碌 脩聛脨驴脨碌脩聛脩聜脩聫脩聜 脩聜脨碌脨路脨赂 脨戮脨鲁脩聙脨戮脨录脨陆脨赂 脨驴脨掳脩聙脨赂, 脨掳 脨赂 脨麓脨掳 脩聛脨碌 脨鲁脨掳脩聙脨掳脨陆脩聜脨赂脩聙脨掳 脩聛脨赂脨鲁脩聝脩聙脨陆脨戮脩聛脩聜脩聜脨掳, 脨碌 脨潞脨掳脩聜脨戮 脩聛脨碌 脨赂脨路脨驴脨戮脨禄脨路脨虏脨掳 脨麓脨赂脨驴脨禄脨戮脨录脨掳脩聠脨赂脩聫脩聜脨掳 脨赂 脩聛脨碌 脨虏脩聤脨路脩聛脩聜脨掳脨陆脨戮脨虏脩聫脩聜 脨陆脨戮脩聙脨录脨掳脨禄脨陆脨赂脩聜脨碌 脨戮脩聜脨陆脨戮脩聢脨碌脨陆脨赂脩聫 脩聛 脨聽脩聝脩聛脨赂脩聫. 脨漏脨戮脨录 脨隆脨聬脨漏 脨录脨戮脨露脨碌 脨麓脨掳 脨鲁脨戮 脨陆脨掳脨驴脩聙脨掳脨虏脨赂, 脨路脨掳脩聣脨戮 脨聲脨虏脩聙脨戮脨驴脨碌脨鹿脩聛脨潞脨赂脩聫脩聜 脩聛脩聤脩聨脨路 脨麓脨掳 脨陆脨碌 脨录脨戮脨露脨碌?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jeroen Lenaers (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, when your neighbour's house is on fire, you don't haggle over the price of the garden hose. With those words, the US under President Roosevelt came to our aid during the Second World War. 80 years later, war is raging again on the European continent, but this time, the US does not only want to negotiate the price of the garden hose, they bully the neighbour into giving up his house altogether before considering any help.

This is the situation we are in today and it's a wake up call like never before for Europe, a wake up call that should not have been necessary. For years and years, we have been discussing the need for Europe to get serious about its own defence. Many European leaders have stood here in this room, passionately arguing the necessity of this.

So it's fair to ask exactly what we have achieved in all these years, and the clear answer is not enough. So in that sense, I really welcome the determination and the ambition shown by President von der Leyen and the European leaders last week. This ambition needs now to be translated into immediate and tangible European action.

Extraordinary circumstances ask for extraordinary measures, and we must be brave and bold not only to be able to help Ukraine, which is, of course, of crucial importance, but ultimately to help ourselves, because Ukraine's interests are Europe's interests, we are defending common European values. We are defending international law and the rules-based order. We are defending the very basic principle that the aggressor should not be rewarded at the expense of the victim.

Today, we must defend a future for our children in freedom and safety on our European continent.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kathleen Van Brempt (S&D). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, Europa was, is en zal altijd een vredesproject zijn. Maar kiezen voor vrede en vrijheid betekent dat we onze veiligheid in eigen handen moeten nemen. Daarom zijn de voorstellen van de Commissie en de Raad een eerste belangrijke stap. Een nieuwe Europese vredesarchitectuur omvat echter veel meer 27 lidstaten die extra investeren. Het vereist ook co脙露rdinatie en strategie.

Veiligheid is ook zoveel meer dan kogels en tanks alleen. Het gaat ook om infrastructuur, technologie en het afbouwen van onze afhankelijkheid. Denk aan energie, grondstoffen aar bijvoorbeeld ook medicijnen. Veiligheid betekent ook niet bezuinigen maar juist verder investeren. Ja, in defensie, maar ook in duurzaamheid, infrastructuur en sociaal beleid. Ons sociaal model is immers uniek in de wereld en dat is wat Europa zo anders maakt dan waar Poetin en Trump voor staan. Dit is waar de Oekra脙炉ners voor vechten: vrede, vrijheid en veiligheid. Dat is wat we nu, meer dan ooit, moeten beschermen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Roberto Vannacci (PfE). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signora von der Leyen: ma dov'脙篓?

Monsieur Costa, elle ne se cache pas derri脙篓re vous, Mme von der Leyen?

Perch脙漏 la signora von der Leyen ha paura di questo Parlamento ed invoca l'articolo 122, lo stato di emergenza, per raggirare l'unica istituzione europea eletta dal popolo e per indebitare noi e i nostri figli per 850 miliardi di euro da spendere in armi.

Eppure i carri russi non sono a Varsavia, neanche a Budapest e neanche a Praga. E Parigi non brucia sotto gragnole di colpi. Quello che brucia in Francia sono le chiese cristiane. Queste sono le vere emergenze signora von der Leyen!

Sono le famiglie europee che non arrivano a fine mese e che non hanno i soldi per pagare le bollette del gas, che le politiche scellerate della sua Commissione hanno fatto lievitare esponenzialmente.

Le vere emergenze sono l'autarchia e la dittatura e la sospensione della democrazia, come 脙篓 avvenuto pochi giorni fa in Romania, che i tecnocrati di Bruxelles appoggiano e sostengono.

Le vere emergenze sono gli attentati e la criminalit脙聽 e gli atti di criminalit脙聽, che quasi giornalmente gli immigrati illegali portano a termine a spese e a danno dei cittadini europei nelle nazioni europee.

Queste sono le vere emergenze, signora von der Leyen, non la paura di vederci un soldato russo col colbacco in testa alle porte di casa. E ci venga in Parlamento, e non abbia paura, perch脙漏 qua non l'aspetta un plotone di cosacchi con le sciabole, ma i rappresentanti del popolo europeo che vogliono dire la loro sul loro futuro.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Charlie Weimers (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Europe's security isn't built on words, it's guaranteed by firepower. Strong national defences are key. I believe we can cooperate, we can scale up production, without surrendering national control. REARM Europe is a start, but without real output, it's just words.

We need bullets, drones, missiles streaming quickly, unimpeded by delays. Last year we produced 500 artillery shells. Ukraine burns through that in two months. Scaling up 芒聙聯 experts say years. We don't have years. Bureaucracy kills. Delays kill. Either we act or we fail. Ukraine needs firepower now. Tanks, jets, not just applause. Drop the taboos 芒聙聯 landmines, cluster bombs 芒聙聯 because Russia has no red lines. It's time to turn plants into production, factories into force. Support Ukraine now. Peace through strength now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dan Barna (Renew). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, domnule comisar, domnule pre脠聶edinte Costa, apreciez 脠聶i v脛聝 mul脠聸umesc pentru prezen脠聸a dumneavoastr脛聝 la dezbatere. Contextul de securitate al Uniunii Europene nu mai este, din p脛聝cate, o certitudine lini脠聶tit脛聝, ci mai degrab脛聝 un c脙垄mp de lupt脛聝. Ne-am v脛聝zut vulnerabilit脛聝脠聸ile dup脛聝 instalarea unei administra脠聸ii americane cu priorit脛聝脠聸i 脠聶i afinit脛聝脠聸i pro-Putin. Ne-am amintit din nou 芒聙聯 脠聶i statele din estul Europei 脠聶tiu asta foarte bine 芒聙聯 c脛聝 pariul pe garan脠聸ii externe este unul foarte periculos pe termen lung.

Nu. Nu ne mai putem permite refugii iluzorii 脙庐n dependen脠聸e strategice, nici militare 脠聶i nici energetice. Acum este momentul ca Uniunea European脛聝 s脛聝 devin脛聝 un adev脛聝rat constructor al propriei sale securit脛聝脠聸i. Propunerile actuale ale Comisiei sunt un pas vital, dar avem nevoie de mai mult dec脙垄t de aloc脛聝ri financiare. Avem nevoie de o industrie de ap脛聝rare robust脛聝 脠聶i unificat脛聝. Avem nevoie de for脠聸e interoperabile, capabile de desf脛聝脠聶urare rapid脛聝. Avem nevoie de o abordare strategic脛聝 comun脛聝, de o 脙庐n脠聸elegere unitar脛聝 a amenin脠聸脛聝rilor actuale. Trebuie s脛聝 devenim st脛聝p脙垄nii propriului nostru destin 脠聶i garantul propriei noastre securit脛聝脠聸i. Doar atunci ne vom putea proteja cu adev脛聝rat valorile, cet脛聝脠聸enii 脠聶i viitorul.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sergey Lagodinsky (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Mr脗聽Vice-President, Mr脗聽President,脗聽our challenges are monumental; our instruments are outdated. But the idea that unites us all 芒聙聯 our Europe 芒聙聯脗聽it is not. I believe in the power of Europe because, frankly, what other choice do we have in the world divided between Trumps and Xis and Putins?

Ideas inspire, but actions define our future. We need real European capabilities, not grand promises; real European money, not 800脗聽billion of hot air that was promised by the Commission.

We need a strategic decision-making centre and we need an opportunity to leave spoilers like Orb脙隆n behind. We need a decision-making centre, not 27 passionate speeches per meeting 芒聙聯 a core Europe for defence that should emerge with the big and bold, with the committed and capable. France and Germany, yes, but also Estonia and Latvia and Lithuania. Let's not forget about those. And London and Oslo.脗聽And yes, we make Europe act again. And yes, we will.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kathleen Funchion (The Left). 芒聙聯 A Uachtar脙隆in, as a Member from a country that cherishes our neutrality and recognises the benefits it has brought to the world, I want to express my opposition to any backsliding when it comes to the neutrality of Ireland.

Our country has a proud history of UN-mandated peacekeeping missions, a tradition that has gone on for decades. Our neutrality has enabled us to maintain an independent foreign policy, and this is a strength that is valued and supported by the Irish people.

It is therefore shameful that our Irish Government would try to use this real emergency to end the 'triple lock' policy, which is in place to protect our neutrality. The 'triple lock' is a core component of Ireland's independent foreign policy, and is enshrined in national declarations to the Irish people by governments in advance of a previous EU referendum. So if the Irish Government wants to remove this protection, it should put it to the people and have a referendum on it.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dolors Montserrat (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, el nuevo orden mundial ha puesto a Europa frente al espejo, oblig脙隆ndonos a elegir en qu脙漏 lado de la historia queremos estar. Tres a脙卤os despu脙漏s de la invasi脙鲁n, Putin ya no busca solo una capitulaci脙鲁n humillante para Zelenski y su pueblo, quiere borrar los valores europeos de libertad, democracia y paz y avanzar en su sue脙卤o imperialista.

Europa lo tiene claro: frente a la sumisi脙鲁n, liderazgo, acci脙鲁n y firmeza. Y esta es la peor noticia para Putin. No renunciaremos a la libertad, no descansaremos hasta una paz justa y duradera. Nuestro apoyo a Ucrania ha sido claro y firme desde el primer minuto, algo que no todos pueden decir aqu脙颅.

Mientras Estados miembros como Polonia y Alemania redoblan su inversi脙鲁n en seguridad y defensa, Espa脙卤a tiene el Gobierno que menos invierte en defensa en Europa, solo un 1,2脗聽%. Espa脙卤a es uno de los pa脙颅ses que m脙隆s gas compra a Putin desde que comenz脙鲁 la guerra: 9脗聽000 millones脗聽de euros. S脙隆nchez es el equilibrista de una coalici脙鲁n de Gobierno sin presupuestos ni respaldo parlamentario que le exige la salida de la OTAN y blanquea a dictadores. Todo con un mismo objetivo: debilitar a Espa脙卤a y a Europa desde dentro.

Europa debe asegurarse de que cada euro europeo para defensa se dedique a ello y no se repita lo que ha hecho S脙隆nchez con los fondos de Next Generation EU: usarlos como si fueran propiedad de 脙漏l y de su Partido Socialista. El Partido Popular Europeo lo dice bien claro y bien fuerte: s脙颅 a la Uni脙鲁n Europea de Defensa y s脙颅 a una soberan脙颅a estrat脙漏gica real. La historia ya nos ha ense脙卤ado el precio de la inacci脙鲁n y esta vez no podemos fallar.

脗聽脗聽Christel Schaldemose (S&D). 芒聙聯 Fru formand! Verden er forandret. Det er en mindre sikker klode, vi har i dag end for bare f脙楼 uger siden. Derfor skal EU ogs脙楼 forandre sig. EU skal tage ansvar for egen sikkerhed. Det har vi v脙娄ret alt for langsom om at g脙赂re. Ukraine er vores lakmuspr脙赂ve p脙楼, om vi vil, og om vi kan. Tendensen er desv脙娄rre klar: Jo l脙娄ngere et EU land ligger fra Ukraine, jo mindre bliver der givet i st脙赂tte. Det duer ikke. Det er ikke m脙楼den, vi viser, at vi kan tage ansvar for Europas sikkerhed p脙楼. Vi siger, at alle i Europa skal st脙楼 sammen, men ikke alle handler derefter. Vi skal alts脙楼 have 脙赂get den st脙赂tte til Ukraine, og det skal v脙娄re nu. Og s脙楼 skal vi selvf脙赂lgelig ogs脙楼 opbygge vores egen europ脙娄iske forsvarskapacitet. Krigen i Ukraine, er simpelthen den pr脙赂ve, der viser, om vi i Europa kan st脙楼 sammen mod trusler udefra. Vi skal have alle lande til at forst脙楼, at det her, det handler faktisk ikke kun om Ukraine. Det handler ogs脙楼 om os selv. Lad os s脙娄tte i gang. Lad os vise, at vi godt kan forsvare Europa selv.

脗聽脗聽Jaroslava Pokorn脙隆 Jermanov脙隆 (PfE). 芒聙聯 Pan脙颅 p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, Evropa je vlastn脙颅 vinou ve slo脜戮it脙漏 situaci, kter脙隆 prov脛聸脜聶脙颅 na脜隆i schopnost spr脙隆vn脛聸 reagovat na osudov脙漏 v脙陆zvy. Bezpe脛聧nostn脙颅 politika se nem脜炉脜戮e tvo脜聶it v Bruselu, ale mus脙颅 vych脙隆zet z pot脜聶eb 脛聧lensk脙陆ch zem脙颅. 脜聽ance Evropy je v transatlantick脙漏 spolupr脙隆ci. Soust脜聶e脛聫me se na posilov脙隆n脙颅 aliance NATO. Investujme diplomatick脙漏 脙潞sil脙颅 i finan脛聧n脙颅 zdroje do tohoto projektu. Je pot脜聶eba se zastavit a o obrann脙漏 politice p脜聶em脙陆脜隆let s chladnou hlavou. Na脜隆e vz脙隆jemn脙漏 vztahy se Spojen脙陆mi st脙隆ty americk脙陆mi nyn脙颅 omezujeme pouze na ot脙隆zku Ukrajiny a nech脙隆v脙隆me je definovat t脙颅mto konfliktem. 脗聽脗聽Roberts Z脛芦le (ECR). 芒聙聯 Cien脛芦t脛聛 priek脜隆s脛聯d脛聯t脛聛jas kundze, Padomes priek脜隆s脛聯d脛聯t脛聛ja, Komisijas vicepriek脜隆s脛聯d脛聯t脛聛ja!

Manupr脛聛t, p脛聛rliecino脜隆i 脛聛tri so脛录i, piem脛聯ram, palielin脛聛t sankcijas krievu 脛聯nu flotei vismaz l脛芦dz ASV sankciju apm脛聯ram pret tiem, nepirkt Krievijas sa脜隆脛路idrin脛聛to g脛聛zi, konfisc脛聯t iesald脛聯tos Krievijas resursus un nos脜芦t脛芦t Ukrainai pal脛芦dz脛芦bu, nu, vismaz apm脛聯ram 1% no IKP milit脛聛raj脛聛 pus脛聯.

J脛聛, nav pat脛芦kama ASV administr脛聛cijas poz脛芦cija, ta脛聧u Eiropas lab脛聛k脛聛 atbilde b脜芦tu r脛芦c脛芦ba 芒聙聰 demonstr脛聛cija, un tas par脛聛d脛芦tu to, ka vien脛芦g脛聛 iz脜隆脛路iro脜隆脛聛 iesp脛聯ja mums ir saglab脛聛t (NATO ietvaros) stingru Rietumu poz脛芦ciju dro脜隆脛芦bas jom脛聛.

脗聽脗聽Daniel Caspary (PPE). 芒聙聯 Sehr geehrte Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, gesch脙陇tzte Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Es ist heute hinreichend oft gesagt worden: Wir haben die letzten drei Jahre nicht hinreichend auf Russlands Aggressionen reagiert. Und es ist auch deutlich geworden: Europa ist immer dann stark, wenn wir besonders unter Druck stehen.

Ich m脙露chte auf eine Sache noch einmal hinweisen, die unser Fraktionsvorsitzender Manfred Weber vorhin vollkommen zu Recht dargestellt hat: Wir sind gut in den Krisenzeiten. Aber schaffen wir es in den Krisenzeiten nicht nur als Europ脙陇ische Union, die Mitgliedstaaten dabei zu unterst脙录tzen, ihre Hausaufgaben zu erledigen, sondern schaffen wir es auch gemeinsam mit den Mitgliedstaaten, endlich in Europa den n脙陇chsten Schritt zu gehen?

Wir haben es doch gesehen bei der Corona-Krise: Der Corona-Wiederaufbaufonds war ein Riesenschritt der Europ脙陇ischen Union, um den Mitgliedstaaten zu helfen. Aber wir haben es nicht geschafft, die europaweiten Stromnetze z.脗聽B. zu bauen, europaweite Eisenbahnnetze zu ert脙录chtigen. Wir haben es nicht geschafft, Europa als Kontinent mit einem gemeinsamen gro脙聼en Projekt zusammenzubringen.

Und auch jetzt脗聽芒聙聯 ReArm Europe ist dringend n脙露tig. Aber auch hier gilt doch wieder: Es fehlen genau die gemeinsamen europ脙陇ischen Projekte. Es geht doch nicht nur darum, die Mitgliedstaaten jetzt alleine dabei zu unterst脙录tzen, die n脙露tige Aufr脙录stung zu machen, endlich verteidigungsf脙陇hig zu werden, sondern wir m脙录ssen doch gemeinsame europ脙陇ische Projekte durchsetzen.

Ich kann den Rat nur aufrufen, Herr Costa, ich kann die Mitgliedstaaten nur aufrufen: Bitte machen Sie im Rat Druck, dass wir einige Projekte auch gemeinsam machen! Die Beispiele, die Manfred Weber heute genannt hat: mit dem gemeinsamen 脙聹berwachungssatellitensystem, mit einem gemeinsamen Luftabwehrschirm, mit einem vielleicht auch gemeinsamen Flugzeugtr脙陇ger, Hubschraubertr脙陇ger, Drohnentr脙陇ger oder was auch immer. Wir m脙录ssen doch bitte auch als Europ脙陇er gemeinsam ein gro脙聼es Projekt voranbringen, um auch nach au脙聼en zu zeigen: Wir meinen die europ脙陇ische Einigung脗聽芒聙聯 gerade auch in Krisenzeiten脗聽芒聙聯 ernst.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicola Zingaretti (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, io penso che l'Europa debba aumentare la sua sicurezza ma il modo per farlo 脙篓 aprire il cantiere per la difesa comune europea. E va detto con chiarezza: il semplice aumento della spesa militare per 27 eserciti nazionali non ci rende pi脙鹿 sicuri.

La deterrenza o 脙篓 europea o non 脙篓 deterrenza e dobbiamo quindi finanziare progetti comuni, promuovere insieme acquisti a favore dell'industria europea, rafforzare il coordinamento dei sistemi e dei comandi, investire massicciamente nella ricerca dell'industria dell'aerospazio. Sicurezza 脙篓 rilanciare il ruolo politico e unitario dell'Europa, il脗聽suo protagonismo come grande attore globale nel commercio, nell'industria e nella cultura, e rafforzare la sua politica estera.

Contro pi脙鹿 nazionalismo occorre pi脙鹿 federalismo e questa stagione si pu脙虏 e si deve aprire senza colpire la spesa e la coesione sociale ma con politiche di investimenti comuni. Perch脙漏 siamo tornati alla sostanza della straordinaria intuizione di Ventotene: l'Europa sar脙聽 libera se sar脙聽 pi脙鹿 unita.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Harald Vilimsky (PfE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, meine sehr geehrten Damen und Herren! F脙录r den Kurs der Europ脙陇ischen Union gilt in abgewandelter Form das Sprichwort: Immer, wenn du glaubst, es geht nicht mehr, kommt schon der n脙陇chste Wahnsinn daher. Und im Konkreten ist es jetzt der Plan, hier eine gewaltige Summe von 800脗聽Milliarden daf脙录r vorzusehen, diesen Kontinent aufzur脙录sten. Und rechnen Sie diese Summe um auf die Zahl der Einwohner Europas, so kommen Sie per capita脗聽芒聙聯 wie es so sch脙露n hei脙聼t, vom Baby bis zum Greis 芒聙聯 auf eine Summe von um die 1脗聽800脗聽Euro. Rechnen Sie das auf die Aktivgeneration, kostet das jeden Europ脙陇er, der heute Steuern beitr脙陇gt, wahrscheinlich zus脙陇tzliche 4脗聽000 bis 5脗聽000脗聽Euro脗聽pro Jahr.

Ich sage: Das ist der falsche Weg. Europa braucht dieses Geld f脙录r neue und gute Jobs. Europa braucht dieses Geld f脙录r soziale Aktivit脙陇ten, f脙录r Spit脙陇ler, f脙录r den Bildungsbereich, f脙录r Schulen, aber nicht, um aufzur脙录sten. Und ins selbe Horn st脙露脙聼t auch der Vorschlag, der jetzt aus Frankreich kommt, n脙陇mlich den ganzen Kontinent mit Atomwaffen quasi zu 脙录bers脙陇en. Das ist der falsche Weg.

Wir haben zurzeit Verhandlungen in Saudi-Arabien mit der Trump-Administration脗聽芒聙聯 und jetzt kann man von Trump halten, was immer man m脙露chte, aber es ist zumindest der Versuch, hier am Tisch Frieden herbeizuf脙录hren. Ich h脙陇tte mir gew脙录nscht, dass die Europ脙陇ische Union dort ankn脙录pft, wo sie 2012, glaube ich, schon einmal war, n脙陇mlich eine Art Friedensnobelpreis erhalten zu haben, und sich jetzt hier in Kooperation und in einem Zusammenspiel mit dem Westen gemeinsam daf脙录r ausspricht, das Sterben endlich zu beenden, endlich daf脙录r Sorge zu tragen, dass es zu qualitativen Friedensverhandlungen kommt und dass wir in Zukunft wieder daran arbeiten k脙露nnen, den Wohlstand auf diesem Kontinent voranzutreiben, und nicht zuzusehen, wie hier mehr und mehr Tote und Verletzte generiert werden.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adam Bielan (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam President, for too long Europe has neglected its defence industry, leaving us dependent on external suppliers and slow to react to crises. While NATO allies uphold Article 5, they have failed to meet Article 3's obligation to develop their own defence capabilities.

EU Member States must urgently invest in their own security, as Poland did under Law and Justice leadership.

Recent events have shown that without the United States, Europe is unfortunately nearly defenceless. Regardless of political turbulence or disagreements, we must maintain strong and reliable transatlantic relations. Our security depends on it. And here, from this very place, I once again urge the Prime Minister of Poland to stop blocking President Duda's proposal to convene an EU芒聙聭US summit in Warsaw under the Polish Presidency. Failing to hold it will only deepen the cracks in our relations with our most important ally. We cannot afford such missteps at a time when security challenges are multiplying across Europe and beyond.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Zolt脙隆n Tarr (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, usually I speak Hungarian, but I'm compelled to speak English because I need to speak to the people of Europe on behalf of my fellow Hungarians.

In an increasingly uncertain global political situation we need a strong Europe, which requires the establishment of a new security system. Unfortunately, there is a risk that my country, Hungary, will not be part of this because the current Hungarian Prime Minister serves foreign interests. The country's economy is in despair, inflation is soaring and corruption is our present and our future.

The government is isolating Hungary. It claims to seek peace, but in reality it endangers the security of Hungarians and that of the whole of Europe. The vast majority of Hungarians believe that Hungary's place is in Europe. Everyone must clearly understand that. Hungary's home is Europe, and Europe's security is equal to Hungary's security.

We, the opposition, the Tisza party, are ready to lead Hungary back to the heart of Europe. Our future lies in a strong, secure and prosperous Europe, not in the shadows of the Kremlin.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alexander Jungbluth (ESN), Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 Sehr geehrter Kollege, Sie haben ja kaum zum Tagesordnungspunkt gesprochen; Sie haben ja eigentlich Ihre Redezeit vor allem dazu genutzt, gegen Ihre eigene Regierung vorzugehen. Aber ich stelle mir mal folgende Frage: Sie wollen ja immer diese st脙陇rkere europ脙陇ische Integration. Sie stellen hier in Aussicht, dass Ihre Delegation daf脙录r steht, Ungarn in das Herz Europas zur脙录ckzuf脙录hren. Was wollen Sie denn eigentlich erreichen? Wollen Sie tats脙陇chlich, dass eine ungarische Armee geopfert wird, damit es eben eine europ脙陇ische Armee gibt, die Ungarn dann mit Sicherheit nicht mehr verteidigen will?

Meinen Sie mit dem Herzen Europas das, was Ihre Fraktionskollegen von der CDU zu verantworten haben, dass es zu Massenvergewaltigungen in Deutschland kommt, dass es zu Terror in Deutschland kommt? Wollen Sie, dass das auch in Ungarn stattfindet? Glauben Sie, dass das der Punkt ist, wo Sie sagen, dass das Herz Europas Sie dahin zur脙录ckf脙录hrt?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Zolt脙隆n Tarr (PPE), k脙漏kk脙隆rty脙隆s v脙隆lasz. 芒聙聯 Azt 脙漏rtem az Eur脙鲁pa sz脙颅ve alatt, hogy egy olyan Eur脙鲁p脙隆ban 脙漏l脙录nk 脙漏s kell 脙漏lj脙录nk, ahol az eur脙鲁pai orsz脙隆gok 脙露nsz脙隆ntukb脙鲁l, saj脙隆t akaratukb脙鲁l egy脙录ttm脜卤k脙露dnek annak 脙漏rdek脙漏ben, hogy egy biztons脙隆gos, biztons脙隆got ny脙潞jt脙鲁, egym脙隆snak seg脙颅t脜聭 Eur脙鲁p脙隆t hozzanak l脙漏tre. Ehhez arra van sz脙录ks脙漏g, hogy az eur脙鲁pai orsz脙隆gok egy脙录ttm脜卤k脙露djenek, seg脙颅ts脙漏k egym脙隆st 脙漏s tisztelj脙漏k egym脙隆st. Erre gondolok akkor, amikor az Eur脙鲁pa sz脙颅v脙漏r脜聭l besz脙漏lek.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Javier Moreno S脙隆nchez (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario, se脙卤or presidente del Consejo Europeo, comienzo enviando, en nombre de la delegaci脙鲁n socialista espa脙卤ola, nuestro recuerdo, apoyo y cari脙卤o a las familias de las v脙颅ctimas del 11M.

Se脙卤or脙颅as, 脗驴guerra o paz? La Uni脙鲁n est脙隆 en una disyuntiva impensable hace unos a脙卤os, especialmente para quienes no hemos vivido la guerra. Crece la inquietud en nuestras sociedades, especialmente entre los j脙鲁venes. Tenemos que hacer part脙颅cipe a la ciudadan脙颅a, con transparencia y pedagog脙颅a, de los cambios que exige la nueva realidad geopol脙颅tica. No podemos dejar el relato en manos de la extrema derecha, que aprovechar脙隆 para seguir destruyendo la esencia de la Uni脙鲁n Europea.

Tienen que decirnos claramente si est脙隆n con Ucrania y con Europa o si est脙隆n con Putin. Tenemos que explicar que la paz y la seguridad en nuestro continente pasan por seguir apoyando a Ucrania para lograr urgentemente una paz justa y duradera en la que participemos ucranianos y europeos. Y, a medio y largo plazo, tenemos que rearmar Europa y desarrollar una pol脙颅tica de defensa com脙潞n que nos permita ser aut脙鲁nomos y garantizar nuestra propia seguridad.

(El orador acepta responder a una pregunta formulada con arreglo al procedimiento de la 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄)

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jaak Madison (ECR), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 I generally agree with you when we are talking about defence policies. We really have to support more Ukraine. I definitely agree.

But, as you are from the Socialist Party from Spain, just a very small, tiny question: what is your opinion on why Spain has provided so little help to Ukraine currently? Because when you look at the GDP, the real help 芒聙聯 military aid 芒聙聯 it has given to Ukraine, it's almost nothing compared to Poland, Estonia, Denmark, Finland and so on. So, why is there so much talk and no actions? What is the problem in Spain with the Socialists? Why are you not acting?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Javier Moreno S脙隆nchez (S&D), respuesta de 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or Jaak Madison, gracias por su pregunta. Desde el primer minuto de la invasi脙鲁n de Putin a Ucrania, el Gobierno de Espa脙卤a ha estado con Ucrania. Haremos todo lo necesario para que consigamos esa paz duradera y justa. Estamos con Ucrania, seguiremos estando con Ucrania y pondremos los medios que hay que poner. No se preocupe que el Gobierno socialista espa脙卤ol est脙隆 en ello y seguiremos apoyando a Ucrania, porque no solo es la paz en Ucrania, es la paz en Europa, es la defensa de nuestras democracias y por eso tenemos que seguir unidos. Por eso pregunto a la extrema derecha europea si est脙隆n con Putin o est脙隆n con nosotros.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marion Mar脙漏chal (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, malheur aux vaincus et aux vassaux! Telle est la grande le脙搂on inculqu脙漏e aux Europ脙漏ens depuis maintenant plusieurs semaines. Si nous ne voulons pas risquer que, un jour, l芒聙聶un de nos chefs d芒聙聶脙聣tat se retrouve dans la position de Volodymyr脗聽Zelensky dans le Bureau ovale, alors oui, il faut augmenter nos stocks, nos munitions, notre mat脙漏riel op脙漏rationnel et le nombre de nos soldats, non pas pour jouer l芒聙聶escalade guerri脙篓re, mais pour 脙陋tre craints par des adversaires convaincus de notre capacit脙漏 脙聽 riposter. Si vis pacem, para bellum.

Pour cela, nous devons d脙篓s maintenant 脙漏carter la dangereuse illusion d芒聙聶une force arm脙漏e au service d芒聙聶une Europe f脙漏d脙漏rale. L芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne peut et doit, bien s脙禄r, faciliter le financement de ce r脙漏armement, mais elle ne peut en aucun cas en 脙陋tre la g脙漏n脙漏rale en chef; c芒聙聶est aux arm脙漏es nationales de d脙漏fendre nos nations. L芒聙聶urgence qui doit nous occuper est d芒聙聶脙漏tablir une pr脙漏f脙漏rence europ脙漏enne en mati脙篓re d芒聙聶achats militaires. Si cette priorit脙漏 ne devient pas la pierre angulaire de la nouvelle architecture de d脙漏fense de notre continent, alors toute vell脙漏it脙漏 d芒聙聶ind脙漏pendance restera illusoire.

Allons au bout du chemin: le r脙漏armement militaire n芒聙聶a de sens qu芒聙聶accompagn脙漏 d芒聙聶un r脙漏armement d脙漏mographique et identitaire. Il est grand temps que cette institution cesse de regarder les menaces uniquement 脙聽 l芒聙聶aune de ses 脜聯ill脙篓res id脙漏ologiques.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebasti脙拢o Bugalho (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Presidente, h脙隆 tr脙陋s anos, ningu脙漏m antecipou que a Europa se unisse em torno da Ucr脙垄nia, negando a invas脙拢o da Federa脙搂脙拢o da R脙潞ssia.

Tr脙陋s anos depois, a instabilidade americana faz com que muitos digam que a Europa n脙拢o existe, que n脙拢o conta, que n脙拢o consegue.

Caros colegas, nos 脙潞ltimos cem dias, as tr脙陋s institui脙搂脙碌es aqui reunidas mostraram que a Europa est脙隆 aqui.脗聽脙聙 amea脙搂a de tarifas respondemos no dia, defendendo a nossa economia. 脙聙 amea脙搂a de al脙颅vio de san脙搂脙碌es respondemos na semana, com o 16.脗潞 pacote de san脙搂脙碌es. 脙聙 suspens脙拢o de apoio 脙聽 Ucr脙垄nia respondemos na hora, n脙拢o abdicando da liberdade ucraniana, da sua constitui脙搂脙拢o, da sua soberania.

A Uni脙拢o tem provado que 脙漏 poss脙颅vel reagir sem provocar, agir sem desistir. Fazemo-lo n脙拢o para invadir ningu脙漏m, mas para impedirmos novas invas脙碌es. Fazemo-lo n脙拢o por oportunidade econ脙鲁mica, mas por necessidade, porque n脙拢o h脙隆 pobreza mais devastadora do que a pobreza da guerra.

Friends, I am Portuguese. I was born in a most distant Member State from this war. So I say to my Polish friends: I know your security is our security. I say to my Romanian friends: I know your democracy is our democracy. I say to my Baltic friends: your defence cannot just be our defence.

To our Ukrainian friends, I say: your freedom is our freedom. Today it is increasingly clear that a common response is the only fair response, that to invest in defence is the most urgent response in solidarity. Let's not waste time. Let's do it.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ren脙漏 Repasi (S&D). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Nach den bizarren Szenen im Wei脙聼en Haus vor elf Tagen wissen wir: Die europ脙陇isch芒聙聭amerikanische Freundschaft hat keine verl脙陇sslichen Freunde mehr auf der anderen Seite des Atlantiks. Wir m脙录ssen uns der bitteren Realit脙陇t stellen: Wir sind alleine f脙录r unsere Sicherheit verantwortlich. Das verlangt echte Anstrengungen und keine Luftbuchungen. 650脗聽Milliarden Euro mehr an m脙露glichen nationalen Schulden f脙录hrt noch lange nicht zu 800脗聽Milliarden Euro zus脙陇tzlichem Geld f脙录r unsere Verteidigung.

Ich verlange einen ambitionierten Vorschlag, der Verteidigung als europ脙陇ische Aufgabe versteht, der echtes europ脙陇isches Geld in die Hand nimmt, ohne den sozialen Zusammenhalt zu schw脙陇chen. Das verlangt gemeinsame Verschuldung f脙录r gemeinsame Verteidigungsaufgaben, ohne nationale Schulden zu erh脙露hen.

Lasst uns mutig sein! Die 脙陇u脙聼eren Gefahren unseres Kontinents sind die Pandemie unserer Zeit.

脗聽脗聽Michael Gahler (PPE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Der Aggressor im Kreml wei脙聼 gar nicht, wie ihm geschieht: Die bisherige F脙录hrungsmacht des Westens wechselte in Sachen Ukraine die Seiten. Es muss uns als Weckruf reichen, dass es Zweifel gibt, ob die USA gegen脙录ber ihren Verb脙录ndeten im Ernstfall ihren Beistandsverpflichtungen nachkommen. Da m脙录ssen wir vom worst case ausgehen und so schnell wie m脙露glich alle Instrumente aktivieren, um uns zu verteidigen und um uns verteidigungsf脙陇hig zu machen auf nationaler und europ脙陇ischer Ebene脗聽芒聙聯 im Prinzip, wie es dem Vorschlag der Kommission entspricht.

Als EP haben wir Probleme mit der Rechtsgrundlage, und ich h脙陇tte mir auch gew脙录nscht, dass wir auch die nationale脗聽escape clause nur insoweit gelten lassen, als die zus脙陇tzlichen Ausgaben in gemeinsame R脙录stungsprojekte investiert werden, um Skaleneffekte zu erzielen, um Interoperabilit脙陇t zu erh脙露hen. Wir brauchen auch europ脙陇ische Projekte, um uns gemeinsam zu sch脙录tzen. Ein European Sky Shield w脙陇re so ein Beispiel; andere Projekte sind genannt worden.

In der Zwischenzeit sehen wir, wie die russische R脙录stungsmaschinerie auf Hochtouren l脙陇uft und schon jetzt mehr produziert, als sie in der Ukraine einsetzt. Wof脙录r wohl? Machen wir uns endlich klar: Die russische Bedrohung richtet sich gegen uns alle, und die Ukraine ist unsere erste Verteidigungslinie, die wir st脙陇rken m脙录ssen, w脙陇hrend wir das eigene Potenzial hochfahren.

Die Ukrainer sind die ersten, die Frieden wollen. Sie wollten auch den Krieg nicht. Aber sie wissen auch, wie ein russischer Frieden aussieht, ein stalinistischer Frieden im russischen Gulag. Das droht auch uns, wenn die Ukraine f脙陇llt und wir nicht verteidigungsf脙陇hig sind. Lassen wir es nicht so weit kommen! Slawa Ukrajini!

脗聽脗聽Michael Gahler (PPE), Antwort auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 Wir werden alles unterst脙录tzen, was zu einem gerechten Frieden f脙录hrt脗聽芒聙聯 und je fr脙录her, desto besser. Die Ukraine braucht Sicherheitsgarantien, die den Namen auch verdienen, und der britische Premierminister hat gesagt 芒聙聻boots on the ground and planes in the air芒聙聹. Ich glaube, das w脙陇re nach Ende des Kriegszustandes eine gemeinsame Antwort auf die russische Bedrohung.

脗聽脗聽Rapha脙芦l Glucksmann (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, le grand 脙漏crivain et combattant de la France libre Romain脗聽Gary se demandait pourquoi les 脙漏lites fran脙搂aises n芒聙聶avaient pas suivi le g脙漏n脙漏ral de Gaulle 脙聽 Londres en脗聽1940. 脙聣tait-ce parce qu芒聙聶elles 脙漏taient violemment antis脙漏mites? Non, pas vraiment, nous dit Gary. Pro-allemandes ou pro-nazis, alors? Non plus. La raison 脙漏tait plus tristement banale, selon Gary: c芒聙聶est qu芒聙聶elles aimaient trop leurs meubles.

Les grandes temp脙陋tes exigent de rompre avec l芒聙聶habitude, et le moment est venu pour nous aussi de nous d脙漏faire de nos meubles. La Russie nous menace directement, et les 脙聣tats-Unis nous abandonnent l脙垄chement. Eh bien, c芒聙聶est le moment de v脙漏rit脙漏 de l芒聙聶Europe.

Il nous revient d脙漏sormais, 脙聽 nous et 脙聽 nous seuls, d芒聙聶aider massivement notre premi脙篓re ligne de d脙漏fense qu芒聙聶est la r脙漏sistance ukrainienne. Le Conseil europ脙漏en l芒聙聶affirme, mais cela suppose de prendre des d脙漏cisions fortes, devant lesquelles nos 脙聣tats reculent encore, comme la saisie des 209脗聽milliards d芒聙聶avoirs publics russes gel脙漏s en Europe et leur affectation 脙聽 l芒聙聶Ukraine.

Il nous revient aussi d脙漏sormais, 脙聽 nous et 脙聽 nous seuls, d芒聙聶assurer notre propre s脙漏curit脙漏 et de construire une d脙漏fense autonome. Nos dirigeants l芒聙聶affirment, mais cela suppose d芒聙聶aller plus loin que ce qui fut annonc脙漏 jeudi. Lan脙搂ons un emprunt commun finan脙搂ant des programmes europ脙漏ens et assurons-nous que cet argent serve 脙聽 d脙漏velopper les industries europ脙漏ennes 芒聙聯脗聽et pas 脙聽 empiler les F-35.

Nous avons besoin d芒聙聶agir plus, et plus en Europ脙漏ens. 脨隆脨禄脨掳脨虏脨掳 脨拢脨潞脩聙脨掳脩聴脨陆脩聳 (脗芦Slava Oukra脙炉ni脗禄)! Vive l芒聙聶Europe libre!

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lud脛聸k Niedermayer (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, the world has changed, not to better, in the last few years, but mostly in the last few weeks.

It's clear what we Europeans have to do: the exact opposite to what Moscow, Beijing, but also Donald Trump, is dreaming about. We must get united and we must get much, much, much stronger.

From that point of view, the summit was a good start. But not everything is about money. We need a strong and clear foreign policy to be able to represent the interest of Europeans. Common purchases are good, but it's not the same as the European defence that we desperately need, and this is what Manfred Weber was talking about.

So for me, the summit was a good start. But we must go much, much further and we must go much, much quicker, because there is very little time left.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ond脜聶ej Dost脙隆l (NI), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Good morning, Mr脗聽Niedermayer. I hope you will respond to my question. Do you really think it would be a good idea to transfer deciding about war and peace from Prague 芒聙聯 or other capitals of sovereign nations 芒聙聯 to Brussels, and let Brussels institutions decide if our sons will go to war? Because this is a necessary aspect of the common European defence you propose.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lud脛聸k Niedermayer (PPE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 First, this is not exactly what I was talking about. But it's absolutely obvious: either we defend ourselves together, or we don't defend ourselves.

What I'm talking about are the projects that will substantially increase our ability to defend, and make it much more effective, and save money 芒聙聯 for example, anti-missile defence, drone defence, electronic defence, and so on.

It would be 芒聙聯 and I guess you understand 芒聙聯 much more effective to make it together than try to develop 10 or 20 different systems at the same time. We don't each need to reinvent the wheel.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dan Nica (S&D). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, domnule pre脠聶edinte Costa, domnule comisar 脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧, discut脛聝m ast脛聝zi despre un program de asigurare, de fapt, a securit脛聝脠聸ii Uniunii Europene. Pentru mine ca rom脙垄n 脠聶i pentru 脠聸ara mea este extrem de important s脛聝 脠聶tim nu c脛聝 suntem doar 脙庐n marginea Europei 脠聶i c脛聝 trebuie s脛聝 ap脛聝r脛聝m Uniunea European脛聝 pe flancul estic, dar trebuie s脛聝 ne asigur脛聝m c脛聝 avem 脠聶i cu ce. Trebuie s脛聝-i asigur pe cet脛聝脠聸enii rom脙垄ni c脛聝 trebuie s脛聝-脠聶i vad脛聝 de via脠聸a lor de zi cu zi, la fel ca to脠聸i cet脛聝脠聸enii europeni, 脠聶i c脛聝 nu exist脛聝 vreo amenin脠聸are care s脛聝 pun脛聝 脙庐n pericol modul lor de a tr脛聝i, de a-脠聶i desf脛聝脠聶ura via脠聸a 脙庐n condi脠聸ii normale.

Totu脠聶i, 脙庐n actuala configura脠聸ie global脛聝, doar dac脛聝 e脠聶ti 脙庐narmat suficient e脠聶ti respectat. Aceasta este o lec脠聸ie pe care am 脙庐nv脛聝脠聸at-o, din p脛聝cate, 脙庐n ultimii ani de zile, iar perioada de naivitate a Uniunii Europene s-a terminat. Trebuie s脛聝 avem scut de ap脛聝rare antiaerian脛聝, s脛聝 avem drone, s脛聝 avem sisteme antidrone, s脛聝 avem sateli脠聸i, s脛聝 avem sisteme care s脛聝 ne protejeze, pentru c脛聝 doar 脠聶i numai atunci vom putea s脛聝 ne desf脛聝脠聶ur脛聝m via脠聸a 脙庐n mod normal. 脙聨n plus, trebuie s脛聝 avem grij脛聝 de industriile noastre strategice. Trebuie s脛聝 avem o脠聸el, trebuie s脛聝 avem aluminiu produs 脙庐n Uniunea European脛聝, pentru c脛聝 altfel nu vom putea realiza nimic din ceea ce 脙庐nseamn脛聝 asigurarea intereselor noastre strategice.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rasa Juknevi脛聧ien脛聴 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Gerbiama pirmininke, lygiai prie脜隆 35 metus pirmajame po okupacijos laisvai i脜隆rinktame parlamente balsavau u脜戮 Lietuvos Nepriklausomyb脛聶, o 脜隆iandien 脜隆i diskusija man 芒聙聯 to paties kelio t脛聶sinys kartu su Europa.

Mano 脜戮inia viena 芒聙聯 Europa bus stipri, jei pad脛聴sime Ukrainai laim脛聴ti. Ir mes galime, tik reikia patik脛聴ti, kad galime. 芒聙聻ReArm Europe芒聙聹 turi b脜芦ti 脛炉gyvendinama solidariai tarp Europos S脛聟jungos nari脜鲁 ir solidariai su Ukraina, kuri jau yra ES nar脛聴 de facto, nes savo krauju gina mus.

Ukrainos fronte sprend脜戮iamas Europos likimas. Ka脜戮kas paskai脛聧iavo, kad iki 脜隆iol kiekvienam Europos S脛聟jungos pilie脛聧iui Europos karin脛聴 parama Ukrainai nekainavo n脛聴 dviej脜鲁 puodeli脜鲁 kavos. Valstyb脛聴s nar脛聴s tikrai gali skirti Ukrainai daugiau. Sutarkim d脛聴l ne ma脜戮iau kaip 0,25脗聽proc. nuo BVP i脜隆 kiekvienos 脜隆alies. I脜隆kelkim ambicij脛聟: Ukraina 芒聙聯 Europos S脛聟jungos nar脛聴 iki 2030脗聽m. Pasikartokim istorijos pamokas ir priimkim i脜隆脜隆脜芦kius. 芒聙聻Keep calm, carry on芒聙聹 芒聙聯 Winston Churchill.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Temido (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Caro Presidente do Conselho, quero come脙搂ar por saudar as conclus脙碌es do Conselho Europeu extraordin脙隆rio da semana passada.

Mais do que nunca, todos compreendemos que a seguran脙搂a europeia est脙隆 estreitamente ligada ao desfecho da guerra na Ucr脙垄nia. O rearmamento da Uni脙拢o Europeia, nomeadamente para responder 脙聽s amea脙搂as h脙颅bridas, n脙拢o 脙漏 uma escolha, 脙漏 uma emerg脙陋ncia de dissuas脙拢o.

A Ucr脙垄nia precisa de apoio imediato, incluindo da mobiliza脙搂脙拢o dos ativos russos congelados, e as conclus脙碌es do Conselho mostram a compreens脙拢o de que vivemos uma rutura hist脙鲁rica.

Mas precisamos de ir mais longe. Da mesma forma que fomos capazes de o fazer na resposta 脙聽 pandemia, tamb脙漏m no investimento em seguran脙搂a e defesa precisamos de maior solidariedade e coordena脙搂脙拢o entre os Estados-Membros, nomeadamente atrav脙漏s de mutualiza脙搂脙拢o da d脙颅vida e de compras conjuntas.

E, ao mesmo tempo, precisamos de fazer tudo para manter a mesma ambi脙搂脙拢o de investimento nas 脙隆reas sociais e ambientais da Uni脙拢o Europeia, que s脙拢o a nossa raz脙拢o de ser.

Por 脙潞ltimo, precisamos de ser claros com os nossos cidad脙拢os: o momento que vivemos 脙漏 grave, e provavelmente n脙拢o o ultrapassaremos sem sacrif脙颅cios.脗聽A paz justa que queremos para a Ucr脙垄nia 脙漏 a paz que nos proteger脙隆, enquanto sociedades democr脙隆ticas e livres, a todos.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tonino Picula (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, I welcome the proposal to strengthen European Union defence capabilities with ReArm Europe, and I call on Member States to proceed forward.

I also believe that this House will support an even more ambitious position. It goes without saying that the transatlantic disruption caused by the new US administration has brought us long-term geopolitical changes. We must not base European security on voter attitudes in US swing states. The EU needs to plan de-risking policy towards Trump's Washington. This is not America that wants its allies to respect it. This is an America that wants its allies to fear it.

Europe must make some historical and responsible steps ahead. Bold leadership and adequate funds are needed, including through seizing EUR 200 billion of Russian assets. I always repeat that our security does not stop at our borders. That's why we have to act fast and to act now. Time is of the essence.

脗聽脗聽Dariusz Jo脜聞ski (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! 芒聙聻Bezpiecze脜聞stwo, Europo芒聙聺 to nie jest tylko has脜聜o polskiej prezydencji. To r脙鲁wnie脜录 jest wyzwanie, przed kt脙鲁rym stoi dzisiaj ca脜聜a Europa. I nie jest prawd脛聟, kiedy m脙鲁wi teraz, 脜录e ju脜录 nie chce, 脜录eby ludzie gin脛聶li, bo gdyby tak by脜聜o, to by trzy lata temu nie atakowa脜聜 Ukrainy.

Ot脙鲁脜录, cho脛聡 wiem, 脜录e jeszcze na tej sali s脛聟 osoby, kt脙鲁re by chcia脜聜y robi脛聡 interesy z Putinem 芒聙聯 cho脛聡 ich jest coraz mniej na szcz脛聶脜聸cie 芒聙聯 i szerz脛聟 tutaj rosyjsk脛聟 propagand脛聶, m.in. to, co m脙鲁wi Russia Today, to jest najwy脜录szy czas, 脜录eby powiedzie脛聡 stop i do脜聸脛聡. Do脜聸脛聡 ju脜录 tego. My dzisiaj musimy zbudowa脛聡 europejsk脛聟 polis脛聶 ubezpieczeniow脛聟 i 脜录eby tak by脜聜o, musimy zainwestowa脛聡 ogromne pieni脛聟dze w uzbrojenie. I ten plan Komisji Europejskiej, 脜录eby wyda脛聡 do o脜聸miuset miliard脙鲁w euro w脜聜a脜聸nie na uzbrojenie jest bardzo dobrym pomys脜聜em, tylko musimy to zrobi脛聡 szybko, bez debat, bez konsultacji, bez rocznych dyskusji. Po prostu musimy to zrobi脛聡 zaraz, bo my nie mamy czasu.

I chc脛聶 powiedzie脛聡 jeszcze jedn脛聟 rzecz. Polska chce przenie脜聸脛聡 z KPO, z krajowego planu odbudowy prawie 30 mld z脜聜 na to, 脜录eby w脜聜a脜聸nie zainwestowa脛聡 w uzbrojenie, bo bezpiecze脜聞stwo dla Europy jest dzisiaj najwa脜录niejsze.

脗聽脗聽Maria Grapini (S&D). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, domnule pre脠聶edinte al Consiliului European, domnule comisar, stima脠聸i colegi, ast脛聝zi trebuia s脛聝 dezbatem despre 脠聶edin脠聸a Consiliului din 20 martie. Este vorba de problema economic脛聝. Nu putem s脛聝 fim puternici 芒聙聯 a脠聸i spus, domnule ministru, 脠聶i pe bun脛聝 dreptate, trei piloni foarte importan脠聸i: simplificare, energie 脠聶i investi脠聸ii. Dac脛聝 aceste trei lucruri nu le vom face, nu avem cum s脛聝 ne ap脛聝r脛聝m.

Dac脛聝 vrei s脛聝 fii puternic 脙庐n lumea global脛聝, trebuie s脛聝 脠聶tii s脛聝 negociezi 脠聶i cu cei mai puternici ca tine 脠聶i cu cei mai slabi ca tine 脠聶i s脛聝 ai clar o economie solid脛聝, care 脙庐脠聸i permite dup脛聝 aceea s脛聝 po脠聸i s脛聝 stai la masa negocierilor. De aceea, domnule pre脠聶edinte Costa, special pentru dumneavoastr脛聝, v脛聝 rog s脛聝 discuta脠聸i 脙庐n 20 martie ce solu脠聸ii avem pentru energie. IMM-urile sunt la p脛聝m脙垄nt, popula脠聸ia nu-脠聶i poate pl脛聝ti energia, cel pu脠聸in 脙庐n 脠聸ara mea 脠聶i 脙庐n alte 脠聸脛聝ri din est.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hilde Vautmans (Renew). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, mevrouw Ursula von der Leyen heeft haar job gedaan. Ze heeft op korte termijn gezorgd voor 800脗聽miljard euro aan extra budgetten en de lidstaten hebben het goedgekeurd en gingen naar huis. Maar ik richt mij nu tot u, want nu begint het echte werk. Ik wil u ertoe oproepen om niet gewoon meer van hetzelfde te doen. We zullen het anders moeten doen. Ik reken erop dat u een "coalition of the buying" aangaat. Koop samen aan. We weten wat er nodig is: een Europees luchtafweersysteem en een Europese nucleaire paraplu.

Ik heb de heren Merz en Macron gehoord. Frankrijk en Duitsland doen mee en als zij meedoen, dan zullen andere landen sowieso mee instappen. Doe dit gewoon. Neem ook de Noren en de Britten mee aan tafel. We weten allemaal waarom. Laat ons niet gewoon meer van hetzelfde doen, alleen maar een "coalition of the willing" vormen. Er is nog 脙漏脙漏n grote stap die u samen met de Commissie moet zetten 芒聙聯 en ik heb dit vandaag maar een paar keer gehoord 芒聙聯脗聽nl. de stap naar een Europees leger. Dat is wat we nodig hebben.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ana Miranda Paz (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤ora Von der Leyen, ante la escalada militarista y belicista, le traslado como eurodiputada de una organizaci脙鲁n pacifista nuestra m脙隆s rotunda oposici脙鲁n a este rearmamento europeo.

A la se脙卤ora de la guerra no le importa que el gasto militar lo paguen los pueblos, no le importa que la factura de la guerra la pague la ciudadan脙颅a, prefiere apoyar el belicismo antes que la pol脙颅tica social, prefiere apoyar m脙隆s gasto militar y menos servicios p脙潞blicos como la vivienda, la salud o las pensiones.

Hay que volver a la defensa de la paz, a la resoluci脙鲁n pac脙颅fica de conflictos, a la diplomacia, a la defensa de los derechos humanos. Hay que volver a la democracia, no a estar supeditado ni a la OTAN ni a los Estados Unidos. No al rearme, ni a la escalada militar y belicista de la Uni脙鲁n Europea. (La oradora pronuncia una frase en gallego).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脙聳zlem Demirel (The Left). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Frau von der Leyen sagte: Die Zeit der Illusionen ist vorbei. Und sie streute dabei aber so viel Sand in die Augen der Bev脙露lkerung, dass man kaum noch richtig schauen kann. Sie nutzt die Verunsicherung in der Bev脙露lkerung f脙录r ein gigantisches Aufr脙录stungspaket. 800脗聽Milliarden脗聽Euro: nicht f脙录r Bildung, f脙录r Jobs oder soziale Gerechtigkeit, sondern f脙录r Waffen. Das wird nicht zu mehr Sicherheit und Frieden hier in Europa beitragen. Nein, es dient nicht unserem Bed脙录rfnis. Es dient der Gro脙聼machtkonkurrenz, 脙录ber die Frau von der Leyen seit 2020 in diesem Parlament immer wieder gesprochen hat.

Liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, an diejenigen, die hier immer die alten R脙露mer zitieren, dass diejenigen, die den Frieden wollen, jetzt f脙录r den Krieg r脙录sten sollten. Ich m脙露chte euch sagen: Dann geht doch ab an die Front, aber schreit nicht nach der Bev脙露lkerung! Unsere S脙露hne, unsere Kinder kriegt ihr nicht.

Die Bev脙露lkerung in Europa sehnt sich nicht nach Krieg, sie sehnt sich nach Frieden.脗聽Und unsere Geschichte in Europa hat uns beigebracht, dass immer mehr Waffen nicht zu Frieden beitragen werden.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Menschen Europas! Unsere Frau Kommissionspr脙陇sidentin von der Leyen wird es nicht wissen, aber sie war einmal meine Vorgesetzte. W脙陇hrend sie in Deutschland Verteidigungsministerin war, diente ich als Ladesch脙录tze bei der Panzertruppe in Munster, in Leopard-2-Panzern, die heute in der Ukraine im Einsatz sind脗聽芒聙聯 und genau dort geh脙露ren sie hin.

Die Europ脙陇ische Union ist ein Friedensprojekt, aber solange die UN ein zahnloser Tiger ist, muss man Frieden leider verteidigen. Und daf脙录r brauchen wir langfristig eine europ脙陇ische Armee, denn wohl kaum werden die Spanier die Portugiesen angreifen, die Polen die Tschechen oder脗聽芒聙聯 Gott beh脙录te脗聽芒聙聯 die Luxemburger uns Deutsche.

Mittelfristig muss die Beistandspflicht aus Artikel脗聽42 Nummer脗聽7脗聽des Vertrags 脙录ber die Europ脙陇ische Union dem Artikel脗聽5 des NATO-Vertrags mindestens gleichkommen, aber kurzfristig wird der Kampf um unsere Freiheit in der Ukraine gef脙录hrt. Und dementsprechend muss es unsere gr脙露脙聼te Priorit脙陇t bei unserer eigenen Verteidigung sein, die weitere und nun mit dem Wegfall der USA noch st脙陇rkere Aufr脙录stung der Ukraine voranzutreiben. Denn der Feind steht脗聽芒聙聯 leider genauso wie bei unseren 脙聹bungen damals auf dem Truppen脙录bungsplatz脗聽芒聙聯 im Osten.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽H脙漏lder Sousa Silva (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Presidente do Conselho, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, as conclus脙碌es do Conselho extraordin脙隆rio representam o reconhecer de uma estrat脙漏gia europeia que, durante d脙漏cadas, desenvolveu um modelo de seguran脙搂a fraco e contradit脙鲁rio, uma Europa geopoliticamente relevante, mas sem os instrumentos de poder necess脙隆rios para influenciar a sua pr脙鲁pria vizinhan脙搂a.

Andamos agora a correr para recuperar o tempo perdido.脗聽A seguran脙搂a n脙拢o 脙漏, t脙拢o芒聙聭s脙鲁, mais uma pol脙颅tica p脙潞blica, mas a condi脙搂脙拢o existencial de todas as pol脙颅ticas p脙潞blicas.

O tempo da guerra 脙漏 tamb脙漏m distinto do tempo da burocracia em Bruxelas. Enquanto a Europa delibera, as linhas da frente movem-se, as capacidades industriais de defesa alteram-se, e as vulnerabilidades aprofundam-se.

Com o plano ReArm Europe, 脙漏 ineg脙隆vel que se abre uma nova janela de oportunidade para a Europa. 脙聣 mesmo tempo de mudar.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Thomas Bajada (S&D). 芒聙聯 Sinjura President, a脛搂na rridu l-pa脛聥i jew li tkompli l-gwerra? B脛搂ala so脛聥jalist u b脛搂ala rappre脜录entant minn pajji脜录, Stat Membru newtrali, g脛搂andi inkwiet trawmatizzanti, li na脛搂dmu biss li nikkreaw ekonomija ta' gwerra. Minn suq uniku Ewropew ta' popli, prodotti, servizzi u kapital, ninbidlu g脛搂al wie脛搂ed ta' munizzjon u armamenti li jeqirdu u mhux jg脛搂aqqdu.

Iva, huwa 脛聥arissimu li hemm b脜录onn u hemm bi脜录a' serju ta' inva脜录joni fit-territorju tal-Unjoni. Spe脛聥jalment issa bl-in脛聥ertezza li 脛隆ejjin min-na脛搂a l-o脛搂ra tal-Atlantiku. U b'hekk nifhem li l-gvernijiet Ewropej iridu jing脛搂aqdu fi sforzi kon脛隆unti fejn jid脛搂ol l-investiment personali tag脛搂hom g脛搂ad-difi脜录a. Argument li jag脛搂mel sens, per脙虏 ma nistg脛搂ux nag脛搂mlu dan askapitu tal-ambizzjonijiet u tat-tama ta脛聥-脛聥ittadini Ewropej. 脛娄bieb, fl-istess waqt u bl-istess sa脛搂脛搂a, ejja ninvestu fin-nies, fil-脛搂ajja tan-nies u mhux inaqqsuhom. Ejja 脛搂a nibba脜录aw l-ekonomija Ewropea fuq il-benesseri u mhux fuq il-gwerra, fuq il-pa脛聥i u mhux fuq il-qerda.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Ende der spontanen Wortmeldungen)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maro脜隆 脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Mr脗聽President of the European Council, honourable minister, my dear colleague Commissioner Kos, honourable Members of the European Parliament, first and foremost, I really would like to thank you for all the interventions and debate, which for sure will enter into history as a debate, as a plenary, where we made a decisive step towards building common European defence.

The spirit of the consensus from the European Council 芒聙聯 unprecedented and unthinkable just a few weeks ago 芒聙聯 was felt, I would say, in most of the debate very strongly here as well, in this House of European democracy.

As many of you said, we have to think differently. Only in that way can we mobilise Europe's massive resources and start the deterrence build-up we need.

Many of you honourable Members were absolutely right to underscore that we cannot benefit from a peace dividend anymore. To the contrary, we must address the running security deficit. It's a huge task, and I would say that we must work on all levels 芒聙聯 European, national. We have to bring together our industry, our economic stakeholders.

As you heard from President von der Leyen, the first-ever Commissioner for Defence, Mr脗聽Kubilius, who also joined our debate this morning, together with the whole College of Commissioners, will be working day and night to achieve these goals.

We all realise that a powerful Europe means just and lasting peace in Ukraine, and a free and sovereign Ukraine means stronger Europe.

My colleague, Commissioner Kos, here with us today, has moved cooperation with Ukraine to a new level. She works tirelessly to support Ukraine in the path towards European membership. And I agree with her that we have to underline that we are living in historic times and therefore extraordinary challenges require extraordinary answers.

As we discussed the last time in this plenary, new geopolitics and transactional fragmentation of the world means that size matters again. Therefore, enlargement of the European family, enlargement of democracy and enlargement of respect for our values are the best answers for today's unstable world. The Commission will work hard to achieve these goals.

Let me conclude by the words of Mr脗聽Glucksmann, who said:

脗芦C芒聙聶est l芒聙聶heure de la v脙漏rit脙漏 pour l芒聙聶Europe脗禄 芒聙聯脗聽et je suis tout 脙聽 fait d芒聙聶accord avec lui.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adam Sz脜聜apka, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam President, President Costa, honourable Commissioners,脗聽honourable Members, thank you very much for your remarks and comments. It's clear for everyone that we are at a crucial juncture. The special European Council last week marked important progress, but we are aware that we have crucial work ahead of us, and an important part of that work must be carried out by the Council.

The Council is ready to do its job and follow up on the special European Council conclusions with the urgency that the moment requires, namely in the setting-up of the necessary instruments to bolster security in the European Union, and the ramping-up of the European defence, technological and industrial base.

Let me address the call of some MEPs on frozen assets. There will be a debate on it tomorrow. Last year, G7 leaders agreed to use the revenues from the immobilisation of Russian assets in the EU for repaying loans to Ukraine granted by the EU and G7 in the amount of USD脗聽50脗聽billion. The Council will consider all possible solutions in coordination with our international partners.

Thank you very much for this debate and thank you very much for your attention.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ant脙鲁nio Costa, President of the European Council. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, honourable Members of the European Commission, Minister Sz脜聜apka, Members of the European Parliament, thank you for this opportunity for this democratic dialogue. Allow me to give five short remarks.

First, peace without defence is an illusion. We need to invest more, better and faster in our own defence. It is what we are doing and we need to do more, to invest more, to invest together, to develop common projects for sure. Last week we made a decisive step, but not the last one. We need to continue and we will continue.

Second, defence is broader than ReArm. We need to invest in our strategic autonomy to reduce our dependencies on energy, to secure supply chains, to fight disinformation and protect the integrity of our democratic process and to keep our borders under control.

Third, the European Union and the Member States do not need to choose to invest in peace or to invest in health systems, education or housing. ReArm and the escape clause provide additional funds for defence. Strengthening our societies, protecting our citizens and boosting our economies is essential to strengthening our defence.

Fourth, this war is not only about Ukraine. In fact, this war is about international law: a rules-based international order. Protecting the universal values enshrined in the United Nations Charter 芒聙聯 sovereignty, territorial integrity, self-determination, respect of international recognised borders 芒聙聯 is essential in Ukraine, as it is essential in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, in the Kingdom of Denmark or in Cyprus. So we need to stand for the international law, because international law must be a must win this war.

A last remark: we need to invest more in diplomacy. We need to engage with partners around the world and widen a global network with, of course, our transatlantic partners and beyond 芒聙聯 by enlargement, by trade 芒聙聯 and stand for a multilateral system, and champion global causes like climate change, fighting poverty or engaging to develop the pact for the future.

These five remarks synthesise what we need to continue to do to ensure our security and to contribute to support for Ukraine for a comprehensive, just and lasting peace.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The debate is closed.

Thank you very much for this interesting debate and for the time discipline. It's really remarkable. We are perfectly on time, so I will suspend for a very short moment until all colleagues have arrived, and then we will continue.

Schriftliche Erkl脙陇rungen (Artikel 178)

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kinga G脙隆l (PfE), 脙颅r脙隆sban. 芒聙聯 H脙隆rom 脙漏ve d脙潞l a puszt脙颅t脙鲁 h脙隆bor脙潞 Ukrajn脙隆ban 脙漏s Eur脙鲁pa hatalmas 脙隆rat fizet 脙漏rte. Miel脜聭bbi fegyversz脙录netre 脙漏s b脙漏ket脙隆rgyal脙隆sokra, tart脙鲁s b脙漏k脙漏re van sz脙录ks脙漏g, nem pedig t脙露bb fegyversz脙隆ll脙颅t脙隆sra, t脙露bb h脙隆bor脙潞ra. Itt az ideje, hogy az Eur脙鲁pai Uni脙鲁 vizsg脙隆lja fel脙录l hib脙隆s Ukrajna-strat脙漏gi脙隆j脙隆t. Eur脙鲁p脙隆nak a b脙漏keteremt脙漏sre kellene 脙露sszpontos脙颅tania ahelyett, hogy m脙漏g t脙露bb p脙漏nzt k脙录ldene Ukrajna katonai meger脜聭s脙颅t脙漏s脙漏re 脙漏s a h脙隆bor脙潞 folytat脙隆s脙隆ra.

Most, hogy az Egyes脙录lt 脙聛llamok is visszavett a h脙隆bor脙潞 t脙隆mogat脙隆s脙隆b脙鲁l, Eur脙鲁p脙隆ban is vissza kellene t脙漏rni a j脙鲁zan 脙漏sz talaj脙隆ra, v脙隆ltoz脙隆sra van sz脙录ks脙漏g az EU h脙隆bor脙潞val kapcsolatos strat脙漏gi脙隆j脙隆ban.

A megv脙隆ltozott vil脙隆gpolitikai helyzetben ugyanakkor kulcsfontoss脙隆g脙潞, hogy Eur脙鲁pa meg tudja v脙漏deni mag脙隆t 脙漏s t脙露bbet tegyen saj脙隆t b脙漏k脙漏j脙漏nek 脙漏s biztons脙隆g脙隆nak garant脙隆l脙隆s脙隆脙漏rt. De az Uni脙鲁 v脙漏delmi k脙漏pess脙漏gei meger脜聭s脙颅t脙漏s脙漏nek k脙漏rd脙漏se nem k脙露thet脜聭 az Ukrajn脙隆nak ny脙潞jtott tov脙隆bbi katonai seg脙颅ts脙漏ghez. Ezeknek k脙录l脙露n脙隆ll脙鲁 k脙漏rd脙漏seknek kell maradniuk.

A v脙漏delem ter脙录let脙漏n t脙隆mogatjuk azt az elk脙漏pzel脙漏st, hogy Eur脙鲁p脙隆nak k脙漏pesnek kell lennie arra, hogy megv脙漏dje mag脙隆t. De a d脙露nt脙漏shozatalnak tag脙隆llami hat脙隆sk脙露rben kell maradnia. Mik脙露zben t脙隆mogatjuk a tag脙隆llamok k脙露z脙露tti ipari egy脙录ttm脜卤k脙露d脙漏st 脙漏s a k脙露z脙露s programokat, ellenz脙录nk minden olyan f脙露deralista t脙潞lkap脙隆st, t脙潞lterjeszked脙漏st a Bizotts脙隆g r脙漏sz脙漏r脜聭l, amely al脙隆脙隆ssa a nemzeti szuverenit脙隆st.
