脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

IN THE CHAIR: ROBERTA METSOLA
President

脗聽

4. International Women芒聙聶s Day
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Dear colleagues, honourable guests, dear Sviatlana, Palina, Leniie and Tata, thank you for joining us as we mark International Women's Day. I am proud that this House has, and will always be, a force for democracy, equality and solidarity.

Today we pay tribute to the courageous women across Europe who are standing up for the values so many of us far too often take for granted. Women who embody the spirit of courage, defiance and the audacity to believe in a better world, who are on the front lines of the struggle for a future of freedom, who are putting everything on the line as they refuse to give up on what they believe in: Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya, leader of the democratic forces of Belarus; Palina Sharenda-Panasiuk, a former political prisoner in Belarus; Leniie Umerova, a former political prisoner from Crimea, and Tata Kepler, a Ukrainian medical volunteer and activist.

Dear friends, dear ladies, your brave actions are an inspiration to this House and to all who cherish liberty everywhere. Thank you for being an example for girls and boys across Europe and the world. Thank you.

(Applause)

In Ukraine today, hundreds of women and men are fighting every day for that dream of peace and freedom. We know their sacrifice and we honour what they stand for.

In Belarus, too many women and men are arbitrarily imprisoned and denied their rights under the boot of dictatorship. We know what they have given up and their plight is not forgotten.

So Sviatlana, Palina, Leniie, Tata, thank you. This House thanks you. Europe thanks you. I now pass the floor to my friend and a force that the world needs to hear more of, Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya, leader of the democratic forces of Belarus. 芒聙聯 Dear Members of the European Parliament, dear President Metsola, dear Roberta, thank you for your powerful words, for our friendship and for your incredible leadership.

I always say that unconventional times need unconventional solutions, and last year, Roberta, we took such a step together. We signed a memorandum of cooperation between Belarusian democratic forces and the European Parliament. It was a clear message to my people: Belarus is Europe. And one day we will take our rightful place in this Chamber.

(Applause)

I want to give thanks to the members of the Delegation for Relations with Belarus, led by Ma脜聜gorzata Gosiewska, and to rapporteur Helmut Brandst脙陇tter. Thank you for working with democratic Belarus and not with the illegitimate regime in Minsk.

Just last week, in Rome, we held a historic summit of the Inter-Parliamentary Alliance for Democratic Belarus, hosted by the Italian Parliament. Deputies from 17 European countries signed the Rome Memorandum, showing that Belarus's fight for freedom is Europe's fight too.

I want to commend the European External Action Service, the European Commission and the European Endowment for Democracy for your support for Belarusian media and civil society. Every euro spent on truth and resistance builds a shield against the spread of tyranny.

Finally, I want to thank all your nations for welcoming Belarusians fleeing repression, especially Poland and Lithuania, which have sheltered hundreds of thousands.

(Applause)

And I want to congratulate Lithuanian friends who celebrate the Day of Restoration of Independence today. From your own history, you know the true value of solidarity when your homeland is under occupation. A脛聧iu, draugai!

(Applause)

Esteemed Members of the European Parliament. I want to thank each of you not only for standing with Belarus and Ukraine, but also Georgia, Moldova and Armenia. We all stand in this in the same 'zone of uncertainty', asking ourselves: Who will stand with us for our freedom? Will our countries ever be safe and free, or will we remain in the shadow of a giant 芒聙聯 a force that does not respect us, does not recognise our sovereignty, and denies our place as free nations of Europe? Are we to be sacrificed, offered to the giant in the hope that giving him just us will make them stop? That he will take what he wants and go no further?

But we know history. The hunger of the empire is never satisfied. Yes, we live in uncertain times, but uncertainty must not lead to hesitation. Who will defend Europe if not Europeans themselves? Must we always look across the Atlantic for protection? Or is it time to say we will stand up for each other? 'For our freedom and yours,' as the famous motto says.

Dear friends, in such times, International Women's Day is not about celebration, it's about struggle. Today, many Belarusian women don't receive flowers, they receive prison sentences. They don't march in parades, they march into courtrooms. They don't enjoy the luxury of peace and democracy, they have to fight for it.

And we fight. We fight not to be in the shadow of the revanchist Russian Empire, seeking to subjugate our nations and to erase everything that connects us to Europe 芒聙聯 our language, our culture, our identity.

We fight for our political prisoners. More than 1脗聽200 behind bars, including 155 women 芒聙聯 isolated, tortured, denied medical care. Yet they refuse to give up.

We fight for justice so that every perpetrator 芒聙聯 of oppression, of torture and war crimes 芒聙聯 is held accountable. Because justice delayed is justice denied.

We fight for the very values that define Europe 芒聙聯 freedom, security and prosperity. And Belarus is proof: without freedom, there is no security, and without security there is no prosperity.

And, of course, we fight for Ukraine because our fates are intertwined. Without a free Ukraine, there will be no free Belarus, and without a free Belarus, there will be no lasting peace in Europe.

And we fight against illusions. Putin and Lukashenka don't want peace. They want submission. Any lasting peace must be built on respect, yet Putin has shown none. Like a drunken neighbour, he acts aggressively whenever he doesn't get his way. Without a real shift in his ambitions, any peace will be just a pause 芒聙聯 a brief reprieve for Belarus and Ukraine.

It may not stop there. Ask Poland, Lithuania, Latvia, Estonia. They all wonder: 'If Ukraine falls, if Belarus disappears, are we next?' There should be no next.

That is why we also fight for Europe 芒聙聯 Europe that doesn't give in to dictators and keeps them isolated, but not appeased. Because we know dictators will not stop until we stop them. We can do it only together.

Dear friends, a free Belarus is only possible when Europe is strong and united. As you discuss rearming Europe, I urge you to see support for Belarus as an investment into your own security. Belarus is key to stopping Russia's advance in Europe. Without a free and independent Belarus, the threat of war will not end 芒聙聯 not for Ukraine, not for Europe.

As some allies pull back, I call on the European Union to step up. On one hand, increase assistance for Belarusian independent media. Strengthen our resistance. Show Belarusians a clear European perspective, so they know what they're fighting for.

On the other hand, intensify pressure on Lukashenka's regime. I urge you to see Belarus not only as a problem, but as an opportunity to make Europe safer. While isolating dictators, don't isolate our people. Provide visas, close borders for goods, not for passengers. We must not allow the dictators to build a new iron curtain and cement Russia's grip on Belarus for years to come.

Of course, change in Belarus is our task and duty. But to win, we need allies. I came here to say: we need you, Europe. And we need each other.

(Applause)

Let me assure you: we will not stop. Belarusians will not stop until every political prisoner is free. Until every Russian soldier leaves our country. Until we hold trials for crimes against humanity. Until we take our country back.

Yes, this fight is not easy. But thanks to you, to your passion, to your continued support, I know that Belarus and Ukraine will be free. And when that day comes, we will stand together in a safe and peaceful Europe 芒聙聯 free of war, free of tyranny and free of fear. A Europe where borders are not redrawn by force, where nations choose their own future, where democracy always prevails and where Belarus, Ukraine and other nations are not in the shadow of the empire, but take our rightful place in the heart of Europe.

Zhyve Belarus! Slava Ukraini! Long live Europe!

(The House rose and accorded the speaker a standing ovation)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Thank you, dear Sviatlana.

I now pass the floor to Belarusian opposition activist and former political prisoner Palina Sharenda-Panasiuk.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Palina Sharenda-Panasiuk, former political prisoner in Belarus. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Members of the European Parliament. My name is Palina Sharenda-Panasiuk and I represent here the European Belarus civil campaign. We support the integration of a free Belarus into Europe and this flag is the symbol of our struggle, of our fight and of our movement. Because of these flags, my friends and I have been beaten, imprisoned and subjected to terrible torture. Our campaign was also involved in the preparation of the revolution of 2020 and that is why I was arrested in January 2021, along with other members of our European Belarus campaign.

I spent four years as a political hostage of the Lukashenka regime. I endured all the hell of the dictator's prisons, where the methods of physical and psychological torture from Stalin's times remain unchanged. Four years of complete isolation from the outside world, from my children and my family. Four years spent in solitary confinement, in prison transport vehicles and a total of 2070 days in so-called punishment cells, places of total isolation and torture. But I endured and I was freed. I was freed in part thanks to your principled position, dear Members of the European Parliament. I am especially grateful to President Roberta Metsola for her tireless efforts in securing my release. I also extend my heartfelt thanks to all the Members of this House who fought for my freedom.

But thousands of other political prisoners remain hostages of the terrorist regime in Belarus. Among them my friends from the European Belarus campaign Yauhen Afnahel, Maksim Vinyarskii, Andrei Voynich and Paval Yukhnevich. For more than two years, there has been no information at all about many political prisoners, including Mikola Statkevich. We don't even know whether he's still alive. The situation with women political prisoners as with Viktoryia Kulsha, Alena Hnauk and Volha Mayorava is also catastrophic. I'm deeply worried for their lives.

Ladies and gentlemen, for 30 years there have been concentration camps in the very heart of Europe, on the very border of the European Union. Places where people are tortured and killed simply because they want freedom. It is unacceptable for the civilised world to allow Belarus to remain under the rule of a mentally deranged terrorist. I was freed only because of the pressure on this regime. Do not believe the regime's false claims. Do not believe the lies of its cronies, that political prisoners are released on humanitarian grounds. They are not. Because people are tortured into signing pardon letters to Lukashenka. They are forced to give confession statements and to sign cooperation agreements with the KGB. They are beaten, mutilated, blackmailed, and they are threatened. Hundreds of thousands of Belarusians were forced to flee the country, threatened with new repressions.

I call for increased pressure on Lukashenka's regime, not only through new sanctions but also with strict monitoring of their enforcement. A full blockade and trade embargo under Article 33 of the International Labour Organisation charter must be imposed and there should be no transit of goods from China and other eastern countries to Europe through the concentration camp named Belarus. The regime must be completely ostracised politically, economically and also during all sports events. These are real actions that will force the regime to release political prisoners. It will be the first step towards the liberation of all the people of Belarus.

Without a free Belarus, the existence of freedom and democracy in Europe will always be under threat, including under threat from Russian aggression. Furthermore, a victory in Ukraine will not be possible without a free Belarus. Belarusian opposition is fighting against dictatorship for the free, independent and democratic Belarus. We are fighting for the values on which the European Union is founded. In this struggle, we need your help. We need your help to protect and defend our own values. You can help us to free our friends and leaders who are currently in prison. You can help Europe get rid of the criminals who unleashed the bloody war on Ukraine, Lukashenka and Putin, for our freedom and yours. Long live Belarus!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Thank you very much, dear Palina.

I now pass the floor to Crimean Tatar and former political prisoner Leniie Umerova.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Leniie Umerova, Tatar former political prisoner from Crimea. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, my name is Leniie Umerova. I am a Ukrainian and Crimean Tatar activist. I spent nearly two years in Russian prison.

In December 2022, I was abducted by Russian security forces right at the border, as I was trying to reach Crimea to visit my critically ill father. My only crime was that I'm Crimean Tatar. I'm Ukrainian, born in Crimea, and I refused to take Russian passport. For Russia, that's enough to label me an enemy and throw me behind bars. During these years, I endured immense injustice and cruelty: abduction, psychological and physical abuse, interrogation, solitary confinement. Most of the time I was held in complete isolation.脗聽But you know what? I'm Crimean Tatar, and these are not the first repression in my family history.

In 1944, my grandmother was just 10 years old when she became a victim of Soviet deportation. Her entire family 芒聙聯 like hundreds of thousands of Crimean Tatars, mostly women and children, as the men were at war 芒聙聯 was declared a traitor of the motherland. They were given 20 minutes to pack. They were forced into cattle wagons and deported to Uzbekistan. Two of my grandmother's younger brothers, three-year-old Kadir-Ilyas and six-year-old Nazim, died of starvation. This is not only my family tragedy. This is the pain of every Crimean Tatar family.

Many countries have already recognised the 1944 deportation as the act of genocide against Crimean Tatar people, because history must be called by its true name. But history is repeating itself. Once again, Russia is trying to destroy us. Once again, it erases identities, rewrites borders, and crushes lives. In the occupied territories, Russia deports even ordinary civilians. They are abused, forcibly deported, subjected to forced labour. Any act of defiance, even silent, is treated as a crime. The punishment is prison.

Let me share the stories of several women I personally know. Oksana from Kherson region: before she was sent to a detention centre, she was held for six months with a bag over the head. One day she was brought to a pit and they told her, 'We will bury you alive here. No one will know where you are.' Yana from Zaporizhzhia region: she was tortured, her jaw was broken, several teeth knocked out, and then she was denied all medical care. Halyna Dovhopola from Crimea: a 70-year-old pensioner. Russian forces beat her, tortured here and tried to break her fingers. Iryna Danylovych, a Crimean journalist: she survived torture. Today she is denied medical help in prison. She has lost hearing in the ear, suffered a mini stroke, half her body is numb. She lives in constant pain with severe headaches and heart problems.

These are just a few stories. There are thousands more. Around 14脗聽000 Ukrainian civilians are currently being held in Russian prison. In prison, I constantly heard the same phrase: your Ukraine has very little time left. But I'm here, and Ukraine continues to fight, even in the face of devastating news. We are fighting for freedom, for dignity, for every human life. And we are not facing Russia alone. Russia is supported by Belarus, Iran, China and North Korea. Authoritarian regimes have united 芒聙聯 against democracy. This is more than a war for territory. This is the battle between light and darkness, between freedom and slavery, between democracy and tyranny. And Ukraine is still fighting and we are fighting for the win. But just imagine 芒聙聯 what if Ukraine falls? Who will be next?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Thank you very much, dear Leniie. Thank you very, very much.

I now pass the floor to Ukrainian frontline medical volunteer and humanitarian activist Tata Kepler.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tata Kepler, Ukrainian medical volunteer and activist. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, I'd like to start, please, with a moment of silence in the memory of all Ukrainian women and men that were killed by Russia during this war.

(The House rose and observed a minute芒聙聶s silence)

Thank you.

My name is Tata Kepler, I'm a volunteer and I deal with military medicine, with the tactical medicine supplies. When the full scale invasion started, I joined the unit that was organised by the Patrol Police of Ukraine. Due to that, I visited with my team more than 700 occupied territories and grey zones all over the frontlines. We were helping the civilian people with the medicine and a lot of different stuff.

I had one project that I talk little about in order to be respectful to survivors. But today I'll tell you something about it. It's called the 'silent' project and it's targeted directly to support raped families. We helped everyone with everything being asked for, from restoring houses to buying clothes and other necessary things. My oldest 'case' in this project was a 75-year-old woman that was raped by Russian soldiers and my youngest 'case' was four. She was a four-year-old girl.

I met her in her deoccupied village and she was, you know, really nice and beautiful and so cute and so sweet. She was raped by Russian soldiers 芒聙聯 not one 芒聙聯 when they came to her village and she did not understand what happened to her and why, as she said, those people were so bad to her.

The family was poor and I was asked to insulate her home and to buy some warm clothes. My friend and I went to the store, we walked between the rows with all those bright children's things and I saw grey warm overalls on pink rubber bands. I said, 'Oh, how nice, how beautiful. She will definitely love this. Let's buy this.' My friend took this one from the hanger and silently turned to me. On the reverse side, on its knees, there was written: 'Stay safe. Always.'

Then I did not cry, no, I howled. I was screaming. I was standing in the middle of that huge store holding this tiny jumpsuit and I howled because I realised that I won't be able to keep her safe anymore. Never.

You know, I understood that I'm still standing there in the middle of the store with the small grey overalls in my hands. I'll still remain forever in Bucha, standing over the people shot point blank because I saw them. I'll be forever holding the hands of a girl from Bucha. Her name was Julia and she was grasping at me and shouting, 'Help me, please help me! My husband and my brother were killed in the basement, their bodies are mined and people don't let me in. So please help me, Tata, help me!'脗聽But I couldn't.

I stand again and again in a small village in Kurakhovo in the Donetsk district and persuaded the grandmother Zoia to go with us. We said that we can help to save her life, but she refused because her only home was there. I'm also there, waiting for my friends to return from captivity, listening to the stories of survivors, and I'm still there, hugging my pharmacist, Ania, who has been waiting for the body of her husband for more than a year to be transported home and to be buried.脗聽I'm here in the stabilisation point, looking at doctors who are bringing back young, wounded soldiers from the dead. I'm still there, carrying flowers with black ribbons to the graves of my friends who are now and forever 20, 25, 30, 40. They freeze in time, as in my memory. Forever.

I'm telling you that because I want you to know that my country and I, like every other citizen, we will never return from this war, unfortunately. We can come home, we can come back, but we will never return. This will stay with us forever. With me, with my friends, with all the citizens of Ukraine and with this four-year-old girl.

We have to talk about the woman that fighting for democracy, it's really a very important topic, but today all women and men in my country are forced to fight for survival. Today, more than 130脗聽000 women in Ukraine serve in various institutions of the security and defence sector. It's a huge number. I believe that it's the biggest in Europe. Today, tens of thousands of women, mothers, sisters and daughters are waiting for their relatives to return from Russian captivity. Tens of thousands of women will never see their beloved again because they were killed by Russia.

These numbers are terrible and they should scare you, because this is reality that happens not somewhere far away, but here in Europe. Here and now, the genocide of Ukrainian people are spreading before your eyes, it's true, and I ask you to stop tolerating it by silence.

Today, more than 1脗聽300脗聽000 Ukrainian children 芒聙聯 again, 1脗聽300脗聽000 Ukrainian children 芒聙聯 are in the occupied territories. One more terrible number: according to Russia itself, 744脗聽000 Ukrainian children were exported from Ukraine and that means kidnapped. 744脗聽000. It's a terrible number. 2脗聽387 children were injured and killed, and this is the number that we know because there are a lot of numbers that we don't know.

What I see? I see the large芒聙聭scale international violence unfolding in front of you and we cannot stop it just by being concerned. I'm really asking you to name names because it's really important. The things that are going on in Ukraine right now, this war, it's a really terrible war. I understand that you, here in Europe, you see the news and you think that you understand us. But I'm telling you all these stories because I want you to try to imagine, just for a moment, this personal hell of every Ukrainian citizen.

Grammatically, Ukraine is a feminine word. We are bombed, they tried to annex us and to destroy us with all this hell, you know. Anyway, I'm so proud to be Ukrainian. I'm so proud that I was born there and that I live in Ukraine. Ukraine is a country of courage. Slava Ukraini!

(The House rose and observed a minute芒聙聶s silence)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Thank you all for your decisive and powerful words. They will resonate in this Chamber long after this event is over. Thank you to our honoured guests for joining us and sharing your experiences with us today. This House stands with you because Europe is on the side of democracy everywhere, every time. Thank you all.

(Applause)

脗聽

Video of the speeches
脗聽脗聽

(The sitting resumed at 12:54)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Yes, Mr脗聽Glucksmann, is this a point of order?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rapha脙芦l Glucksmann (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, it is actually a point of order. It's a point that will show that we have solidarity among ourselves as Members of this Parliament.

Yesterday evening, on Russian TV, Vladimir Solovyov, the main propagandist of Putin's regime, attacked, insulted, threatened one of us: Pina Picierno, Vice-President of this House, has been assaulted with terrific violence on Russian TV.

And then you had campaigns of trolls and bots inside our own territory. So today it's time for us to show solidarity in front of fascist propagandists ...

(The President cut off the speaker)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Okay, Mr脗聽Glucksmann, thank you. The point is made. But can I please once again repeat that points of order are the only things that will be accepted just before the vote. It has to be done. Otherwise, we will never have rules that are followed. But thank you very much for the point you made.

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next vote is on the report by Christian Ehler on the assessment of the implementation of Horizon Europe in view of its interim evaluation and recommendations for the 10th Research Framework Programme (see minutes, item 6.8).

I give the floor to the rapporteur for two minutes.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

芒聙聯 Before the vote:

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Christian Ehler, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, this is not the usual comment of a rapporteur highlighting his sunny personality.

This is a decisive vote of this Parliament. This is about whether we are going to have a framework programme, an independent research framework programme, in the future or not. Whether it's squeezed into an absurd idea of an all-in-all competitiveness programme, which is squeezing together 35 years of independent research policies.

The committee had been clearly committing to that, and that's the second shot for the Commission today, because the Warsaw Declaration 芒聙聯 the unanimous declaration of all research ministers in Warsaw 芒聙聯 had been saying no to that endeavour.

We need an independent program. It has a mid-term, a long-term perspective. It must be open, it must be blue sky. It's not an instrument to have a seamless possibility to shift money from A to B. So vote for that and we will have a vivid discussion with the Commission on that.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(The vote closed)

9. Roadmap for Women`s Rights (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next item is the debate on the Council and Commission statements on the Roadmap for Women's Rights (2025/2592(RSP)).

The first speaker, speaking on behalf of the Council, will be Mr Sz脜聜apka, Poland's Minister for European Union Affairs.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adam Sz脜聜apka, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, honourable Members, it is a great honour for me to address this assembly shortly after the celebration of the International Women's Day here in the hemicycle.

This year is an important year for gender equality. The world marks the 30th anniversary of the Beijing Platform for Action, and the Commission sets out its vision for the next five years and its roadmap. But before looking forward, we should also look back with satisfaction at the legislation that was recently put in place, most of which resulted from the agreement between Council and Parliament.

We have improved pay transparency, helping to reduce the pay gap between women and men. We have introduced rules for improving the gender balance in company boards, bringing equality and diversity into corporate boardrooms. We have created minimum standards for equality bodies, giving victims of discrimination an ally in claiming their rights. And we have provided women in Europe the protection of the first ever directive on combating violence against women and domestic violence.

Through these and other achievements, we can surely say that the College administrators have delivered for Europe's women. Therefore, let me take this opportunity to thank the Parliament for tirelessly campaigning for women's rights and for its constructive partnership with the Council in this endeavour.

But the work is never over. There is still a gender pay gap that stands at almost 13脗聽%. There is still an employment gap of more than 10脗聽%, with women and girls still performing a disproportionate share of informal care tasks. And we must, in particular, address the disproportionate impact that armed conflict and economic crisis have on women and girls.

At times when some voices may try to reverse the progress we have made in the EU to promote and protect women's rights, we should concentrate our efforts on stepping up action and working with all partners to promote a model in our Union which is more inclusive and provides a more just society.

We will continue to do so through policy and legislative measures, as well as by tackling the root causes of inequality.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Roxana M脙庐nzatu, Executive Vice-President of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, minister, honourable Members, every year on International Women's Day, we celebrate women's achievements and contributions across the world. But despite progress, full gender equality is decades 芒聙聯 if not centuries 芒聙聯 away.

At the current rate, it will take until 2158, another 134 years, to reach full gender parity at global level, according to data provided by the World Economic Forum. Within the European Union, it will take us more than 60 years. That is why the theme for this year's International Women's Day is 'accelerate action'. We need to take swift and decisive steps to achieve gender equality. We need to address the barriers and biases women face in every aspect of their lives.

Equality between women and men is one of the foundational values of our Union and a strategic interest in all our policies. In the European Union, we have made historic progress with rules on pay transparency, work-life balance, gender balance on company boards and combating violence against women. But we know there is still lots and lots more to do.

This is a special year. We are celebrating the 30th anniversary of the Beijing Platform for Action. Commissioner脗聽Lahbib is now in New York leading the EU's delegation to the 69th session of the Commission on the Status of Women. She will pass on a clear message: enabling women's full and equal participation in all parts of society is key to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals.

On this International Women's Day, we show that we are serious about accelerating action with a new roadmap for women's rights. The roadmap sets out a long-term political vision to advance women's rights.

Our priorities are strategic. We have to think that 1 in 3 women has suffered violence in their lifetime and that is why we aim to eradicate all forms of violence against women and girls, including femicide, domestic violence, including coercive control, cyber violence, sexual harassment or exploitation. Every girl, every woman in Europe has the right and should feel safe.

Women's health. It has been under researched and under prioritised. That is why we propose a dedicated focus on women's health in full respect of Member States' competences in this field. That includes promoting gender-sensitive medical research, clinical trials, diagnostics and treatments, and it includes ensuring women's access to sexual and reproductive health and rights.

Employment and pay gaps. There is still much work to do there as well. We need to eliminate gender pay and pension gaps to promote equal employment and financial opportunities for women. Women in Europe should be economically independent and enjoy the same opportunities men do.

Our education should be free from discrimination, from sexism, from stereotypes and violence to encourage both girls and boys to seek the future that they want, because women should be at every table where decisions are made.

This is another objective of the roadmap: equal political participation and representation at all levels of political power. Gender balance at all levels of management, so that women's voices can be heard across the public and private sectors and shape the policies of tomorrow.

And we aim at better safety of women in public life, as they are more likely to be victims of hate speech than their male counterparts.

We also need strong institutional mechanisms that deliver on women's rights. This, among others, includes an effective gender mainstreaming approach. The gender mainstreaming principle that was adopted 30 years ago in the Beijing Platform for Action means that all policy areas should apply a gender perspective.

Striving for gender equality is not only the right thing to do, but it is also the smart thing to do. Gender equality is essential for building strong competitive economies, but also for building sustainable, democratic and inclusive societies.

I invite the European Parliament, the Council and other institutions and stakeholders to unite around this roadmap to put forward actions under their remit and make it so that we turn these principles into an enduring reality for every girl and every woman across our European Union.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lina G脙隆lvez, on behalf of the S&D Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, Vice-President, we really welcome this roadmap on women's rights and gender equality. So thank you very much, Vice-President. Thank you also to Commissioner Lahbib to show a clear commitment to gender equality.

In a moment, we are facing a backlash on gender equality and women's rights. So especially also for including sexual and reproductive health rights. It is very important and we really appreciate that this is finally in.

However, this is not the end of this journey. True equality means more than what we have achieved so far. It is about creating a world where every woman in every corner has the freedom and the material preconditions to choose her own path and the power to shape her future, and to participate in an equal path in our common future.脗聽Because we have to design also this common future.

So let's do that. This roadmap will be really the origin of the new strategy and work together on this.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Margarita de la Pisa Carri脙鲁n, en nombre del Grupo PfE. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora comisaria, se脙卤or脙颅as, aunque la presidenta sea una mujer, parece que la Comisi脙鲁n no termina de entender nuestras aspiraciones. No odiamos a los hombres. Valoramos la familia, la maternidad, la vida y poder cuidar de los nuestros, as脙颅 como poder desarrollar una vocaci脙鲁n profesional. Queremos ser respetadas y amadas, no cosificadas. Nos gustar脙颅a estar tranquilas tanto en casa como en la calle, y ser libres. Sin embargo, se responde con m脙隆s normas de g脙漏nero que acaban con nuestra prosperidad y no nos protegen.

No se protege a la mujer gastando nuestro dinero en fomentar una ideolog脙颅a que nos denigra, confunde, victimiza y enfrenta, una ideolog脙颅a que nos est脙隆 destruyendo como sociedad. No se protege a la mujer callando ante los fundamentalismos que no respetan ni la vida ni la dignidad de las mujeres. No se protege a las mujeres cuando se trata de cambiar los valores fundacionales de la Uni脙鲁n Europea por la ideolog脙颅a woke o el islamismo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Laurence Trochu, au nom du groupe ECR. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Madame le Commissaire, mes chers coll脙篓gues, la feuille de route pour les droits des femmes pr脙漏sent脙漏e vendredi dernier oscille entre autosatisfaction et d脙漏claration de principe, plus utile dans les h脙漏micycles feutr脙漏s que contre les r脙漏elles difficult脙漏s auxquelles font face les femmes dans la vraie vie, au脗聽XXIe脗聽si脙篓cle.

Vous souhaitez prot脙漏ger r脙漏ellement les femmes? Alors, commencez par fermer les fronti脙篓res et cessez d芒聙聶importer sur notre sol des millions de personnes qui consid脙篓rent la femme comme une possession, qui la voilent et qui lui interdisent tout libre arbitre. Vous voulez prot脙漏ger r脙漏ellement les femmes? Eh bien, cessez d芒聙聶importer des pr脙漏dateurs sexuels qui sont surrepr脙漏sent脙漏s dans les agressions et 脙聽 cause desquels nous pleurons Lola et Philippine en France.

Vous souhaitez prot脙漏ger r脙漏ellement les femmes? Poursuivez en condamnant ceux qui marchandisent leur corps, en interdisant la GPA et en contr脙麓lant la pornographie. Vous souhaitez prot脙漏ger r脙漏ellement les femmes? Eh bien, donnez-leur la possibilit脙漏 d芒聙聶脙陋tre m脙篓res, en restaurant la figure familiale, dont la destruction engendre pour elles toujours plus de pr脙漏carit脙漏 et de responsabilit脙漏s lourdes 脙聽 porter. Alors, et alors seulement, l芒聙聶action de l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne sera plus qu芒聙聶un v脜聯u pieu.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Abir Al-Sahlani, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, on behalf of the Renew Group, we would like to welcome the Commission's Roadmap for Women's Rights. The backlash on gender equality is here, ongoing every day, and now it's cheered on by President Trump.

Every victory we have made for women's rights, it has been a hard fight to win it and it has brought prosperity and development to the entire humanity. This movement is so eager on attacking fundamental rights and human rights.脗聽The EU is no exception.脗聽Ladies and gentlemen, the anti-gender movement is growing in our continent and in this House.

The trend of mainstreaming misogyny must end now. We have to mainstream gender equality. This is a call for action to us all feminists to mobilise, unite and to rebuild a better world based on feminism free from patriarchy. We must increase funding for gender equality in the next MFF; enshrine the right to abortion in the EU Charter of Fundamental Rights; criminalise rape with a consent-based definition of rape; and finally, the EU needs a feminist foreign policy.

It will take us 130 years to eradicate extreme poverty for women and girls, and 60 years to reach gender equality in Europe. It is time to put in a higher gear.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽M脙漏lissa Camara, au nom du groupe Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Madame la Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, connaissez-vous ce visage? Il s芒聙聶agit de Justyna. Je l芒聙聶ai rencontr脙漏e il y a deux semaines en Pologne. Pourquoi montrer son visage aujourd芒聙聶hui? Parce que, comme de nombreuses femmes dans le monde, Justyna fait preuve d芒聙聶un courage extraordinaire pour prot脙漏ger les droits fondamentaux des femmes. C芒聙聶est une militante exceptionnelle du droit 脙聽 l芒聙聶avortement, condamn脙漏e en脗聽2023 pour avoir aid脙漏 une femme 脙聽 avorter dans son pays. En Pologne, il est quasiment impossible de b脙漏n脙漏ficier d芒聙聶un avortement l脙漏gal et s脙禄r. Partout, les droits sexuels et reproductifs sont en danger. Partout, nos vies, nos corps, nos familles, nos choix sont remis en cause. On ne le r脙漏p脙漏tera jamais assez, mais l脙聽 o脙鹿 elle passe, l芒聙聶extr脙陋me droite fait des d脙漏g脙垄ts, pour des ann脙漏es.

Le droit 脙聽 l芒聙聶avortement est un droit fondamental. Des femmes comme Justyna se mettent en danger tous les jours pour l芒聙聶acqu脙漏rir, le pr脙漏server et le r脙漏tablir. Pourtant, la Commission n芒聙聶a pas jug脙漏 bon d芒聙聶en faire mention dans sa feuille de route sur les droits des femmes. Nous ne le r脙漏p脙漏terons jamais assez: nos corps, nos choix. Je compte, Madame la Commissaire, sur vous pour d脙漏fendre avec courage ce droit durant ce mandat, avec autant de courage que Justyna. Pour Justyna et toutes celles qui se battent chaque jour, merci!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hanna Gedin, f脙露r The Left gruppen. 芒聙聯 Herr talman! Det 脙陇r 30 脙楼r sedan Pekingdeklarationen, och vi har inte kommit l脙陇ngre. Det h脙陇r 脙陇r ett alldeles f脙露r svagt och urvattnat 脙楼tagande fr脙楼n kommissionen. Det 脙陇r mycket fokus p脙楼 f脙露retagande, konkurrens och tillv脙陇xt. Vi har en r脙陇ttighetsstadga som v脙陇rnar m脙陇nniskors r脙陇tt att starta f脙露retag men inte kvinnors r脙陇tt till v脙楼ra kroppar. Var 脙陇r f脙露rslagen om samtyckeslagstiftning? 脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Irmhild Bo脙聼dorf, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Reem Alsalem darf heute nicht mitmachen. Die Sonderbotschafterin der UN f脙录r Gewalt gegen Frauen und M脙陇dchen wurde von unserem FEMM-Ausschuss f脙录r die gro脙聼e heute stattfindende Konferenz in New York ausgeladen. Was ist ihr Vergehen? Sie war schon vor Jahren so frech zu fragen, wie es denn aussieht mit einer geschlechtergerechten Auslegung der Scharia. Doch es kam noch schlimmer. Sie markierte den potenziellen Missbrauch und die Aush脙露hlung des rechtlich m脙露glichen Geschlechtswechsels in Deutschland als frauenfeindlich脗聽芒聙聯 die Quittung folgte nun.

Die ach so toleranten Mitstreiter im FEMM-Ausschuss beantragten ihre Ausladung, die dann auch prompt erfolgte. So ernst meinen es also unsere selbsternannten weiblichen Vork脙陇mpfer mit der politischen Teilnahme und der gleichen Repr脙陇sentation von Frauen. Nicht einmal in den eigenen weiblichen Reihen werden andere Meinungen toleriert. Reem Alsalem脗聽芒聙聯 merkt euch den Namen der echten K脙陇mpferin f脙录r Frauenrechte!

脗聽脗聽El脜录bieta Katarzyna 脜聛ukacijewska (PPE). 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Pani Komisarz! Prze脜聜amujmy stereotypy, promuj脛聟c r脙鲁wno脜聸脛聡 p脜聜ci w ka脜录dej spo脜聜eczno脜聸ci. Niech nasza mapa drogowa dla kobiet b脛聶dzie 脜聸cie脜录k脛聟, kt脙鲁r脛聟 pod脛聟脜录aj脛聟 wszystkie kobiety, niezale脜录nie od miejsca, gdzie mieszkaj脛聟, czy w sercu miasta, czy w ma脜聜ej wiosce. Bo tylko wtedy Europa naprawd脛聶 b脛聶dzie zjednoczona w r脙鲁wno脜聸ci i sprawiedliwo脜聸ci. I my脜聸l脛聶, 脜录e o tym marz脛聟 wszystkie kobiety, zw脜聜aszcza te ze wsi i z ma脜聜ych miasteczek.

脗聽脗聽Mathilde Androu脙芦t (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, alors que nous c脙漏l脙漏brons les droits des femmes en Europe et partout dans le monde, ce chiffre est tomb脙漏 hier en France: le nombre de victimes de violences sexuelles dans les transports a augment脙漏 de 86脗聽% en dix ans en 脙聨le-de-France. Pour ne parler que des populations qui int脙漏ressent la gauche, 90脗聽% des femmes migrantes traversant la M脙漏diterran脙漏e sont victimes de viol, selon un rapport de l芒聙聶ONU de novembre dernier.

Dans un monde o脙鹿 1脗聽femme sur 8 dans le monde a d脙漏j脙聽 subi un viol ou une agression sexuelle enfant, le fait que les hommes ne suivent pas de stage en d脙漏construction, financ脙漏 par l芒聙聶argent public, ce m脙陋me argent qui manque pour installer des maternit脙漏s ou des services gyn脙漏cologiques sur tous nos territoires, rel脙篓ve plus de l芒聙聶脙漏lucubration de gauche que du f脙漏minisme sens脙漏. D脙漏fendre les droits des femmes, ce n芒聙聶est pas imposer des dogmes; c芒聙聶est assurer leur s脙漏curit脙漏 et leur dignit脙漏 face aux vraies menaces, et avoir 脙漏galement la lucidit脙漏 de reconna脙庐tre que certaines cultures sont ouvertement misogynes. Quand je dis cela, je ne parle pas 脙漏videmment d芒聙聶un patriarcat europ脙漏en fantasm脙漏.

Alors, ma question, largement partag脙漏e par des millions de femmes qui attendent qu芒聙聶on change leur quotidien, est la suivante: qu芒聙聶est-ce que nous en avons 脙聽 faire de vos propositions fumeuses sur la th脙漏orie du genre, l芒聙聶aide 脙聽 la transition de genre ou l芒聙聶脙漏criture inclusive, quand des millions de femmes craignent de sortir ne serait-ce que dans leur propre rue?

(L'oratrice accepte une question carton bleu)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Abir Al-Sahlani (Renew), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Dear colleague, you said that the patriarchy that we are talking about that has been oppressing women in Europe, and even in this House to some extent, at points when you see the number of chairs that are women or the positions that are, you know, given to women in politics, but also in finance and all of that and boards.

Is that a made up concept or is it a reality that we actually are living day to day?

And then you also say that there is an ideology that is called gender equality. So you do not adhere to the fact that women are also humans and they have also human rights?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mathilde Androu脙芦t (PfE), r脙漏ponse carton bleu. 芒聙聯 Votre question est tellement outranci脙篓re qu芒聙聶elle pr脙陋te plus 脙聽 rire qu芒聙聶脙聽 autre chose. En l芒聙聶occurrence, je n芒聙聶ai pas de souci; je pense qu芒聙聶en effet, nous sommes tr脙篓s privil脙漏gi脙漏es dans ce Parlement europ脙漏en.

Ce proc脙篓s est tr脙篓s malvenu, compte tenu du fait que cela fait des ann脙漏es que je suis une femme qui s芒聙聶appelle Marine脗聽Le脗聽Pen, qui va peut-脙陋tre devenir la premi脙篓re femme pr脙漏sidente de la R脙漏publique en France. C芒聙聶est donc assez malvenu de votre part.

Je pense que nous sommes des femmes 脙聽 l芒聙聶脙漏gal des hommes, donc je suis contre l芒聙聶id脙漏ologie du genre, qui consiste 脙聽 faire de nous des sortes de sous-hommes ou des hommes d脙漏riv脙漏s. Les femmes sont 脙聽 part et singuli脙篓res, et je milite pour leur singularit脙漏.

脗聽脗聽Chiara Gemma (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, signor Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, questa tabella include davvero tutte le donne? O forse, troppo spesso, c'脙篓 una categoria che continua ad essere dimenticata? Credo di s脙卢.

Eppure, pi脙鹿 di una donna su quattro nell'Unione europea vive con una disabilit脙聽. Queste donne devono affrontare doppie e triple discriminazioni: come donne, come persone con disabilit脙聽 e, in molti casi, anche in base alla loro et脙聽 o condizioni socio economiche. Diciamo basta a queste donne invisibili. Diamo loro voce.

Ci aspettiamo, allora, che questa tabella di marcia riconosca esplicitamente anche i diritti delle persone delle donne con disabilit脙聽, promuovendo misure concrete per garantire loro accesso al lavoro, all'indipendenza economica, protezione dalla violenza e dagli abusi, diritto alla salute e alla maternit脙聽, parit脙聽 nell'educazione, nella formazione, nella partecipazione politica.

La credibilit脙聽 di questa tabella si giocher脙聽 sull'inclusione di queste donne: diamo aiuto a queste donne, e ci aiuti, Commissaria.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Raquel Garc脙颅a Hermida-Van Der Walle (Renew). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, condooms, de pil, spiraaltjes en de morning-afterpil: ik heb ze allemaal gebruikt. Ik ben altijd vrij geweest om te bepalen wanneer en met wie ik kinderen zou krijgen. In Europa zou dit de norm moeten zijn. Helaas staat deze vrijheid stevig onder druk. Ultraconservatieve bewegingen investeren honderden miljoenen euro's om deze rechten in heel Europa in te perken. Ze maken voorbehoedsmiddelen moeilijk verkrijgbaar en onbetaalbaar. Ze promoten kansloze, natuurlijke contraceptie en criminaliseren abortus. Vrouwen met een gewenste zwangerschap die een late miskraam krijgen, worden naar huis gestuurd om te wachten totdat hun foetus vanzelf sterft, omdat artsen anders een gevangenisstraf riskeren. De Europese Unie heeft de plicht om vrouwen te steunen wanneer hun overheden ze in de steek laten, zodat zij, net als ik en mijn drie dochters, baas in eigen buik kunnen blijven.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alice Kuhnke (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Herr talman! Vi lever i en tid av maktfullkomliga m脙陇n. M脙陇n som er脙露vrar, m脙陇n som anv脙陇nder v脙楼ld f脙露r att f脙楼 sin vilja igenom, m脙陇n som vill kontrollera s脙楼v脙陇l landomr脙楼den som v脙楼ra livm脙露drar. Jag t脙陇nker p脙楼 flickorna och kvinnorna i Demokratiska republiken Kongo, som betalar med sina kroppar och sina liv i skuggan av m脙陇nnens krig om naturresurser. Jag t脙陇nker p脙楼 kvinnorna i Iran som forts脙陇tter kampen mot ayatollornas f脙露rtryck. Jag t脙陇nker p脙楼 kvinnorna i Ukraina som mitt under brinnande krig organiserar sig i kampen mot m脙陇ns v脙楼ld mot kvinnor. Jag t脙陇nker p脙楼 alla de miljontals kvinnor genom historien som g脙楼ng p脙楼 g脙楼ng rest sig, organiserat sig och k脙陇mpat och drivit igenom f脙露r脙陇ndring. Kunde de s脙楼 kan vi.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Carolina Morace (The Left). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghe e colleghi, la road map per i diritti delle donne ignora i diritti sessuali e riproduttivi, incluso l'aborto, e questo 脙篓 inaccettabile. L'estrema destra sta imponendo la sua agenda nelle nostre istituzioni, mettendo a rischio conquiste fondamentali.

L'aborto 脙篓 una scelta difficile, e spesso dolorosa: ma negarlo significa condannare le donne a sofferenze e pericoli.

La road map affronta temi cruciali, come la violenza di genere, le disuguaglianze economiche. Ma senza impegni concreti resta solo carta e la carta da sola non protegge nessuna donna, non garantisce i diritti e non cambia la realt脙聽.

L'Europa non pu脙虏 permettersi passi indietro. Mentre negli Stati Uniti i diritti delle donne sono erosi, noi dobbiamo essere un baluardo. Servono azioni e non simboli, perch脙漏 la libert脙聽, la dignit脙聽 e i diritti non si negoziano mai.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Christine Anderson (ESN). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Die linke Von der Leyen芒聙聭Kommission will uns die L脙录ge auftischen, ein Gr脙录ndungswert der EU sei es, die Gleichheit zwischen Frauen und M脙陇nnern herzustellen. Tats脙陇chlich steht in Artikel脗聽119 des Vertrags nichts davon; dort ist nur der Grundsatz der Chancengleichheit und der Gleichberechtigung zwischen Mann und Frau genannt. Chancengleichheit und Gleichberechtigung sind aber etwas v脙露llig anderes als Gleichheit.

Wenn die EU芒聙聭Kommissare von Gleichheit zwischen M脙陇nnern und Frauen sprechen, dann verdrehen sie die EU芒聙聭Vertr脙陇ge in ihr Gegenteil. Zu dieser Rechtsverdrehung gesellt sich dann auch noch eine Tatsachenverdrehung, denn M脙陇nner und Frauen sind nun mal nicht gleich. Frauen k脙露nnen Kinder geb脙陇ren und M脙陇nner eben nicht. Jedes Kindergartenkind kann den Unterschied zwischen einem Mann und einer Frau mit blo脙聼em Auge erkennen. Das ist eine Anerkennung, die Sie hier aber beharrlich verweigern.

Wenn man von falschen Ideen und falschen Tatsachen ausgeht, dann kann nat脙录rlich auch eine Roadmap f脙录r Frauenrechte nur falsch sein. Diese geh脙露rt als Desinformation in die Tonne gekloppt, samt der uns脙陇glichen Gender芒聙聭Ideologie. Halten Sie sich doch einfach aus dem Leben von uns Frauen heraus! Damit w脙陇re uns am meisten geholfen.

脗聽脗聽Judita La脜隆脜隆脙隆kov脙隆 (NI). 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙陆 p脙隆n predsedaj脙潞ci, v脙隆脜戮en脙颅 kolegovia, v脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pani komis脙隆rka, bl脙颅脜戮i sa 75. v脙陆ro脛聧ie popravy doktorky Milady Hor脙隆kovej. To bolo v roku 1918. Po脛聧as druhej svetovej vojny bola v脙陇znen脙隆 nemeck脙陆mi nacistami. Nemeck脙陆 nacistick脙陆 prokur脙隆tor v roku 1944 脜戮iadal pre 脜聢u trest smrti. Vykon脜隆truovan脙漏 s脙潞dne procesy proti nieko脛戮k脙陆m stovk脙隆m odporcov komunistick脙漏ho re脜戮imu naprie脛聧 Eur脙鲁pou sprev脙隆dzala mas脙颅vna medi脙隆lna kampa脜聢. Pou脛聧en脙隆 z na脜隆ej vlastnej hist脙鲁rie mi dovo脛戮te by脜楼 tro脜隆i脛聧ku kritickej脜隆ou k tomu, 脛聧o novin脙隆ri dok脙隆脜戮u vyprodukova脜楼. 脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Katarina Barley (S&D). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Schauen wir uns um in der Welt: Wer steht in der ersten Reihe, wenn es darum geht, die demokratischen Werte zu verteidigen? Sehr oft sind es die Frauen. Ob in Polen, in der Ukraine, in Belarus, im Iran, in Afghanistan, auch in den USA: Es sind oft Frauen mit ihrem Mut und ihrer Zivilcourage, die den Autokraten und Despoten dieser Welt furchtlos die Stirn bieten.

Sind Frauen die besseren Menschen? Nein, nicht unbedingt脗聽芒聙聯 das sehen und h脙露ren wir ja auch manchmal hier in diesem Plenum 芒聙聯, aber die Chance, dass sie sich nicht nur um ihre eigenen Interessen k脙录mmern, sondern um die der Gesellschaft, ist etwas h脙露her, und vor allen Dingen, sie wissen auch, wenn es der Demokratie an den Kragen geht, dann sind davon vor allen Dingen auch ihre eigenen Rechte immer betroffen.

Frauen haben so viel erreicht f脙录r die Gleichstellung der Geschlechter, und darauf sind wir alle stolz. Gleichzeitig sind wir noch lange nicht am Ziel, und schlimmer noch, wir wissen, dass auch das Erreichte nicht sicher ist. Hier in Europa und weltweit脗聽芒聙聯 Frauenrechte sind in Gefahr: gleicher Lohn, gleiche Chancen, Schutz vor Gewalt und vor allem auch das Recht, 脙录ber ihren eigenen K脙露rper zu bestimmen.

Doch wir sind stark, und wir k脙陇mpfen weiter; das ist auch unsere Aufgabe in der EU. Die Roadmap ist ein guter Beginn, aber am Ende brauchen wir gute Resultate.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Kruis (PfE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, we hebben het vandaag over de "Roadmap for Women's Rights", maar als ik de linkse vrouwen in deze zaal beluister, voelt het meer als een "highway to hell": genderwaanzin, abortus, klimaatverandering en subsidies is wat de klok slaat. Dit zijn allemaal "first world problems", terwijl de grootste bedreiging voor vrouwen in Europa de import van een derdewereldwoestijnideologie is. Vrouwenbesnijdenis, geweld, sluiers, eerwraak en gedwongen huwelijken zijn inmiddels een bittere realiteit geworden voor veel vrouwen in Europese steden.

Met de komst van de islam hebben we massaal vrouwenhaat ge脙炉mporteerd. Onze vrouwen worden bespuugd, uitgescholden en verkracht. Ze zijn soms bang om 脙录berhaupt nog over straat te gaan. E脙漏n op de drie vrouwen in Europa heeft inmiddels te maken gehad met seksueel of fysiek geweld. Waar minaretten rijzen, verdwijnen vrouwenrechten.

Deze roadmap zou moeten gaan over het verzet daartegen, omdat dit de enige manier is om recht te doen aan tientallen jaren vrouwenemancipatie in Europa. Wie voor vrouwen is, is tegen de islam.

(De spreker stemt ermee in om te antwoorden op een 芒聙聹blauwe kaart芒聙聺-vraag)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Abir Al-Sahlani (Renew), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 A lot of what you said was utter nonsense, among others, about being a Muslim, that if you are against Islam, then you are for women's rights. Of course, that is just utter nonsense, because in the Islamic world there are women that are so much stronger than you are.

Nevertheless, to my question: you don't see yourself as a threat to women's rights? Because I am so much more afraid of you and what you can do with your power here 芒聙聯 to my rights, to my girls' rights 芒聙聯 with your anti-gender approach and with your racist approach. Don't you consider yourself as a threat to us?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Kruis (PfE), 芒聙聹blauwe kaart芒聙聺-antwoord. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, deze vraag toont aan dat vrouwen en meisjes overal in Europa "vol in de wind staan" en zich niet meer beschermd voelen door politici die massaal wegkijken voor de import van vrouwenhaat. Als deze mevrouw mij onderbreekt en zegt dat de islam een soort baken van vrouwelijke emancipatie is, dan antwoord ik natuurlijk dat dit absoluut niet waar is. Noem mij 脙漏脙漏n land in de islamitische wereld waar vrouwen dezelfde rechten hebben als mannen. Die zijn er simpelweg niet. Kijk hoe vrouwen door het leven moeten gaan in de straten van Kabul, om maar een voorbeeld te noemen.

Als ik een dochter zou hebben, dan zou ik alvast een helmpje gaan kopen om haar te beschermen, want op deze manier worden vrouwen volledig in de steek gelaten door politici zoals u. Helaas.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Beatrice Timgren (ECR). 芒聙聯 Herr talman! Islamisering, hedersf脙露rtryck, g脙陇ngens terror och den destruktiva kvinnokultur som f脙露ljer i massinvandringens sp脙楼r. Det 脙陇r det verkliga hotet mot kvinnors r脙陇ttigheter. V脙陇nstern vill inte prata om det h脙陇r, utan de diskuterar hellre genusperspektiv och pronomen. Dagens s脙楼 kallade feminister lever i en l脙楼tsasv脙陇rld d脙陇r man p脙楼st脙楼r att m脙陇n kan bli gravida. Kvinnokampen i脗聽dag; det handlar om trygghet, lag och ordning. Om att stoppa den migrationspolitik som f脙露rst脙露rt v脙楼rt land. Min mamma v脙陇xte upp i ett s脙陇krare land 脙陇n vad jag gjorde. Men jag v脙陇xte upp i ett s脙陇krare land 脙陇n vad min ton脙楼rsdotter v脙陇xer upp i i脗聽dag. F脙露r att bryta trenden m脙楼ste vi fatta de beslut som etablissemanget inte v脙楼gat. Sverige ska vara ett land d脙陇r kvinnor kan k脙陇nna sig s脙陇kra 芒聙聯 inte ett land som b脙露jer sig f脙露r hederskultur, islamism och g脙陇ngv脙楼ld. D脙陇rf脙露r ska vi skicka hem kriminella illegala migranter.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dainius 脜陆alimas (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, the fight for women's rights is not just a political choice. It is also a choice between civilisations or between inclusive, participatory democracy and authoritarianism, usually accompanied with violence and sexism.

However, progress on gender equality in the EU still seems to be uneven and fragile. A new EU-wide gender-based violence survey exposes that violence against women remains a daily reality. The EU directive on violence against women fails to include a consent-based definition of rape. Five EU Member States: Bulgaria, Czechia, Lithuania, Hungary and Slovakia, refused to ratify the Istanbul Convention, sounds like a betrayal of our core values.

The Women's Rights Roadmap is a step forward to the single European human rights space, however not sufficiently concrete. It should be implemented by ambitious legislative proposals, appropriate education and conditionality instruments for those who do not adhere to European standards of gender equality.

If we need a reminder of what is at stake, look at Ukraine. Even at war, Ukraine has launched its first gender equality index because of understanding that there is no civilised world without women's rights and gender equality.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Petras Gra脜戮ulis (ESN). 芒聙聯 Gerbiamas pirmininke, pirmiausiai tai nor脛聴脛聧iau pasakyti replik脛聟 pirmininkaujantiems. Vienai moteriai 脛聧ia buvo leista u脜戮duoti [klausim脛聟] ta pa脛聧ia diskusija du kartus. O kada a脜隆 nor脛聴jau paklausti ir 脛炉 klausim脛聟 mano buvo neatsakyta, kod脛聴l Europos S脛聟junga po 脜隆iai dienai Ukrainos nepri脛聴m脛聴 脛炉 Europos S脛聟jung脛聟, man 脛炉 klausim脛聟 neatsak脛聴 ir j脜芦s neleidote pakartoti klausimo.

Gal j脜芦s, pirmininkaujantis, pasakysite, gal 脛聧ia Trumpas kaltas, kad Ukraina 脜隆iandien n脛聴ra Europos S脛聟jungos nar脛聴?

O dabar a脜隆 i脜隆 esm脛聴s. Gerbiamieji, 脜聽ventas Ra脜隆tas kalba, kad yra dvi lytys. T脛聟 pasak脛聴 ir Trumpas. J脜芦s gi pabr脛聴脜戮脛聴t moters lyt脛炉, o sakot, kad yra septyniasde脜隆imt dvi ar jau 脜隆imtas keturios lytys. Tai j脜芦s diskriminuojat savo pat脛炉 absurd脛聟, pabr脛聴脜戮dami moter脛炉 diskriminuojat kitas lytis. Ar j脜芦s 脜戮inote patys, 脛聧ia susirink脛聶 europie脛聧iai, kokios j脜芦s esat lyties? Kas 脜戮inot? Pakelkit rankas. Pasirodo, 脛聧ia s脛聴dintys sal脛聴je net ne脜戮inote, kokios lyties esate. A脜隆 nor脛聴jau pirmininkaujan脛聧io paklausti, jeigu nedraud脜戮iama skaityti 脜聽vento Ra脜隆to, nes mane net teis脛聴 Lietuvoje, kad skaitau 脜聽vent脛聟 Ra脜隆t脛聟, tai a脜隆 jums noriu paskaityti, k脛聟 Dievas kalba ir k脛聟 脜聽ventas Ra脜隆tas kalba apie santykius 脜隆eimos. Pirmas, j脜芦s, vyrai, myl脛聴kite savo 脜戮monas, kaip Kristus pamilo Ba脜戮ny脛聧i脛聟 ir atidav脛聴 u脜戮 j脛聟 savo gyvyb脛聶. J脜芦s, moterys, b脜芦kite klusnios savo vyrams lyg Vie脜隆pa脛聧iui, ir jeigu bus, vyraus meil脛聴, tada bus santaika ir pagarba vienas kitam.

(Kalb脛聴tojas sutiko atsakyti 脛炉 m脛聴lynosios kortel脛聴s klausim脛聟)

脗聽脗聽Petras Gra脜戮ulis (ESN), atsakymas 脛炉 pak脛聴lus m脛聴lyn脛聟j脛聟 kortel脛聶 pateikt脛聟 klausim脛聟. 芒聙聯 A脜隆 noriu pasakyti, kad j脜芦s sakote, kad n脛聴ra nei vyro, nei moters 芒聙聯 ly脛聧i脜鲁 n脛聴ra. 脗聽脗聽Sun脛聧ana Glavak (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i, povjerenice, kolegice i kolege, 脜戮ene i dalje 脛聧ine manjinu u vrhu politi脛聧kih, ekonomskih i dru脜隆tvenih struktura. To smo danas zaklju脛聧ili. Mislim da nismo otkrili ni脜隆ta novo.

Me脛聭utim, 脜戮elim vas podsjetiti da za svaki euro koji zaradi mu脜隆karac, 脜戮ena zaradi 0,87 eura. Trenutno stanje u Europskom parlamentu, 脜戮ena ima oko 40 posto, ali me脛聭u liderima politi脛聧kih grupacija i pojedinaca, predsjednicima vlada ta brojka puno je manja.

Naravno da prepreke poput rodnog jaza u pla脛聡ama, staklenog stropa i napla脛聡enog ku脛聡anskog rada i dalje ograni脛聧avaju 脜戮ene. Klju脛聧ne su nam politike koje podr脜戮avaju pristup kvalitetnom obrazovanju, poduzetni脜隆tvu i financijskim resursima i naravno bolja ravnote脜戮a izme脛聭u privatnog i poslovnog 脜戮ivota, pa i u ruralnim podru脛聧jima, nemojmo na to zaboraviti.

Me脛聭utim, vi脜隆e od polovice svjetske populacije 脛聧inimo mi, 脜戮ene. U Europskoj uniji ima nas 52 posto. Imamo potencijala preoblikovati sustave mo脛聡i, ekonomiju i dru脜隆tvene vrijednosti. Ali ne kao ra脜隆timani orkestar, ne kao ideologija, ve脛聡 kao one koje doista 脜戮ele politiku iskoristiti u svrhu pobolj脜隆anja uvjeta 脜戮ena i da svim 脜戮enama u Europskoj uniji bude bolje.

Ne脛聡emo to posti脛聡i ako 脛聡emo se samo nadmetati u tome tko je u pravu, a tko nije.

Zaklju脛聧it 脛聡u, ekonomska neovisnost omogu脛聡uje jedino slobodu izbora i na tome trebamo raditi.

脗聽脗聽Catherine Griset (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, dans cet h脙漏micycle, l芒聙聶autosatisfaction r脙篓gne en ma脙庐tresse. Au sein de la commission脗聽FEMM, elle s芒聙聶accompagne d芒聙聶un f脙漏minisme misandre et d芒聙聶une haine des hommes. Ces institutions, sous couvert d芒聙聶脙漏galit脙漏, sombrent dans l芒聙聶hypocrisie. Elles attaquent les hommes tout en soutenant un islam radical qui inf脙漏riorise les femmes. Ce f脙漏minisme 脙聽 g脙漏om脙漏trie variable fait des technocrates europ脙漏ens les complices de l芒聙聶islamo-gauchisme, qui d芒聙聶une main soumet les femmes et de l芒聙聶autre d脙漏truit les relations hommes-femmes, sous pr脙漏texte de lutte contre un pr脙漏tendu patriarcat.

Alors, oui 脙聽 un f脙漏minisme qui d脙漏fend vraiment les femmes, comme le collectif N脙漏m脙漏sis, qui d脙漏nonce ces hypocrisies. Oui 脙聽 un soutien r脙漏el 脙聽 leur carri脙篓re et 脙聽 leur vie familiale, sans id脙漏ologie ni culpabilisation. L芒聙聶脙漏galit脙漏 ne se construira pas dans la compromission avec ceux qui pr脙麓nent la soumission des femmes.

脗聽脗聽Maria Guzenina (S&D), sinisen kortin kysymys. 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, mielt脙陇ni h脙陇kellytt脙陇脙陇 kovasti, kun t脙陇脙陇ll脙陇 v脙陇itet脙陇脙陇n, ett脙陇 naisten oikeudet ja niiden puolesta taisteleminen olisi pois miehilt脙陇. En ymm脙陇rr脙陇 lainkaan t脙陇llaista retoriikkaa. Yhteiskunnat, joissa naiset ovat tasa-arvoisia, ne yhteiskunnat kukoistavat. Niiss脙陇 naisilla, miehill脙陇 ja lapsilla on parempi olla.

Te v脙陇ititte juuri 脙陇sken, ett脙陇 t脙陇脙陇ll脙陇 puhutaan puppua ja alistetaan miehi脙陇. Mill脙陇 tavalla naisten oikeuksien esiin tuominen alistaa miehi脙陇? Ei mill脙陇脙陇n tavalla. Vastatkaa, mihin perustatte v脙陇itteenne?

脗聽脗聽Catherine Griset (PfE), r脙漏ponse carton bleu. 芒聙聯 Je ne vois pas en quoi vous avez compris que je disais que les femmes et les hommes n芒聙聶脙漏taient pas 脙漏gaux. D芒聙聶ailleurs, je suis contente de voir que le cordon sanitaire est rompu, puisque vous nous adressez la parole. Bravo! C芒聙聶est bien. Je dis simplement que vous m脙漏langez tout. Oui, l芒聙聶islam radical est un danger pour pour les femmes. J芒聙聶ai deux filles; je ne veux pas qu芒聙聶elles soient voil脙漏es dans dix ans. En fait, vous m脙漏langez tout. Nous sommes les seuls 脙聽 d脙漏fendre les femmes, ici, avec le Rassemblement national, et je profite de ce micro pour dire que la condescendance que vous avez vis-脙聽-vis de nous vous fait oublier que nous avons gagn脙漏 les 脙漏lections europ脙漏ennes. 脗聽脗聽Lara Magoni (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, oggi discutiamo i diritti delle donne e vorrei soffermarmi sugli episodi di gravit脙聽 inaudita accaduti lo scorso weekend in Italia, dove, compresa nella mia Bergamo, sono stati dati alle fiamme manichini del presidente del Consiglio, Giorgia Meloni, prima donna premier nella storia della Repubblica italiana.

Questo atto non pu脙虏 essere giustificato: 脙篓 un episodio di violenza gravissimo, che trascende la satira e diventa un pericolosissimo strumento di incitamento all'odio verso le donne.

Si cerca di attaccare una donna che, invece di essere un bersaglio di violenza, dovrebbe essere vista come un esempio di come la forza, il merito e l'ambizione possano permettere a chiunque di raggiungere i propri sogni.

Giorgia Meloni rappresenta milioni di donne italiane che credono nel proprio potenziale, nella propria indipendenza, nel diritto di aspirare a grandi traguardi senza essere fermate dalla discriminazione e dai pregiudizi.

E tutto questo avviene nel silenzio pi脙鹿 assordante della sinistra e delle istituzioni. Sarebbe con questa violenza e intolleranza verso Giorgia Meloni che la sinistra intende difendere i diritti delle donne?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lucia Yar (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, during this discussion, we need to acknowledge that gender equality is not just a matter of justice, but also of security. I think we hear it over here. Women need to be secure from physical and sexual violence, and they also need to be present when looking for safe solutions.

Thanks to this roadmap, we now have a path forward, and I am honoured to shadow its implementing strategy, but what should we do? First, we need to combat violence, including integrating consent-based rape laws. Second, we must ensure equality in all areas of life and work, and this means gender mainstreaming and gender budgeting. It is not an ideology.

Commissioner Lahbib is not here with us, but I want Commissioner M脙庐nzatu to tell her that we really rely on her, because promoting equality is the key to a future where women 芒聙聯 but eventually all of us 芒聙聯 can live free from fear and discrimination. And the EU needs to lead this gender equality fight that we have worldwide, especially in those insecure times when geo-policy is changing.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rosa Estar脙聽s Ferragut (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidente, la hoja de ruta por los derechos de las mujeres marca nuestro firme compromiso de construir una Uni脙鲁n Europea igualitaria, donde los derechos de las mujeres est脙漏n en el centro de la pol脙颅tica y las acciones de la Uni脙鲁n. Tenemos derecho a no ser v脙颅ctimas de violencia por el hecho de ser mujeres. Romper el silencio es fundamental para erradicar esta violencia.

En mi pa脙颅s, Espa脙卤a, se han tapado episodios de acoso sexual por parte de dirigentes de la izquierda al mismo tiempo que se ped脙颅a a la poblaci脙鲁n que no guardara silencio. 脗驴C脙鲁mo se puede guardar silencio ante los m脙潞ltiples esc脙隆ndalos relacionados con prostituci脙鲁n por parte de dirigentes socialistas del Gobierno de Espa脙卤a 芒聙聰al parecer, pagados con dinero p脙潞blico芒聙聰 y seguir defendiendo, al mismo tiempo, que su objetivo es acabar con la prostituci脙鲁n? Flaco servicio a la igualdad y los derechos de las mujeres. No deber脙颅amos proclamar en p脙潞blico lo que luego no hacemos en privado.

Tenemos derecho a una educaci脙鲁n de calidad y romper con los estereotipos: contar con las mujeres con discapacidad en todos los sectores de nuestras vidas; derecho a la igualdad salarial y a tener oportunidades en el mercado de trabajo con condiciones dignas, derecho a una salud de calidad y derecho tambi脙漏n a la conciliaci脙鲁n. Sin conciliaci脙鲁n ni corresponsabilidad las mujeres siempre hacen las renuncias.

Hago un llamamiento desde aqu脙颅 al Gobierno de Espa脙卤a para que traspase ya el permiso parental remunerado; si no, hay muchas mujeres que en estos momentos no se lo cogen por esa falta de transposici脙鲁n que ha llevado a la Comisi脙鲁n a abrir un proceso de infracci脙鲁n. Est脙隆 el tema en el Tribunal de Justicia de la Uni脙鲁n Europea con una sanci脙鲁n que puede llegar a los siete millones.

Nos desmarcamos del negacionismo de unos y de la hipocres脙颅a demag脙鲁gica de otros.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marko Ve脜隆ligaj (S&D). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, evo, na po脛聧etku hvala povjerenici Lahbib koja je bila zadu脜戮ena za procese oko teksta ovoga putokaza za 脜戮enska prava, ali posebno hvala na脜隆oj kolegici, povjerenici Roxani M脙庐nzatu, koja je danas ovdje s nama i koja se 脜戮ustro borila da svi prioriteti va脜戮ni za djevoj脛聧ice i 脜戮ene diljem Europe budu dio ove vizije.

Tekst koji je zapravo predstavljen je dobar i pokazuje da unato脛聧 otporima koje imamo na najvi脜隆im razinama u Komisiji postoji dovoljno snage da se 脜戮enska prava i za脜隆tite, ali i unaprijede. No, moramo biti svjesni da je ovaj tekst samo iscrtavanje svojevrsne vizije i da nam tek pravi posao predstoji u novoj strategiji rodne ravnopravnosti.

Ta strategija mora uklju脛聧ivati konkretne obaveze i prijedloge te opipljive zakonodavne i nezakonodavne mjere. Osim toga, ono 脜隆to je va脜戮no za re脛聡i da strategiju trebaju pratiti vrlo konkretna financijska sredstva i tra脜戮it 脛聡emo zasebnu i jasnu prora脛聧unsku liniju za ostvarivanje svih prioriteta u podru脛聧ju rodne ravnopravnosti jer u vremenima kakva su danas, prepuna nestabilnosti i usmjeravanja sredstava prema obrani i sigurnosti, va脜戮no je naglasiti da je rodna ravnopravnost jedan od temeljnih uvjeta za dru脜隆tvenu sigurnosti i napredak.

Ne脛聡emo dozvoliti da se ova tema marginalizira i da se napori u ovom dijelu umanje ili zanemare. U to budite sigurni.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maria Noichl (S&D). 芒聙聯 Sehr geehrte Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kommissarin! Als allererstes m脙露chte ich mich bei den Zuschauerinnen und Zuschauern, die vielleicht am Fernsehger脙陇t sitzen, am PC sitzen, entschuldigen脗聽芒聙聯 entschuldigen f脙录r manche Reden, die hier im Haus gehalten wurden. Dieser Teil des Parlaments ist konstruktiv. Dieser Teil des Parlaments arbeitet freundschaftlich miteinander, auch wenn wir nicht immer einer Meinung sind. Aber es gibt auch einen Teil hier im Haus脗聽芒聙聯 dieser Teil ist destruktiv von Anfang an. Dieser Teil ist r脙录ckw脙陇rtsgewandt und teilweise einfach nur dumm脗聽芒聙聯 ich m脙露chte mich daf脙录r entschuldigen.

Aber jetzt zum Thema: Wir haben einen Fahrplan bekommen. Einen Fahrplan: Schon allein das Wort bedeutet, wir sind immer noch nicht am Ziel脗聽芒聙聯 ein Fahrplan. Und ich muss ganz klar sagen: Ja, er ist nicht schlecht, der Fahrplan. Wir haben keine Erkenntnisdefizite, 脙录berhaupt keine Erkenntnisdefizite脗聽芒聙聯 wir haben Umsetzungsdefizite, Handlungsdefizite. Deswegen w脙录nsche ich mir so sehr, dass dieser Fahrplan jetzt wirklich in diesem Jahr noch ganz konkrete Initiativen, ganz konkrete legislative Papiere, ganz konkrete Papiere, die uns voranbringen, bekommt. Denn Fahrpl脙陇ne haben wir schon so lange脗聽芒聙聯 Umsetzung brauchen wir in den verschiedenen Bereichen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Emma Rafowicz (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, partout l芒聙聶ombre r脙漏actionnaire s芒聙聶脙漏tend, et avec elle le recul de nombreux droits pour les femmes. L芒聙聶Europe doit se r脙漏veiller et notre r脙漏veil doit 脙陋tre f脙漏ministe. La Commission avait une occasion majeure de tenir t脙陋te aux r脙漏actionnaires et de r脙漏affirmer son engagement pour l芒聙聶脙漏galit脙漏. Cette feuille de route comporte des avanc脙漏es importantes que les socialistes, avec Mme脗聽M脙庐nzatu, ont arrach脙漏es 脙聽 la Commission: lutte contre les violences sexistes et sexuelles, 脙漏galit脙漏 salariale, participation politique, combat contre le sexisme dans les m脙漏dias芒聙娄

Pourtant, une fois de plus, la Commission europ脙漏enne d脙漏脙搂oit. Ursula脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen c脙篓de face aux conservateurs. Elle nous livre une op脙漏ration de communication digne de la politique脗聽RSE d芒聙聶un grand groupe priv脙漏, o脙鹿 l芒聙聶脙漏galit脙漏 femmes-hommes est d脙漏fendue uniquement sous le prisme de b脙漏n脙漏fices 脙漏conomiques. J芒聙聶accueille donc ce texte avec lucidit脙漏. C芒聙聶est un premier pas, mais il est insuffisant.

Nous ne pourrons pas nous contenter de d脙漏clarations d芒聙聶intention. Il nous faut des engagements clairs, 脙聽 la hauteur des enjeux. 脙聙 l芒聙聶heure o脙鹿 Poutine, mais aussi tous les imp脙漏rialistes et les int脙漏gristes religieux que nous combattons, n芒聙聶attaquent pas seulement nos fronti脙篓res europ脙漏ennes, mais m脙篓nent une guerre contre les femmes, il nous faut assumer une diplomatie f脙漏ministe europ脙漏enne et leur opposer notre mod脙篓le f脙漏ministe europ脙漏en ambitieux, universaliste et combatif.

脗聽脗聽Vilija Blinkevi脛聧i脜芦t脛聴 (S&D). 芒聙聯 Pirmininke, komisare, kolegos, i脜隆 tikr脜鲁j脜鲁 per pastar脛聟j脛炉 de脜隆imtmet脛炉 buvo padaryta pa脜戮anga ly脛聧i脜鲁 lygyb脛聴s srityje, ta脛聧iau tas kelias ir tas darbas dar tikrai nepadarytas iki galo. Ir m脜芦s脜鲁 pilie脛聧iai labai ai脜隆kiai pasak脛聴, jog pagrindin脛聴mis problemomis i脜隆lieka smurtas prie脜隆 moteris, niekam ne脛炉veikiamas, moter脜鲁 ir vyr脜鲁 darbo u脜戮mokes脛聧io skirtumai u脜戮 t脛聟 pat脛炉 darb脛聟, profesinio ir asmeninio gyvenimo suderinimas ir u脜戮tikrinimas. Pagaliau, moter脜鲁 reprodukcin脛聴s teis脛聴s. Komisijos pateiktose veiksm脜鲁 gair脛聴se dar kart脛聟 pakartojama Europos S脛聟jungos, m脜芦s脜鲁 bendrijos, 脛炉sipareigojimas gerinti moter脜鲁 teises. Ta脛聧iau pirmiausia, komisare, kreipiuosi, b脜芦tina i脜隆samiai 脛炉vertinti, ar jau priimti teis脛聴s aktai i脜隆 tikr脜鲁j脜鲁 veikia ir 脛炉gyvendinami valstyb脛聴se nar脛聴se. Ko gero, toli gra脜戮u ne, tod脛聴l mums b脜芦tina tur脛聴ti veiksming脛聟 ir 脛炉pareigojan脛聧i脛聟 strategij脛聟 po 2025 m.

脗聽脗聽Gabriela Firea (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, thank you for the opportunity to talk about such an important topic.

Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, dragi colegi, 脙庐mi aduc aminte, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, 脙庐n momentul 脙庐n care eram ministra familiei 脙庐n Rom脙垄nia 脠聶i dumneavoastr脛聝 ministra fondurilor europene, c脙垄te lucruri frumoase am realizat 脙庐mpreun脛聝 pentru femeile din Rom脙垄nia 脠聶i sunt absolut convins脛聝 c脛聝 ve脠聸i pune aceast脛聝 experien脠聸脛聝 pe care o ave脠聸i 脙庐n slujba tuturor femeilor din Europa. Noi, femeile, suntem obi脠聶nuite ca 脙庐n jurul datei de 8 martie s脛聝 primim foarte multe flori 脠聶i promisiuni, inclusiv sub forma unor proiecte frumoase care, din p脛聝cate, pot s脛聝 spun, r脛聝m脙垄n pr脛聝fuite 脙庐n anumite sertare p脙垄n脛聝 anul viitor.

Totu脠聶i, vreau s脛聝 v脛聝 asigur c脛聝 nu suntem blazate 脠聶i nici nu renun脠聸脛聝m, ci vom lupta 脙庐mpreun脛聝 pentru dezideratele noastre 脠聶i 脙庐n special pentru femeile care au mai pu脠聸ine oportunit脛聝脠聸i. Pare ireal, dar 脙庐n 2025 trebuie 脙庐nc脛聝 s脛聝 vorbim despre protec脠聸ia drepturilor femeilor. Avem 脙庐n continuare probleme de reprezentare: prea pu脠聸ine femei iau parte la decizii care le privesc direct. Avem nevoie s脛聝 impunem cote de gen pentru accesul femeilor 脙庐n pozi脠聸ii de conducere politic脛聝 脠聶i administrativ脛聝. 脙聨mpreun脛聝 cu doamna comisar脛聝 european脛聝, ca ministr脛聝 a familiei, am propus 脙庐n Rom脙垄nia o lege prin care 脙庐n toate consiliile de administra脠聸ie s脛聝 fie incluse 脠聶i femei, 30脗聽% femei. R脛聝spunsul a fost unul dezarmant. R脛聝spund sub form脛聝 de 脙庐ntrebare, dar vom avea at脙垄t de multe femei (...)

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Giuseppe Lupo (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, accolgo con molto favore la presentazione della tabella di marcia per i diritti delle donne e ringrazio la vicepresidente esecutiva, Roxana M脙庐nzatu, per l'ottimo lavoro svolto.

Nonostante gli importanti passi avanti, permangono forti divari di genere, soprattutto nelle aree pi脙鹿 svantaggiate, come la Sicilia, la Sardegna, il Sud Italia, dove purtroppo l'occupazione femminile 脙篓 ancora troppo bassa anche a causa dell'insufficienza di servizi alla famiglia.

脙聢 necessario investire, investire in asili nido, in scuole per l'infanzia, in particolare dove la carenza di servizi 脙篓 tuttora causa di forti diseguaglianze.

Dobbiamo sostenere le politiche di conciliazione tra vita privata e lavoro, incentivando i congedi parentali, le politiche di flessibilit脙聽 oraria, favorendo il lavoro delle donne, elemento essenziale per la crescita dell'intero sistema economico per una societ脙聽 pi脙鹿 giusta.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Elisabeth Grossmann (S&D). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, werte Vertretungen von Kommission und Rat! Gut, dass es diese Roadmap gibt. Im Sinne des Gender-Mainstreamings ist sie Handlungsauftrag f脙录r die gesamte Kommission, damit es eben nicht bei 脙聹berschriften bleibt. Was ist also zu tun?

Zum Prinzip脗聽1: Freiheit von genderbasierter Gewalt. Das Verbrechen der Vergewaltigung ist als EU-weiter Straftatbestand zu definieren; das muss sofort geschehen. Online芒聙聭Gewalt ist aktiv zu bek脙陇mpfen. Der DSA muss konsequent umgesetzt werden, und die Plattformen m脙录ssen in die Pflicht genommen werden, menschenverachtende Inhalte sofort zu l脙露schen. Und die laufenden Verfahren m脙录ssen auch z脙录gig abgeschlossen werden, auch wenn es sich um Riesenkonzerne handelt.

Zum Prinzip脗聽2: Zugang zu sexueller und reproduktiver Gesundheit. Das bedeutet auch das Recht auf sichere Abtreibung, auch das muss klar zum Ausdruck kommen.

Die Prinzipien脗聽3 bis 5: Gleichstellung am Arbeitsmarkt und Familienleben. Die Lohntransparenz芒聙聭Richtlinie muss in allen Mitgliedstaaten rasch umgesetzt werden, und Berufe, wo viele Frauen t脙陇tig sind, wo haupts脙陇chlich Arbeit am Menschen geleistet wird, geh脙露ren dringend aufgewertet. Wenn mehr Mittel f脙录r die Verteidigung umgeschichtet werden, dann darf das nicht auf Kosten der Frauen gehen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Angel (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Commissioner, Vice-President, Minister, dear colleagues, when talking about women's rights, the European Union has to be clear and precise, otherwise it creates potential for regress. The Commission must unquestionably support the right of women to have authority over their own body. If not, the far right, sponsored by the anti-gender movement, will do its absolute worse to backtrack on sexual and reproductive health and rights. The European Union either truly defends a union of equality or open its gates to the far-right extremists within and outside of Europe to dilute women's rights in all walks of life. Make no mistake: promising in the roadmap quality and inclusive education to all girls and women and then tiptoeing around the essence of it, such as the promotion of comprehensive sexual education, is playing in the hands of the far right and patriarchy.

There is a desperate need for progress and real equality, as the people marching on the streets all over Europe on 8脗聽March showed it very clearly, so we have to listen to those voices. As our feminist and democratic values are under attack, there is no middle ground or room for hesitation. It's either mainstreaming gender equality or mainstreaming misogyny and sexism. You have our fullest support, Commissioner, on this roadmap, and the S&D Group is with you.

脗聽脗聽Nikolina Brnjac (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, povjerenice, dragi kolegice i kolege, plan za prava 脜戮ena ukazuje na tragi脛聧nu 脛聧injenicu kako se u Europskoj uniji, koja je primjer po脜隆tivanja 脜戮enskih prava, jo脜隆 uvijek u prevelikoj mjeri suo脛聧avamo s nasiljem nad 脜戮enama.

Istra脜戮ivanje objavljeno u studenom pro脜隆le godine pokazuje kako je nasilje nad 脜戮enama i dalje gotovo obi脛聧ajno, 脛聧ak i u Europi. Svaka tre脛聡a 脜戮ena do脜戮ivjela je fizi脛聧ko ili seksualno nasilje u odrasloj dobi, a svaka peta se suo脛聧ila s obiteljskim nasiljem.

I dok stariji oblici nasilja nad 脜戮enama nisu iskorijenjeni, na脜戮alost svjedo脛聧imo novima. Posljednje godi脜隆nje izvje脜隆脛聡e o ravnopravnosti spolova ukazuje kako je nasilje nad 脜戮enama na internetu postalo sveprisutno. Od 2023. godine 97 posto online prijava seksualnog zlostavljanja djece na internetu odnosilo se na seksualno zlostavljanje djevojaka i djevoj脛聧ica.

Europska unija ima najnaprednije zakonodavstvo koje obuhva脛聡a za脜隆titu od nasilja na internetu, uklju脛聧uju脛聡i nasilje koje se temelji na neeti脛聧kom kori脜隆tenju umjetne inteligencije, ali samo zakonodavstvo nije dovoljno. Moramo educirati 脜戮ene, a osobito djevoj脛聧ice kako bismo se osna脜戮ili u pogledu nasilja svih vrsta, uklju脛聧uju脛聡i nasilje na internetu i nasilje putem dru脜隆tvenih mre脜戮a.

Kao zastupnici u parlamentu moramo u脛聧initi sve 脜隆to je u na脜隆oj mo脛聡i kako bi se 脜戮ene osje脛聡ale sigurno i slobodno.

脗聽脗聽Maria Grapini (S&D). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, 脙庐nt脙垄i a脠聶 vrea s脛聝 subliniez ceva pozitiv, 脠聶i anume c脛聝 pentru prima dat脛聝 avem patru institu脠聸ii europene conduse de femei. Sunt m脙垄ndr脛聝 c脛聝 Rom脙垄nia, 脠聸ara mea, de trei mandate are comisar femeie. Iat脛聝 c脛聝 sunt lucruri care s-au dezvoltat 脙庐n societatea noastr脛聝 democratic脛聝. A脠聶 mai vrea ceva: s-a folosit foarte mult cuv脙垄ntul 芒聙聻a proteja芒聙聺 芒聙聯 haide脠聸i s脛聝-l 脙庐nlocuim cu 芒聙聻asumare芒聙聺, nu 芒聙聻protejare芒聙聺. De ce? Femeile sunt puternice. Eu vreau s脛聝 m脛聝 adresez acum partenerilor no脠聶tri b脛聝rba脠聸i. Asuma脠聸i-v脛聝 s脛聝 pune脠聸i accent pe criteriul valorii, c脛聝-i femeie, c脛聝-i b脛聝rbat, pe criteriul valorii. Atunci vom fi competitivi 脠聶i eficien脠聸i.

Vreau s脛聝 v脛聝 mai spun ceva: 脙庐n 脠聸ara mea, 脙庐n 8 martie s脛聝rb脛聝torim 芒聙聯 脠聶i 脙庐n toat脛聝 lumea, bine脙庐n脠聸eles 芒聙聯 Ziua Interna脠聸ional脛聝 a Femeii, dar 脙庐n 9 martie e Ziua B脛聝rbatului 脠聶i ne respect脛聝m reciproc 脠聶i eu cred 脙庐n acest parteneriat pentru a putea s脛聝 facem proiecte 脙庐mpreun脛聝. Conduc o asocia脠聸ie a femeilor din Rom脙垄nia de 28 de ani 脠聶i crede脠聸i-m脛聝 c脛聝 lucr脛聝m foarte bine cu partenerii b脛聝rba脠聸i. 脙聨n foaia de parcurs, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, 脠聶i sunt m脙垄ndr脛聝 c脛聝 sunte脠聸i aici, trebuie s脛聝 vorbi脠聸i 脠聶i cu ceilal脠聸i comisari, s脛聝 avem m脛聝suri concrete. A脠聶a cum am spus 脠聶i la alte dezbateri, nu e suficient s脛聝 dezbatem, ci trebuie s脛聝 avem m脛聝suri concrete pentru a face progrese.

IN THE CHAIR: JAVI L脙聯PEZ
Vice-President

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, one in three women report having experienced physical or sexual violence in the past 12 months. This should be a wake-up call. It begs the question: why have our streets become so unsafe for women?

The Finnish police recently published statistics on sex crime rates by nationality. The results are revealing. Compared to Finnish nationals, the rates of sexual offences committed are six times higher for Somalis; ten times higher for Iraqis; 12 times higher for Afghans and Congolese; and as much as 15 times higher for Syrians. Absolutely shocking numbers.

Colleagues, let us stand up for our daughters, sisters and wives. Let us make our streets safe again 芒聙聯 let's protect our borders from the type of immigration that is harmful.

脗聽
