脧聙脧聛脧聦脦驴脦麓脦驴脧聜 脧聙脦驴脧聟 脦颅脧聡脦驴脧聟脦录脦碌 脧聙脦碌脧聞脧聧脧聡脦碌脦鹿 脦赂脦卤 脧聝脧聟脦陆脦碌脧聡脦鹿脧聝脧聞脦碌脦炉 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦麓脦碌脦陆 脦赂脦卤 脦潞脦卤脦赂脧聟脧聝脧聞脦碌脧聛脦庐脧聝脦碌脦鹿 脦路 脦碌脧聙脦炉脧聞脦碌脧聟脦戮脦路 脦录脦鹿脦卤脧聜 脦麓脦炉脦潞脦卤脦鹿脦路脧聜, 脦卤脧聝脧聠脦卤脦禄脦驴脧聧脧聜 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear people of Europe, my mother was born in 1966, in a small village in western Germany. Around the age of 16, she became the first female member of the local rifle club. A few years later, she became the first woman in our family to study - dentistry. Together with my father, she opened a dental practice and had two children, one of whom, as you know, has a special level of energy.

When my parents' marriage fell apart, it was my mother who mainly provided for us, ensured we received a good education and continued to run this practice on her own.

Women can achieve incredible things if we just let them, and that alone is why we should let them.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Arba Kokalari (PPE). 芒聙聯 Herr talman! Otryggheten 脙陇r v脙楼r tids st脙露rsta frihetsfr脙楼ga, och kvinnors r脙陇ttigheter 脙陇r helt centralt i den frihetskampen n脙陇r en av tre kvinnor i Europa har utsatts f脙露r v脙楼ld, n脙陇r de ryska soldaterna anv脙陇nder kvinnors kroppar som en del av den ryska krigf脙露ringen. Jag vill att alla flickor och unga tjejer som v脙陇xer upp i v脙楼rt Europa i脗聽dag ska k脙陇nna att vi st脙楼r upp f脙露r deras framtid och deras frihet. D脙楼 m脙楼ste vi g脙露ra g脙露r allt vi kan h脙陇r i脗聽dag f脙露r att st脙露tta ukrainska kvinnor, f脙露r att se till att direktivet om v脙楼ld mot kvinnor och Istanbulkonventionen mot v脙楼ld mot kvinnor implementeras v脙陇l. Att vi st脙楼r upp och grundlagsskyddar abortr脙陇tten f脙露r alla kvinnor i Europa och att vi inf脙露r en samtyckeslag i hela Europa. Historieskrivningen sker just nu, och kvinnors r脙陇ttigheter och kampen f脙露r friheten avg脙露rs nu. Nu m脙楼ste vi se till att Europa st脙楼r p脙楼 r脙陇tt sida av historien.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marit Maij (S&D). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, we vieren Internationale Vrouwendag dit jaar, terwijl we zien dat vrouwenrechten en gendergelijkheid achteruitgaan. Jonge mannen vinden geweld tegen vrouwen tegenwoordig vaker prima dan oudere generaties. Er zijn in de EU meer vrouwen onnodig overleden vanwege inperkingen van de abortusmogelijkheden. Vrouwen verdienen nog steeds 13脗聽% minder dan mannen, voor hetzelfde werk. Ook vindt 脙漏脙漏n op de tien mannen het prima als een man af en toe zijn vrouw of vriendin slaat.

Dit jaar is het dertig jaar na de Beijing芒聙聭verklaring en nog altijd is dat de meest vooruitstrevende en omvangrijke mondiale agenda voor gendergelijkheid. En veel van de rechten uit die verklaring staan tegenwoordig op het hakblok. Dit jaar voelde ik op de Dam, bij de demonstratie op de Feminist March, samen met twintigduizend anderen, de urgentie, omdat we na decennia weer achteruitgaan.

De routekaart van de Commissie moet ons terugbrengen op het pad van gendergelijkheid, voor meisjes en voor vrouwen wereldwijd en in Europa. Want vrouwenrechten zijn mensenrechten.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nina Carberry (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, I want to start by welcoming the new Roadmap for Women's Rights. Gender equality is a fundamental principle of the EU, and this roadmap is a crucial step forward. However, we must ensure it leads to real change.

In these times of geopolitical uncertainty, we cannot afford to backslide on gender equality. Across Europe, we are seeing growing threats to these rights, from severe underfunding of critical support services for women to gaps in legal frameworks addressing gender-based violence.

An excellent example of progress, however, is the Zero Tolerance Meath pilot project in Ireland, led by Meath Women's Refuge and Support Services, to tackle domestic sexual and gender-based violence.

And so I urge the Commission to use this roadmap as a foundation for the post-2025 Gender Equality Strategy, while also ensuring the full and timely implementation of the Directive on gender-based violence. Words are not enough. Action is needed now!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alessandra Moretti (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, desidero ringraziare la Commissione e la commissaria Minzatu per questa road map sui diritti delle donne. Sono felice di aver ritrovato nel testo riferimenti a battaglie cruciali per la libert脙聽 di tante donne. Tuttavia chiedo che queste non siano solo belle parole spese in occasione dell'8 marzo: servono impegni concreti da realizzare attraverso misure legislative e non tangibili.

La violenza di genere resta una piaga: una donna su tre ne 脙篓 vittima in Europa. Quanto tempo ci vorr脙聽 ancora per concordare una definizione di stupro legata alla mancanza di consenso? Come possiamo promuovere progetti che insegnino ai nostri ragazzi il rispetto dei corpi e delle persone?

Poi le crisi che abbiamo affrontato, che ancora non abbiamo risolto: penso al COVID, alla crisi energetica, alla povert脙聽, al cambiamento climatico. Le donne pagano il prezzo pi脙鹿 alto. Servono misure concrete per alleviarne gli effetti.

Infine il tema della salute: dobbiamo investire in programmi che mettano finalmente in luce una medicina di genere appropriata. La salute deve essere un diritto accessibile e di qualit脙聽 per tutti. Non vogliamo pi脙鹿 continuare a curarci con diagnosi e medicinali pensati per un corpo maschile.

Grazie.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maria Walsh (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President,脗聽according to the European Commission, there's 229 million women living in the EU, 5脗聽% more than men. Again, I point out 芒聙聯 just ironic 芒聙聯 about 5脗聽% of elected officials here sit in this House to debate such an important issue, ignoring the 229 million people that elected us to be here. I certainly welcome the roadmap, but let's be very clear to all EU citizens on the realities facing women in today's world.

87脗聽000 women across the world will die because they're women. Over the course of a decade, that's the city of Amsterdam. 96脗聽% of AI芒聙聭generated content known as deepfakes are pornographic in nature, sexualised content targeting women and young girls. We know this from reports that 99脗聽% of them are indeed women and young girls.

The foundation of gender equality is built on the rights to live free from all injustices. Yet, at the current pace of progress, gender equality will only be achieved by 2158. Yet I ask, what are we doing in here for the 229 million citizens that elected us and expect us to do better?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jessika Van Leeuwen (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, women's rights. Our grandmothers and mothers fought for these rights. And today, if we're not careful, we will lose them again. And as a mother of three daughters, I am a strong advocate for gender equality.

However, I do believe two things. First of all, without allowing men to actively, actively participate in gender equality, we will never achieve anything. So this feminist hatred against men will not bring us any further, but will polarise society instead.

Secondly, we women are part of the problem. As long as we victimise ourselves, we continue to keep the vicious circle of gender inequality intact. It's time to take responsibility for our actions and to refuse to be treated as victims. We are not victims. If we refuse to be, we are not victims.

It's time we take our rightful place in the society just because we can and not because a gender quota assigns us a spot.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Roxana M脙庐nzatu, Executive Vice-President of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, thank you for a very meaningful conversation. Women's rights are human rights and fighting for women's rights means being a democrat and then a feminist, and however we may call it.

We fight for our democracies when we fight for the rights of women in employment, in health, on the job market, in public positions, in research, in what concerns their safety, in their homes, on the streets, online, that I think should unite us.

And脗聽this roadmap 芒聙聭 and I am also a woman of action and not necessarily just of words脗聽芒聙聭 is an invitation in these somewhat disruptive times, where we see some pullback. It's an invitation to all of us, to you Members of the European Parliament, to Member States in the Council, to all the stakeholders, social partners, all that are watching us, all that have seen this roadmap, to reaffirm, to recommit to all values for nothing can be taken for granted.

And yes, once we reaffirm and recommit - action. And we have such an important package of EU legislation that has been recently adopted, the directives that need to be transposed and implemented, and we will come with a gender equality strategy for 2026-2030, the next generation with new measures, new actions, and we need to think about how we mainstream the gender dimension in our funding policies, in the MFF, in every policy. And I would give just my own example because I recently adopted my first initiative, the Union of Skills, where it's not just about a general discussion, but for example, in STEM education I committed and I invite all to train 1 million girls and women in STEM education by 2028. And many said it's too much. What are you talking about? It's that kind of ambition.

In the end, I would address the elephant in the room. I come from Romania. I was born in 1980, in Romania, from 1966 until the communist regime was overthrown, overthrown, abortion rights were prohibited. Thousands of women died because of illegal interruptions of the pregnancies. Many remain traumatised physically and mentally. Many children were abandoned in state facilities that were absolutely horrendous.

When I speak and when I fought with Commissioner Lahbib and the Commission to have a reaffirmation of a clear principle stating that women should have access to sexual and reproductive health and rights, I do not speak from the books. I speak from reality. I come from a family where stories of women dying and suffering were told at coffee every morning, every neighbour, every aunt, it was normality.

Please think twice how much we need to commit to women's health and try to connect with the reality of transposing these rights. Our action starts from committing to principles and values. Our common action is the one that can deliver the rights and protections that women need.

And I would say this roadmap is important in 2025. And I invite you to join to support it, to sign it, to implement it and to work together. We need to be together in this fight every day, every year, no matter where our position is in Commission, in Parliament, in national politics or any other place.

Thank you and I'm looking forward to working with you on these topics.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adam Sz脜聜apka, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, Commissioners, honourable Members, yes, women's rights are human rights. Women's rights concern all of us and I'm very thankful for this debate. As we heard, the Commission has charted a path forward with its Roadmap for Women's Rights and will be tabling new strategic documents on different aspects of equality in due course.

We are looking forward to these new initiatives while Member States implement the legislation that has recently been adopted. I can also inform you that the Polish Presidency will be hosting the informal meeting of equality ministers in April. Let us move forward together and make equality a reality for all women and men, boys and girls.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The debate is closed.

Written Statements (Rule 178)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Caterina Chinnici (PPE), per iscritto. 芒聙聯 Celebriamo oggi la Giornata internazionale della donna.

Come donna, madre, magistrato e deputata al Parlamento europeo, ritengo di rappresentare emblematicamente la molta strada, non facile, che 脙篓 stata fatta sul fronte della parit脙聽 di genere ma, 脙篓 innegabile, non 脙篓 ancora cos脙卢 per tante, troppe donne e ragazze!

脙聢 infatti ancora lungo il cammino per assicurare l芒聙聶attuazione concreta dell芒聙聶art. 23 della Carta dei diritti fondamentali dell'Unione europea che stabilisce: 芒聙聹La parit脙聽 tra uomini e donne in tutti i campi, compreso in materia di occupazione, di lavoro e di retribuzione.芒聙聺

Bene quindi l芒聙聶introduzione della roadmap dell芒聙聶UE per i diritti delle donne, presentata dalla Commissione nei suoi primi 100 giorni di lavoro, che rappresenta un rinnovato impulso a rafforzare la parit脙聽 di genere, consolidando i progressi compiuti nell'ambito della strategia 2020-2025, e fornisce una panoramica dettagliata degli ambiti in cui sono necessari ulteriori interventi.

C芒聙聶脙篓 ancora molto lavoro da fare: per far fronte alla piaga silenziosa e inaccettabile della violenza contro le donne che, purtroppo, non accenna a fermarsi, per colmare il divario retributivo di genere e, soprattutto, per garantire concretamente, ad ogni donna, la libert脙聽 di non dover mai scegliere tra vita professionale e vita familiare!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Loucas Fourlas (PPE), in writing. 芒聙聯 On the occasion of International Women's Day, we celebrate the women who have always been at the forefront of change 芒聙聯 leading movements, shaping communities and breaking barriers. Today, we recognise their strength, achievements and unwavering drive.

But it芒聙聶s important to remember that this celebration is not just for one day 芒聙聯 it is a commitment to equality every day. True equality goes beyond recognition; it芒聙聶s about ensuring equal opportunity for all. It芒聙聶s about creating a world where no girl grows up believing she has to fight harder to be heard. We should dismantle barriers, challenge outdated mindsets and build an environment where talent, not gender, defines success.

From the boardroom to the classroom, from the脗聽front lines to the voting booths, women are constantly redefining leadership. Yet too many still face discrimination, unequal opportunities and violence. These injustices must have no place in our societies.

Our words today should turn into actions tomorrow. The future we strive for 芒聙聯 a world where every woman and girl can dream without limits 芒聙聯 is not just an aspiration; it is a necessity. And as the European Parliament, we must work hard to make this future a reality for all.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gabriella Gerzsenyi (PPE), 脙颅r脙隆sban. 芒聙聯 Van egy fiam 脙漏s egy l脙隆nyom. Amikor megsz脙录lettek, azt k脙颅v脙隆ntam, hogy mindketten megval脙鲁s脙颅thass脙隆k az 脙隆lmaikat. 2025-ben a l脙隆nyom h脙隆tr脙隆nyban van a fiv脙漏r脙漏vel szemben a t脙隆rsadalomban fenn脙隆ll脙鲁 es脙漏lyegyenl脜聭tlens脙漏g miatt.

N脙漏gy kulcspontot emeln脙漏k ki a n脜聭k helyzet脙漏r脜聭l:

1. B脙漏regyenl脜聭tlens脙漏g: Az EU-ban a nemek k脙露z脙露tti b脙漏rszakad脙漏k 脙隆tlagosan 13%, Magyarorsz脙隆gon 17%. Ez nemcsak a n脜聭ket 脙漏rinti h脙隆tr脙隆nyosan, hanem a f脙漏rfiakat is, mivel a csal脙隆d fenntart脙隆s脙隆nak terhe ar脙隆nytalanul r脙隆juk h脙隆rul.

3. D脙露nt脙漏shozatalban val脙鲁 r脙漏szv脙漏tel: A politikai 脙漏s gazdas脙隆gi d脙露nt脙漏shozatalban a n脜聭k ar脙隆nya eleny脙漏sz脜聭. Eur脙鲁p脙隆ban a parlamenti k脙漏pvisel脜聭k 33%-a n脜聭, Magyarorsz脙隆gon csup脙隆n 15,7%-a!

4. A l脙隆thatatlan munka elismer脙漏se: A n脜聭k hozz脙隆j脙隆rul脙隆sa a t脙隆rsadalomhoz 芒聙聯 a gyermeknevel脙漏s, id脜聭sgondoz脙隆s, h脙隆ztart脙隆si munka 芒聙聯 az EU GDP-j脙漏nek 10%-脙隆t teszi ki.

A n脜聭k jogainak v脙漏delme 脙露sszt脙隆rsadalmi 脙漏rdek!脗聽Az脙漏rt dolgozunk, hogy legyen igazs脙隆gos b脙漏rez脙漏s, minden sz脙录l脜聭 sz脙隆m脙隆ra el脙漏rhet脜聭 脙漏s megb脙颅zhat脙鲁 b脙露lcs脜聭de 脙漏s 脙鲁voda, szabad orvosv脙隆laszt脙隆s, valamint hogy a csal脙隆dok ne anyagi gondok k脙露z脙露tt d脙露nts脙漏k el, mernek-e gyermeket v脙隆llalni.

脗聽脗聽Sirpa Pietik脙陇inen (PPE), kirjallinen. 芒聙聯 Ihmisoikeudet ovat oikeusvaltion perusta. Naiset ovat ihmisi脙陇, ja siksi naisten oikeudet ovat erottamaton osa ihmisoikeuksia 芒聙聯 my脙露s seksuaali- ja lis脙陇脙陇ntymisterveysoikeudet. Ilman n脙陇it脙陇 ei oikeusvaltioperiaate toteudu.

On t脙陇rke脙陇脙陇, ett脙陇 komission uudessa naisten oikeuksien tiekartassa mainitaan digiv脙陇kivalta, joka kohdistuu erityisesti naisiin ja tytt脙露ihin, rajoittaen heid脙陇n ilmaisunvapauttaan, osallistumistaan yhteiskunnalliseen keskusteluun ja turvallisuuttaan verkossa. T脙陇m脙陇 on jatkumoa laajemmalle sukupuolittuneelle, rakenteelliselle v脙陇kivallalle, joka yll脙陇pit脙陇脙陇 eriarvoisuutta ja pelon ilmapiiri脙陇. Pekingin julistuksessa tunnistettu rakenteellinen v脙陇kivalta tarkoittaa yhteiskunnallisia rakenteita ja k脙陇yt脙陇nt脙露j脙陇, jotka j脙陇tt脙陇v脙陇t naiset marginaaliin, esimerkiksi rajoittamalla heid脙陇n vaikutusmahdollisuuksiaan p脙陇脙陇t脙露ksenteossa, sek脙陇 yll脙陇pit脙陇m脙陇ll脙陇 palkkaeroja ja kasautuvia hoivavastuita.

Ilman m脙陇脙陇r脙陇tietoisia toimia n脙陇m脙陇 rakenteet jatkavat sukupuolten ep脙陇tasa-arvon syvent脙陇mist脙陇. Siksi EU:n on rakennettava parempi tulevaisuus jokaiselle tyt脙露lle ja naiselle vahvistamalla perusoikeusn脙陇k脙露kulmaa kaikessa lains脙陇脙陇d脙陇nn脙露ss脙陇.

脗聽

10. Presentation of the proposal on Critical Medicines Act (CMA) (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next item is the debate on the Commission statement on the presentation of the proposal for a Critical Medicines Act (2025/2568(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Oliv脙漏r V脙隆rhelyi, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, honourable Members, today the Commission adopted the Critical Medicines Act. This proposal is very much needed to ensure security of supply and availability of critical medicines, as well as accessibility of other key medicines where the market is not delivering. This is a priority file for the Commission, which is adopted on its hundredth day as promised.

Why, you might ask? Because medicine shortages are a long standing concern for the EU and our citizens. This concern has been raised multiple times by this House, as well. Enough to mention that a number of shortage notifications has increased substantially and persistently since 2013, reaching the peak in 2019 with close to 14脗聽000 notifications. Enough to mention that most EU countries report increasing numbers of medicine shortages, with the great majority having recorded shortages for 200 or more medicines in 2023.

With the help of this House, we have already taken a number of steps at EU level to improve the situation. Together, honourable Members, we are reforming the general pharmaceutical legislation and have extended the mandate of the European Medicines Agency. These two building blocks establish a regulatory framework to prevent and mitigate shortages during crises and at all times. We have established together a Critical Medicines Alliance, bringing together more than 300 stakeholders from the patient and the scientific communities, health professionals, Member States and industry to explore ways to strengthen the supply of critical medicines in the EU. The Alliance has already provided us with recommendations in its strategic report.

The Critical Medicines Act builds on all this work we have done together. It provides a comprehensive approach to improve the availability of critical medicines and accessibility of other key medicines where there is a market failure. It complements the ongoing revision of the pharmaceutical legislation and aims to reduce dependencies for critical medicines and their ingredients to reinforce the EU's strategic autonomy in health.

It will also have a positive impact on the Union's competitiveness by fostering a more stable and predictable market environment, encouraging investment and supporting innovation in the pharmaceutical sector. The act has different mechanisms to achieve these goals. First, it will facilitate investments for companies that create or increase manufacturing capacity of critical medicines in the EU. It will become possible for these projects to be identified as strategic projects. This will give them easier access to funding and fast-track administrative, regulatory and scientific support.脗聽The Commission has today also published State aid guidance to help Member States to finally support such strategic projects or projects pursuing similar objectives.

Second, the act will increase the resilience of supply chains. Procurers will have to use criteria other than only price in their procurement procedures. For instance, these criteria could be the use of diversified suppliers or contractual clauses on timely delivery. There are also provisions for critical medicines with high dependency on supply from one or from a limited number of countries outside the EU. In this case, procurers will also be required, where justified, to favour suppliers that manufacture a significant proportion of these critical medicinal products in the EU, as long as they comply with the Union's international commitments.

Third, we will offer Member States the possibility to make use of aggregated demands. If interested, EU countries will be able to request support from the Commission to use different collaborative procurement tools, facilitating access to the medicines they need, building on the experience of jointly procuring medical countermeasures.

Finally, the Commission will seek to enhance existing cooperations and conclude new international partnerships to diversify supply chains for these critical medicines. When it comes to the new international partnerships, of course, I very much rely on the candidate countries and countries in our closest neighbourhood who should be in pole position to help us to bring back production in the EU or closer to the EU.

Honourable Members, together, these measures will reduce the EU's dependency on individual suppliers of critical medicines, improve the security of supply of affordable medicines, and increase the EU's manufacturing capabilities. So, to sum up, the Critical Medicines Act ensures that EU patients have access to the medicines they need, when and where they need them at an affordable price. In the current geopolitical context, this has become an even bigger priority.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tomislav Sokol, u ime kluba PPE. 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i, povjereni脛聧e, kolegice i kolege, farmaceutska industrija jedan je od najva脜戮nijih strate脜隆kih gospodarskih sektora za Europsku uniju. Bilo kakva nesta脜隆ica lijekova predstavlja zdravstvenu, ali i egzistencijalnu, sigurnosnu prijetnju za Europu.

Na脜戮alost, Europska unija sve vi脜隆e postaje ovisna o uvozu lijekova iz tre脛聡ih dr脜戮ava, a upravo je nedavna pandemija najbolje pokazala koliko smo ranjivi kad je u pitanju nesta脜隆ica lijekova i medicinske opreme.

Ovakva situacija dodatno je pogor脜隆ana zbog geopoliti脛聧kih okolnosti u kojima se nalazimo. Budu脛聡i da je do脜隆lo vrijeme brutalne sile u me脛聭unarodnim odnosima te se svatko treba pobrinuti, koliko god je to mogu脛聡e, za vlastite interese.

Krajnje je vrijeme da preuzmemo stvari u svoje ruke, a ovim prijedlogom zakona o kriti脛聧nim lijekovima 脛聧inimo korak u pravom smjeru. Zato pozdravljam mehanizme, posebno financijske potpore i ubrzavanje administrativnih procedura za strate脜隆ke projekte kojima olak脜隆avamo gradnju tvornica lijekova na europskom tlu.

Tako脛聭er, podr脜戮avam zajedni脛聧ku nabavu kriti脛聧nih lijekova, 脜隆to je posebno va脜戮no za manje dr脜戮ave poput moje Republike Hrvatske jer time ja脛聧amo njihovu pregovara脛聧ku mo脛聡 i osiguravamo bolje uvjete nabave.

Pri tome izuzetno je va脜戮no uvo脛聭enje dodatnih kriterija odabira u javnoj nabavi lijekova osim cijene, poput sigurnosti opskrbe i proizvodnje na europskom teritoriju, 脜隆to 脛聡e svakako olak脜隆ati dostupnost kriti脛聧nih lijekova za na脜隆e gra脛聭ane i smanjiti ovisnost o pojedinim dobavlja脛聧ima izvan EU-a.

Jedna od stvari koje, me脛聭utim, nisu toliko nagla脜隆ene je stvaranje zaliha kriti脛聧nih lijekova. Ne smije se nikada vi脜隆e dogoditi da pojedine velike dr脜戮ave 脛聧lanice u zdravstvenoj krizi zadr脜戮avaju klju脛聧ne medicinske potrep脜隆tine i time doprinose produbljivanju krize umjesto njenom rje脜隆avanju. Zato nam treba ja脛聧anje zajedni脛聧kih europskih zaliha i koordinacija na razini EU-a kako bi lijekovi i druge medicinske potrep脜隆tine u slu脛聧aju nesta脜隆ice stigli upravo onima koji ih najvi脜隆e trebaju.

Sve u svemu, pozdravljam ovaj prijedlog jer on doprinosi ekonomskoj sigurnosti EU-a smanjenjem ovisnosti o stranim dobavlja脛聧ima i osiguravanju stabilne opskrbe. Osim toga, njime 脛聡e se pove脛聡ati konkurentnost EU-a na globalnom farmaceutskom tr脜戮i脜隆tu, privu脛聡i ulaganja u istra脜戮ivanje i inovacije te stvoriti nova radna mjesta.

Vrijeme je da interes Europske unije i na脜隆ih pacijenata doista postane prioritet i drago mi je da sada idemo u pravom smjeru.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Christophe Clergeau, au nom du groupe S&D. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, nous sommes cinq ans apr脙篓s la crise de la COVID-19 et, malheureusement, nous serions probablement confront脙漏s 脙聽 la m脙陋me situation si une nouvelle crise sanitaire arrivait aujourd芒聙聶hui, et aux m脙陋mes d脙漏faillances dans l芒聙聶acc脙篓s aux m脙漏dicaments. Nous avons donc besoin de ce texte pour prot脙漏ger les Europ脙漏ens et garantir l芒聙聶acc脙篓s aux m脙漏dicaments, en p脙漏riode de crise comme en p脙漏riode normale: c芒聙聶est l脙聽 une question de souverainet脙漏 de l芒聙聶Europe. Nous avons besoin d芒聙聶une politique incitative pour encourager la production de m脙漏dicaments en Europe; cependant, nous n芒聙聶avons pas besoin d芒聙聶un texte pour l芒聙聶industrie pharmaceutique, mais bien d芒聙聶un texte pour les citoyens.

脙聙 cette fin, nous devons avoir un cadre rigoureux, clarifier les d脙漏finitions des m脙漏dicaments critiques et des p脙漏nuries, assurer la transparence de la cha脙庐ne de valeur, avoir des conditionnalit脙漏s sur les march脙漏s publics et favoriser des march脙漏s ou des achats collectifs 脙聽 l芒聙聶脙漏chelle europ脙漏enne 芒聙聯脗聽autrement dit, la solidarit脙漏 europ脙漏enne. Ce qui est le plus important, pour les socialistes, c芒聙聶est peut-脙陋tre de garantir 脙聽 toutes et 脙聽 tous en Europe l芒聙聶acc脙篓s aux m脙漏dicaments 芒聙聯脗聽leur disponibilit脙漏 un prix accessible, et des stocks disponibles脗聽芒聙聯, et ce partout, au plus pr脙篓s des gens. Il s芒聙聶agit bien, 脙聽 travers ce texte, d芒聙聶une politique de sant脙漏 publique autant que d芒聙聶une politique industrielle.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ond脜聶ej Knotek, on behalf of the PfE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, Mr Commissioner, the Critical Medicines Act is an extremely important file. The world is in a de- globalisation phase. Dependencies reduction and self-sufficiency have become a logical trend. This for sure includes medicines, their availability and affordability simply matter.

Therefore, we welcome the ambition to maintain a list of critical medicines for which European research and production capacities would be granted. But it is not an easy task. As critical, we consider a wide range of medicines, including those old simple molecules whose production was, due to cost optimisation, transferred outside the EU in the past decades. And now we want them back.

Also, we shall consider what should be the role of the EU here. In my opinion, mainly coordination of fair distribution of supportive projects among Member States to avoid duplication and at the end, after implementation, coordination of logistics of critical medicines.

However, we believe that tools like CMA and Pharma Package can contribute positively to increase medicine supplies resilience on our continent, and in this regard, Mr Commissioner, you have our full support.

However, I would like to extend that attracting private investments of pharma industry in the EU would require at the same time, in parallel, energy cost reductions, decreasing of obligations linked to the medicines lifecycle and also less bureaucracy, which is exactly the opposite of which some of your colleagues, Mr Commissioner, are doing.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ruggero Razza, a nome del gruppo ECR. 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, signor Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, anzitutto grazie, Commissario, per avere rispettato uno degli impegni che lei aveva annunciato nel corso della Sua audizione, e cio脙篓 di essere qui entro i 100 giorni e presentare un atto normativo di straordinaria importanza.

Atto normativo che era largamente atteso, non soltanto per ci脙虏 che 脙篓 emerso all'indomani della pandemia, quando abbiamo scoperto come Europa che la gran parte della nostra produzione avveniva al di fuori dei 27 Paesi e quindi era difficile un approvvigionamento, soprattutto in momenti di difficolt脙聽.

Oggi c'脙篓 una questione molto importante che questo atto inizia ad affrontare ed 脙篓 quella di rendere competitiva e di rendere realizzabile la produzione di medicinali, soprattutto generici, anche in Europa, mentre oggi sappiamo che la principale criticit脙聽 sta tutta qui: nel fatto che produrre il farmaco in Europa 脙篓 diventato antieconomico.

E allora 脙篓 un lavoro che inizia col Critical Medicines Act, 脙篓 un lavoro che 脙篓 stato preso dalla bussola della competitivit脙聽. Noi siamo ottimisti perch脙漏 脙篓 anche questa una questione di sovranit脙聽.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Vlad Vasile-Voiculescu, 脙庐n numele grupului Renew. 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, avem, iat脛聝, un prim produs legislativ al noii Comisii 脠聶i 脙庐i mul脠聸umesc domnului comisar pentru p脛聝strarea cuv脙垄ntului dat 脙庐n primele 100 de zile. Mul脠聸umesc, de asemenea, tuturor celor care au contribuit, 脙庐n special celor din Critical Medicines Alliance. Avem ast脛聝zi un pas necesar pentru a proteja pacien脠聸ii din Europa de criza medicamentelor esen脠聸iale. 脙聨n Rom脙垄nia, am fost martorii unor situa脠聸ii alarmante: spitalele de oncologie din Rom脙垄nia 芒聙聯 nu 脙庐n ultimul r脙垄nd, oncologie pediatric脛聝 芒聙聯 raporteaz脛聝 frecvent lipsa medicamentelor esen脠聸iale pentru diferite tipuri de cancer, compromi脠聸脙垄nd astfel 脠聶ansa copiilor 脠聶i 脠聶ansa adul脠聸ilor la un tratament corect.

Spitalele din Satu Mare din Rom脙垄nia s-au confruntat de cur脙垄nd cu lipsa hidrocortizonului. De asemenea, lipsesc 脙庐n continuare multe medicamente antiepileptice, 脙庐n special pentru copii 脠聶i nu numai. Aceast脛聝 lege propune independen脠聸脛聝 strategic脛聝, coordonare a politicilor de stocuri, produc脠聸ie european脛聝 脠聶i achizi脠聸ii coordonate 脠聶i poate, 脙庐ntr-adev脛聝r, s脛聝 scad脛聝 dependen脠聸a de lan脠聸urile de aprovizionare globale 脠聶i s脛聝 creasc脛聝 accesul europenilor la medicamente. Totu脠聶i, f脛聝r脛聝 mecanisme clare de implementare, bugete adecvate 脠聶i sanc脠聸iuni pentru nerespectarea obliga脠聸iilor, acest act risc脛聝 s脛聝 r脛聝m脙垄n脛聝 doar o declara脠聸ie de inten脠聸ie.

IN THE CHAIR: SOPHIE WILM脙聢S
Vice-President

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tilly Metz, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, this project is a perfect example of what you get when one writes legislation on the fly and in a hurry. Exactly what I fear has happened. The challenges for availability of critical medicines are enormous in Europe, the expectations of both patient and the pharmaceutical industry regarding effective solutions proposed by the European Union are as great as the challenges. Unfortunately, what has been presented today falls far short from the expectations. The measures proposed are too weak. There is nothing on EU stockpiling, nothing on the public medicine infrastructure, nothing to ensure transparency for the public, nothing innovative in terms of announcing. Nothing that would ensure public support actually translate into the availability of medicines. Only joint procurement and fast-tracking of administrative procedures will not solve the problem. The Critical Medicines Act must become an ambitious legislation to meet citizens' expectations.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Catarina Martins, em nome do Grupo The Left. 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, a escassez de medicamentos 脙漏 um problema crescente na Uni脙拢o Europeia, h脙隆 ruturas at脙漏 em medicamentos para o cancro, e ter ou n脙拢o acesso 脙漏 uma lotaria geogr脙隆fica. E a defini脙搂脙拢o de medicamentos cr脙颅ticos, ela pr脙鲁pria, esquece algumas doen脙搂as raras ou a sa脙潞de reprodutiva das mulheres.

脙聣, portanto, urgente agir.脗聽Mas a proposta da Comiss脙拢o responde ao fundamental?

A cadeia do medicamento est脙隆 ref脙漏m das grandes farmac脙陋uticas e sujeita 脙聽 vontade de pa脙颅ses terceiros. A maioria dos medicamentos gen脙漏ricos produzidos na Europa depende de subst脙垄ncias ativas produzidas na China e na 脙聧ndia.脗聽Garantir a produ脙搂脙拢o e o abastecimento de medicamentos exige, por isso, transpar脙陋ncia, novas regras de mercado, incluindo sobre as patentes, e investimento p脙潞blico.

Ministros da Sa脙潞de de 11 Estados芒聙聭Membros pedem agora que este investimento seja inclu脙颅do nos programas de defesa. O pedido p脙碌e o dedo na ferida. Afinal, para o armamento h脙隆 800 mil milh脙碌es de euros e para a sa脙潞de s脙鲁 h脙隆 restri脙搂脙碌es.

E sem capacidade soberana nos medicamentos, como no digital ou na energia, n脙拢o h脙隆 seguran脙搂a na Europa.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adam Jarubas (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Panie Komisarzu! Dzi脛聶kuj脛聶 za t脛聶 propozycj脛聶. Akt o lekach krytycznych to brakuj脛聟cy element odpowiedzi Komisji Europejskiej na niedobory lek脙鲁w po wykazie i Sojuszu na rzecz Lek脙鲁w Krytycznych. To wi脛聶ksze bezpiecze脜聞stwo lekowe Europejczyk脙鲁w.

Co z tego, 脜录e wynaleziono lek, 脜录e by脛聡 mo脜录e mamy nawet na niego fundusze, je脜聸li nie jest fizycznie dost脛聶pny w Europie, bo trafia na inne rynki, jest przedmiotem prac, presji w polityce mi脛聶dzynarodowej lub dostawy zostaj脛聟 odci脛聶te z powodu pandemii, napi脛聶脛聡 geopolitycznych, sankcji czy otwartych konflikt脙鲁w.

Europa dzi脜聸 sprowadza 80% lek脙鲁w lub substancji do ich produkcji. W niekt脙鲁rych przypadkach w og脙鲁le ich nie produkuje i w stu procentach zale脜录y od importu. Co je脜聸li, tak jak z energi脛聟 z Rosji, przyjdzie nam zmierzy脛聡 si脛聶 z dnia na dzie脜聞 z odci脛聶ciem importu tych lek脙鲁w. Potrzebujemy zatem ambitnego Critical Medicines Act, kt脙鲁ry jak najszerzej b脛聶dzie korzysta脛聡 z propozycji Sojuszu na Lek脙鲁w Krytycznych.

Istnieje pewien poziom produkcji lek脙鲁w, od kt脙鲁rego zale脜录y zdrowie i 脜录ycie Europejczyk脙鲁w, kt脙鲁ry wyznacza granice pomi脛聶dzy prostym biznesem a bezpiecze脜聞stwem. Potrzebujemy funduszu lek脙鲁w krytycznych, kt脙鲁ry pozwoli zabezpieczy脛聡 ten poziom w Unii, zasilanego na przyk脜聜ad z uelastycznienia niewykorzystanych 脜聸rodk脙鲁w z polityki sp脙鲁jno脜聸ci lub z procentowego udzia脜聜u w zliberalizowanej krajowej pomocy publicznej pa脜聞stw Unii, o co apelowa脜聜 w swoim sprawozdaniu Enrico Letta. Taki fundusz wzmacnia脜聜by europejsk脛聟 autonomi脛聶 i odporno脜聸脛聡 na przysz脜聜e kryzysy, wojny handlowe i r脙鲁wnowa脜录y脜聜 zaburzenie jednolitego rynku. W pe脜聜ni popieram ide脛聶 aktu o lekach krytycznych.

脗聽脗聽Tiemo W脙露lken (S&D). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Arzneimittelengp脙陇sse k脙露nnen f脙录r Patientinnen und Patienten zu einer Frage von Leben und Tod werden; deshalb ist es h脙露chste Zeit, dass wir dieses Problem angehen. Zwar ver脙露ffentlicht die Kommission schon lange eine Liste der kritischen Medikamente, aber wir haben bisher noch keinen Gesetzgebungsvorschlag gehabt. Wir als Parlament haben in der Pharma芒聙聭Verordnung und auch in der Richtlinie脗聽schon versucht, erste Schritte zu gehen, aber es ist h脙露chste Zeit, dass wir dieses dramatische Problem endlich vollumf脙陇nglich anpassen; deswegen k脙露nnen und wollen wir auch Engp脙陇sse nicht mehr hinnehmen.

Wir brauchen mehr Produktionskapazit脙陇ten in der Europ脙陇ischen Union. Wir brauchen eine ausfallsichere Lieferkette, das hei脙聼t, nicht nur ein Hersteller darf ein Produkt liefern. Und wir m脙录ssen die Abh脙陇ngigkeit von Drittstaaten drastisch reduzieren. Daf脙录r brauchen wir gezielte Investitionen, wir brauchen strategische Unabh脙陇ngigkeit. Es ist gut, dass die Kommission die MEAT-Kriterien endlich wahrnimmt und ernst nimmt und damit nicht nur den Preis als entscheidendes Kriterium hat. Als Sozialdemokratinnen und Sozialdemokraten haben wir das schon lange gefordert, aber die Wahrheit ist auch: Wir werden daf脙录r Geld brauchen, und daran m脙录ssen wir jetzt gemeinsam arbeiten.

(Der Redner ist damit einverstanden, auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹 zu antworten.)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tomislav Sokol (PPE), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 I'm glad that you support the overall concept and the direction of the Critical Medicines Act, the same as myself, but there is one thing that I think is really missing and that is the provisions on stockpiling.

We remember from the last big crisis that we had a problem that some big Member States essentially kept everything that they could for themselves at the time where some other Member States had much bigger needs and where these medical countermeasures were more necessary there.

So, my question is: would you support that we do whatever we can to add to this Critical Medicines Act also provisions of common stockpiling and more coordination so that we make sure that patients actually get those medicines, which they need in times of crisis?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tiemo W脙露lken (S&D), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 Stockpiling is an essential part, yes, I totally agree and I'm happy that it is also part of the MEAT criteria.

I agree that in some Member States, medicine is more accessible than in others, and this is something we shouldn't accept. We should work together to have a European Union, a true European health Union, where every European citizen has equal access to medicines.

I'm really sure that we as a Parliament together can work on this commonly shared aim.

脗聽脗聽Gerald Hauser (PfE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, sehr geehrter Herr Kommissar! Bei dem Ziel, gen脙录gend kritische Arzneimittel und 脙录berhaupt Arzneimittel f脙录r die europ脙陇ische Bev脙露lkerung zu haben, wird Sie nat脙录rlich jeder unterst脙录tzen. Aber der erste Schritt脗聽芒聙聯 das wurde schon angesprochen脗聽芒聙聯 dazu ist, dass man sich einmal bem脙录ht, die Wirkstoffe, die zu 80脗聽% von China und von Indien produziert werden, auch in die Produktion nach Europa r脙录ckzuverlagern脗聽芒聙聯 脙录ber das m脙录sste dann wirklich separat gesprochen werden.

Aber die Frage, die sich mir stellt, ist: Wieso hat man 脙录berhaupt diese Allianz gr脙录nden m脙录ssen? Wir haben ja bereits gen脙录gend Gesundheitsbeh脙露rden in der Europ脙陇ischen Union脗聽芒聙聯 neben der Kommission gibt es die EMA, wir haben die Europ脙陇ische Gesundheitsbeh脙露rde, wir haben die HERA. Also wieso gr脙录ndet man eine eigene Beh脙露rde? Und das wird mir schon eine Spur verd脙陇chtig. M脙露chte man da wieder intransparent arbeiten, weil diese Beh脙露rde ja nur beratend t脙陇tig ist? Und da entsteht dann immer wieder das Problem, dass man sagt, 芒聙聻na, wir sind eh nur beratend芒聙聹, wissend, dass es hier um ein Milliardengesch脙陇ft geht.

Und deswegen ist es wichtig, Herr Kommissar, dass man hier die Transparenz voll und ganz walten l脙陇sst und dass es nicht so wie bei der Beschaffung der COVID-19-Impfstoffe ist脗聽芒聙聯 vollkommen intransparent脗聽芒聙聯 und man die Antwort bekommt: 芒聙聻Die Vertraulichkeit muss gewahrt werden.芒聙聹 Deswegen 芒聙娄

脗聽脗聽Aurelijus Veryga (ECR). 芒聙聯 Pirmininke, komisare, d脛聴koju u脜戮 iniciatyv脛聟 spr脛聶sti kritin脛聴s svarbos vaist脜鲁 tr脜芦kumo problem脛聟.

脗聽脗聽Olivier Chastel (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, l芒聙聶Europe ne peut plus rester spectatrice de sa propre vuln脙漏rabilit脙漏. Nous nous souvenons tous des p脙漏nuries qui ont frapp脙漏 notre continent ces derni脙篓res ann脙漏es: amoxicilline, traitements contre le cancer, parac脙漏tamol, anesth脙漏siants芒聙娄 Autant de m脙漏dicaments essentiels qui ont cruellement manqu脙漏, dans nos h脙麓pitaux comme dans nos pharmacies, ce qui a mis des vies en danger. Ces ruptures de stock n芒聙聶ont pas touch脙漏 que des traitements rares; elles ont aussi concern脙漏 des m脙漏dicaments de premi脙篓re n脙漏cessit脙漏, dont la disponibilit脙漏 aurait d脙禄 脙陋tre une 脙漏vidence.

Depuis trop longtemps, nous avons laiss脙漏 nos m脙漏dicaments critiques devenir les otages des d脙漏localisations et des tensions g脙漏opolitiques. D脙漏pendre de quelques usines 脙聽 l芒聙聶autre bout du monde, c芒聙聶est accepter l芒聙聶impuissance face 脙聽 l芒聙聶urgence. L芒聙聶acte l脙漏gislatif sur les m脙漏dicaments critiques doit donc b脙垄tir une v脙漏ritable souverainet脙漏 pharmaceutique europ脙漏enne. Cela passera par une l脙漏gislation ambitieuse: relocalisation strat脙漏gique des productions, 脙漏tablissement de stocks de s脙漏curit脙漏 et coordination renforc脙漏e entre les 脙聣tats membres.

Il ne s芒聙聶agit pas seulement d芒聙聶un enjeu industriel; il s芒聙聶agit 脙漏galement d芒聙聶un imp脙漏ratif de sant脙漏 publique.

脗聽脗聽Ignazio Roberto Marino (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, signor Commissario, onorevoli colleghi, discutiamo di come condividere fucili e cannoni, strumenti di morte. Ma gli strumenti di vita? Che valore ha un'Unione europea se un bambino in uno dei nostri Paesi pu脙虏 accedere a un farmaco salvavita e in un altro no? Se la nostra salute dipende dal mercato, dagli interessi, dai confini?

Abbiamo visto cosa significa essere impreparati, lo abbiamo visto col Covid: mancavano le mascherine, i medicinali essenziali, i respiratori. Oggi in alcuni ospedali continuano a mancare farmaci oncologici, farmaci salvavita. L'Europa, culla della sanit脙聽 pubblica, non pu脙虏 diventare il continente delle disuguaglianze sanitarie.

Il Critical Medicines Act 脙篓 un passo ma non basta. Dobbiamo trasformarlo in realt脙聽, perch脙漏 milioni di cittadini aspettano risposte e molti, nell'attesa, muoiono, signor Commissario.

La salute non 脙篓 un privilegio: 脙篓 un diritto; un diritto che siamo chiamati a proteggere adesso, ora, qui! Dobbiamo ambire all'indipendenza strategica, non solo sulle armi, ma anche sui farmaci.

Mindannyian eml脙漏ksz脙录nk a j脙隆rv脙隆ny legs脙露t脙漏tebb pillanataira, amikor a tag脙隆llamaink 脙漏s azok k脙鲁rh脙隆zai k脙漏ts脙漏gbeesetten pr脙鲁b脙隆ltak gy脙鲁gyszerekhez jutni, 脙漏s az ell脙隆t脙隆si l脙隆ncok hi脙隆nyoss脙隆gai miatt emberek haltak meg. A t脙露redezett szab脙隆lyoz脙隆si k脙露rnyezettel, orsz脙隆gokon 脙隆tny脙潞l脙鲁 strat脙漏gi脙隆k n脙漏lk脙录l egy kr脙颅zis idej脙漏n 脙漏rt脙漏kes id脜聭t vesz脙颅t脙录nk, mik脙露zben a tag脙隆llamok egym脙隆ssal versengenek. Mindannyiunk sz脙隆m脙隆ra vil脙隆gos, hogy 脙露sszefog脙隆sra van sz脙录ks脙漏g az 脙漏letment脜聭 脙漏s kritikus fontoss脙隆g脙潞 gy脙鲁gyszerek biztos脙颅t脙隆sa 脙漏s igazs脙隆gos eloszt脙隆sa 脙漏rdek脙漏ben. Az Uni脙鲁nak kez脙漏be kell vennie az ir脙隆ny脙颅t脙隆st, hogy el脙漏rhet脜聭 脙漏s megfizethet脜聭 gy脙鲁gyszereket biztos脙颅tson az 脙隆llampolg脙隆raink r脙漏sz脙漏re.

Ehhez elengedhetetlen, hogy 脙露sszehangoljuk a szab脙隆lyoz脙隆st, 脙露szt脙露nz脜聭 k脙露rnyezetet teremts脙录nk az innov脙隆ci脙鲁nak, bele脙漏rtve az antibiotikum-kutat脙隆st. Meg kell 脜聭rizn脙录nk 脙漏s meg kell er脜聭s脙颅ten脙录nk az Eur脙鲁p脙隆n bel脙录li gy脙鲁gyszergy脙隆rt脙隆st, hogy cs脙露kkents脙录k a kiszolg脙隆ltatotts脙隆gunkat. A kritikus fontoss脙隆g脙潞 gy脙鲁gyszerek eset脙漏n egy k脙露z脙露s k脙漏szenl脙漏ti tartal脙漏kot kell l脙漏trehoznunk, amely biztos脙颅tja, hogy a tag脙隆llamokban ne forduljanak el脜聭 脙隆tmeneti gy脙鲁gyszerhi脙隆nyok. 脙聣s k脙录l脙露n脙露s figyelmet kell ford脙颅tanunk az 脙隆rva gy脙鲁gyszerekre 脙漏s a ritka betegs脙漏gek kezel脙漏s脙漏re. Ehhez 脙露sszehangolt fell脙漏p脙漏sre 脙漏s tag脙隆llamokon 脙隆tny脙潞l脙鲁 finansz脙颅roz脙隆si programokra van sz脙录ks脙漏g. A Critical Medicines Act garant脙隆lja ezeket, 脙漏s biztos脙颅tja, hogy Eur脙鲁p脙隆ban t脙露bb脙漏 ne forduljanak el脜聭 a pand脙漏mia alatt tapasztalt gy脙鲁gyszerhi脙隆nyok. Mert a nap v脙漏g脙漏n egyetlenegy dolog sz脙隆m脙颅t, hogy rendelkez脙漏sre 脙隆ll a gy脙鲁gyszer, amivel meg tudjuk menteni a beteg 脙漏let脙漏t, vagy nem.

脗聽脗聽Nicol脙隆s Gonz脙隆lez Casares (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario, por fin tenemos esta Ley de Medicamentos Esenciales, que es un paso m脙隆s en esa Uni脙鲁n Europea de la Salud que queremos construir y que construimos en gran medida a trav脙漏s de los efectos de la COVID芒聙聭19 y de los pasos que dimos en el pasado, por ejemplo con una lista de medicamentos esenciales o con una plataforma de seguimiento de la escasez de f脙隆rmacos.

Pero ahora vemos claramente que la escasez de f脙隆rmacos es tambi脙漏n uno de los talones de Aquiles de la seguridad en Europa. Tanto que hablamos de defensa, recordemos que la seguridad es un concepto m脙隆s amplio y que tiene que ver tambi脙漏n con el suministro de f脙隆rmacos para actuar ante las enfermedades de los europeos y europeas. No lo olvidemos, porque la seguridad de las cadenas de suministro no puede quedar solamente en manos de las ayudas de Estado o de la simplificaci脙鲁n administrativa. Hacen falta fondos europeos para fomentar esta autonom脙颅a estrat脙漏gica y creo que falta ambici脙鲁n en la propuesta. Esperamos mejorarla en este Parlamento, pero, desde luego, esos medicamentos esenciales, adem脙隆s, deben ser fabricados en Europa en la medida de lo posible.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie-Luce Brasier-Clain (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, notre syst脙篓me de sant脙漏 est en crise, nos h脙麓pitaux et nos pharmacies manquent de m脙漏dicaments. Des d脙漏cennies de d脙漏sindustrialisation et de d脙漏pendance aux importations, notamment depuis la Chine et l芒聙聶Inde: voil脙聽 o脙鹿 nous m脙篓nent la libre concurrence et la recherche du moindre co脙禄t. L芒聙聶acte l脙漏gislatif sur les m脙漏dicaments critiques repose sur une approche technocratique et centralis脙漏e. Mutualiser les stocks 脙聽 l芒聙聶脙漏chelle europ脙漏enne ne garantit en rien le bon fonctionnement de nos syst脙篓mes de sant脙漏, dont la gestion reste nationale.

Sans r脙漏industrialisation, pas de production. Elle passe par la priorit脙漏 donn脙漏e 脙聽 nos entreprises dans les march脙漏s publics, par la relocalisation, chaque fois qu芒聙聶elle est possible, de la production de m脙漏dicaments et de principes actifs et par la protection de nos int脙漏r脙陋ts strat脙漏giques contre les pratiques commerciales d脙漏loyales et la pr脙漏dation 脙漏conomique. Nous voulons relancer la production en Europe et r脙漏duire nos d脙漏pendances. Comment garantir qu芒聙聶une gestion centralis脙漏e respectera la souverainet脙漏 des 脙聣tats? La Commission assumera-t-elle un virage protectionniste, quitte 脙聽 revenir sur certaines r脙篓gles du commerce international? Le d脙漏fi est de taille.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Laurence Trochu (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, mes chers coll脙篓gues, l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne, nous avait-on promis, nous assurera comp脙漏titivit脙漏, bien-脙陋tre et r脙漏silience. Pourtant, nous voil脙聽 oblig脙漏s aujourd芒聙聶hui de traiter du sujet central des m脙漏dicaments, en commen脙搂ant par les plus critiques. Serions-nous devenus un continent du tiers-monde?

Puisqu芒聙聶il faut r脙漏pondre 脙聽 ce probl脙篓me, faisons-le en essayant, pour une fois, de ne pas r脙漏pondre aux erreurs du pass脙漏 par une fuite en avant. Il nous faut r脙漏duire notre d脙漏pendance aux pays asiatiques et d脙漏velopper notre propre industrie pharmaceutique 脙聽 l芒聙聶脙漏chelle europ脙漏enne, mais pas au prix de la souverainet脙漏 des nations 芒聙聯脗聽sacrifi脙漏e par l芒聙聶acte l脙漏gislatif sur les m脙漏dicaments critiques, qui pr脙漏cise que les stocks d芒聙聶un 脙聣tat membre ne doivent pas pouvoir porter pr脙漏judice 脙聽 un autre脗聽芒聙聯 ni au prix de la transparence 芒聙聯脗聽qui a tant fait d脙漏faut lors de l芒聙聶脙漏pid脙漏mie de COVID-19, ce qui a provoqu脙漏 une d脙漏fiance g脙漏n脙漏ralis脙漏e des Europ脙漏ens vis-脙聽-vis de leurs dirigeants sur ces sujets.

Rien dans l芒聙聶acte l脙漏gislatif sur les m脙漏dicaments critiques ne cherche 脙聽 y rem脙漏dier de mani脙篓re concr脙篓te. La protection de la sant脙漏 des Europ脙漏ens doit, elle aussi, se d脙漏barrasser des id脙漏ologies, accepter l芒聙聶existence d芒聙聶int脙漏r脙陋ts divergents au sein de l芒聙聶Union et laisser aux nations europ脙漏ennes le monopole de leurs comp脙漏tences propres.

(L'oratrice refuse de r脙漏pondre 脙聽 une question carton bleu de Sieper.)

脗聽脗聽Veronika Cifrov脙隆 Ostriho脜聢ov脙隆 (Renew). 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pani predsedaj脙潞ca, v脙隆脜戮en脙陆 komis脙隆r, mil脙颅 kolegovia, sila a konkurencieschopnos脜楼 Eur脙鲁py nespo脛聧脙颅vaj脙潞 len v nav脙陆脜隆en脙颅 obrany, ale aj v posilnen脙颅 zdravotnej starostlivosti na脜隆ich ob脛聧anov a ob脛聧ianok. Na Slovensku sa 脛戮udia v zdrav脙颅 do脜戮脙颅vaj脙潞 v priemere 57 rokov. V najlep脜隆ej krajine 脙職nie je to a脜戮 70. Tento rozdiel nie je len 脜隆tatistika. Je to 13 rokov plnohodnotn脙漏ho 脜戮ivota s rodinou, s de脜楼mi, s vnukmi a vnu脛聧kami. Je to 13 rokov v plnom zdrav脙颅, ktor脙漏 n脙隆m ch脙陆baj脙潞.

Jedn脙陆m zo sp脙麓sobov, ako tieto nerovnosti vyrovna脜楼, je spolo脛聧n脙陆 eur脙鲁psky pr脙颅stup k r脙陆chlej脜隆ej dostupnosti liekov. Pand脙漏mia COVID-19 n脙隆m uk脙隆zala, ako r脙陆chlo a ne脛聧akane m脙麓脜戮eme 脛聧eli脜楼 kritick脙漏mu nedostatku liekov, a nem脙麓脜戮eme si dovoli脜楼 by脜楼 z脙隆visl脙颅 od dod脙隆vok z tret脙颅ch kraj脙颅n, ako je 脛聦脙颅na 脛聧i India. Mus脙颅me investova脜楼 do eur脙鲁pskych kapac脙颅t na v脙陆robu liekov, podpori脜楼 v脙陆skum a zabezpe脛聧i脜楼 dostupnos脜楼 liekov pre v脜隆etk脙陆ch. To v脜隆ak zvl脙隆dneme len spolo脛聧ne. Obrana, 脙隆no, konkurencieschopnos脜楼, 脙隆no, no rovnak脙漏 脙隆no plat脙颅 pre kvalitn脙潞 zdravotn脙潞 starostlivos脜楼 pre v脜隆etk脙陆ch ob脛聧anov a ob脛聧ianky Eur脙鲁py.

脗聽脗聽Oliver Schenk (PPE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, meine Damen und Herren, Herr Kommissar! Die Liste kritischer Arzneimittel ist mittlerweile 脙录ber 270脗聽Wirkstoffe und Substanzen lang, darunter einfache Hustens脙陇fte f脙录r Kinder, und macht die Dringlichkeit f脙录r den Critical Medicines Act deutlich. Globalisierung und starker Kostendruck haben zu einer Abwanderung der Produktion und zu einer Konzentration auf wenige Hersteller in China und Indien gef脙录hrt.

Aber Lieferengp脙陇sse sind vermeidbar. Sie sind das Ergebnis von politischen Rahmenbedingungen, die die nationale und europ脙陇ische Politik setzen. Wir k脙露nnen diese Rahmenbedingungen 脙陇ndern; der CMA wird dazu einen Beitrag leisten. Wir k脙露nnen die Abh脙陇ngigkeiten reduzieren und stabile Lieferketten schaffen, wenn wir sie f脙录r uns f脙录r eine starke, nachhaltige und international wettbewerbsf脙陇hige Pharmaindustrie in Europa einsetzen. Die aktuellen Engp脙陇sse sind ein wake芒聙聭up call. Europa braucht eine starke Pharmaindustrie. Dazu m脙录ssen wir Industrie芒聙聭, Forschungs芒聙聭 und Gesundheitspolitik zusammen denken, die gesamte Wertsch脙露pfungskette in den Blick nehmen und die richtigen Signale an die Unternehmen aussenden.

Wir m脙录ssen bei kritischen Wirkstoffen und Antibiotika, von denen heute zwei Drittel in Asien produziert werden, Anreize f脙录r eine eigene Produktion in Europa schaffen. Daf脙录r brauchen wir finanzielle Anreize, und wir brauchen schnellere Genehmigungsverfahren, die unternehmerisches Engagement honorieren. Meine Erwartung an den CMA ist deshalb ganz klar: Er muss einen Beitrag zur besseren Verf脙录gbarkeit von Arzneimitteln durch Anreize f脙录r mehr Produktion, stabilere Lieferketten und mehr Solidarit脙陇t untereinander liefern.

脗聽脗聽Nikos Papandreou (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, the Critical Medicines Act is our answer to shortages and supplies and less dependence on China and India. Very simple to say that.

What medicines are we talking about? Basic ones like penicillin, amoxicillin, but even infusions for chemotherapy.

The task will not be easy. We have many instruments at our disposal. We have the new pharma legislation. We have our effort on competition, AI effort and innovation. We're trying the Capital Markets Union. We want to industrialise. I don't even know if it's bringing back pharma to Europe. It's creating new pharma in Europe. It's not only reshoring. We are not going to use tariffs. We are going to produce it in our own country. We'll succeed on some. We won't succeed on others. That's how it works.

But I think today we heard that everybody agrees with the CMA with its own criticisms. So I think we're on a good road. Congratulations for bringing this in time. And we're all here to work with you for the health of European citizens.

Ennek okai a geopolitikai t脙漏nyez脜聭k, mint p脙漏ld脙隆ul az orosz-ukr脙隆n h脙隆bor脙潞 vagy a koronav脙颅rus j脙隆rv脙隆ny, valamint a termel脙漏s 脙漏s az alapanyagok beszerz脙漏s脙漏nek t脙潞lzott koncentr脙隆ci脙鲁ja 脙聛zsi脙隆ban. A Kritikus Gy脙鲁gyszerek Sz脙露vets脙漏ge keret脙漏n bel脙录l az 脙漏rdekelt felek kifejezhett脙漏k v脙漏lem脙漏ny脙录ket 脙漏s k脙颅v脙隆ns脙隆gaikat. 脙聹dv脙露z脙露lj脙录k, hogy a Bizotts脙隆g lefolytatta ezt a p脙隆rbesz脙漏det, 脙漏s javaslata reflekt脙隆l a strat脙漏giai jelent脙漏sre.

A betegek helyzet脙漏nek jav脙颅t脙隆sa 脙漏rdek脙漏ben kiemelten fontos a gy脙鲁gyszerek el脙漏rhet脜聭s脙漏ge 脙漏s megfizethet脜聭s脙漏ge, mik脙露zben biztos脙颅tjuk az eur脙鲁pai gy脙鲁gyszeripar versenyk脙漏pess脙漏g脙漏t 脙漏s rezilienci脙隆j脙隆t. Fontos hangs脙潞lyozni, hogy a Critical Medicines Act ne n脙露velje az adminisztrat脙颅v terheket, 脙漏s ne legyen jogi 脙隆tfed脙漏s, p脙漏ld脙隆ul a gy脙鲁gyszercsomaghi脙隆nnyal foglalkoz脙鲁 r脙漏sz脙漏vel. 脙聹dv脙露zl脙露m a bemutatott javaslatot, 脙漏s k脙露sz脙露n脙露m V脙隆rhelyi biztos 脙潞rnak 脙漏s csapat脙隆nak, hogy ilyen gyorsan elk脙漏sz脙颅tett脙漏k azt.

脗聽脗聽Michele Picaro (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, fino a quando possiamo tollerare le carenze di farmaci essenziali in Europa? Colleghi, questa crisi 脙篓 un problema strutturale che mette a rischio la salute dei nostri cittadini.

Il nostro obiettivo 脙篓 un'Europa indipendente nella produzione di farmaci, perch脙漏 la salute non pu脙虏 dipendere da attori esterni. 脙聢 una questione di sicurezza e sovranit脙聽 per tutti gli Stati membri: senza un'industria forte e autonoma restiamo esposti a vulnerabilit脙聽 inaccettabili.

Per questo motivo vigileremo affinch脙漏 il Critical Medicines Act persegua due obiettivi fondamentali strategici: il primo 脙篓 evitare nuove sovrastrutture burocratiche e valorizzare gli strumenti di monitoraggio e risposta gi脙聽 esistenti; il secondo 脙篓 rafforzare l'autonomia strategica dell'Europa, garantendo sicurezza sanitaria e leadership globale nel settore, sia nella produzione di farmaci essenziali che nello sviluppo delle innovazioni future.

E per riuscirci abbiamo bisogno di strumenti concreti, incentivi alla propriet脙聽 intellettuale, norme sugli appalti pubblici che premino l'innovazione e procedure di approvazione pi脙鹿 rapide per i farmaci innovativi.

脗聽脗聽Elena Nevado del Campo (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or脙颅as, se脙卤or comisario, yo creo que el mensaje es claro y es casi un脙隆nime: Europa tiene que ser autosuficiente y, con esta ley que se presenta hoy, la Comisi脙鲁n debe asegurar que el acceso de los ciudadanos a los medicamentos esenciales est脙隆 garantizado.

La escasez de medicamentos esenciales se ha convertido en un desaf脙颅o en la Uni脙鲁n Europea. Las cadenas de suministro fr脙隆giles y dependientes suponen una vulnerabilidad para nuestros sistemas de salud y nuestros pacientes. Es esencial fortalecer la innovaci脙鲁n, la producci脙鲁n y la distribuci脙鲁n dentro de la Uni脙鲁n Europea, como se est脙隆 haciendo, por ejemplo, en mi tierra, Extremadura, donde empresas como Natac est脙隆n innovando en el sector vegetal y est脙隆n ayudando en la fabricaci脙鲁n de medicamentos hu脙漏rfanos脗聽como los que tratan la ataxia de Friedreich.

La Ley de Medicamentos Esenciales es muy necesaria. Sabemos qu脙漏 hay que hacer; d脙颅ganos, se脙卤or comisario, cu脙隆ndo y c脙鲁mo, porque se necesita ya, no le quepa la menor duda.

脗聽脗聽Marta Temido (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, a proposta de lei dos medicamentos cr脙颅ticos 脙漏 uma pe脙搂a essencial da arquitetura da nossa seguran脙搂a e defesa comuns, como aprendemos durante a pandemia da COVID芒聙聭19.

Conhecemos, nos nossos pa脙颅ses, a experi脙陋ncia da rutura de medicamentos vitais (como anest脙漏sicos, antibi脙鲁ticos, mesmo insulina), como conhecemos o diagn脙鲁stico das nossas fragilidades: depend脙陋ncia de menos de um punhado de origens geogr脙隆ficas em mat脙漏rias芒聙聭primas e eros脙拢o da base industrial europeia.

A nossa a脙搂脙拢o 脙漏 urgente porque os resultados exigem tempo, e as respostas legislativas s脙拢o mesmo a parte mais f脙隆cil daquilo que precisamos de fazer. Sim, precisamos de uma lista de medicamentos cr脙颅ticos, de stocks de conting脙陋ncia, de novas pr脙隆ticas de aprovisionamento p脙潞blico. Precisamos mesmo de investir no fabrico europeu.

Mas n脙拢o basta. Precisamos de reconhecer que apenas com uma coordena脙搂脙拢o e investimento europeus comuns poderemos ser bem芒聙聭sucedidos nesta estrat脙漏gia, e que a disponibilidade de medicamentos no mercado n脙拢o serve os cidad脙拢os europeus, se o acesso, sob o ponto de vista da comportabilidade financeira, estiver em causa.

Sim, precisamos tamb脙漏m de falar do pre脙搂o dos medicamentos, e estes s脙拢o aspetos dos quais n脙拢o vamos desistir.

脗聽脗聽Liesbet Sommen (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, elke dag opnieuw zien wij dat de wereldorde verschuift en dat internationale samenwerking steeds moeilijker wordt. Als EU zullen wij voor strategische sectoren sterk moeten zijn en onze eigen boontjes moeten doppen. Zo ook voor de farmaceutische industrie. Want elke dag opnieuw ervaren zorgverleners en pati脙芦nten tekorten aan geneesmiddelen. Dat is een groot probleem.

De geopolitieke instabiliteit, gecombineerd met onze afhankelijkheid van China en India op het vlak van geneesmiddelen, maakt ons kwetsbaar. Zeker in tijden van hybride oorlogsvoering. Want 脙漏脙漏n geopolitiek evenement, 脙漏脙漏n zotte beslissing van een bepaalde beleidsmaker of 脙漏脙漏n probleem in een fabriek hier ver vandaan kan ons in een ongeziene gezondheidscrisis storten. Dat is een groot probleem. Geneesmiddelentekorten kunnen mensenlevens kosten. Reden te meer om dit in Europa samen aan te pakken met de volle impact van onze interne markt.

Catch-the-eye procedure

脗聽脗聽S脙漏rgio Humberto (PPE). 芒聙聯 Cara Presidente, Caro Comiss脙隆rio, caros colegas, numa Uni脙拢o Europeia com sistemas de sa脙潞de diversificados, o Critical Medicines Act permite uma resposta mais eficiente a emerg脙陋ncias sanit脙隆rias e, por isso, os meus parab脙漏ns.

Vivemos num tempo em que o acesso a medicamentos essenciais 脙漏 mais urgente do que nunca e n脙拢o podemos, como j脙隆 disseram os meus colegas, estar dependentes de pa脙颅ses como a China ou a 脙聧ndia, que fornecem entre 60脗聽% a 80脗聽% das subst脙垄ncias ativas utilizadas na produ脙搂脙拢o de medicamentos na Europa.脗聽Esta depend脙陋ncia exp脙碌e-nos a riscos significativos, como vimos no caso da pandemia da COVID芒聙聭19, quando as interrup脙搂脙碌es nas cadeias de abastecimento resultaram em faltas de medicamentos essenciais.

O Critical Medicines Act tem um papel crucial para garantir que todos, mesmo todos, tenham acesso a medicamentos essenciais, reduzindo a nossa vulnerabilidade e refor脙搂ando a nossa autonomia, sa脙潞de p脙潞blica e seguran脙搂a.

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, l脙陇脙陇kkeiden saatavuus kriiseiss脙陇 on j脙陇senvaltioiden elinehto. Sen on oltava varmaa 芒聙聯 ei toivotaan toivotaan -hommaa. EU puskee parhaillaan l脙陇脙陇kkeiden toimitusketjuja paremmiksi, mutta tekemist脙陇 viel脙陇 riitt脙陇脙陇.

Korona-ajan alku paljasti heikkoutemme. Tarvittavat v脙陇lineet olivat Kiinassa ja eurooppalaiset paniikissa. Sama ei saa en脙陇脙陇 toistua.

Kuten moneen muuhunkin asiaan, eurooppalaiset tehtaat ovat vastaus t脙陇h脙陇nkin. Vahva ja itsen脙陇inen l脙陇脙陇keteollisuus, joka ei kuikuile it脙陇脙陇n taikka l脙陇nteen. Euroopan ei pid脙陇 olla maanosa, joka j脙陇脙陇 aina pahan paikan tullen odottelemaan apua. Se, jos mik脙陇, on heikkoutta.

Ja muistakaa, juuri nyt on tekojen hetki. Jos toimet aloitetaan seuraavan kriisin vy脙露ryess脙陇 p脙陇脙陇lle, silloin on jo my脙露h脙陇ist脙陇.

脗聽脗聽Raquel Garc脙颅a Hermida-Van Der Walle (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, in 2023, when the United States reversed Roe v Wade, it had a direct effect on the accessibility of abortion medication in the EU, because American states 芒聙聯 fearing further restrictions on abortion medication 芒聙聯 stocked up. And that led to shortages, for example in France.

Now, the worldwide backlash against abortion, in combination with increasingly restrictive US policies, could lead to stockpiling again and shortages. And that is why I urge this Commissioner to aim to include mifepristone and misoprostol on the list of essential medications.

As the Commissioner himself said, it's about providing critical medication there and where needed. And if we don't do this, we really risk a lot of women conducting unsafe, not legal or illegal abortions at home, especially in those Member States where this right is severely restricted.

脗聽脗聽Marc Botenga (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, 脙漏coutez, c芒聙聶est en effet hallucinant de s芒聙聶imaginer que, sur un continent aussi riche que le n脙麓tre, les m脙漏dicaments les plus essentiels, du parac脙漏tamol 脙聽 l芒聙聶amoxicilline, manquent, et que l芒聙聶ann脙漏e pass脙漏e, dans mon pays, la Belgique, 1脗聽famille sur脗聽3 a 脙漏t脙漏 confront脙漏e 脙聽 de telles p脙漏nuries et脗聽n芒聙聶a pu avoir acc脙篓s 脙聽 certains m脙漏dicaments parce qu芒聙聶ils 脙漏taient indisponibles.

Ce n芒聙聶est pas tellement la faute des Indiens ou des Chinois, non: c芒聙聶est la faute des Europ脙漏ens. Pourquoi? Parce que nous sommes d脙漏pendants en premier lieu des choix que font les grandes multinationales pharmaceutiques. Ce sont elles qui d脙漏cident quoi produire, o脙鹿 produire, combien produire et 脙聽 quel prix vendre,脗聽m脙陋me lorsqu芒聙聶elles ont re脙搂u des subventions publiques 脙聽 la recherche ou 脙聽 la production.

Ce qu芒聙聶il faut casser aujourd芒聙聶hui, c芒聙聶est le monopole qu芒聙聶ont ces entreprises, et pour ce faire les ONG, les associations et m脙陋me les syndicats nous ont d脙漏j脙聽 donn脙漏 la marche 脙聽 suivre: il s芒聙聶agit de cr脙漏er une infrastructure publique pour la recherche et le d脙漏veloppement en Europe. C芒聙聶est possible. Vous trouvez 800脗聽milliards pour les armes; vous allez bien trouver quelques milliards pour la sant脙漏. Qu芒聙聶est-ce que vous attendez?

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Menschen Europas, Herr Kommissar! Stellen Sie sich vor, jemand in Ihrer Familie braucht dringend ein Medikament, aber in Ihrem Heimatland gibt es das gerade nicht, w脙陇hrend es im Nachbarland in rauen Mengen vorhanden ist. Absurd, leider aber bittere europ脙陇ische Realit脙陇t. Und ironischerweise erlaubt das EU-Recht genau das: Alle vier Grundfreiheiten des Binnenmarkts kennen Ausnahmen aus Gr脙录nden der 脙露ffentlichen Gesundheit.

Diese Regelungen aus dem letzten Jahrhundert geh脙露ren abgeschafft,脗聽denn gerade bei lebenswichtigen Arzneimitteln ist die 脙露ffentliche Gesundheit keine nationale, sondern eine europ脙陇ische Frage. Den Critical Medicines Act begr脙录脙聼e ich ausdr脙录cklich, aber seien wir ehrlich: Wenn wir wirklich eine europaweite Versorgung sicherstellen wollen, dann m脙录ssen wir tiefer ansetzen und endlich die legislativen Wurzeln von Ausnahmen in den europ脙陇ischen Vertr脙陇gen selbst beseitigen.

Die Pandemie hat uns gezeigt: Krankheiten kennen keine Grenzen, und so sollten wir auch in Europa keine kennen. Denn Medikamente m脙录ssen dorthin, wo die Menschen sie brauchen脗聽芒聙聯 ganz ohne Grenzen.

脗聽脗聽Peter Agius (PPE). 芒聙聯 Napprezza li l-Kummissjoni 脜录ammet kelmitha u ning脛搂aqad mas-sens ta' ur脛隆enza biex a脛搂na na脛搂dmu fuq li脛隆i Ewropea dwar id-disponibilit脙聽 tal-medi脛聥ini. Jiena 脛隆ej minn Malta, g脛搂alina l-joint procurement li sar fil-COVID kien essenzjali u rridu narawh isir aktar sistematikament. Irridu nindirizzaw l-istockpiling. Bla dubju. Imma脛隆inaw, ta' spiss f'Malta medi脛聥ini out of stock, mentri l-istess medi脛聥ina jkollhom bil-qabda l-Italja u Franza u l-脛聽ermanja.

Ejja na脛搂dmu flimkien biex ikollna Suq Ewropew g脛搂all-Medi脛聥ini g脛搂all-Ewropej kollha.

脗聽脗聽Kate脜聶ina Kone脛聧n脙隆 (NI). 芒聙聯 Pan脙颅 p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, na z脙隆klad脛聸 toho, co jsme tady dnes sly脜隆eli, se ob脙隆v脙隆m velk脙漏 byrokratick脙漏 z脙隆t脛聸脜戮e, kter脙隆 u脛聧in脙颅 n脙隆vrh prost脛聸 nerealizovateln脙陆m.
