脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hilde Vautmans (Renew). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, het is een heel belangrijk voorstel waarover we hier vanmiddag gedebatteerd hebben. U heeft het zelf gezegd: het is eigenlijk het sluitstuk. Dit ontbrak nog. De nationale terugkeersystemen zijn te gefragmenteerd en u heeft het aangekaart: we moeten ervoor zorgen dat als iemand in een Europees land wordt uitgewezen, hij wel degelijk de Unie verlaat en het niet elders gaat proberen. Dus dat vind ik een absoluut noodzakelijke stap. Wij als liberalen zijn voorstander van een streng maar rechtvaardig asielbeleid en daar hoort snelle uitwijzing bij.

Maar het moet me toch van het hart: de onzin, de leugens, de stigmatisering van extreemrechts hier in dit halfrond tarten alle verbeelding. Ik ga vragen aan die zijde. 脙聣脙漏n: kijk eens in de spiegel. Als jullie geboren worden in een land in oorlog en chemisch gebombardeerd worden, ga je niet vluchten? En twee: u mag van geluk spreken dat u geboren bent in Europa, in een land in vrede.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脙聳zlem Demirel (The Left). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Herr Kommissar! Mit Ihrem populistischen Vorschlag f脙录r eine Abschieberichtlinie haben Sie heute eigentlich nur ein Signal gesendet: Die EU will abschieben脗聽芒聙聯 schneller, mehr, r脙录cksichtsloser. Und wenn Sie ganz ehrlich sind, haben Sie heute deutlich gemacht, dass Sie noch nicht einmal einen Plan haben, wie Sie es machen wollen.

Herr Kommissar, Sie werden Menschenrechte mit F脙录脙聼en treten mit Ihren Vorschl脙陇gen. Sie haben noch nicht mal eine Liste von Drittstaaten, die Sie uns vorschlagen wollen, aber Sie scheinen sehr zuversichtlich zu sein, dass Sie genug Autokraten in dieser Welt finden, die sich mit Ihnen auf diesen dreckigen Deal einlassen. Ich finde es wirklich bemerkenswert, Herr Kommissar, wie viele Rechtsau脙聼enparteien heute Ihnen die Hand gesch脙录ttelt haben bei dieser Debatte.

Herr Kommissar, wenn Sie wirklich L脙露sungen suchen wollen, auch f脙录r das Thema der Migration, dann tun Sie was daf脙录r, dass Menschen nicht mehr ihre Heimat verlassen m脙录ssen. Tun Sie was daf脙录r, dass die EU芒聙聭Staaten nicht mehr Waffen in alle Welt schicken. Tun Sie was gegen den Klimawandel, der zu einer neuen Fluchtursache wird. Tun Sie was dagegen, wie die multinationalen europ脙陇ischen Konzerne den globalen S脙录den auspl脙录ndern. Tun Sie was ...

(Die Pr脙陇sidentin entzieht der Rednerin das Wort.)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Thank you very much, colleagues. Just to be very transparent: there are a couple of groups who did not get speaking time, but that was because I had no indications of people wanting to take the floor during the catch-the-eye procedure.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Magnus Brunner, Mitglied der Kommission. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, sehr geehrte Damen und Herren! Frau Demirel, ich w脙录rde Ihnen nat脙录rlich auch gerne die Hand sch脙录tteln, wenn Sie ... Ich kann nachher gerne vorbeikommen, das hat mit dem 脙录berhaupt nichts zu tun. Aber gut. Und es ist auch wichtig und richtig, dass wir脗聽芒聙聯 das habe ich vorher versucht zu sagen脗聽芒聙聯 Menschenrechte nat脙录rlich auch entsprechend immer im Blick haben und die f脙录r uns nicht verhandelbar sind.

But let me change and swap to the whole discussion maybe we were facing this this afternoon. I listened, of course, very carefully to the discussion. Thank you to all the speakers of this afternoon.

For me, the clearest message is that the European Union needs a migration and asylum policy that is 100脗聽% credible. That is important, and that's what we try to deliver.

The pact has laid down a strong foundation and, of course, that has to be implemented now. That's the basis we have. But as a lot of you said, there was this missing piece we are talking about today.

I think EU citizens expect us to complete the whole picture and have this comprehensive approach. Also, this whole picture has to be completed. They want to know that the system operates efficiently; that is, I think, very important, so that we are able to properly integrate all migrants who do have a right to stay.

For migrants with no right to stay, they want them to be treated fairly, yes, but firmly, as you rightly mentioned. They want to know that dangerous individuals with no right to be in the European Union will not pose a threat, at the end of the day, for all of us. They are not anti-immigration, but they want migration that is legal and targeted at our labour market's needs also.

At this stage they are no longer asking us, actually. They are demanding that we that we act. It's up to us to listen carefully, to assume our responsibilities and also to finish building the common European migration system we promise. One hundred percent脗聽of it, actually. I'm looking very much forward to discussing all the topics in a constructive way in the next weeks to bring these rules into effect.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The debate is closed.

Written Statements (Rule 178)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mariusz Kami脜聞ski (ECR), na pi脜聸mie. 芒聙聯 Polityka migracyjna UE w ostatniej dekadzie to kompletna pora脜录ka. Stworzono dysfunkcyjny system, kt脙鲁ry zamiast s脜聜u脜录y脛聡 bezpiecze脜聞stwu obywateli, sta脜聜 si脛聶 narz脛聶dziem dla przemytnik脙鲁w i przest脛聶pc脙鲁w. W efekcie ponad p脙鲁脜聜tora miliona os脙鲁b przebywa w UE nielegalnie, a system deportacji to jeden wielki 脜录art. Europa che脜聜pi si脛聶 rz脛聟dami prawa, a tymczasem z p脙鲁脜聜 miliona wydanych decyzji powrotowych tylko co pi脛聟ta jest egzekwowana. Decyzje s脜聜u脜录b imigracyjnych musz脛聟 by脛聡 skutecznie egzekwowane, a nie ignorowane lub blokowane przez armie prawnik脙鲁w, kt脙鲁rzy zrobili biznes na obecnym chaotycznym systemie. To niedopuszczalne i niesie za sob脛聟 tragiczne konsekwencje. Osoby nielegalnie przebywaj脛聟ce na terytorium Unii musz脛聟 wyjecha脛聡. To nie jest radykalne stanowisko 芒聙聯 to po prostu prawo i zdrowy rozs脛聟dek. Panie Komisarzu, aby dyrektywa by脜聜a skuteczna, osoby odsy脜聜ane musz脛聟 by脛聡 przyjmowane przez kraje pochodzenia 芒聙聯 a w tej kwestii Unia wci脛聟脜录 ponosi pora脜录k脛聶. Umowy o readmisji powinny by脛聡 podstaw脛聟 wszelkiej wsp脙鲁脜聜pracy mi脛聶dzynarodowej. UE powinna tak脜录e rozwija脛聡 sie脛聡 centr脙鲁w powrotowych poza Uni脛聟, kt脙鲁re przyspiesz脛聟 deportacje. Potrzebujemy jasnych przepis脙鲁w, surowych zobowi脛聟za脜聞 i realnych konsekwencji dla tych, kt脙鲁rzy je 脜聜ami脛聟. Mam nadziej脛聶, 脜录e Komisja wyka脜录e si脛聶 determinacj脛聟 polityczn脛聟, by wypracowa脛聡 skuteczne narz脛聶dzia prawne i egzekwowa脛聡 je bez wyj脛聟tk脙鲁w. Moja grupa poprze tylko takie negocjacje nad dyrektyw脛聟, kt脙鲁re przywr脙鲁c脛聟 Europie prawo, porz脛聟dek i bezpiecze脜聞stwo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Piotr M脙录ller (ECR), na pi脜聸mie. 芒聙聯 Obecny kryzys migracyjny to efekt b脜聜脛聶d脙鲁w Komisji Europejskiej i Niemiec, kt脙鲁re w 2015 roku otworzy脜聜y granice, zapraszaj脛聟c setki tysi脛聶cy migrant脙鲁w. Skutek? Niekontrolowany nap脜聜yw ludzi, wzrost przest脛聶pczo脜聸ci, zamachy terrorystyczne.

Zamiast bezpiecze脜聞stwa 芒聙聯 chaos, kt脙鲁ry dotyka zwyk脜聜ych obywateli.

Polityka 芒聙聻otwartych granic芒聙聺 okaza脜聜a si脛聶 katastrofalnym b脜聜脛聶dem. Obci脛聟脜录one systemy socjalne, rosn脛聟ce napi脛聶cia spo脜聜eczne i brak kontroli nad tym, kto wje脜录d脜录a do Europy, to rzeczywisto脜聸脛聡, kt脙鲁rej nie da si脛聶 ignorowa脛聡.

Komisja Europejska zamiast rozwi脛聟zywa脛聡 problem, jedynie go pog脜聜脛聶bia, zmuszaj脛聟c kraje cz脜聜onkowskie do przyjmowania migrant脙鲁w bez wzgl脛聶du na ich mo脜录liwo脜聸ci i wol脛聶 spo脜聜ecze脜聞stw.

Dodatkowo, pakt migracyjny UE stanowi kolejny impuls dla masowej migracji, zach脛聶caj脛聟c kolejne fale ludzi do nielegalnego przekraczania granic.

Przymusowa relokacja i fa脜聜szywa solidarno脜聸脛聡 os脜聜abiaj脛聟 suwerenno脜聸脛聡 pa脜聞stw. Ka脜录de pa脜聞stwo powinno mie脛聡 prawo decydowa脛聡 o w脜聜asnej polityce migracyjnej, bez narzucanych przez Bruksel脛聶 regulacji!

Czas na zdecydowane kroki. Natychmiastowe wycofanie si脛聶 z paktu migracyjnego i wdro脜录enie programu przymusowych deportacji to jedyne skuteczne rozwi脛聟zanie.

Europa musi jasno okre脜聸li脛聡, 脜录e nielegalna migracja nie b脛聶dzie tolerowana, a jej granice b脛聶d脛聟 realnie chronione.

Europa potrzebuje ochrony swoich granic i bezpiecze脜聞stwa obywateli.

Czas dzia脜聜a脛聡 芒聙聯 zanim b脛聶dzie za p脙鲁脜潞no.

脨垄脨掳脨潞脨掳 脩聣脨碌 脨赂脨路脨驴脩聙脨掳脩聜脨赂脨录 脨潞脨掳脩聜脨碌脨鲁脨戮脩聙脨赂脩聡脨碌脨陆 脩聛脨赂脨鲁脨陆脨掳脨禄, 脩聡脨碌 脨陆脨碌脨路脨掳脨潞脨戮脨陆脨陆脨戮脩聜脨戮 脨驴脩聙脨碌脨卤脨赂脨虏脨掳脨虏脨掳脨陆脨碌 脨陆脩聫脨录脨掳 脨麓脨掳 脨卤脩聤脨麓脨碌 脩聜脨戮脨禄脨碌脩聙脨赂脩聙脨掳脨陆脨戮. 脨聺脨碌脩聣脨戮 脨驴脨戮脨虏脨碌脩聡脨碌 芒聙聯 脩聣脨碌 脨赂脨录脨掳 脩聛脨碌脩聙脨赂脨戮脨路脨陆脨赂 脨驴脨戮脩聛脨禄脨碌脨麓脨赂脩聠脨赂 脨路脨掳 脨虏脩聛脨赂脩聡脨潞脨赂, 脨潞脨戮脨赂脩聜脨戮 脨陆脨掳脩聙脩聝脩聢脨掳脨虏脨掳脩聜 脨驴脩聙脨掳脨虏脨赂脨禄脨掳脩聜脨掳. 脨聲脨虏脩聙脨戮脨驴脨掳 脨陆脨碌 脨碌 脩聙脨掳脨路脨鲁脩聙脨掳脨麓脨碌脨陆 脨麓脨虏脨戮脩聙 脨赂 脨陆脨碌 脨卤脨赂脨虏脨掳 脨麓脨掳 脩聛脩聜脨掳脨虏脨掳 脨路脨掳脨禄脨戮脨露脨陆脨赂脨潞 脨陆脨掳 脩聛脨虏脨戮脩聫脩聜脨掳 脨赂脨陆脨碌脩聙脩聜脨陆脨戮脩聛脩聜!

脗聽

12. White paper on the future of European defence (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next item is the debate on the Council and Commission statements on the white paper on the future of European defence (2025/2565(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adam Sz脜聜apka, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Mr Commissioner, honourable Members, we are witnessing a paradigm shift in European security. We are witnessing unprecedented geopolitical changes that affect us 芒聙聯 Europeans 芒聙聯 directly. And the conclusion is clear: we need to take greater responsibility for our own security and defence. This requires a collective effort of EU institutions and Member States. The debate we have convened today is timely. It takes place between last week's Special European Council and the communication on the future of European defence that the Commission and the High Representative are expected to issue next week.

As I said, the direction of travel is clear: a Europe with more responsibility for its defence 芒聙聯 the pace of our work is clearly accelerating towards that aim. Indeed, the European Council, at its special meeting on 6脗聽March, marked an important moment in this context. As we have discussed this morning, leaders held a first discussion on the ReArm Europe plan set out by the President of the Commission and provided guidance for future EU actions. Let me briefly summarise how the Council of the European Union is working on boosting Europe's defence readiness.

We must strive for a stronger and more capable Union in the field of security and defence. Work in the Council takes place along five main priorities for EU security and defence. Firstly, we must spend more and better, and strengthen our defence, technological and industrial base. Deliberations are ongoing towards a Council general approach on the regulation establishing a European defence industry programme. We are looking forward to negotiations with Parliament. The Council is also currently conducting a strategic review of the Permanent Structured Cooperation, which will result in a revision of its legal framework.

Secondly, the EU must continue its unwavering support for Ukraine's independence, sovereignty and its territorial integrity. We must use all of our financial regulatory power to provide financial and military aid as long as it takes.

Thirdly, we need to increase the EU's ability to act, notably by effectively using both military and civilian means in a joined up manner. Efforts to operationalise the EU rapid development capacity are well underway, and military exercises will continue in 2025, increasing the EU's ability to act.

Fourthly, we must strengthen the EU's resilience and security, its access to strategic environments such as the space, maritime and air domains. To secure our access to these domains, we have agreed on strategies on cyber defence and space, and we updated the EU maritime security strategy. In October脗聽2024, the Council approved the first ever EU cyber census, which tracks our progress in implementing the EU policy on cyber defence.

Finally, the EU should continue to strengthen and expand tailored and mutually beneficial partnerships with bilateral and multilateral partners. We stepped up political dialogue with NATO and we have also concluded a security and defence partnership with six key partners and more will follow. We must also preserve our transatlantic bonds. The step undertaken recently by the US administration may worry us, but this is our existential interest and we should keep it above the current political developments.

These are the basic aspects of what the Council considers as the priorities for developing the EU's defence capacities. We look forward to examining how these priorities are addressed in the upcoming white paper. To conclude, let me reaffirm that we are committed to building a safe and secure Europe with the same determination and a great sense of urgency. There is still a lot of work ahead of us.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andrius Kubilius, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, thank you, first of all, for putting this debate and the morning debate on the future of European defence on the agenda.

In my view, those are the most important debates in the history of this House, because the days we are living through are days which will define the history of Europe and of the European Union.

This is a once-in-a-generation moment: we face a clear and present danger seen by none of us in our lifetimes. More than ever, Europe must prepare for the worst to prevent the worst: the possibility of military aggression against us. Such preparation is the only way to deter the worst. Russia's war industry is operating at full blast. Russia could be ready for a confrontation with NATO in five years or less. American actions are a wake-up call: their policy shift and pivot towards Asia.

Geopolitical reality is changing before our eyes. More than ever, we must stand on our two feet, take charge of our own defence and of deterrence. More than ever, we must support and defend Ukraine. Yes, there must be a peace, but a strong peace. Peace through real strength. A peace with Ukraine and Europe at the table. A just peace, not just a pause for Russia to lick its wounds and start a new, bigger war.

A strong peace also means a strong Europe, able to deter aggression and prevent war. To do that, we must completely overhaul our defence industry, because the gaps are colossal between the defences we have and the defences we need to protect our people. Already now there is a lack of thousands of tanks, fighting vehicles, armoured vehicles, pieces of artillery, as we can guess from publications about NATO's capability targets 芒聙聯 a shortfall of EUR脗聽500脗聽billion at least. Even more hundreds of billions are needed for real air defence, space defence, military mobility. Member States need to invest massively to fill these gaps and the EU will support Member States with European Union added value, European scale, European coordination, European money and European laws.

This Commission put defence on the top of the European agenda since before our mandate even started. Defence was top priority in President von der Leyen's political guidelines, and she appointed me as the first-ever European Union Defence Commissioner. She charged me in my mission letter with High Representative Kaja Kallas to present a white paper on the future of European defence, and since the very first day of my mandate, we have been working non-stop in the college, with my services, in plenty of seminars, conferences and discussions, including in this House. I have met many ministers, Members of Parliament, CEOs of defence and space industry. We received many contributions and your very valuable resolution. This month of discussions helped prepare the ground, allowing us to present key proposals already last week with the historical decisions on ReArmEU, far ahead of the white paper's publication. Because if history is running, we cannot be walking.

Last week, President von der Leyen presented the ReArmEU plan, unanimously approved two days later by a historic European Council, with key proposals to supercharge our defence spending up to EUR脗聽800脗聽billion, such as activating the national escape clause of the Stability and Growth Pact and the European Union instrument to support Member States with loans, redirecting existing European funding for defence like cohesion funds, encouraging investment by private banks and the European Investment Bank.

We welcome the leaders' call to reconsider excluded activities to increase funding into defence, and we encourage Member States to continue support for Ukraine by buying arms in Ukraine, with Ukraine and for Ukraine, like Denmark and Czechia are already doing. All initiatives that ensure Ukraine can stay strong and defend itself need to be supported. We will present four legal proposals on ReArmEU before the next European Council.

It is not enough to spend more: spending more in a fragmented market will only fragment it more. Spending more can also mean spending more outside the European Union. This will only increase our dependence. We need to spend better, spend together and spend European, as well as work together on research and development, build more bridges between civil and military research and innovation. Artificial intelligence and quantum technology will change the nature of war, so we must leverage deep tech to level up defence readiness. We must work on priority areas for action at EU level in the field of capabilities like air and missile defence, strategic enablers 芒聙聯 including in relation to space 芒聙聯 and military mobility.

This is where European Union programmes for joint procurement and joint development programmes like EDIP are crucial. With these programmes, we can incentivise Member States to spend together, to overcome fragmentation, to spend smart and to spend the European. This is how we will build up our own European defence industry, because the defence industry is no ordinary industry, but a resource for our defence. We are encouraging joint procurement to give our European industry the big orders it needs, to simplify production, to reduce the price of armaments and reduce fragmentation. We will simplify our laws and rules to remove all obstacles that stand in the way of ramping up our defence industry. All that is what the white paper is about, and now we need to scale up and speed up. I know this House strongly supports European defence, as President Metsola made clear, as many of you made clear to me during last week when I spoke with the ITRE Committee.

This is why I call on you urgently to agree the Parliament's negotiating position on EDIP, the European defence industry programme, which will allow us to be much more effective in bringing European Union added value to help Member States spend their national defence money in the most useful way. I welcome the Council's call to conclude negotiations as soon as possible. EDIP was proposed a year ago. History will not wait for us. Putin will not wait for us. Next week we will present the white paper to rethink European defence in this strategic moment.

Let me close with some inspiration from history. I recently read the memoirs of Jean Monnet. Jean Monnet was a founding father of the European Union, our great project of peace. But did you know that Jean Monnet was also a father of victory in the Second World War? Jean Monnet helped Churchill and Roosevelt prepare the so-called 'victory programme' to ramp up military production in the United States to defeat the Nazis. He would have recognised many of our current challenges.

Jean Monnet also said 'people only make great decisions when crisis is on their doorstep.' This is the greatest security crisis of our lifetimes, and we must now take the great decisions. All of Europe is a target of Russian aggression. We are all frontline Member States. The white paper is the basis for our industrial victory programme. Victory in defence of peace on the European continent. The white paper and ReArmEU are just the beginning of our road: the road to the victory of peace and democracy in Europe, and we shall prevail.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rasa Juknevi脛聧ien脛聴, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, if we want to avoid even greater war in Europe, we must be prepared for war with Russia in the shortest possible time. War with today's Russia, whose ideology 芒聙聯 Russkiy Mir 芒聙聯 means destroying or enslaving everything that is not Russian.

A challenge for Europe in the short term is on Ukraine's battlefield. The future of Europe will be decided there. Member States should commit at least 0.25脗聽% of their GDP to military aid to Ukraine. Ukraine's defence industries should be integrated with the European one.

In the longer term, to ensure that most EU policies align with defence and security needs. Defence is the priority of priorities. We need to increase our defence spending to at least 3脗聽% of GDP in 2026 as a start. The EU was not created as a defence union. Europe flourished on US security guarantees. Everything has changed.

We will have to fill the huge security capabilities gap and diversify our security policy to build our own capabilities while trying to maintain and strengthen the transatlantic bond.

Tomorrow we will vote for a resolution on the white paper on defence. But this is just the very beginning of a difficult but very necessary journey 芒聙聯 an existential journey.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pierre-Romain Thionnet, au nom du groupe PfE. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Ministre, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, je veux vous faire part d芒聙聶une inqui脙漏tude et d芒聙聶un doute. L芒聙聶inqui脙漏tude, c芒聙聶est celle d芒聙聶une excitation europ脙漏enne passag脙篓re, sans lendemain; le doute, c芒聙聶est 脙聽 propos de la r脙漏elle d脙漏termination 脙聽 faire en sorte que l芒聙聶Europe ne d脙漏pende de personne pour sa d脙漏fense. Les d脙漏clarations de l芒聙聶administration am脙漏ricaine ont provoqu脙漏 un 脙漏tat de stup脙漏faction sur le continent. Une parenth脙篓se historique s芒聙聶appr脙陋te 脙聽 se refermer: celle qui voulait que les 脙聣tats-Unis s芒聙聶engagent 脙聽 prot脙漏ger l芒聙聶Europe. Elle durait depuis soixante-dix ans pour certains 脙聣tats, depuis trente ans pour d芒聙聶autres. Toutefois, est-ce que les appels 脙聽 b脙垄tir cette autonomie strat脙漏gique europ脙漏enne sont vraiment sinc脙篓res?

Imaginons un sc脙漏nario: apr脙篓s avoir conditionn脙漏 le maintien de la protection am脙漏ricaine 脙聽 l芒聙聶augmentation des budgets de d脙漏fense, Donald脗聽Trump annonce demain une condition suppl脙漏mentaire: le r脙漏armement de l芒聙聶Europe doit se faire aupr脙篓s de l芒聙聶industrie militaire am脙漏ricaine. Ce sera le moment de v脙漏rit脙漏 pour l芒聙聶Europe, le moment o脙鹿 elle sera confront脙漏e 脙聽 un choix existentiel: notre destin commun doit-il rester entre les mains du locataire de la Maison-Blanche, ou bien les nations europ脙漏ennes sont-elles capables de retrouver confiance en elles, de croire en leur g脙漏nie, d芒聙聶exploiter leur potentiel immense et de s芒聙聶affirmer sur la sc脙篓ne internationale comme une union d芒聙聶脙聣tats libres, capables de d脙漏cider et d芒聙聶agir selon ses propres int脙漏r脙陋ts?

Chers coll脙篓gues, disons-le tout haut: nous ne voulons ni d芒聙聶une Europe russe, ni d芒聙聶une Europe rendue barbare par l芒聙聶islamisme, mais pas non plus d芒聙聶une Europe am脙漏ricaine. Ce que nous devons rechercher et b脙垄tir, c芒聙聶est l芒聙聶Europe europ脙漏enne.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Reinis Poz脜聠aks, on behalf of the ECR Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Mr Commissioner, dear colleagues, over the last thirty years, we have seen dozens of white papers come and go 芒聙聯 we are good at this, but I cannot imagine any of them being more important and coming at a more critical moment than this one.

Despite everything we see happening all around us, we must recognise this white paper for what it is 芒聙聯 an opportunity to shape our own future for the next generations on our own terms with our own tools. It is time to be bold in ensuring that we work towards a total defence strategy, one that combines military and civilian assets to ensure that we are no longer exposed to hostile powers to our east. One that demonstrates our commitments to our allies. One that does everything in our power to invest our political, economic, and military capital into securing our citizens.

Restoring deterrence is our primary objective. Our enemies must know and see that war against Europe and our allies is hopeless, and to do so requires us to undertake a strategic audit of European military forces and civilian assets to ensure that we outpower our enemies. The next years are a key opportunity for us to turn Europe from security consumer to security provider. We have all the tools we need to achieve this. We only need to maintain our political will.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nathalie Loiseau, au nom du groupe Renew. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, l芒聙聶Europe de la d脙漏fense est n脙漏e jeudi dernier. Ce fut malheureusement un accouchement dans la douleur. Il aura fallu le rapprochement entre Donald脗聽Trump et Vladimir脗聽Poutine pour que l芒聙聶on 脙漏coute enfin ceux qui plaident depuis des ann脙漏es pour l芒聙聶autonomie strat脙漏gique europ脙漏enne. Je ne c脙漏derai pas 脙聽 la facilit脙漏 de vous rappeler que nous vous l芒聙聶avions bien dit, car personne n芒聙聶imaginait que les 脙聣tats-Unis tourneraient le dos 脙聽 leurs alli脙漏s et s芒聙聶aplatiraient devant leurs adversaires. Je pr脙漏f脙篓re vous dire qu芒聙聶il n芒聙聶y a plus une minute 脙聽 perdre. Aussi, je salue le plan propos脙漏 par la pr脙漏sidente de la Commission, mais je nous alerte tous: ce que propose Ursula脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, c芒聙聶est peu et c芒聙聶est tard.

C芒聙聶est peu, car cela repose presque exclusivement sur une d脙漏marche volontaire des 脙聣tats membres. Or, si beaucoup ont d脙漏j脙聽 augment脙漏 leurs d脙漏penses de d脙漏fense, certains restent obstin脙漏ment 脙聽 la tra脙庐ne. C芒聙聶est peu, car rien ne dit que l芒聙聶on r脙漏soudra nos principales faiblesses, celles qui r脙漏sultent de la fragmentation de nos industries de d脙漏fense et celles qui sont le fruit d芒聙聶un paradoxe toxique: nos industries exportent en priorit脙漏 hors de l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne, et la plupart de nos 脙聣tats ach脙篓tent essentiellement hors d芒聙聶Europe. Nous ne pouvons plus nous le permettre et nous devons absolument assumer une pr脙漏f脙漏rence europ脙漏enne marqu脙漏e 芒聙聯脗聽enfin!

Le plan d芒聙聶Ursula脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen arrive apr脙篓s des d脙漏cennies d芒聙聶indolence. Surtout, il ne dit pas clairement comment nous allons massivement aider l芒聙聶Ukraine; massivement, parce que notre s脙漏curit脙漏 en d脙漏pend; massivement, parce que nous sommes de plus en plus seuls 脙聽 le faire. Saluons donc le Conseil de jeudi dernier, mais soyons lucides: ce n芒聙聶est qu芒聙聶une premi脙篓re 脙漏tape. D脙漏fendre les Europ脙漏ens est une ardente obligation, qui va n脙漏cessiter des efforts et, parfois, des sacrifices. Pr脙漏server la paix en Europe est 脙聽 ce prix.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Reinier Van Lanschot, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, what do Putin, Trump and Xi have in common? They are all afraid of a united Europe. Our ambition in this vision for the future of European defence is far too low. Too many of you still believe that 27 Member States should simply do more, instead of doing things together. Too many of you still believe we can sit back and rely on NATO instead of saving NATO through European leadership. And too many of you still believe nation states alone can provide protection, instead of trusting in the protection of a united Europe.

Integrate our 27 little armies to fight as one. As President Zelenskyy said, the time has come for the armed forces of Europe to be created. So let's take the first concrete steps today. Make NATO's command and control European so we can operate as one. Jointly procure strategic enablers so we can fight effectively. Create permanent multinational units so we can get 50 extra brigades.

Europe is a peace project, but how can we be strong enough to defend it? The answer to that existential question is simple and consists of three things: Unite! Unite! Unite!

Let's not wait for war to unite us. Let's unite to prevent war. And to my Dutch colleagues in the national parliament who just voted against the REARM plans from Ms von der Leyen, I say you're making a massive mistake.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Botenga, au nom du groupe The Left. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers repr脙漏sentants du Conseil, chers coll脙篓gues, 800脗聽milliards non pas pour augmenter les salaires des Europ脙漏ens, ni pour augmenter les pensions, ni pour garantir des soins de sant脙漏 de qualit脙漏 脙聽 tous les Europ脙漏ens sans liste d芒聙聶attente, ni m脙陋me pour avoir des bus ou des trains ponctuels dans chaque ville et chaque village. Non, vous voil脙聽 脙漏pris de la folie guerri脙篓re. F脙漏licitations!

Disons les choses, cependant: nous d脙漏pensons d脙漏j脙聽 aujourd芒聙聶hui de deux 脙聽 trois脗聽fois plus que la Russie pour la guerre. Selon la presse allemande, nous avons actuellement quatre脗聽fois plus de navires de guerre que la Russie, trois脗聽fois plus de chars de combat et d芒聙聶artillerie et deux脗聽fois plus d芒聙聶avions de chasse, tandis que la Russie n芒聙聶arrive heureusement pas 脙聽 conqu脙漏rir m脙陋me un quart de l芒聙聶Ukraine. Avant que les Russes ne d脙漏boulent sur la Grand-Place de Bruxelles, il faudra du temps! Si vous ne voulez pas l芒聙聶entendre, ce n芒聙聶est pas grave. Ce sont les faits, mais vous pr脙漏f脙漏rez l芒聙聶id脙漏ologie.

Arr脙陋tez votre cin脙漏ma: tous ceux qui veulent plus d芒聙聶armes et plus de soldats, vous allez y envoyer vos enfants 脙聽 vous? C芒聙聶est ce que vous allez faire? Non, ce seront encore les enfants de la classe ouvri脙篓re qui devront mourir! Eh bien, nous ne l芒聙聶accepterons pas. Nous devons coordonner plus, pas d脙漏penser plus, et investir enfin dans la diplomatie.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Petr Bystron, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! W脙陇hrend wir hier diskutieren, demonstrieren gerade in Rum脙陇nien Hunderttausende gegen diese EU und f脙录r ihren Kandidaten. F脙录r den Kandidaten des Volkes, dem die Wahl gestohlen wurde, weil er gegen den Krieg war, gegen weitere Waffenlieferungen an die Ukraine. Und wie hat die EU reagiert? Wie hat dieses Parlament reagiert? Hier wurde die Debatte dar脙录ber verhindert. Das ist eine Schande! Stattdessen haben Sie hier Vertreter von dubiosen NGOs aus der Ukraine und aus Wei脙聼russland eingeladen, die hier gegen Russland gehetzt haben. Was haben die hier im Plenum 脙录berhaupt zu suchen im Parlament? Die sind durch niemanden gew脙陇hlt, durch niemanden demokratisch legitimiert, und deren L脙陇nder geh脙露ren nicht mal zur EU. Wer das zu verantworten hat, der tritt die Demokratie mit F脙录脙聼en.

Ja, wer hat uns verraten? Die Christdemokraten. Die CDU stimmt hier mit den Sozialisten, Kommunisten und mit den Gr脙录nen, obwohl es rechts hier rechtskonservative Mehrheiten gibt. Und das ist Verrat am W脙陇hler, mein lieber Kollege. Das will Merz in Deutschland genauso tun; da will man sogar das Grundgesetz 脙陇ndern wegen 800脗聽Milliarden neuer Schulden脗聽芒聙聯 300脗聽Milliarden f脙录r Waffen, 500脗聽Milliarden, um die Infrastruktur waffen芒聙聭 und panzertauglich zu machen. Sie wollen uns in den Krieg treiben. Das ist es脗聽芒聙聯 und das v脙露llig illegal, denn Merz will das sogar mit dem alten, abgew脙陇hlten Bundestag machen, weil er die Mehrheiten in dem neuen Bundestag gar nicht hat. Das ist wirklich verfassungswidrig. Es gibt da 脙录berhaupt keine Eilbed脙录rftigkeit.

Die Menschen wollen keine Kriegstreiberei, die Menschen wollen Frieden. Weil das so ist, m脙录ssen Sie hier solche Spielchen spielen wie am Vormittag mit diesen NGOs. Deswegen bauen Sie hier Russland zu einem Feind auf. Von wegen Russland will uns angreifen; das ist doch v脙露lliger Schwachsinn. Was will denn Russland, wenn sie uns hier besetzen? Wir haben doch gar nichts au脙聼er Schulden 芒聙聯 2,5脗聽Billionen Schulden. Meinen Sie, das russische Volk will das abzahlen f脙录r uns? Und die 800脗聽Milliarden obendrauf? Sicher nicht.

(Die Pr脙陇sidentin entzieht dem Redner das Wort.)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Colleagues, please calm down. Please. No, I have closed the microphone.

I will ask my colleagues to remember that you have the blue-card option very soon, so you have a chance to ask questions for your colleagues. And when you don't have the blue card, please let people speak quietly. Please, colleagues. Thank you.

So now we are here where I open up for the blue-card option. We always do that after the first round of speakers on behalf of the political groups. And we ended that.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽David McAllister (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Commissioner, ladies and gentlemen, I can echo a lot of things that have been said already in this debate, but not a single word of my previous speaker.

After numerous wake-up calls we have discussed here, Europe's moment of truth has finally arrived. We're not starting from scratch. In March脗聽2022, the 27脗聽heads of state or government adopted the Strategic Compass. A year later, the Commission published the European Defence Industrial Strategy, and we also adopted ASAP and EDIRPA and agreed on the capability development priorities.

All these initiatives are slowly but surely pushing Member States to step outside their national corridors in pursuit of a genuine European Defence Union. But now we really have to make a great leap.

Therefore, dear Commissioner Kubilius, the white paper must build on these achievements, and propose regulatory and programmatic initiatives at European level, rather than confining itself to a description of threats and shared needs.

You, dear Commissioner, and the HR/VP have a chance to significantly steer this debate. I look forward to discussing the white paper thoroughly at committee level.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sven Mikser (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, colleagues, the fragile entire security situation around us makes it absolutely imperative that we improve our ability to deter our adversaries, but also, if and when necessary, fight and win wars.

The dramatic reversal, of course, by our traditional American allies further underscores the need to achieve European strategic autonomy and industrial sovereignty. It actually underscores that some dependencies that we used to regard as security guarantees may, in this changed situation, prove to be vulnerabilities instead.

A stronger focus on defence and defence industrial base is not merely expenditure. It actually provides a boost for our economic competitiveness and economic activity. But it's also important to see that this additional impetus to our economic growth be shared equitably across our Union.

It has been said that we need to spend smarter and do more together, but it is obvious it's inevitable that we also need to start spending more, both at EU level, but also in our national budgets, where, let's be honest, the bulk of our defence spending and also capability generation will continue to happen.

We must be open to considering all novel ways of financing our ambitious programmes, both public and private sources. But while it may be tempting to think that at this critical time everything except defence can be put on the backburner, this is not the case, because there are two types of challenges our democracies face, both external 芒聙聯 of military nature 芒聙聯 but also internal. We must not provide additional fodder for the pro-Putin illiberal sentiment.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Roberto Vannacci (PfE). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, oggi la signora von der Leyen 脙篓 Polifemo e io mi chiamo Nessuno e posso dirvi con assoluta certezza che il libro bianco 脙篓 un cavallo di Troia.

Perch脙漏, chi 脙篓 il nemico? E quali sarebbero gli scenari strategici? Perch脙漏 se il nemico 脙篓 la Russia, la Russia spende in termini assoluti, in difesa, due volte e mezzo in meno di quanto non spenda l'Unione europea e ha un PIL pi脙鹿 basso dell'Italia ed 脙篓 un nono di quello dell'Unione europea ed ha una popolazione che 脙篓 un terzo di quella dell'Unione europea ed ha una capacit脙聽 industriale che 脙篓 ridicola di fronte alla capacit脙聽 industriale del Vecchio continente.

Ma come: negli ultimi tre anni ci avete dipinto una Russia infognata nel Donbass e che perde di fronte a un modesto avversario come l'Ucraina e oggi ce la volete dipingere come il nemico dell'intera Unione europea? Fate pace con il cervello.

La realt脙聽 脙篓 che Bruxelles vuole ingoiare la sovranit脙聽 nazionale con la scusa e il pretesto della difesa comune. Quello di cui abbiamo bisogno sono nazioni forti con eserciti....

(La Presidente toglie la parola all'oratore)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Elena Donazzan (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, se questo 脙篓 il tempo delle scelte forti, Commissario, come ha detto lei, perch脙漏 il contesto 脙篓 completamente mutato e oggi dobbiamo discutere di alcuni temi che pensavamo lasciati alla tranquillit脙聽: dobbiamo difendere la nostra civilt脙聽, la nostra economia e i nostri confini in un contesto di conflitto ibrido permanente.

Accanto ad alcuni che abbiamo identificato come problemi, per脙虏, ce ne sono altri, di scenari: oltre a quello Russia-Ucraina, c'脙篓 il Medio Oriente, c'脙篓 il Centrafrica, ci sono altri soggetti pericolosi per noi, c'脙篓 la Cina, c'脙篓 il ruolo della Turchia, c'脙篓 il ruolo dell'Iran, c'脙篓 l'islamismo che avanza.

Ecco, nel libro bianco mi aspetto che ci siano valutazioni di questo tipo, perch脙漏 avremmo bisogno di un'industria della difesa, avremo bisogno di dual use, avremo bisogno di forze armate sempre pi脙鹿 preparate e di nostri soldati sempre pi脙鹿 sicuri, perch脙漏 a loro va il nostro grazie.

E, come ha detto nella relazione di Niinist脙露, c'脙篓 anche da guardare alla popolazione, che deve essere pi脙鹿 preparata. E soprattutto ai giovani dobbiamo parlare, affinch脙漏 possano avere pi脙鹿 amor di patria e capacit脙聽 di difesa.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie-Agnes Strack-Zimmermann (Renew). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Dass es jetzt turbulent zugeht, ist angesichts des Themas nicht 脙录berraschend. Es geht hier um Krieg oder Frieden und darum, wie unsere Zukunft gestaltet werden wird, und wir haben heute auch Zuschauerinnen und Zuschauer hier. Ich glaube, so eine Debatte ist nicht nur wichtig, sondern es geht auch darum, darauf hinzuweisen, was gerade um uns herum passiert.

Die Welt brennt, und Russland bombardiert mehr denn je die Ukraine, und wir konnten uns heute Morgen noch einmal ein Bild davon machen, was sich dort alles abspielt脗聽芒聙聯 das ist unmenschlich, das ist schrecklich, und noch schrecklicher ist es, dass so etwas ignoriert wird.

Europa stellt sich auf脗聽芒聙聯 auch im Rahmen der NATO m脙录ssen wir st脙陇rker werden: die europ脙陇ischen Staaten, die EU-Staaten, aber auch die europ脙陇ischen Staaten, die nicht in der EU sind. Und das ist die gute Nachricht: 29 von 32脗聽NATO-Staaten haben jetzt die Chance, auch unabh脙陇ngiger zu werden.

Wir m脙录ssen aber auch wettbewerbsf脙陇higer werden. Wettbewerbsf脙陇higer, damit wir eben auch in die Zukunft gehen k脙露nnen, denn das Geld, was wir heute in die Hand nehmen, brauchen wir, aber es muss am langen Ende auch wieder erwirtschaftet werden. Das, was wir in der Ukraine sehen脗聽芒聙聯 ich komme noch einmal darauf zur脙录ck脗聽芒聙聯, zeigt ja Bilder, wie wir sie aus dem Ersten Weltkrieg kennen, aber auch die moderne, schreckliche Kriegsf脙录hrung, und darauf m脙录ssen wir Antworten finden in Forschung, in Technik.

Ich bin dem neuen Kommissar f脙录r Verteidigung und Sicherheit sehr dankbar, dass er mit gro脙聼em Elan an die Arbeit herangegangen ist. Und nat脙录rlich ist das harte Arbeit, jetzt die Nationen zu 脙录berzeugen, die einzelnen Staaten, dass sie auch Europa vertrauen k脙露nnen. Und das wird die Zukunft weisen, dass wir auch vertrauensw脙录rdig sind, dass wir das Geld, was wir in die Hand nehmen, auch sinnvoll und richtig einsetzen. Und das Wei脙聼buch muss脗聽芒聙聯 hoffe ich脗聽芒聙聯 sehr klar und deutlich sein. Die Zeit von 芒聙聻lala芒聙聹 und 芒聙聻wir gucken mal芒聙聹 und 芒聙聻business as usual芒聙聹 ist vorbei.

Meine Damen und Herren, so wie ich gerade angefangen habe, sage ich Ihnen, dass die Welt brennt, und wir in Europa k脙露nnen den Unterschied machen als gr脙露脙聼tes Friedensprojekt der Welt.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

PRE脠聵EDIN脠職IA: NICOLAE 脜聻TEF脛聜NU脠職脛聜
Vicepre脠聶edinte

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hannah Neumann (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, I guess you all remember those painful images from the Oval Office when Trump and Vance openly joined the autocrats' camp, bullying and humiliating President Zelenskyy in front of cameras? If this, dear colleagues, is not the world we want to submit to 芒聙聯 and I certainly don't 芒聙聯 then we must be the alternative.

We must defend the rules-based international order, basing decisions on facts, treating partners with respect, and we must finally stand on our own feet. This means weapons and funding, but most crucially, it means attracting the brightest minds in science, tech and engineering. And here, dear colleagues, is the perfect match because many in the US also reject this administration's agenda 芒聙聯 they too, are desperately looking for an alternative. So let's roll out the red carpet, launch a new blue card for a new era, a fast-track visa for skilled professionals from the US who want to work in Europe and build a future based on ethics, sustainability and fair growth. And to the scientists, engineers and innovators in the US: come to Europe. When autocrats embolden autocrats, democrats stand with democrats.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脙聳zlem Demirel (The Left). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Bl脙录hende Munitionslandschaften, bl脙录hende Waffenlandschaften, bl脙录hende Kriegslandschaften脗聽芒聙聯 das ist das Versprechen der Europ脙陇ischen Kommission an die B脙录rgerinnen und B脙录rger der EU. Die Gelder aus den Koh脙陇sionsfonds, die eigentlich daf脙录r vorgesehen sind, die Lebensverh脙陇ltnisse in der EU anzugleichen, k脙露nnen weiterhin umgeschichtet werden in die Waffenindustrie. Milliarden werden seit geraumer Zeit von der EU in den Rachen der Waffenindustrie geschmissen, und seit Beginn des Ukraine芒聙聭Krieges ist die Rheinmetall芒聙聭Aktie um das Zehnfache gestiegen脗聽芒聙聯 von 98脗聽芒聜卢 auf 980脗聽芒聜卢.脗聽Ja, es gibt jene, die Partys machen aufgrund von Kriegen, und es gibt jene, die in Kriegen sterben, und das sind die Armen und die Arbeitenden.

Sie f脙露rdern脗聽芒聙聯 das muss man sich mal vergegenw脙陇rtigen脗聽芒聙聯 Sie f脙露rdern und hegen und pflegen mehr die R脙录stungsindustrie als das, was Sie im Moment in der EU f脙录r die Kinder, f脙录r ihre Zukunft investieren und tun, und das ist inakzeptabel. Aufr脙录stung wird weder Europa noch die Welt sicherer machen. Sie behaupten, damit w脙录rden Sie sogar die Demokratie st脙陇rken. Im Gegenteil: Militarismus schw脙陇cht die liberale Demokratie. Deshalb sagen wir Nein!

(Die Rednerin ist damit einverstanden, auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹 zu antworten.)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI), Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 Frau Kollegin! Meine Frage an Sie w脙陇re: Glauben Sie wirklich, dass irgendjemand Bock auf Krieg hat? Glauben Sie wirklich, dass wir die R脙录stungsindustrie, dass wir die Verteidigung st脙陇rken, weil uns das Spa脙聼 macht, weil wir gerne Waffen bauen? Oder glauben Sie nicht viel eher, dass wir das zum Beispiel machen, weil ein blutr脙录nstiger Diktator im Osten dieses Kontinents einen Angriffskrieg gestartet hat und seine Armee unz脙陇hlige Kriegsverbrechen begeht?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脙聳zlem Demirel (The Left), Antwort auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 Herr Sieper, ich kann nicht in Ihren Kopf kucken und wei脙聼 nat脙录rlich nicht genau, was Sie m脙露chten oder nicht m脙露chten. Die Sache ist aber die, dass ich mir die Realit脙陇t ankucke, und die Sache ist die, dass ich mir auch die Geschichte Europas ankucke. Wenn ich mir die Geschichte Europas ankucke, dann gab es eine Zeit der radikalen Aufr脙录stung; das war vor dem Ersten Weltkrieg, und das m脙录ndete darin. Die Lehre aus dem Ersten Weltkrieg war 脙录brigens, dass man in Diplomatie und Abr脙录stung investieren sollte.

Wenn Sie uns heute erz脙陇hlen, dass Abschreckung mit Aufr脙录stung erreicht wird, dann sage ich Ihnen, auch im Kalten Krieg war es die Diplomatie, die uns davor besch脙录tzt hat, einen schlimmeren Krieg zu erleben als die Aufr脙录stung selber. Deshalb, wirklich, wenn Sie keinen Krieg wollen, Herr Sieper, dann pl脙陇dieren Sie bitte f脙录r einen anderen Kurs in der EU als f脙录r die Aufr脙录stung!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sarah Knafo (ESN). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, nous assistons depuis deux mois 脙聽 une sc脙篓ne improbable. Les plus fervents atlantistes de cette assembl脙漏e d脙漏couvrent soudain qu芒聙聶il ne faut plus d脙漏pendre de Washington. M脙陋me ceux qui se taisaient quand Barack脗聽Obama pla脙搂ait les dirigeants europ脙漏ens sur 脙漏coute, m脙陋me ceux qui ne disaient rien quand Joe脗聽Biden emp脙陋chait la construction de sous-marins fran脙搂ais en Australie, m脙陋me ceux-l脙聽 viennent nous dire aujourd芒聙聶hui qu芒聙聶on ne peut finalement faire confiance qu芒聙聶脙聽 nous-m脙陋mes. Vous d脙漏couvrez l芒聙聶ind脙漏pendance par antitrumpisme. Nous, nous n芒聙聶avons pas attendu le nom du locataire de la Maison-Blanche pour savoir qu芒聙聶il fallait d脙漏fendre notre pays nous-m脙陋mes. Cela fait soixante-dix ans que le g脙漏n脙漏ral de Gaulle vous le disait. Nos nations ont un devoir de puissance. Nous ne pouvons pas compter 脙漏ternellement sur un pays dont les int脙漏r脙陋ts vitaux sont situ脙漏s 脙聽 un oc脙漏an des n脙麓tres.

Attention, toutefois, ne remplacez pas une d脙漏pendance par une autre. Ce n芒聙聶est pas la Commission europ脙漏enne qui vous d脙漏fendra. La d脙漏fense d芒聙聶une nation, c芒聙聶est sa survie, et la survie ne se n脙漏gocie pas entre bureaucrates, 脙聽 la majorit脙漏 qualifi脙漏e. Ne donnez pas 脙聽 Mme脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen le pouvoir d芒聙聶envoyer vos fils sur le champ de bataille. Il faut nous r脙漏armer, et on ne r脙漏arme pas une nation avec des comptes publics en faillite. Une 脙漏conomie de guerre, ce n芒聙聶est pas une machine 脙聽 jeter notre argent par les fen脙陋tres au profit de pays du monde entier. Si nous voulons gagner la prochaine guerre, il nous faut d芒聙聶abord gagner tout de suite la guerre contre le gaspillage, les gabegies et le d脙漏sordre budg脙漏taire.

Notre r脙漏armement doit b脙漏n脙漏ficier exclusivement 脙聽 notre industrie militaire. Si vous achetez am脙漏ricain, vous restez sous tutelle am脙漏ricaine 芒聙聯脗聽c芒聙聶est aussi simple que cela. La France, seule puissance nucl脙漏aire du continent, dot脙漏e d芒聙聶une arm脙漏e exceptionnelle, a un r脙麓le historique 脙聽 jouer. Pour les nations qui voudraient se placer sous notre protection, il faudra contribuer et donner 脙聽 la France les moyens n脙漏cessaires, car la puissance exige des moyens. En revanche, notre dissuasion nucl脙漏aire ne sera jamais 脙聽 partager avec quiconque, car la puissance ne se partage pas, la puissance ne se d脙漏l脙篓gue pas, et la s脙漏curit脙漏 ne se sous-traite pas. C芒聙聶est 脙聽 nous d芒聙聶assumer l芒聙聶effort, ou 脙聽 nous de nous pr脙漏parer 脙聽 en payer le prix.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fabio De Masi (NI). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, sehr geehrte Damen und Herren! Seit drei Jahren wird nun ein sinnloser und vermeidbarer Krieg in der Ukraine gef脙录hrt, bei dem schon viel zu viele Menschen gestorben sind und der sich im Wesentlichen um die NATO芒聙聭Perspektive der Ukraine drehte. Der Krieg ist illegal, aber auch zahlreiche ranghohe US芒聙聭Sicherheitsexperten haben gesagt, es war eine gro脙聼e Dummheit, immer weiter an die Tore einer Nuklearmacht heranzur脙录cken.

800脗聽Milliarden脗聽Euro sollen nun in R脙录stung verausgabt werden, w脙陇hrend unsere Wirtschaft im Koma liegt und unsere Infrastruktur kollabiert. Dabei geben die europ脙陇ischen NATO-Staaten bereits das Dreifache Russlands f脙录r R脙录stung aus. Russland bei脙聼t sich seit drei Jahren in 20脗聽% der Ukraine die Z脙陇hne aus. Deswegen ist es v脙露llig illusion脙陇r anzunehmen, dass sie morgen auf dem Grand Place oder vor dem Brandenburger Tor st脙录nden.

Eines ist aber richtig: Russland verf脙录gt 脙录ber 5脗聽000脗聽Atomsprengk脙露pfe, Frankreich 脙录ber 290. Und die AfD fordert jetzt脗聽芒聙聯 脙聹berraschung脗聽芒聙聯 in Deutschland deutsche Atombomben. Mehr Sicherheit erfordert aber wechselseitige Abr脙录stungsinitiativen und eine neue europ脙陇ische Sicherheitsarchitektur. W脙陇hrend Herr Trump uns Strafz脙露lle androht, hauen wir der US芒聙聭R脙录stungsindustrie die Auftragsb脙录cher voll. Das ist v脙露llig widersinnig, und deswegen ist es Zeit, mit diesem Unsinn Schluss zu machen, bevor sich Generationen von Untersuchungsaussch脙录ssen mit dem Filz im R脙录stungssektor befassen.

(Der Pr脙陇sident entzieht dem Redner das Wort.)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Siegfried Mure脜聼an (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, it looks like Mr De Masi's only concern is that we stop helping Ukraine. You cannot make up your mind whether Putin is strong or whether he is weak. The only thing that you care is that we stop helping Ukraine, which is, in fact, exactly what Mr Putin wants. This is exactly why we should continue to support Ukraine, because that is in the fundamental interest of European citizens. And whoever says we should stop helping Ukraine betrays the interest of European citizens and acts against the security of Europe.

If we are weak, we are a target. If we are strong, we are safe. This is the principle that needs to guide us. This is why I say invest more in defence, to keep our citizens safe and invest more in common European projects in defence.

Commissioner Kubilius, we very much welcome the work that you're doing, and we're very much looking forward to the white paper that you're going to propose. Our expectation is to see projects in there which we at European level can do better that than Member States can do alone. And then we should also find the funds, the resources, to support the projects which the European Commission puts forward. We should show to the people of Europe that spending money at European level on defence is worth it, and that it is better spent than at national level.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Javi L脙鲁pez (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or comisario, un Libro Blanco de la defensa para tener hoy una Europa aut脙鲁noma que se haga cargo plenamente de su seguridad.

脗驴Por qu脙漏? Porque no podemos dar por garantizado el v脙颅nculo atl脙隆ntico y tenemos una guerra en suelo europeo con una fuerza hostil a nuestras puertas.

脗驴C脙鲁mo? Coordinando mejor nuestro gasto, creando capacidades europeas, gastando m脙隆s europeo con una base industrial europea y gastando m脙隆s en proporci脙鲁n al reto que tenemos enfrente.

脗驴Para qu脙漏? Para tener una fuerza de disuasi脙鲁n suficiente como para evitar la guerra, es decir, para proteger la paz, para proteger nuestras democracias y tambi脙漏n para tener una diplomacia y ser un actor global cre脙颅ble en el mundo.

Y, al mismo tiempo, no solo vale con cr脙漏ditos y presupuesto nacional, necesitamos recursos y canales financieros europeos para hacerlo, para asegurar que, sobre todo, no entra en competici脙鲁n el gasto social con el gasto en defensa, algo que ser脙颅a un suicidio pol脙颅tico para Europa.脗聽Y, al mismo tiempo, diversificar nuestras alianzas tambi脙漏n en materia de seguridad en un mundo crecientemente multipolar. Eso es una Europa aut脙鲁noma.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Vilis Kri脜隆topans (PfE). 芒聙聯 God脛聛tais s脛聯des vad脛芦t脛聛j! P脛聯d脛聯jos piecus gadus Eiropa ir r脜芦p脛聯jusies par s脜芦n脛聛m un odiem, bet cilv脛聯kus nezin k脛聛p脛聯c aizmirsusi. Eiropa ir ekonomiski atpalikusi un k脛录uvusi v脛聛ja bez strat脛聯脛拢iska redz脛聯juma.

Pat tad, kad pirms trim gadiem Krievija iebruka Ukrain脛聛, m脜芦su l脛芦deri joproj脛聛m nesaprata vai negrib脛聯ja saprast, k脛聛d脛聛 situ脛聛cij脛聛 m脛聯s esam. Tikai tagad, kad amerik脛聛脜聠i ir par脛聛d脛芦ju脜隆i m脜芦su v脛聛jumu, m脛聯s kaut ko cen脜隆amies dar脛芦t.

Tagad tiek pazi脜聠ots par 800 miljardiem lielu ieguld脛芦jumu Eiropas dro脜隆脛芦b脛聛, bet t脛聛 ir tikai un vien脛芦gi iesp脛聯ja aiz脜聠emties. Vai m脛聯s gribam v脛聯l vienu infl脛聛cijas vilni? Vai m脛聯s gribam v脛聯l vair脛聛k samazin脛聛t Eiropas konkur脛聯tsp脛聯ju? Nekav脛聯jo脜隆i ir piln脛芦b脛聛 j脛聛atce脛录 za脛录ais kurss un visa nauda j脛聛novirza aizsardz脛芦bas industrijai. Ja m脛聯s to neizdar脛芦sim t脜芦l脛芦t, tad m脛聯s neizgl脛聛bsim ne klimatu, ne dro脜隆脛芦bu.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adam Bielan (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, the white paper and its implementation present a timely opportunity to ensure Europe's boosted commitments translate into real capabilities and enhance protection for our citizens.

The war in Ukraine has highlighted the consequences of inaction and overreliance on external partners for European defence. Beyond Ukraine, Europe has increasingly become the target of hybrid attacks and disinformation campaigns, directly threatening our political and economic stability.

But, despite all possible efforts, NATO remains the cornerstone of our security. If Europe needs to step up, any European-led initiatives must complement, not duplicate, NATO's role.

In its current form, the joint resolution is concerning. Instead of strengthening Europe's security, it promotes an alternative to NATO, undermines the autonomy of Member States in defence matters and risks deepening confrontation with the United States 芒聙聯 our most important ally.

Instead, Europe must focus on real security: replenishing stockpiles, strengthening transatlantic ties and investing in credible deterrence.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hilde Vautmans (Renew). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris Kubilius, dit is het belangrijkste debat van de geschiedenis, want op dit ogenblik hebben we 27脗聽legers, 27脗聽defensiebudgetten en 27脗聽verschillende aankoopstrategie脙芦n. Nu zeggen we hier allemaal samen dat we meer gaan uitgeven aan defensie. Ik zeg dat hier al tien jaar. Dus zeg ik: ja, meer uitgeven, maar niet m脙漏脙漏r van hetzelfde. Het zal anders moeten, het zal samen moeten en het zal Europees moeten.

Ten tweede, commissaris, maakt u gebruik van artikel脗聽122 van het Verdrag om het Parlement buitenspel te zetten. Van mij mag het, maar dat gaat de kwaliteit niet verbeteren. Een goed Europees defensiebeleid voer je niet in achterkamertjes.

Ten derde, commissaris, komt u volgende week met een witboek. Ik hoop dat het duidelijker en concreter is dan u vandaag bent geweest. Er moeten echt grote Europese programma芒聙聶s in staan en een routekaart voor een Europees leger. Ook dit zeg ik hier al tien jaar. U wilt de geschiedenis ingaan? Wel, commissaris, maak die routekaart voor dat Europees leger, of u mist de afspraak met de geschiedenis.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ville Niinist脙露 (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, coming from a country that shares 1脗聽300 kilometres of border with Russia and a country that has had over 50 wars or border skirmishes with Russia in its history, I must say to you that we must show respect to Ukraine that is protecting democracy today, every day, with their lives.

This is not a theoretical discussion about where Europe shall head. This is a very serious discussion about how we can protect the international rules-based order, the national sovereignty of countries, and the right of countries to determine their own fate. Russia and Putin do not believe in that. They want their empire, they want their sphere of influence, and they are building a war economy that is threatening Europe, but also countries outside of the EU.

By this paper 芒聙聯 and I urge you, Commissioner, to be very strong and ambitious 芒聙聯 we show that Europe is united, that we can invest in defence, we can create military capabilities where Europe is united and can also act alone without the support of the United States. And that is something that negates the threat of Russia and protects democracy and peace in Europe 芒聙聯 that is what we must achieve.

脗聽

脗聽

脗聽脗聽脦聯脦鹿脧聨脧聛脦鲁脦驴脧聜 脦聯脦碌脧聣脧聛脦鲁脦炉脦驴脧聟 (The Left). 芒聙聯 脦職脧聧脧聛脦鹿脦碌 脦聽脧聛脧聦脦碌脦麓脧聛脦碌, 脧聞脦碌脦禄脦鹿脦潞脦卢 脦赂脦卤 脧聞脦驴 脦潞脦卢脦陆脦碌脧聞脦碌, 脦碌;脗聽脦聹脦颅脧聝脦卤 脦卤脧聙脧聦 脦颅脦陆脦卤脦陆 脧聙脦驴脦禄脦碌脦录脦鹿脦潞脧聦 脧聙脦卤脧聛脦驴脦戮脧聟脧聝脦录脧聦, 脦赂脦卤 脦麓脦卤脧聙脦卤脦陆脦庐脧聝脦碌脧聞脦碌 800 脦麓脦鹿脧聝., 脧聙脦驴脧聟 脦麓脦碌脦陆 脧聞脦卤 脦颅脧聡脦碌脧聞脦碌, 脦鲁脦鹿脦卤 脧聞脦路 脦麓脦庐脦赂脦碌脦陆 脦潞脦驴脦鹿脦陆脦庐 脦卢脦录脧聟脦陆脦卤. 脦聹脦卤, 脦鲁脦鹿脦卤 脦陆脦卤 脧聟脧聙脦卢脧聛脧聡脦碌脦鹿 脦潞脦驴脦鹿脦陆脦庐 脦卢脦录脧聟脦陆脦卤, 脧聙脧聛脦颅脧聙脦碌脦鹿 脦陆脦卤 脧聟脧聙脦卢脧聛脧聡脦碌脦鹿 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦潞脦驴脦鹿脦陆脦庐 脦碌脦戮脧聣脧聞脦碌脧聛脦鹿脦潞脦庐 脧聙脦驴脦禄脦鹿脧聞脦鹿脦潞脦庐. 脦聲脦麓脧聨 脧聟脧聙脦卢脧聛脧聡脦驴脧聟脦陆 脧聙脦卢脧聛脦卤 脧聙脦驴脦禄脦禄脦驴脦炉 脦卤脧聙脧聛脧聦脦赂脧聟脦录脦驴脦鹿. 脦職脦卢脦陆脦碌脧聞脦碌 脧聙脦卤脧聛脦卢脦陆脦驴脦录脦碌脧聜, 脦卤脦陆脧聞脦鹿-脦赂脦碌脧聝脦录脦鹿脦潞脦颅脧聜 脧聙脦卤脧聛脦卤脧聝脧聟脦陆脦卢脦戮脦碌脦鹿脧聜 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦卤脦陆脦卤脦潞脦路脧聛脧聧脧聝脧聝脦碌脧聞脦碌 脧聞脦路脦陆 脦陇脦驴脧聟脧聛脦潞脦炉脦卤 脦碌脦鲁脦鲁脧聟脦路脧聞脦庐 脧聞脦路脧聜 脦碌脧聟脧聛脧聣脧聙脦卤脧聤脦潞脦庐脧聜 脦卤脧聝脧聠脦卢脦禄脦碌脦鹿脦卤脧聜. 脦聺脧聞脧聛脦驴脧聙脦庐! 脦聰脦庐脦赂脦碌脦陆 脦鲁脦鹿脦卤 脦陆脦卤 脧聝脧聞脦路脧聛脦炉脦戮脦碌脧聞脦碌 脧聞脦路脦陆 脦聼脧聟脦潞脧聛脦卤脦陆脦炉脦卤 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脧聞脦驴脦陆 脦禄脦卤脧聦 脧聞脦路脧聜.

脦職脦卤脦鹿 脧聞脧聛脦炉脦卤 脦潞脦卤脦炉脧聛脦鹿脦卤 脦碌脧聛脧聣脧聞脦庐脦录脦卤脧聞脦卤:

脗聽脗聽Michael Gahler (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, colleagues, dear Commissioner, when the Lisbon Treaty entered into force, we began checking the potential of the treaty in the area of defence, starting with the costs of non-Europe in this policy area. Simply adding all national defence budgets showed how inefficient the expenditure was 芒聙聯 almost no common planning, no common procurement. And since 2011, instead of making use of the single market on defence products, the abuse of Article 346, which made Member States step out of European spending.

Times have changed: there is war in Europe, Putin threatens all of us, and this US administration leaves doubt whether they will live up to their NATO commitments. That is why I thank the Commissioner for presenting this white paper on the future of European defence, together with the earlier announcement on the programme to ReArm Europe. Let us make the best out of it 芒聙聯 let us defend our European way of life together and let us start on the front line, supporting Ukraine with whatever it takes as they defend their and our freedom. Slava Ukraini!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lucia Annunziata (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, mentre discutiamo, giustamente la nostra decisione importantissima di preparare la difesa dell'Europa, Trump, Putin e Zelensky sono, mi sembra, totalmente impegnati, e molto attivi, a preparare il tavolo delle trattative.

Un tavolo, da cui ricordo, noi siamo stati espulsi senza neanche essere stati avvertiti, a meno che il ministro Lubilius non lo fosse stato. Questo 脙篓 un vulnus incredibile, 脙篓 il vero punto di queste trattative: tenere fuori l'Europa.

Allora, io vi domando: mentre prepariamo giustamente questo piano di difesa, non dobbiamo porci il problema e la priorit脙聽 di tornare a quel tavolo? Ministro, noi rischiamo oggi di fare un grande discorso militare ma di tenere fuori la politica a cominciare dal ruolo del Parlamento.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alberico Gambino (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, il Libro bianco sul futuro della difesa europea rappresenta un'opportunit脙聽 cruciale per delineare una strategia chiara, realistica ed efficace. L'Europa deve rafforzare la propria capacit脙聽 difensiva, evitando, per脙虏, di duplicare la NATO o creare nuove sovrastrutture burocratiche prive di utilit脙聽.

L'Italia sostiene una difesa pi脙鹿 forte e fondata su investimenti mirati, su un'industria competitiva nei settori strategici dello spazio, delle comunicazioni e dell'innovazione tecnologica. Investire nella sicurezza non significa alimentare una corsa al riarmo ma dare stabilit脙聽, autonomia strategica e crescita economica.

Ecco perch脙漏 il piano presentato dalla presidente von der Leyen, a mio avviso, deve essere rinominato come Defence Europe.

La sicurezza 脙篓 la vera garanzia di pace ed 脙篓 per questo che voglio stigmatizzare anche il comportamento di alcuni che si professano pacifisti solo per raccogliere pochi consensi senza pensare agli interessi nazionali.

Il Libro bianco deve essere l'occasione per costruire una difesa europea solida, rispettando le specificit脙聽 di ogni Stato membro, e rafforzare il ruolo dell'Europa nel mondo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next speaker is Petras Au脜隆trevi脛聧ius. Congratulations on the independence of your country.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Petras Au脜隆trevi脛聧ius (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr President, thank you, it is indeed a special day. Commissioner Kubilius, Minister, dear colleagues, I must repeat myself, what I have written in my speech, sorry for certain repetitions. Exactly 35 years ago on this day, Lithuania declared the restoration of its independence. We knew about the evil Soviet Empire from our own painful experience, but we had no illusions about the better future of Russia.

Now, we must not only change our thinking and policies, but also defend Europe and our fellow Europeans. We must ensure European security, which is measured by the weakest link in its defence. Therefore, we must act together, not selectively. We must act now, not tomorrow.

Standardisation of weapons and military equipment, mobilisation of financial resources for their innovation, and speedy production and further development of military mobility and capabilities 芒聙聯 these are the most important things now.

For the European defence community should involve our partners from the very beginning, especially candidate countries like Ukraine. Close cooperation in this area will undoubtedly benefit all participant countries and, most importantly, our citizens.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mounir Satouri (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, le monde que nous avons connu n芒聙聶est plus. Celui dont le projet europ脙漏en a r脙陋v脙漏 ne na脙庐tra pas par magie, m脙陋me avec 800脗聽milliards d芒聙聶euros d芒聙聶investissements dans l芒聙聶armement. L芒聙聶argent est indispensable, mais pas n芒聙聶importe comment. Il ne doit pas venir des fonds structurels ou des fonds agricoles. Il doit financer une industrie de d脙漏fense europ脙漏enne et garantir l芒聙聶interop脙漏rabilit脙漏 de nos forces, et non alimenter des int脙漏r脙陋ts 脙漏trangers. L芒聙聶argent seul ne suffit pas. Sans volont脙漏 politique et sans strat脙漏gie de d脙漏fense commune, ces milliards ne feront que servir les projets de Trump.

Notre strat脙漏gie doit reposer sur notre 脙漏mancipation de la d脙漏fense am脙漏ricaine et s芒聙聶appuyer sur une force europ脙漏enne permanente, dot脙漏e de son propre commandement et capable de d脙漏fendre nos int脙漏r脙陋ts et nos valeurs. Elle doit 脙陋tre fond脙漏e sur le respect du droit international, un droit que nous devons d脙漏fendre, de Gaza 脙聽 l芒聙聶Ukraine. Notre place est aux c脙麓t脙漏s des peuples qui se battent pour la justice. Nous aurions d脙禄 脙陋tre aux c脙麓t脙漏s de l芒聙聶Afrique du Sud devant la Cour internationale de justice. Nous aurions d脙禄 soutenir la Ligue arabe dans son plan de reconstruction de Gaza face au diktat de Trump.

L芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne ne peut plus rester spectatrice. Elle ne peut plus garder le silence sur les grandes initiatives de notre monde. Elle n芒聙聶a plus le droit d芒聙聶avoir peur. Elle doit s芒聙聶脙漏manciper et agir.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marina Mesure (The Left). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, penser l'avenir de la d脙漏fense de l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne sans penser l'avenir de notre industrie de d脙漏fense est absurde. Penser l'avenir de notre industrie de d脙漏fense sans penser l'avenir des industries lourdes europ脙漏ennes est une voie sans issue. Prenons l芒聙聶exemple de la sid脙漏rurgie: aux derni脙篓res nouvelles, pour produire des armes, nous avons besoin d芒聙聶acier, sauf que les hauts fourneaux europ脙漏ens ferment les uns apr脙篓s les autres, faute de r脙漏gulation des prix de l芒聙聶脙漏nergie et de protectionnisme. Ainsi, au moment m脙陋me o脙鹿 nous discutons ici d芒聙聶autonomie dans le domaine de la d脙漏fense, ArcelorMittal, qui est pourtant biberonn脙漏 aux aides publiques, pr脙漏voit de d脙漏localiser sa production en Inde, l脙聽 o脙鹿 nous allons signer des accords de libre-脙漏change. Sans acier europ脙漏en, il ne peut y avoir d芒聙聶industrie europ脙漏enne de d脙漏fense r脙漏ellement autonome, et sans industrie europ脙漏enne autonome, les 800脗聽milliards d芒聙聶euros annonc脙漏s par脗聽Ursula脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen n芒聙聶auront qu芒聙聶un seul d脙漏bouch脙漏: celui de l芒聙聶industrie militaire am脙漏ricaine.

Donnons-nous donc les moyens de cette autonomie, en changeant radicalement nos politiques industrielles et commerciales, en pr脙麓nant moins de libre-脙漏change et plus de protectionnisme, et en achetant europ脙漏en.

脗聽脗聽Ruth Firmenich (NI). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, meine Damen und Herren! Die EU, die sich gerne als Friedensprojekt bezeichnet, will zur europ脙陇ischen NATO werden脗聽芒聙聯 800脗聽Milliarden脗聽Euro sollen in Aufr脙录stung flie脙聼en. Und genau wie die NATO ist die EU weder Verteidigungs- noch Wertegemeinschaft. Denn wo sind die europ脙陇ischen Werte, wenn es um Gaza geht? Wo sind sie, wenn die EU Wahlf脙陇lschung in Rum脙陇nien bejubelt? Und warum gab es nie eine Initiative der EU f脙录r eine friedliche Beilegung in der Ukraine? Jetzt wollen Sie stattdessen den Stellvertreterkrieg in der Ukraine auch ohne die USA fortf脙录hren.

Frieden gilt Ihnen als Verrat脗聽芒聙聯 das erinnert an George Orwell. Sie sagen Verteidigung und meinen Angriff. Sie sind dabei, einen Krieg gegen Russland vorzubereiten. Einige tr脙陇umen sogar von franz脙露sischen Atomwaffen f脙录r die EU, auch wenn die Reichweite der franz脙露sischen Bomber nur bis Hannover langt. Die Zeit der Illusionen ist vorbei, sagte Frau von der Leyen heute. Dies sollte allerdings vor allem f脙录r die hemmungslose Selbst脙录bersch脙陇tzung der EU gelten, bei der man sich an das deutsche Kaiserreich erinnert f脙录hlt. Die EU verkommt zum Europa der R脙录stungskonzerne, f脙录r deren Profite Sie 脙录ber Leichen gehen. Die Kriegskredite m脙录ssen gestoppt werden, wir brauchen Entspannungspolitik.

(Die Rednerin lehnt eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹 von Virginijus Sinkevi脛聧ius ab.)

脗聽脗聽Micha脜聜 Szczerba (PPE). 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Nie miejmy z脜聜udze脜聞. Za oceanem nast脛聟pi脜聜a zmiana paradygmat脙鲁w, cho脛聡 bardzo nam si脛聶 to nie podoba, a m脙鲁wi脛聟c wprost 芒聙聯 jeste脜聸my w脜聸ciekli. Nigdy wcze脜聸niej tak bardzo nie potrzebowali脜聸my jedno脜聸ci i planu. Nigdy wcze脜聸niej nie byli脜聸my tak bardzo zdani na siebie. Za d脜聜ugo byli脜聸my zale脜录ni od innych. Czas jazdy na gap脛聶 w wydatkach na obronno脜聸脛聡 si脛聶 sko脜聞czy脜聜. To, z czym si脛聶 mierzymy jako Europa, to deficyt bezpiecze脜聞stwa. To, co powinno nas po脜聜脛聟czy脛聡, to odpowiedzialno脜聸脛聡. Na nowo trzeba przedefiniowa脛聡 relacje transatlantyckie i sojusze, kt脙鲁rych trwa脜聜o脜聸脛聡, ale te脜录 wzajemne zobowi脛聟zania, nigdy wcze脜聸niej nie by脜聜y kwestionowane. Opuszczenie Ukrainy przez sojusznika i gwaranta jej integralno脜聸ci musi rozpocz脛聟脛聡 dyskusj脛聶 o ryzyku i konsekwencjach opuszczania Europy. Rosja rozpocz脛聶脜聜a wy脜聸cig zbroje脜聞. Europa musi na to odpowiedzie脛聡.

Deklaracja niezachwianego wsparcia dla Ukrainy musi wyra脜录a脛聡 si脛聶 w konkretach. Potrzeba odwa脜录nych dzia脜聜a脜聞, by to aktywa rosyjskie finansowa脜聜y obron脛聶 Ukrainy. Bia脜聜a ksi脛聶ga to mapa drogowa, kt脙鲁rej musimy si脛聶 trzyma脛聡 konsekwentnie, odwa脜录nie i hojnie. Musimy dozbroi脛聡 Europ脛聶. Musimy zabezpieczy脛聡 nasze granice. Musz脛聟 by脛聡 jak tarcza. Musimy zape脜聜ni脛聡 nasze magazyny i musimy wykorzysta脛聡 ten czas.脗聽

Europe: stay the course.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tobias Cremer (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, Vance, Musk and Trump have achieved in just about one month what Stalin, Khrushchev and Putin have failed to do in 80 years: they have undermined the transatlantic alliance, they have surrendered America's claim to global leadership and dismantled the legacy of US presidents from FDR to Ronald Reagan.

I am a committed transatlanticist, and I still refuse to believe that this is the end. I still have faith that America will be back, but I also know that Europe cannot wait for that. This is why it is good that European leaders are finally waking up. It is good to invest EUR脗聽800脗聽billion in defence. It is good that countries like Germany are finally breaking free from self-imposed fiscal straitjackets.

But funds alone are not enough: we must anchor this investment in a broader security strategy, one that recognises that, in an era of hybrid warfare, social cohesion and resilience are just as vital for our security as tanks and drones. This white book offers the opportunity for us to do just that, to be a blueprint for a whole-of-society approach to defence. One that defends Europe, regardless of who is sitting in the White House.

脗聽

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lucia Yar (Renew). 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙陆 p脙隆n predsedaj脙潞ci, kolegovia, kolegyne, priznajme si to a hovorme to u脜戮 nahlas. V Eur脙鲁pe m脙隆me vojnu, ak脙潞 sme neza脜戮ili od 脜隆tyridsiatych rokov a pod tlakom novej geopolitickej situ脙隆cie hroz脙颅, 脜戮e sa naozaj skon脛聧脙颅 prehrou Ukrajiny. Vojna sa ale m脙麓脜戮e posun脙潞脜楼 aj hlb脜隆ie do Eur脙鲁py a presne o to Putinovi ide. Porazi脜楼 Ukrajinu, z脙颅ska脜楼 脛聧as na prezbrojenie a destabilizova脜楼 n脙隆s medzit脙陆m dezinform脙隆ciami. Koho potom obetujeme? Pobaltie, Po脛戮sko, moje Slovensko?

Nem脙隆me desiatky rokov, mo脜戮no m脙隆me mesiace, aby sme vytvorili spolo脛聧n脙潞 obranu, ktor脙隆 Rusko odstra脜隆脙颅, ale presne o tom odstra脜隆en脙颅 je t脙隆to debata. O asert脙颅vnosti a o na脜隆ej jednote. A o tom by mala by脜楼 aj debata o bielej knihe. 脙聛no, bude n脙隆s to nie脛聧o st脙隆脜楼, ale bude to cena prevencie a udr脜戮ate脛戮n脙漏ho mieru ur脛聧ite ni脜戮脜隆ia ako cena vojny. A aby zv脙陆脜隆en脙漏 n脙隆klady nepadli na t脙陆ch najbli脜戮脜隆脙颅ch, na to m脙隆me v Eur脙鲁pskej 脙潞nii a v 脛聧lensk脙陆ch 脜隆t脙隆toch n脙隆stroje.

Tak脜戮e v脛聧era bolo neskoro. My sa mus脙颅me poisti脜楼 a invest脙颅cia do spolo脛聧nej bezpe脛聧nosti je na脜隆ou poistkou. Eur脙鲁pa sa mus脙颅 br脙隆ni脜楼.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Mr Sinkevi脛聧ius, happy Independence Day!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Virginijus Sinkevi脛聧ius (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear Commissioner, dear colleagues, the upcoming white paper on the future of European defence is a crucial step. But the expectation is that it's going to be finally more than just a paper. It must be a real catalyst for action. We cannot afford to continue business as usual.

The security of our citizens and the integrity of our borders depend on our ability to act swiftly and decisively. We must accelerate our efforts to enhance interoperability among Member States' armed forces. This is not just about spending more on defence; it's about ensuring that our forces can work seamlessly together.

We need also to integrate our defence industries and streamline our procurement processes to avoid duplication and inefficiencies. We also should move beyond intergovernmental agreements and build a genuine European Defence Union.

Our support for Ukraine is not just a moral imperative. It's a strategic necessity. We must provide more arms and ammunition, expand training operations and integrate Ukraine's defence industry into our own.

In doing this, we must remember that our ultimate goal is peace. We are strengthening our defence to avoid war, protect our values and ensure that the EU remains a beacon of stability in the world.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, werte Abgeordnete! Wenn wir 脙录ber Verteidigung sprechen, dann sprechen wir 脙录ber Krieg. Einen Krieg, der hoffentlich niemals kommt, aber einen Krieg, den nicht der durchschnittliche Abgeordnete hier, mit seinen 55脗聽Jahren, f脙录hren wird, sondern meine Generation. Meine Generation! Aber wenn es so weit kommt, dann garantiere ich Ihnen: Meine Generation, die mit Frieden und Freiheit aufgewachsen ist, wird sich diese Freiheit niemals nehmen lassen.

Wir werden unsere Union verteidigen, koste es, was es wolle. Wir werden an unseren K脙录sten k脙陇mpfen, wir werden an unseren Grenzen k脙陇mpfen. Wir werden auf unseren Feldern und Stra脙聼en k脙陇mpfen, und wenn es sein muss, auf jedem H脙录gel und in jedem Tal Europas. Denn Europa ist unsere Heimat, und f脙录r unsere Heimat werden wir uns wehren. Wir wollen keinen Krieg, aber wenn es sein muss, dann werden wir f脙录r unsere Freiheit k脙陇mpfen.

脗聽脗聽Elio Di Rupo (S&D). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Monsieur le Commissaire, l芒聙聶histoire des civilisations nous enseigne qu芒聙聶il n芒聙聶existe pas de puissance politique respect脙漏e sans une force militaire capable de d脙漏fendre son peuple. Dans votre strat脙漏gie, je pense que chaque euro investi dans la d脙漏fense doit l芒聙聶脙陋tre en faveur de nos entreprises et de nos PME, ainsi que de nos ouvriers, employ脙漏s et ing脙漏nieurs qui y travaillent.

Qui dit s脙漏curit脙漏 des peuples dit aussi s脙漏curit脙漏 sociale. D脙漏fense et s脙漏curit脙漏 sociale vont de pair 芒聙聯脗聽lutte contre la pauvret脙漏, logement, sant脙漏, retraites, et j芒聙聶en passe芒聙娄

Enfin, Monsieur le Commissaire, pensez-vous vraiment qu'il soit possible de construire une Europe de la d脙漏fense avec 27脗聽pays? Vu l'urgence, ne faudrait-il pas inciter un groupe restreint d芒聙聶脙聣tats membres 脙聽 aller de l芒聙聶avant 脙聽 ce sujet, comme nous l芒聙聶avions fait pour Schengen?

脗聽脗聽Rihards Kols (ECR). 芒聙聯 God脛聛tais s脛聯des vad脛芦t脛聛j! Eiropas aizsardz脛芦bai ir vajadz脛芦gas skaidras priorit脛聛tes un str脛聛d脛聛jo脜隆i meh脛聛nismi. Eiropas p脛聛rapbru脜聠o脜隆ana nevar balst脛芦ties tikai uz papla脜隆in脛聛tu aizdevumu iesp脛聯ju pied脛聛v脛聛脜隆anu. Ir dal脛芦bvalstis, kas jau gadiem b脜芦tiski audz脛聯 savu aizsardz脛芦bas finans脛聯jumu krietni virs minim脛聛l脛聛 l脛芦me脜聠a, un tas tiek dar脛芦ts uz nacion脛聛lo bud脜戮etu r脛聯脛路ina. Papildus 150 miljardiem aizdevumos j脛聛ievie脜隆 ar脛芦 grantu meh脛聛nismi, lai vair脛聛k atbalst脛芦tu t脛聛s valstis, kas jau dara vair脛聛k, gan dotu stimulu starpvalstu kop脛芦giem iepirkumiem. Nosakot kvantitat脛芦vus krit脛聯rijus un sniedzot tie脜隆us stimulus, mudin脛聛s valstis veikt kop脛芦gus iepirkumus, kas attiec脛芦gi veicin脛聛s liel脛聛kas invest脛芦cijas kop脛聯j脛聛 dro脜隆脛芦b脛聛 un nodro脜隆in脛聛s ieg脛聛d脛聛t脛聛 bru脜聠ojuma un tehnikas savienojam脛芦bu un savietojam脛芦bu, kas ir neap脜隆aub脛聛ma priorit脛聛te.

Mums ir j脛聛izmanto t脛聛s priek脜隆roc脛芦bas, ko sniedz Ukrainas aizsardz脛芦bas industrija 芒聙聰 Ukrainas att脛芦st脛芦t脛聛s milit脛聛r脛聛s tehnolo脛拢ijas jau ir pier脛聛d脛芦ju脜隆as sevi kaujas lauk脛聛. Iep脛聯rkot bru脜聠ojumu no Ukrainas, m脛聯s pal脛芦dzam ne tikai t脛聛s ekonomikai, bet ar脛芦 invest脛聯jam sav脛聛 dro脜隆脛芦b脛聛.

Aizdevuma pl脛聛ns ir iez脛芦m脛聯ts 脛聧etriem gadiem. Sagaidu, ka paredz脛聯tie un papildu finan脜隆u avoti b脜芦s dal脛芦bvalst脛芦m pieejami t脜芦l脛芦t脛聯ji, nevis kaut kad gada beig脛聛s.

脗聽脗聽Bart Groothuis (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, having heard the President of the Commission and the President of the Council this morning, and you, dear Commissioner, just now, I worry that we got it wrong, at least partially.

It's not about rearming individual countries, as in the Cold War. That would be insufficient, as it does not deter our adversaries. It is about collective rearming into an alliance which is able to compete with the deterrence that NATO had in the 1980s. That is the task.

For Europe, this requires delivering strategic enablers to operate on a divisional level, Commissioner, and the majority of those enablers are currently being paid for and operated by the US, which is still our ally. But Europe needs to develop or buy such enablers ourselves now.

Secondly, continental Europe is much bigger than the European Union. So the solution when it comes to a solid security architecture should, therefore, also include the UK, Norway, Turkey, Iceland, even Canada in a European security council 芒聙聯 of which nothing has been mentioned. The industrial policy in the white paper should follow this security architecture. I ask you to take this on board, dear Commissioner, and get it right this time.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Benedetta Scuderi (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, "ReArm EU": ma quando ci siamo disarmati? Spendiamo in armi pi脙鹿 di Russia e Cina; eppure nessuno si sente al sicuro. E invece di coordinarci, ora vogliamo gettare altre risorse in un sistema disfunzionale, legandoci a un'economia di guerra con sussidi per i gi脙聽 alti profitti dell'industria militare.

Trump e Putin ridono della nostra disorganizzazione e ci impongono le loro regole: un'Europa sempre pi脙鹿 povera, disorganizzata, priva di una governance politica e una difesa comune 脙篓 ci脙虏 che vogliono e che gli stiamo consegnando con la nostra idea di difesa: 800 miliardi per 27 eserciti, senza una missione comune e senza vaglio del Parlamento.

Pi脙鹿 sicurezza 脙篓 una governance unica e democratica, non 27; interoperabilit脙聽, non altre armi; infrastrutture, diritti, welfare e rinnovabili, non tagliare questi investimenti; diplomazia, corpi di pace, nuovi alleati, non escalation dei conflitti.

Nasciamo per costruire la pace: non sono le nostre armi a fare paura ma il nostro potenziale come unione politica, federale e democratica. Spinelli diceva: un'Europa unita 脙篓 il miglior modo per garantire libert脙聽 e pace.

(L'oratrice accetta di rispondere a una domanda "cartellino blu")

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Wcis脜聜o (PPE), pytanie zadane przez podniesienie niebieskiej kartki. 芒聙聯 M脙鲁wi Pani o dyplomacji? M脙鲁wi Pani o pokoju, o demokracji? A s脜聜ysza脜聜a Pani dzisiaj kobiety z Ukrainy, z Bia脜聜orusi? S脜聜ysza脜聜a Pani o gwa脜聜tach? S脜聜ysza脜聜a Pani o gwa脜聜cie na 4-letniej dziewczynce? W jakiej rzeczywisto脜聸ci Pani 脜录yje? Chce Pani 500 mln obywateli Unii Europejskiej nara脜录a脛聡? We脜潞mie kto脜聸 za to odpowiedzialno脜聸脛聡? Czy Pani wie, co czuj脛聟 obywatele w Europie Wschodniej? Bo ja z takiej pochodz脛聶. Skoro Pani nie wie, to niech Pani przyjedzie i z nimi porozmawia.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Benedetta Scuderi (Verts/ALE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 Thank you for your question. Have I ever said that? Have I ever said that we shouldn't have a common defence? Have I ever said that Europe should not try to build a common defence and a defence that is democratic? I have not said that. I've just said that we spend on weapons already, but we do not co-ordinate weapons. We do not have energy security, we do not have energy independence. If we got cut out from US and Russia, which is happening at the moment, and we do not invest in our welfare and in our energy, how do we make defence? We cannot make defence 芒聙聯 we need a union, we need to invest in our people, we need to invest in a Europe, but especially we need to have a more democratic and federal Union. Otherwise the dream of Europe will go away from our population 芒聙聯 and if that goes, no one will fight for Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pekka Toveri (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, some people in this plenary question the need for defence investments. Either they say that we should try diplomacy or that Russia is not a threat 芒聙聯 both are wrong. Russia has broken the Minsk agreements and other agreements hundreds of times and it is well recorded. We can't trust Russia. Diplomacy won't work.

Those who say that Russia is not a threat normally come from countries that has not been attacked, occupied, raped and pillaged dozens of times through history like Finland, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, Poland and many other countries have. Don't lecture us about peaceful Russia, we know better!

Europe has finally 芒聙聯 after decades of inaction 芒聙聯 started to take the right steps towards our security and defence. Let us continue on this path.

I have three children, all in the Finnish army reserves, ready to defend us all. I want them to be properly equipped to do that. We must and we can afford to do that. Otherwise, saying that is not true, it is a lie.

And to those who claim that Russia's defence budget is much smaller than Europe's, please Google purchasing power parity and the Russian defence budget 芒聙聯 you will be surprised.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Merja Kyll脙露nen (The Left), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Diplomacy really did not help Finland when our neighbour Russia decided to come across the border.

The US has also been a long-term partner, but now it seems that President Trump is using the divide-and-rule model known since Roman times to get China off Russia's side to divide Europe and oppress others under its own power.

One should not side with those who dream of Tsarist power or world empire. Mr脗聽Toveri, how do we find European cooperation and understand that the situation is that only a united and strong Europe, which covers its powers in all areas of activity, will survive?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Excuse me, colleagues, blue cards are not an occasion for a speech in itself. Please stick to the question.

脗聽脗聽Pekka Toveri (PPE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 Thank you for the good question. I think we are living in very dangerous times, and this won't end in one year or two years, or not even in President脗聽Trump's term, it will continue for decades.

So Europe has to grow up and be strong 芒聙聯 both economically and militarily 芒聙聯 so that we can take care of our own independently, hopefully with good cooperation with the United States some time after Trump's term. But if not, we still have to be able to do it independently. We have people, we have money, we have innovation. We can do it.
