脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Barry Andrews (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, colleagues, the path to a sustainable peace in Syria will, of course, not be a straight one. We all know that. But there are positive signs. We saw yesterday's announcement in relation to the SDF and the government, and that is very, very welcome. However, the horrific violence in the coastal area must not go unpunished, and making sure that there is transitional justice is essential for that sustainable peace.

The EU needs to invest in the political economy of reconciliation as a matter of urgency. The Syria conference that takes place next Monday is the ninth one, but different from every other 芒聙聯 for some very obvious reasons and some less obvious reasons. The most obvious is the change in regime. The less obvious is the fact that international aid has collapsed in the last 12脗聽months.

My plea to you, Commissioner, is to concentrate on the agricultural industry, to concentrate on replanting, on irrigation, on bakeries, on the core of the regrowth of the Syrian economy and the pathway to that sustainable peace.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Katrin Langensiepen (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, werte Kommissarin, werter Rat! Nur weil man einen Diktator vom Hof gejagt hat, hei脙聼t es nicht, dass eine komplette Diktatur verschwunden ist; L脙陇nder, die Erfahrungen mit Diktaturen gemacht haben, wissen das. Assad ist weg, aber die Diktatur ist geblieben. Die Angst ist noch da, das Misstrauen unterhalb der Menschen ist noch da. Al-Scharaa ist der neue Pr脙陇sident脗聽芒聙聯 ich w脙录rde mal sagen, er ist der Ansprechpartner in Syrienfragen, aber noch weit davon entfernt, als gew脙陇hlter Pr脙陇sident gesehen zu werden. Und er hat es bisher nicht wirklich 脙录berzeugend darstellen k脙露nnen, wie er sich ein geeintes Syrien vorstellt.

Ich war vor 14 Tagen in Syrien, in Suweida und in Damaskus, und die gro脙聼e Angst ist nat脙录rlich, dass ein Staat zerf脙陇llt. Aber dann muss mich dieser Staat, dann m脙录ssen mich auch die Menschen in diesem Staat interessieren; dann muss ich auch mal nach Suweida fahren, dann muss ich mich auch mal in den Kurdengebieten zeigen und nicht nur sagen: Ich will ein geeintes Syrien, aber bitte kommt bei mir vorbei.

Wir m脙录ssen die Armutsproblematik l脙露sen, die Jugendarbeitslosigkeit, die Gewalt an Frauen脗聽芒聙聯 ich wei脙聼, wir wissen nicht, wo wir noch l脙露schen sollen. Bitte gebt das Geld direkt vor Ort hin und nicht einem Diktator, einem Terroristen im Anzug脗聽芒聙聯 das Bild wurde ja schon h脙陇ufig bem脙录ht.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Reinhold Lopatka (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, we have to monitor developments in Syria very closely. We see, on the one hand, progress; on the other hand, of course, also setbacks.

It is vital for the country to continue developing national unity. It's good to see that the government finalised an agreement with the Kurdish-led Syrian Democratic Forces, and they have to use the chance to integrate them into the national army and into the governing structure.

While these developments are promising, challenges, of course, persist. The recent violence in Latakia, where more than 1脗聽500 people lost their lives, highlights the fragile security situation. And this needs our engagement. No Syrian should live in fear anymore. Therefore, it is positive that we discuss Syria and that we work for a good future for the Syrian people. They deserve it.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nacho S脙隆nchez Amor (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, despu脙漏s de un fin de semana sangriento que nos habla de la fragilidad de las nuevas instituciones y del peligro inherente a las milicias incontroladas, tuvimos anoche una buena noticia que parece despejar, aunque sea provisionalmente, una de las inc脙鲁gnitas m脙隆s urgentes del laberinto sirio: el encaje nacional de la minor脙颅a kurda del norte, de sus instituciones de facto y de su fuerza militar.

En lo que afecta a las comunidades kurdas parece haber esperanza a los dos lados de la frontera, aunque con l脙鲁gicas y ritmos diferentes. El acuerdo inicial alcanzado anoche tiene al menos dos ra脙颅ces: las masacres del ataque de estos d脙颅as y el riesgo de descontrol, y la llamada de 脙聳calan al desarme y disoluci脙鲁n de la organizaci脙鲁n terrorista PKK, cuyas ondas han alcanzado a las fuerzas militantes turcas del norte de Siria.

Es un acuerdo inicial y, por tanto, sujeto a sacudidas, pero nuestra voluntad pol脙颅tica hacia ese marco es que haya una Siria unida, soberana, inclusiva y en manos de los propios sirios. Por lo tanto, fuera Turqu脙颅a, fuera Israel y fuera Rusia.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gy脙露rgy H脙露lv脙漏nyi (PfE). 芒聙聯 Eln脙露k Asszony! Tisztelt k脙漏pvisel脜聭t脙隆rsaim! Eur脙鲁pa sz脙隆m脙隆ra els脜聭dleges a sz脙颅riai menek脙录ltek hazat脙漏r脙漏se. Dac脙隆ra a helyzet minden ellentmond脙隆soss脙隆g脙隆nak, t脙颅z 脙漏v ut脙隆n most el脜聭sz脙露r van lehet脜聭s脙漏g re脙隆lisan ez脙漏rt valamit is tenni. A 235 milli脙鲁 eur脙鲁s uni脙鲁s humanit脙隆rius seg脙漏ly, valamint a Tan脙隆cs d脙露nt脙漏se a szankci脙鲁k felf脙录ggeszt脙漏s脙漏r脜聭l pozit脙颅v fejlem脙漏ny. Az elm脙潞lt napok tragikus esem脙漏nyei azonban jelzik, jelenleg Sz脙颅ria egyben tart脙隆sa a t脙漏t. 脙聣ppen ez脙漏rt fontos, hogy a de facto korm脙隆ny 芒聙聯 s nem 脙漏rdemes azt min脜聭s脙颅teni, hogy van rajta nyakkend脜聭, vagy nem 芒聙聯, minden 脙漏rdekeltet bevonva alak脙颅tott egy vizsg脙隆l脙鲁bizotts脙隆got. Ez is egy pozit脙颅v t脙漏ny.

脙聹dv脙露zlend脜聭 a kurd vezet脜聭kkel val脙鲁 megegyez脙漏s, az is, ami n脙漏lk脙录l nincs egys脙漏ges Sz脙颅ria. Elej脙漏t kell venni a k脙录l脙露nb脙露z脜聭 vall脙隆si, etnikai csoportok megk脙录l脙露nb脙露ztet脙漏s脙漏nek. Nem csak a kisebbs脙漏gi jogokr脙鲁l van sz脙鲁, ahogyan ezt a helyi kereszt脙漏ny egyh脙隆zi vezet脜聭k megfogalmazt脙隆k. Sz脙颅ria minden k脙露z脙露ss脙漏g脙漏nek egyenl脜聭 szabads脙隆gjogokat kell 脙漏lveznie, ide脙漏rtve a vall脙隆sszabads脙隆got is. Ebben kell Eur脙鲁p脙隆nak tart脙鲁s strat脙漏giai t脙隆mogat脙隆st ny脙潞jtania. A j脙露v脜聭 heti sz脙颅riai konferencia lehet脜聭s脙漏get ad az EU-tag脙隆llamok sz脙隆m脙隆ra, hogy biztos脙颅ts脙隆k az Uni脙鲁 hiteles humanit脙隆rius szerepv脙隆llal脙隆s脙隆t, 脙漏s tegyenek az 脙潞jj脙隆脙漏p脙颅t脙漏s脙漏rt. Vagyis lehet脜聭v脙漏 tegy脙漏k a 14 milli脙鲁 elv脙隆ndorolt sz脙颅riai hazat脙漏r脙漏s脙漏t 脙漏s t脙漏nyleg az orsz脙隆g egyben tart脙隆s脙隆t. L脙隆tni kell, sz脙隆mukra munkahely, eg脙漏szs脙漏g脙录gyi ell脙隆t脙隆s, gyermekeiknek oktat脙隆s kell. Ma 3,7 milli脙鲁 gyermek egyszer脜卤en Sz脙颅ri脙隆ban nem j脙隆r iskol脙隆ba. Ezek a t脙漏nyek.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Cecilia Strada (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, cara Commissaria, onorevoli colleghi, a dicembre quest'Aula ha salutato la fine del regime di Bashar al Assad. Purtroppo, gi脙聽 nei giorni successivi, molti politici hanno sfruttato l'occasione per chiedere il rimpatrio dei rifugiati siriani, ignorando le difficolt脙聽 della transizione.

Il Paese rimane instabile: i diritti non sono garantiti, come dimostrano anche le esecuzioni sommarie dei cittadini alawiti sulla costa occidentale, negli ultimi giorni. Vogliamo sperare che le parole della nuova leadership siriana sull'inclusione delle minoranze e delle donne non siano soltanto parole.

L'Europa ha il dovere di ascoltare e proteggere il popolo siriano, tutto: le organizzazioni della societ脙聽 civile, le diverse confessioni religiose, quello straordinario esperimento che 脙篓 il confederalismo del Rojava, fondato su democrazia, ecologia e femminismo.

Ci auguriamo che il vertice sul futuro della Siria possa andare in questa direzione. Purtroppo, lo scarso coinvolgimento della societ脙聽 civile non 脙篓 un bel segnale. Rimane cruciale, nel breve termine, l'impegno di garantire aiuti umanitari alla popolazione e il sostegno. nel lungo periodo. a un percorso veramente democratico e inclusivo per il futuro della Siria.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Davor Ivo Stier (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, gospo脛聭o povjerenice, kolegice i kolege, Sirija je imala re脜戮im koji se odr脜戮avao potporom Rusije i Irana. Sada ima prijelaznu vlast koja se odr脜戮ava potporom Turske.

BDP u Siriji i dalje je manje od polovice vrijednosti iz 2011. godine. Infrastruktura naravno da je zna脛聧ajno o脜隆te脛聡ena, a ve脛聡ina Sirijaca 脜戮ivi i dalje u siroma脜隆tvu. Tro脜隆kovi ponovne izgradnje zemlje mogli bi se kretati pa do 200 milijardi eura.

Europska unija i njezine dr脜戮ave 脛聧lanice su mobilizirale ve脛聡 sada vi脜隆e od 35 milijardi, ali, naravno, pitanje je sada koji 脛聡e biti prioriteti na脜隆eg djelovanja. To naravno da jesu mir, sigurnost, jedinstvo zemlje, za脜隆tita svih manjina, ali posebno je zabrinjavaju脛聡a situacija kr脜隆脛聡ana. Do 2011. u Siriji je 脜戮ivjelo oko milijun kr脜隆脛聡ana, a danas ih je oko 300 tisu脛聡a i oni svaki dan strepe za vlastitu sigurnost.

Dakle, sve su manjine va脜戮ne, ali ako se Europa ne zauzme za kr脜隆脛聡ane, onda to nitko ne脛聡e. Stoga, ne smijemo ih zaboraviti.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Mandl (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Commissioner, colleagues, I visited Syria three years ago. In the main place, I met with international organisations present there. Already then their recommendation was to make exceptions in humanitarian cases from the sanctions, which would have been important and which remain important, while sanctions also remain important, as long as we don't know whether jihadists have turned.

Have we learned our lessons from the other so-called 'Arab Spring', which was nothing but the change from winter to winter in many Arab countries? We don't yet know. The outrage and violence last weekend show a different picture. We should align with international organisations, Commissioner. We should align with organisations important for us in Europe, especially in the field of border management, such as the UNODC.

We should also align with our strong partner in the region, Israel, which has taken care to ensure that the military threat from whatever regime will not increase but decrease, and which takes care of stability in the region, in the buffer zone there.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

Spontane Wortmeldungen

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sander Smit (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, duizenden alawieten zijn vermoord door jihadistische milities, gelieerd aan de nieuwe machthebbers in Syri脙芦. Vrouwen en kinderen zijn de bergen in gejaagd. Hele gezinnen zijn afgeslacht, ook christenen.

Toch nodigt de Europese Unie dit nieuwe Syrische regime voor maandag uit alsof er niets is gebeurd. Dat is, net zoals de persverklaring van de Europese buitenlandse diplomatieke dienst afgelopen weekend, schandalig. Terwijl wij de verantwoordelijken uitnodigen, kan Rusland zich neerzetten als een humanitaire opvanglocatie op zijn legerbasis Khmeimim. Dit is moreel failliet beleid.

Een nieuwe vluchtelingenstroom dreigt. Zolang de islamistische machthebbers in Damascus dit sektarische en genocidale geweld tegen niet-soennieten niet stoppen en daders niet bestraffen, mogen geen sancties worden opgeheven, noch donaties worden gedaan. Europa mag geen cynische politiek bedrijven die alawieten, sjiieten, Armenen, Aramese christenen, Koerden, druzen en jezidi芒聙聶s opoffert.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Paolo Inselvini (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, Commissario 脜聽uica, onorevoli colleghi, stiamo parlando di transizione e ricostruzione giusta della Siria. Ma 脙篓 davvero giusto parlare di questo con chi governa ora, la Siria? Con coloro che negli ultimi giorni hanno trucidato e assassinato migliaia e migliaia di donne, di bambini, di civili cristiani e alawiti? 脙聢 con loro che vogliamo fare tutto questo?

Lo avevamo detto dopo la caduta di Assad, che non c'era da esultare per coloro che avevano preso il potere. Lo avevamo detto. Eppure, qui c'era qualcuno che aveva reagito festante per l'avvento del regime di coloro, che anche noi qui ritenevamo terroristi e, che da un momento all'altro, si sono dimostrati, invece, per questo Parlamento, moderati.

Dobbiamo, invece, difendere da costoro la nostra civilt脙聽, rappresentata dai cristiani che soffrono in tutto il mondo e, in questo momento, stanno soffrendo in Siria. Ed 脙篓 questo che dobbiamo fare. Agiamo ora, prima che sia troppo tardi, prima che vengano sterminati o cacciati dalla propria terra.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脦聹脦卤脧聛脦炉脦卤 脦聳脦卤脧聡脦卤脧聛脦炉脦卤 (NI). 芒聙聯 脦職脧聟脧聛脦炉脦卤 脦聽脧聛脧聦脦碌脦麓脧聛脦碌, 脧聝脧聟脦陆脦卢脦麓脦碌脦禄脧聠脦驴脦炉 脦录脦驴脧聟, 脦驴脦鹿 脦聲脧聟脧聛脧聣脧聙脦卤脦炉脦驴脦鹿 脦路脦鲁脦颅脧聞脦碌脧聜, 脦卤脧聠脦驴脧聧 脧聙脧聛脧聨脧聞脦卤 脧聟脧聙脦驴脦麓脦颅脧聡脧聞脦路脦潞脦卤脦陆 脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜 脦碌脦鲁脦潞脦禄脦路脦录脦卤脧聞脦炉脦碌脧聜 脧聞脦露脦鹿脧聡脦卤脦陆脧聞脦鹿脧聝脧聞脦颅脧聜 脧聝脧聞脦路脦陆 脦潞脧聟脦虏脦颅脧聛脦陆脦路脧聝脦路 脧聞脦路脧聜 脦拢脧聟脧聛脦炉脦卤脧聜 脦录脦碌脧聞脦卢 脦虏脦卤脦聬脧聣脦陆 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦潞脦禄脦卢脦麓脧聣脦陆, 脦录脦碌 脧聡脦卤脦录脧聦脦鲁脦碌脦禄脦卤 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦麓脦路脦禄脧聨脧聝脦碌脦鹿脧聜 脧聙脦碌脧聛脦炉 脧聞脦卢脧聡脦卤 脦麓脦路脦录脦驴脦潞脧聛脦卤脧聞脦炉脦卤脧聜, 脧聝脦庐脦录脦碌脧聛脦卤 脦碌脧聙脦鹿脦潞脧聛脦卤脧聞脦碌脦炉 脦路 脦碌脦潞脦潞脧聣脧聠脦卤脦陆脧聞脦鹿脦潞脦庐 脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜 脧聝脦鹿脧聣脧聙脦庐 脦鲁脦鹿脦卤 脧聞脦路脦陆, 脧聦脧聙脧聣脧聜 脧聞脦路脦陆 脦驴脦陆脦驴脦录脦卢脦露脦驴脧聟脦陆, 脧聝脧聟脦录脧聙脦碌脧聛脦鹿脦禄脦路脧聙脧聞脦鹿脦潞脦庐 脦拢脧聟脧聛脦炉脦卤. 脦陇脧聨脧聛脦卤 脧聙脦驴脧聟 脧聝脧聞脦路 脦拢脧聟脧聛脦炉脦卤 脧聝脧聟脦陆脧聞脦碌脦禄脦碌脦炉脧聞脦卤脦鹿 脦鲁脦碌脦陆脦驴脦潞脧聞脦驴脦陆脦炉脦卤, 脧聙脦驴脧聧 脦碌脦炉脦陆脦卤脦鹿; 脦拢脧聠脦卤脦鲁脦颅脧聜 脦卤脦录脦卢脧聡脧聣脦陆, 脦鲁脧聟脦陆脦卤脦炉脦潞脦碌脧聜, 脦鲁脦颅脧聛脦驴脦陆脧聞脦碌脧聜, 脧聙脦卤脦鹿脦麓脦鹿脦卢 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦虏脧聛脦颅脧聠脦路 脧聙脦驴脧聟 脦潞脦卤脧聞脦卤脦潞脧聛脦碌脦驴脧聟脧聛脦鲁脦驴脧聧脦陆脧聞脦卤脦鹿. 脦搂脧聛脦鹿脧聝脧聞脦鹿脦卤脦陆脦驴脦炉, 脦聰脧聛脦驴脧聧脦露脦驴脦鹿, 脦聭脦禄脦卤脦驴脧聟脦炉脧聞脦碌脧聜, 脦職脦驴脧聧脧聛脦麓脦驴脦鹿, 脦卤脦赂脧聨脦卤 脦赂脧聧脦录脦卤脧聞脦卤. 脦職脦卤脧聞脦卤脦鲁脦鲁脦颅脦禄脦禄脧聣 脧聞脦驴 脦卤脦炉脧聝脧聡脦驴脧聜 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聟脧聙脦驴脦潞脧聛脦鹿脧聝脦炉脦卤. 脦聺脧聞脧聛脦驴脧聙脦庐! 脦聦脧聞脦卤脦陆 脦路 脦聲脧聟脧聛脧聣脧聙脦卤脧聤脦潞脦庐 脦聢脦陆脧聣脧聝脦路 脧聝脦鹿脧聣脧聙脦卢 脦录脧聙脧聛脦驴脧聝脧聞脦卢 脧聝脦碌 脦碌脦鲁脦潞脦禄脦庐脦录脦卤脧聞脦卤 脦潞脦卤脧聞脦卢 脧聞脦路脧聜 脦卤脦陆脦赂脧聛脧聣脧聙脧聦脧聞脦路脧聞脦卤脧聜, 脦鲁脦炉脦陆脦碌脧聞脦卤脦鹿 脧聝脧聟脦陆脦碌脧聛脦鲁脧聦脧聜. 脦聦脧聞脦卤脦陆 脦驴脦鹿 脦露脧聣脦颅脧聜 脦卤脦赂脧聨脧聣脦陆 脦赂脧聟脧聝脦鹿脦卢脦露脦驴脦陆脧聞脦卤脦鹿 脦鲁脦鹿脦卤 脧聝脧聟脦录脧聠脦颅脧聛脦驴脦陆脧聞脦卤, 脦卤脧聟脧聞脧聦 脦麓脦碌脦陆 脦碌脦炉脦陆脦卤脦鹿 脧聙脦驴脦禄脦鹿脧聞脦鹿脦潞脦庐, 脦碌脦炉脦陆脦卤脦鹿 脧聝脧聟脦陆脦碌脦陆脦驴脧聡脦庐 脧聝脧聞脦路 脧聝脧聠脦卤脦鲁脦庐.

(Ende der spontanen Wortmeldungen)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dubravka 脜聽uica, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear Members of the Parliament, thank you for your contributions to this debate. All of us know, and all of us agree, that Syria's transition is in the making, that the path to stability and prosperity is long, and that the challenges are numerous, but we should remain there. This is something I hope all of us agree on.

Only a few words on the latest outbreak of violence. So we welcome, as I already said, the announcement by the interim authorities to support the investigative commission which is being established. Everything must be done to prevent any such crimes from happening again. This is why I said at the beginning that they had to translate their commitment into action.

On the Brussels conference, once again we think that it is a very important one, and a little bit different than earlier. For the first time, the Syrian interim authorities have been invited together with the United Nations, alongside Syria's neighbouring countries and other regional partners. There will be two different panels and two different pledges. One will be to address the immediate needs of the Syrian people, with the focus on socio芒聙聭economic recovery. At the same time, it will be addressing the serious humanitarian needs which still exist.

On the different groups, some of you mentioned the Christians and the protection of Christian minorities or communities. You all know that the new authorities in Damascus have a major responsibility to ensure the protection of all components of society and to refrain from acts of vengeance. We are sure that this their task, but let's see what the future will bring. Of course, we have to protect all Syrians in their diversity, based on the equal rights of citizens.脗聽Once again, a very important issue is to support the inclusive transition in Syria, because it is critical for the regional security.

On returns 芒聙聯 returns cannot and should not be rushed. In the context of a very fragile transition, something which we want to see is whether and at what moment Syria will be proclaimed a safe third country. So let's see how this will go.

On sanctions, we have already suspended sectoral sanctions, but now let's see how to proceed with financial sanctions. We know that American sanctions are already there. So let's see.

All that we have been doing in January and February at the Foreign Affairs Council has been in a gradual and irreversible manner. I want to thank you for the support you have shown this evening. Syria's reconstruction will need the backing of all of us 芒聙聯 of the Commission, of the Member States and of Parliament 芒聙聯 in a Team Europe spirit. Parliamentary diplomacy, outreach and expertise in state芒聙聭building are of the essence at this critical juncture for Syria. What is important is that we here in Europe remain united and committed, and Syria's future depends on our collective action today. So we are looking forward to the resolution, which I hope will be voted tomorrow.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adam Sz脜聜apka, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, honourable Members, let me reiterate that the EU condemns in the strongest terms the violent acts that have taken place and calls for the perpetrators to be held accountable.

In addition, we need to be vigilant towards the information manipulation by foreign actors to steer further violence and instability. Those foreign actors do not share our vision for a stable and inclusive Syria.

It is for the EU to support the transition and to become a reliable partner for Syria's future. The coming days and months will be decisive.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Zum Abschluss der Aussprache wurden gem脙陇脙聼 Artikel脗聽136 Absatz脗聽2 der Gesch脙陇ftsordnung acht Entschlie脙聼ungsantr脙陇ge eingereicht.

Die Aussprache ist geschlossen.

Die Abstimmung findet morgen, Mittwoch, den 12.脗聽M脙陇rz 2025, statt.

Schriftliche Erkl脙陇rungen (Artikel 178)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tomasz Froelich (ESN), schriftlich. 芒聙聯 In Syrien morden die neuen islamistischen Machthaber wahllos Minderheiten - Alawiten und Christen. Und was macht die EU? Sie schickt noch mehr Geld nach Syrien. Sie verurteilt diejenigen, die sich dem Blutterror nicht beugen. Und sie stellt sich auf die Seite des islamistischen Terrorregimes, das vor den Augen der Welt脙露ffentlichkeit Alawiten und Christen ermordet. Eine moralische Bankrotterkl脙陇rung. Man muss dieses Verhalten n脙录chtern einordnen: Br脙录ssel biedert sich aus geopolitischen Gr脙录nden einem Terrorregime an - nur um konsequent gegen Assad zu bleiben. Warum? Weil man gegen Russland k脙陇mpfen will und Assads Syrien als russischen Partner betrachtete. Das offenbart die Heuchelei der 芒聙聻wertegeleiteten Au脙聼enpolitik芒聙聹, an der man in Br脙录ssel trotz R脙录ckzug der USA festh脙陇lt. Die Konfrontation mit Moskau wird damit begr脙录ndet, dass man dem autorit脙陇ren, despotischen und m脙露rderischen Regime in Moskau entgegentreten m脙录sse. Dass Ahmed al-Scharaas Syrien diese Karikatur eines despotischen Regimes tats脙陇chlich verk脙露rpert, ist Br脙录ssel egal, wenn's um Machtpolitik geht. Man kann aber nicht Ungarn f脙录r konservative Kulturpolitik r脙录gen und Rum脙陇niens Wahlen r脙录ckg脙陇ngig machen und zeitgleich ein islamistisches Terrorregime legitimieren. Und man kann nicht Abschiebungen nach Afghanistan mit Verweis auf die Taliban-Regierung ablehnen, aber dann mit Al-Kaida in Damaskus kooperieren. Genau das tun die Br脙录sseler Eliten. Und genau deshalb werden sie scheitern.

脗聽

16. Deteriorating situation in Gaza following the non-extension of the ceasefire (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Als n脙陇chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgt die Aussprache 脙录ber die Erkl脙陇rungen des Rates und der Kommission zur Verschlechterung der Lage im Gazastreifen nach dem Auslaufen der Waffenruhe (2025/2603(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adam Sz脜聜apka, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Madam Commissioner, honourable Members, I would like to focus on the situation in Gaza 芒聙聯 in light of the latest developments around the ceasefire and the release of hostages 芒聙聯 by elaborating on three aspects: first, the humanitarian situation; second, our support to the Palestinian Authority; and third, our commitment to a two-state solution.

The EU has been clear on the utmost importance of maintaining the ceasefire. For the moment, the ceasefire holds, but it remains very fragile. We now need to move towards negotiations on phase two of the ceasefire and a hostage release agreement.

The first aspect is to improve the humanitarian situation, which is again deteriorating. The UN tells us that food prices in Gaza have spiked and aid food parcels could soon run out. There is an urgent need to resume a consistent flow of supplies, and the EU continues to play its part.

The EU has also made an active contribution to the ceasefire through its CSDP mission, EUBAM Rafah, which is operational at the Rafah crossing point. More than 3脗聽000 people have crossed the border since it reopened on 1脗聽February. In addition, the EU is carrying out medical evacuations.

The second aspect is our continued support for the Palestinian Authority in its reforms and its gradual return to Gaza. We believe that the Palestinian Authority is best placed to govern Gaza. It needs to be strengthened and supported financially. There is no place for Hamas in Gaza.

On concrete support to the Palestinians, the EU is ready to work with the entire international community, our partners and the United Nations. The EU position is clear: we support UN Security Council Resolution 2735. This means rejecting any attempts at demographic and territorial changes in the Gaza Strip and stressing the importance of unifying the Gaza Strip with the West Bank under the Palestinian Authority. We therefore welcomed the initiative by the Arab countries to draw up a plan which covers interim measures, reconstruction and governance, and does not foresee any displacement of Gaza's population.

The third aspect is the two-state solution. It is the only way to ensure sustainable peace and security for Israel, the Palestinians and the entire region. The EU will continue to work to review the political process, in particular as part of the Global Alliance for the Implementation of the Two-State Solution. We are also opening a new chapter of strengthened engagement through the recent EU-Israel Association Council, and the first ever EU-Palestine high-level dialogue with Prime Minister Mustafa, which will take place in April on the margins of the Foreign Affairs Council.

To conclude, the EU is committed to play its role in contributing to a more stable situation in Gaza and supporting the Palestinian Authority. The ceasefire must be maintained and the negotiations for its second phase must start as soon as possible.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Before I pass the floor to our Commissioner, I would like to announce that we will not open the catch-the-eye or脗聽the blue card because of the time delay.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dubravka 脜聽uica, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Council President, honourable Members, we are very worried by the evolution of the situation in Gaza and the threats to the fragile ceasefire. We continue to call on both parties to ensure that the ceasefire lasts and that all hostages can and will be released.

The first priority for Gaza currently is to resume full access to humanitarian aid. Let us recall that there are 3.1脗聽million people who are affected by 15 months of conflict ahead of the January ceasefire. The local population should not again suffer from the hostilities from all sides.

The European Union and its Member States are among the biggest financial supporters of the Palestinians, and currently the largest donors to the Palestinian Authority and to UNRWA. It has already mobilised an additional amount of EUR脗聽120脗聽million for humanitarian assistance. Since the onset of the Gaza crisis, this brings our total contribution to EUR脗聽450脗聽million.

The脗聽package of essential services includes food, clean water, healthcare and shelter. So far, 4脗聽500 tonnes of humanitarian cargo have been transferred via humanitarian air bridge flights to Gaza. The European Union has also expanded its civil protection mechanism for medical evacuations in Gaza. A blockage of the delivery of humanitarian assistance and interruption of services such as electricity would have devastating consequences for the population on the ground.

Second, the extension of the ceasefire or a move to the second stage of the ceasefire and release of all hostages are the only ways to permanently end the hostilities, and it would allow us to start the early recovery and reconstruction of Gaza.

While European Union efforts are currently focusing on scaling up humanitarian support, we are also preparing for a long recovery and reconstruction phase. On 18脗聽February, the European Union released its Gaza Rapid Disaster Needs Assessment, together with the United Nations and the World Bank. This assessment looks at the damages, losses and needs. It estimates that EUR脗聽49脗聽billion are required over the next decade. The Egyptian-led reconstruction plan came to similar conclusions.

Housing requires the largest share of recovery needs 芒聙聯 about 30脗聽% 芒聙聯 followed by the health sector, commerce and industry, and agriculture and food. Given the magnitude of needs, the European Union cannot act alone. The international community must take collective and coordinated efforts to support Gaza's recovery and reconstruction. This is why we are working closely with our Arab partners. We welcome the Arab Recovery and Reconstruction Plan that was endorsed at the emergency summit of the League of Arab States in Cairo on 4脗聽March. We are studying the technical details of the plan in order to identify and create possible synergies, and we look forward to the upcoming international conference on Gaza that Egypt will host soon.

Large-scale recovery and reconstruction can only start when there is stability on the ground. This includes arrangements on the governance and security of Gaza. We firmly reject any attempt of relocation of the population or of territorial change in Gaza and the West Bank. Discussions on the recovery and reconstruction of Gaza must involve the Palestinians. The Palestinian Authority is their legitimate body.

When it comes to Gaza governance, we see the Palestinian Authority as the only viable partner. This is why we are committed to supporting a reformed Palestinian Authority and avoid its fiscal collapse. Last year, the Commission disbursed almost EUR脗聽400脗聽million in emergency funding to address the most urgent needs.

We are now preparing a multi-year, comprehensive programme for the Palestinian recovery and resilience. It is based on the reform agenda of the Palestinian Authority, and we are encouraging Israel to support it by releasing withheld tax revenues.脗聽We hope that other regional stakeholders, including in the Gulf, will also support this approach.

Finally, I want to highlight the essential role that UNRWA has played. It has delivered more than half of the entire emergency response in Gaza over the last 15 months. As a provider of many essential services to Palestinians, also beyond Gaza, UNRWA has an important role. They are a humanitarian and development actor. As European Commission, we will continue our support to the Palestinian refugees and to the UN agency.

Let me finish by saying that I am looking forward to high-level political dialogue with the Palestinian Authority in April. It will take place on 13 or 14脗聽April and will be a good occasion to take stock of the reform achievements.

We must not forget the central role of the Palestinian Authority and the role which it will have in the day after in Gaza. Thank you and I am looking forward to your debate.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Micha脜聜 Szczerba, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Madam Commissioner, dear colleagues, we are witnessing a deeply concerning deterioration of the situation in Gaza following the non-extension of the ceasefire. The humanitarian toll is tragic, and our thoughts remain with all innocent civilians caught in this devastating conflict.

On 7 October 2023, Hamas, supported by other armed groups from the Gaza Strip, carried out a coordinated terrorist attack on Israel. Over 3脗聽000 rockets were fired from Gaza and up to 3脗聽000 terrorists infiltrated Israeli territory. Around 1脗聽200 people were killed and approximately 250 others were kidnapped. In response, Israel officially declared that it was a state of war, launched air strikes on the Gaza Strip, and after about two weeks began ground operations.

The recent ceasefire between Israel and Hamas began on 19 January, following extensive diplomatic efforts by Egypt, Qatar, and the United States. It marked a significant de芒聙聭escalation after 15 months of conflict, with both parties committing to halt hostilities and engage in negotiations.

High humanitarian costs in Gaza are unacceptable. We recognise the right of states to defend themselves, but at the same time Europe has a great role to play in promoting stability. We stand for peace, security and for the right of all people to live without fear.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Evin Incir, on behalf of the S&D Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, colleagues, I am T-I-R-E-D, tired! Session after session, we are taking note of the horrific situation in Gaza. But my question is when are we going to do something about it? If I feel this tired about words remaining words, imagine how the people of Gaza are feeling right now. Family members murdered, mutilated, traumatised, electricity cut and threats to halt all humanitarian aid entering Gaza and using starvation as a weapon against innocent Palestinians.

I have seen it with my own eyes when I visited Rafah in Egypt three weeks ago, how children being mutilated and how the Israeli right-wing government refused to let basic humanitarian aid enter. And don't forget the Israeli annexation of the West Bank that is taking place right now.

Colleagues, Netanyahu is prolonging the conflict, refusing to move to the next phase of the ceasefire. To all colleagues still defending the Israeli far-right government, what more do you want Netanyahu to do before enough is enough? Take off your political blinders.

Wasn't it some of you who said all lives matters when we were discussing racism in EU? So what about the Palestinians' lives? Aren't they a part of all of us? International law is not cherry picking.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastiaan St脙露teler, namens de PfE-Fractie. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, 7脗聽oktober 2023 zal voor altijd in ons geheugen gegrift staan. De barbaarse slachting die de terreurgroepen uit Gaza pleegden onder leiding van Hamas was de zwaarste klap voor Isra脙芦l sinds de Holocaust. Pas 471脗聽dagen later werden Romi, Emily en Doron als eerste vrijgelaten en die weken daarna waren weken van hoop. Hoop voor de families van de gegijzelden en hoop voor Isra脙芦l.

Isra脙芦l heeft 1脗聽904脗聽gevangenen vrijgelaten, onder wie terroristen met veroordelingen voor meervoudige moord, terreuraanslagen, ontvoering, seksueel geweld en marteling. In ruil daarvoor kreeg Isra脙芦l dertig gijzelaars vrij die nog, godzijdank, in leven waren, zij het amper, en acht lichamen, waaronder dat van de negen maanden oude baby Kfir Bibas. Mogen zij rusten in vrede.

Hamas houdt vandaag de dag nog 59脗聽gijzelaars vast, waarvan wordt vermoed dat er nog slechts 24 in leven zijn. Al die 59 moeten worden vrijgelaten. Dat is prioriteit nummer 脙漏脙漏n, commissaris.脗聽Bring them home now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bert-Jan Ruissen, namens de ECR-Fractie. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, in de discussie over Gaza hoor ik heel vaak dat de situatie volstrekt uitzichtloos is. En zo voelt dat inderdaad. Toch zijn de bouwstenen voor de aanpak om uit deze mis脙篓re te komen, volstrekt helder, namelijk onmiddellijke vrijlating van alle gijzelaars en harde veiligheidsgaranties, zodat 7脗聽oktober zich nooit meer kan herhalen.

Zodra aan deze voorwaarden is voldaan, zal er ook van Isra脙芦lische kant weer heel veel mogelijk worden. Maar dat betekent wel dat er in het toekomstige bestuur van Gaza geen plek is voor Hamas. Ik ben het eens met de heldere stellingname van de hoge vertegenwoordiger op dat punt. Maar het vraagt ook om actie van de EU nu: namelijk om vervanging van de humanitaire steun via UNRWA door steun via instanties die niet met Hamas samenwerken, en om een EU-verbod op ons eigen grondgebied voor organisaties met duidelijke banden met Hamas.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Barry Andrews, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, first of all, I welcome your endorsement of UNRWA. When I visited Rafah a year ago, Israel was blockading humanitarian access. The excuse given at that time was that UN agencies were disorganised, could not be relied upon, or that humanitarian items could be used for military purposes.

Israel has shut off electricity and aid again, but on this occasion, it is so emboldened by US support and EU inaction that it doesn't even offer excuses for the blockade of humanitarian access anymore. And in any case, what type of ceasefire is this? 100 people have been killed in Gaza since the ceasefire. We have seen the outlawing of passive collaboration with the ICC. We've seen the 'Gazafication' of the West Bank. We've seen vicious settler violence and Ben-Gvir rescinding the Oslo agreement, and we're told it's Palestinians that are breaching the ceasefire.

As we know, there can be no lasting peace and durable solution without full respect for Palestinian people's right to self-determination.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mounir Satouri, au nom du groupe Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, m脙陋me en l芒聙聶absence de notre haute repr脙漏sentante, j芒聙聶aimerais m芒聙聶adresser 脙聽 elle. Madame Kallas, vous avez r脙漏cemment tenu un conseil d芒聙聶association avec Isra脙芦l. Mais comment avez-vous pu? Comment avez-vous pu maintenir cet accord sans condition, sans exigence, sans un mot pour le droit humanitaire? Comment avez-vous pu fermer les yeux, alors que l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne avait un levier, un poids, une voix?

Depuis cette abdication, regardons la r脙漏alit脙漏 en face: le cessez-le-feu est mort, enterr脙漏 sous les bombes. L芒聙聶aide humanitaire est 脙漏trangl脙漏e. Pas un sac de farine, pas un bidon d芒聙聶eau n芒聙聶entrent 脙聽 Gaza. Isra脙芦l a plong脙漏 Gaza dans le noir: 600脗聽000脗聽personnes n芒聙聶ont plus d芒聙聶脙漏lectricit脙漏, plus d芒聙聶eau, plus d芒聙聶espoir. C脙麓t脙漏 Cisjordanie, 40脗聽000脗聽personnes ont fui, chass脙漏es de leur camp par l芒聙聶arm脙漏e isra脙漏lienne, et les exactions ne font qu芒聙聶augmenter. Que fait l芒聙聶Europe? Elle d脙漏tourne le regard.

Notre responsabilit脙漏 est 脙漏crasante. Nous avions un devoir: emp脙陋cher le pire, mais nous avons laiss脙漏 faire. Nous avions un principe: la clause des droits de l芒聙聶homme. Mais vous l芒聙聶avez pi脙漏tin脙漏e.脗聽Madame la Haute Repr脙漏sentante, il y a un mot pour ce que vous venez de faire: la complaisance. Et l芒聙聶histoire retiendra un mot: la l脙垄chet脙漏.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lynn Boylan, on behalf of The Left Group. 芒聙聯 A Uachtar脙隆in, the decision by the Israeli Government to cut off electricity to Gaza is just the latest in a series of actions designed to make life for Palestinians unliveable. The decades-long illegal occupation and the 18-year blockade and siege of Gaza have all been allowed to continue because of the inaction of the international community, and that includes the EU.

'Attacks against civilian infrastructure, especially electricity, are war crimes': those are not my words, those are the words of the President of the Commission, Ursula von der Leyen, who rightfully called out Russia but refuses to call out Israel when they deprive Palestinians of water, food and electricity. War crimes are war crimes, and international law applies to everyone. The obvious double standards that we are seeing are undermining the EU's credibility across the world and even with its own citizens.

The provisional measures ordered by the International Court of Justice in weighing the allegation of genocide brought against Israel specifically referred to the facilitation of aid deliveries. In the International Criminal Court, one of the charges against Netanyahu is that he is using starvation as a method of warfare.

We had the Association Council meeting in February, and some of us naively thought that maybe some strong words would be said to the Israeli authorities; maybe they would be asked to uphold international law. But instead, what we have seen is that things have gotten worse since that meeting. Israel is escalating its violation of international law.

So the question is: when is the EU going to finally stand up for international law and human rights?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Davor Ivo Stier (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, povjerenice, kolegice i kolege, 15. sije脛聧nja je zapo脛聧eta prva faza primirja, 脜隆to je omogu脛聡ilo razmjenu 33 izraelska taoca za dvije tisu脛聡e palestinskih zatvorenika.

Druga faza, to脛聧nije druga faza primirja, nije zapo脛聧eta, ali nisu niti eskalirali sukobi, barem ne do onog intenziteta koji je bio prije 15. sije脛聧nja.

Me脛聭utim, 59 talaca jo脜隆 nije oslobo脛聭eno. Mo脜戮da ve脛聡ina njih, na脜戮alost, vi脜隆e nisu 脜戮ivi, ali je nu脜戮no da Hamas oslobodi sve taoce i to odmah kako bi mir imao 脜隆ansu na Bliskom istoku. Hamas je zapo脛聧eo ovo poglavlje sukoba na Bliskom istoku teroristi脛聧kim napadom 7. listopada 2023. godine i stoga Hamas mo脜戮e i mora to poglavlje odmah zatvoriti osloba脛聭anjem talaca.

脗聽脗聽Hana Jalloul Muro (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤ora comisaria, Gaza necesita un alto el fuego permanente tanto para los gazat脙颅es como para los rehenes israel脙颅es que deben ser liberados. El bloqueo de ayuda humanitaria a Gaza por parte del Gobierno de Israel supone un crimen de guerra. El sufrimiento de la poblaci脙鲁n civil no debe ser una herramienta de presi脙鲁n. Los beb脙漏s est脙隆n muriendo de fr脙颅o en Gaza.

La Uni脙鲁n Europea debe impulsar las fases dos y tres del alto el fuego o solo quedar脙隆n dos escenarios posibles: una nueva guerra o el plan del resort de Trump.

Existe una estrategia silenciosa de ocupaci脙鲁n por parte de Israel que se ha apropiado el 16脗聽% de las tierras agr脙颅colas de Gaza, al igual que en Cisjordania y Jerusal脙漏n, donde la expansi脙鲁n de asentamientos ilegales israel脙颅es, la confiscaci脙鲁n de tierras y el desplazamiento forzoso se han intensificado. Los puestos fronterizos han aumentado un 228脗聽% y se est脙隆n eliminando barrios enteros. Los palestinos no tienen la culpa del terrorismo de Ham脙隆s,脗聽que hay que combatir con firmeza. La ocupaci脙鲁n se consolida en prime time.

Mientras tanto, el Gobierno de extrema derecha israel脙颅 proh脙颅be que el OOPS proporcione ayuda a m脙隆s de 500脗聽000脗聽refugiados y asfixia, con un 80脗聽% de impuestos sobre la financiaci脙鲁n extranjera, a las ONG que no comulgan con dicho Gobierno, silenciando a los ciudadanos israel脙颅es dem脙鲁cratas. Debemos seguir hablando de paz en Gaza, de los dos Estados y, sobre todo, del cumplimiento del Derecho internacional.

脗聽脗聽Hermann Tertsch (PfE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, afortunadamente, Israel tiene una sociedad con un instinto y una voluntad de supervivencia muy superiores a los europeos actuales. Si lo hab脙颅an olvidado algunos israel脙颅es, las salvajadas de Ham脙隆s del 7 de octubre se lo refrescaron. Y Ham脙隆s, eso est脙隆 claro, volver脙隆 a ser esa inmensa base terrorista que fue y que, de alguna forma, sigue siendo. Por eso, la poblaci脙鲁n 脙隆rabe de Gaza deber脙颅a haber entendido que su 脙潞nica posibilidad de seguir viviendo all脙颅 es deshacerse de Ham脙隆s, superar su victimismo odiador y aceptar que Israel existe para siempre.

Los Acuerdos de Abraham de sus hermanos 脙隆rabes vecinos debieron avisarles. Pero como Gaza, y no solo Gaza, se acostumbr脙鲁 a vivir de dinero ajeno y Europa, los Estados Unidos y otros han pagado a los gazat脙颅es para ser refugiados eternos y odiar y querer asesinar jud脙颅os, han seguido abrazados al terrorismo, algo que se ha vuelto a ver en los canjes por rehenes: no parece que hayan aprendido.

Gaza tiene que ser limpiada de terroristas y se ha visto en estos meses que se est脙隆 a脙潞n lejos de ello. Convendr脙颅a a todos que la poblaci脙鲁n gazat脙颅 colaborara, que soltaran a todos los rehenes y se entregaran los t脙潞neles, todas las armas y esos uniformes planchaditos que se han utilizado en esos desfiles de propaganda cuando han soltado 芒聙聰humill脙隆ndolos芒聙聰 a los rehenes. Si no lo hacen, lo har脙隆n las fuerzas israel脙颅es.

Si los vecinos 脙隆rabes 芒聙聰Jordania y Egipto芒聙聰 acogieran a los gazat脙颅es, como los vecinos de Ucrania acogieron a los ucranianos, ser脙颅a m脙隆s f脙隆cil hacer todo este proceso, pero vamos a ver qu脙漏 disposici脙鲁n hay. En todo caso, la base terrorista no puede subsistir.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Irena Joveva (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, and then there was darkness. And then we reached the point where a person 芒聙聯 a human being at least 芒聙聯 has no words left. Not even someone who, despite the darkness, clearly sees what is happening. Someone who will never turn a blind eye, who will never sway their position, who clearly and loudly speaks about facts.

The fact is that after Israel once again cut off electricity to Gaza, nine out of ten people no longer have access to safe drinking water. Nine out of ten.

The fact is that after Israel once again halted the delivery of humanitarian aid to Gaza, the genocide is only escalating. Generations are being wiped out before our eyes. And still the powerful are turning a blind one or worse.

If those of you who had even a shred 芒聙聯 just a shred 芒聙聯 of humanity in you, you would not look away. You would not excuse the inexcusable. You would do something that should have been done long ago. You would deliver.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Catarina Vieira (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Commissioner, we celebrated a ceasefire, but it was short芒聙聭lived. After a few weeks, Netanyahu unleashed full war on civilians once again. The cutting of aid and electricity in Gaza, as well as the intensified attacks on the West Bank, can be added to the ever芒聙聭expanding list of war crimes that he should be imprisoned for.

Instead of supporting and upholding international justice, the EU continues business as usual with Israel. In the meantime, our credibility in the global south crumbles by the day with these double standards. As Israel's largest trading partner, we have a responsibility to force change and end the atrocities in Palestine 芒聙聯 stop arms trade, cease this association agreement and impose sanctions.

So tonight, here are my questions: when will we finally stop business as usual with the aggressor? When will we stop applying these double standards? And when will we start treating Palestinian lives as human lives?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pernando Barrena Arza (The Left). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, en los 70 los soldados israel脙颅es se pon脙颅an delante de un bloque en Gaza y pitaban con un silbato. Todos los 脙隆rabes del bloque ten脙颅an que salir corriendo porque si no eran apaleados, fuesen mujeres, ancianos o beb脙漏s. Estas son palabras de Itzhak Ini Abadi, gobernador israel脙颅 de Gaza en los 70. Por lo tanto, que nadie venga a decirnos que todo empez脙鲁 el 7 de octubre de 2023.

En Palestina, una espiral de violencia ha terminado con el respeto a los derechos humanos y en el v脙鲁rtice de esa violencia est脙隆 la ocupaci脙鲁n ilegal de Palestina por parte de Israel, que tiene en vigor un sistema de脗聽apartheid pol脙颅tico que trata a los palestinos como infrahumanos sin derechos.

Denunciamos el genocidio de 60脗聽000 personas en Gaza y ahora debemos exponer las condiciones inhumanas de vida en la zona, los cortes de luz y agua y la ausencia de atenci脙鲁n sanitaria. Israel no atiende a razones porque no es un pa脙颅s democr脙隆tico, pero la Uni脙鲁n Europea debe exigirle que se permita la entrada del OOPS, de la ayuda humanitaria y de todo tipo de suministros y que cumpla con sus compromisos de alto el fuego.

Se脙卤ora comisaria, a estas alturas seguir manteniendo en vigor el Acuerdo de Asociaci脙鲁n con Israel no es negligencia, es colaboracionismo con cr脙颅menes de guerra. Est脙隆n hundiendo el prestigio reputacional internacional de la Uni脙鲁n Europea. No en nuestro nombre.

脗聽脗聽Alice Teodorescu M脙楼we (PPE). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Situationen i Gaza f脙露rs脙陇mras f脙露r varje dag som vapenvilan inte f脙露rl脙陇ngs. Men varf脙露r har den inte f脙露rl脙陇ngts? Varf脙露r h脙楼ller Hamas fortfarande 59 oskyldiga m脙陇nniskor, varav merparten sannolikt 脙陇r d脙露da, som gisslan? Varf脙露r l脙陇gger inte Hamas ner vapnen, kapitulerar och utlyser demokratiska val, om de oroar sig f脙露r situationen f脙露r civilbefolkningen i Gaza?

Svaret 脙陇r att kapitulation inte ligger i deras intresse utifr脙楼n det l脙楼ngsiktiga m脙楼let att utrota Israel. Svaret 脙陇r att de inte oroar sig f脙露r Gazas civila, som de fr脙陇mst ser som civila sk脙露ldar som kan offras i kampen mot Israel. S脙楼 l脙陇nge som dessa omst脙陇ndigheter kvarst脙楼r kommer ingen l脙楼ngvarig fred att kunna uppn脙楼s. D脙陇rf脙露r beh脙露ver EU 脙露ka trycket p脙楼 palestinierna och deras ledare i s脙楼v脙陇l Gaza som p脙楼 V脙陇stbanken, genom att villkora bist脙楼ndet och rikta sanktioner mot Hamas ekonomiska struktur.

Den palestinska myndigheten beh脙露ver i handling ta avst脙楼nd fr脙楼n Hamas, och Gaza demilitariseras. Det palestinska folket beh脙露ver inse att v脙陇gen till fred f脙露ruts脙陇tter ett totalt avst脙楼ndstagande fr脙楼n terrorism.

脗聽脗聽Marta Temido (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, o fr脙隆gil cessar芒聙聭fogo entre Israel e o Hamas est脙隆 num tr脙隆gico impasse.

Para pressionar, Israel cortou todo o fornecimento de eletricidade a Gaza (o que tamb脙漏m impede o acesso a 脙隆gua pot脙隆vel dessalinizada) e suspendeu toda a entrada de ajuda humanit脙隆ria.

N脙鲁s n脙拢o podemos continuar a assistir passivamente 脙聽 degrada脙搂脙拢o da vida em Gaza. Israel tem de voltar a permitir a entrada de ajuda humanit脙隆ria 芒聙聰 que n脙拢o 脙漏 um instrumento de press脙拢o sobre o Hamas, mas um direito da popula脙搂脙拢o civil de Gaza.

O prolongamento da guerra em Gaza s脙鲁 serve os interesses pol脙颅ticos e pessoais de Netanyahu, apoiado por uma coliga脙搂脙拢o de extrema芒聙聭direita ultraortodoxa. As desrespeitosas propostas da administra脙搂脙拢o Trump para o territ脙鲁rio s脙鲁 refor脙搂am esta lideran脙搂a, que est脙隆 cada vez mais longe de representar todos os israelitas.

As feridas de d脙漏cadas s脙鲁 come脙搂ar脙拢o a sarar se construirmos uma solu脙搂脙拢o baseada em dois Estados e em direitos iguais para israelitas e palestinianos. E, para isso, Israel e o Hamas precisam de voltar a avan脙搂ar imediatamente para cumprir as fases subsequentes do acordo de cessar芒聙聭fogo.

脗聽脗聽Vicent Marz脙聽 Ib脙隆脙卤ez (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, el domingo Israel cort脙鲁 el 脙潞nico suministro el脙漏ctrico que le quedaba a Gaza. En estas 脙潞ltimas semanas han expulsado de forma ilegal a miles de trabajadores humanitarios, entre ellos del UNRWA, la organizaci脙鲁n que usted nombraba, se脙卤ora comisaria. Hoy, ahora mismo, hay militares que est脙隆n destruyendo, ocupando e inutilizando la 脙潞nica franja f脙漏rtil que le quedaba a Gaza, en la que los palestinos trabajaban su tierra para poder comer; y eso est脙隆 pasando hoy, en pleno alto el fuego.

Y mientras tanto, 脗驴qu脙漏 est脙隆 haciendo Europa, se脙卤ora comisaria? 脗驴Mirar hacia otro lado para contentar a Trump? 脗驴El mismo Trump que acaba de detener a un estudiante palestino en la Universidad de Columbia para impedir que pueda expresarse libremente y seguir intentando que no se cumplan los derechos humanos ni en Gaza ni en los Estados Unidos?

Se脙卤or脙颅as, los valores europeos no son solo un eslogan, se tienen que cumplir cada d脙颅a y, para ello, debemos proteger los derechos humanos en Gaza, aumentar la ayuda humanitaria, hacer misiones de paz y construir, de verdad, la paz en todo el mundo.

脗聽脗聽Marc Botenga (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, est-ce que je peux d芒聙聶abord demander au Conseil d芒聙聶arr脙陋ter de rigoler? Vous 脙陋tes en train de rigoler ici, pendant que nous parlons de la mort de je ne sais combien de Palestiniens, d芒聙聶une population qui se fait affamer, et vous 脙陋tes l脙聽 en train de rigoler et de faire des blagues! Ce n芒聙聶est pas dr脙麓le! Les Palestiniens sont des 脙陋tres humains, eux aussi, et vous devriez avoir honte!

C芒聙聶est l脙聽, la caract脙漏ristique de cette Union europ脙漏enne, aujourd芒聙聶hui: un m脙漏pris pour la vie des Palestiniens! Parce que oui, vous avez r脙漏ussi 脙聽 faire un discours en disant: 脗芦Ah! La situation 脙聽 Gaza empire芒聙娄脗禄, sans mentionner pourquoi elle empire. Pourquoi est-ce qu芒聙聶elle empire? Eh bien, parce qu芒聙聶Isra脙芦l bloque toute aide humanitaire, toute nourriture, et m脙陋me l芒聙聶脙漏lectricit脙漏. Qu芒聙聶est-ce que cela veut dire? Sans 脙漏lectricit脙漏, pas de d脙漏salinisation de l芒聙聶eau, et donc pas d芒聙聶eau potable. Ainsi, les gens, les familles, les enfants n芒聙聶ont pas d芒聙聶eau potable, et vous, vous 脙陋tes l脙聽 en train de rigoler! Comment est-ce possible, Monsieur le Ministre? Comment est-ce possible, s芒聙聶il vous pla脙庐t?

Alors, aujourd芒聙聶hui, cette complicit脙漏 europ脙漏enne et ce manque de respect pour la vie des Palestiniens doivent cesser, parce que cela va se payer cash! Je vous le promets!

脗聽脗聽Fidias Panayiotou (NI). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, it's time for Palestinians to have their own state. Most countries around the world support a two芒聙聭state solution for the Israel-Palestine conflict, but the biggest obstacle for this to really happen is the United States. The United States has used its veto power in the United Nations Security Council dozens of time to block resolutions to recognise a Palestinian state.脗聽Alongside the United States, only eight other countries have refused to recognise Palestine. On the other hand, 143 countries around the world recognise Palestine, many of them represented here in the European Parliament.

I believe the EU should push for a two芒聙聭state solution and challenge its biggest ally. Now, more than ever, we should act independently from the United States in order to stop the genocide of Palestinians and finally achieve a two芒聙聭state solution.

脗聽脗聽Aodh脙隆n 脙聯 R脙颅ord脙隆in (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Israel's breach of the ceasefire, cutting off electricity, food and fuel to over 2脗聽million people is a calculated act of collective punishment, a violation of international law. For 10脗聽consecutive days, starvation has become a weapon of war, and the EU Commission responds by continuing to hold meetings of the EU-Israel Association Council. Business as usual. This is a disgrace! It tarnishes our moral standing and exposes the hypocrisy in our commitment to human rights.

The recent obstruction of a European Parliament delegation and the deportation of MEPs and EU officials is an affront to our democratic processes, and I want to express my solidarity to my constituency colleague and Chair of the delegation, Lynn Boylan. You mess with one Dub; you mess with all Dubs! We stand with you, Lynn, in demanding the strongest possible response from President Metsola.

Far from business as usual, Israel must face sanctions now! Saoirse don Phalaist脙颅n!

脗聽脗聽Jaume Asens Llodr脙聽 (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, con Trump y Palestina hemos vuelto al viejo Oeste, a cuando Estados Unidos extermin脙鲁 a los norteamericanos nativos en 1830, o a cuando la Alemania nazi hizo lo mismo un siglo despu脙漏s con los jud脙颅os. Hay patrones que se repiten: una misma narrativa deshumanizadora, los guetos, los muros, la segregaci脙鲁n, el exterminio, la deportaci脙鲁n, la confiscaci脙鲁n de los bienes, de las tierras... Es lo que hay detr脙隆s del v脙颅deo macabro de Trump: donde hab脙颅a Gaza hay un resort tur脙颅stico con estatuas de oro al estilo hitleriano, en honor a 脙漏l, con hoteles construidos desde las cenizas de ese horror.

Pero esa distop脙颅a no es nueva, forma parte del proyecto sionista del Gran Israel como Estado 脙漏tnicamente puro. Y nosotros ahora no podemos decir que no sab脙颅amos. De hecho, no podemos decir ni tan siquiera que no participamos, porque lo hemos apoyado. Y ante la Corte Penal Internacional no solo deber脙颅a juzgarse a Trump y a Netanyahu, sino tambi脙漏n a aquellos que desde Europa lo han apoyado envi脙隆ndoles armas y participando en ese plan monstruoso.

脗聽脗聽Hanna Gedin (The Left). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! EU:s flathet mot den israeliska regeringen kommer att g脙楼 till historien som ett exempell脙露st svek, inte bara mot den palestinska befolkningen utan ocks脙楼 mot internationell r脙陇tt. Som jurist f脙露rf脙陇ras jag. Jag f脙露rf脙陇ras 脙露ver att de regler som vi kom 脙露verens om efter andra v脙陇rldskriget 芒聙聯 att fasorna som vi d脙楼 hade bevittnat aldrig skulle f脙楼 ske igen 芒聙聯 verkar vara v脙陇rda noll och ingenting.

F脙露r hur kan EU tr脙陇ffa israeliska representanter och prata om samarbete n脙陇r folkmord p脙楼g脙楼r? Hur kan EU acceptera att medlemsl脙陇nder struntar i den internationella brottm脙楼lsdomstolens arresteringsorder mot israeliska ministrar? Hur kan EU blunda n脙陇r Israel saboterar vapenvilan, hindrar n脙露dhj脙陇lp fr脙楼n att n脙楼 Gaza och l脙楼ter bulldozrarna rulla in i V脙陇stbanken?

EU m脙楼ste nu klart och tydligt p脙楼 riktigt, inte bara i ord, st脙露tta de initiativ som finns kring 脙楼teruppbyggnad av Gaza och p脙楼 riktigt, inte bara i ord, ta avst脙楼nd fr脙楼n alla f脙露rslag som inneb脙陇r folkf脙露rdrivning och brott mot internationell r脙陇tt.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dubravka 脜聽uica, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, thank you very much for your contributions. I have been listening carefully.

In my introductory statement, I already said a lot. So, against all odds, there are also opportunities with the dire situation in Gaza.

As a short-term measure, we need to focus on the resumption of full access to humanitarian aid. After all, it is about the most pressing needs of the population on the ground, as I already said: food, clean water and health services and, of course, electricity.

The longer-term path to recovery and reconstruction will be complex and challenging. I already explained that there is a plan, but let's see, which was endorsed by Arab countries. We are looking into it, and let's see, but there are some preconditions, meaning a ceasefire which should hold and last.

We want to go this path together with our partners in the region and with the Palestinian people. The relocation of the population or the questioning of the territorial integrity cannot have a place in a sustainable solution in Gaza.

There were some diverging views here, but I am grateful for the vast support you have shown this evening. I think that parliamentary diplomacy is also very important. Your outreach, your expertise in state-building could be essential for the future of Gaza.

Gaza's reconstruction needs the backing of all of us. So, once again, the Commission, Member States and Parliament in a team Europe spirit.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adam Sz脜聜apka, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Madam Commissioner, honourable Members, the EU has been clear on the importance of continuing the ceasefire and the resumption of the negotiation on phase two.

The EU continues to support the mediators and remains on the ground thanks to our civilian mission, EUBAM Rafah.

The EU is committed to reviving the political process and working towards the two-state solution, with Israel and Palestine living side by side in peace within secure and recognised borders. The Council will continue to monitor the situation.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Die Aussprache ist geschlossen.

Ich h脙陇tte mir gew脙录nscht, dass die, die an der Debatte teilgenommen haben, vielleicht auch noch bis zum Ende da geblieben w脙陇ren.

Ich bedanke mich ausdr脙录cklich bei der Kommission und beim Rat f脙录r die Anwesenheit bis zum Schluss.

Schriftliche Erkl脙陇rungen (Artikel 178)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Matja脜戮 Nemec (S&D), pisno. 芒聙聯 Spo脜隆tovane kolegice in kolegi, 脛聧eprav o脛聧i svetovne javnosti niso ve脛聧 tako mo脛聧no uprte v Gazo, se no脛聧na mora Palestincev nadaljuje. Premirje, ki bi 脜戮e moralo preiti v drugo fazo, se vse bolj krha. V izraelskih napadih, predvsem z droni, pa 脜隆e naprej umirajo nedol脜戮ni Palestinci tako v Gazi kot na Zahodnem Bregu. 脜陆e ve脛聧 kot 10 dni humanitarna pomo脛聧 ne dose脜戮e Gaze, saj dostop blokirajo izraelska stran. Najostreje obsojam izklop elektri脛聧ne energije s strani Izraela. V nevzdr脜戮nih razmerah v Gazi 脜戮e mo脛聧no primanjkuje hrane, raven vode je kriti脛聧no nizka.

Spo脜隆tovani, Izrael uporablja stradanje kot vojno sredstvo. To je vojni zlo脛聧in. Evropska unija in mednarodna skupnost morata ukrepati!

Ali bomo 脜隆e naprej gledali stran in dovoljevali nadaljevanje napadov na Gazo kot na Zahodnem bregu. In to medtem ko na eni strani Trump sanja o rivieri, Izrael na novo ri脜隆e zemljevide, Palestinci 脜隆e naprej brezupno umirajo.

Vse napore moramo usmeriti k dosegu dogovora druge faze, ki vklju脛聧uje trajno premirje kot tudi izpustitev vseh talcev. Evropska unija pa mora odlo脛聧no podpreti in aktivno pristopiti k arabskemu na脛聧rt za obnovo Gaze. Ra脛聧un za obnovo pa moramo izstaviti Izraelu, ki je odgovoren za skoraj popolno uni脛聧enje Gaze, ter tistim dr脜戮avam, ki so Izraelu ves 脛聧as vojne 脜隆e naprej dostavljale oro脜戮je. Hvala

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Georgiana Teodorescu (ECR), in writing. 芒聙聯 So many innocent civilians have been murdered, women and men raped, bodies mutilated, and children taken hostage! And yet, almost two years later, this Parliament still FAILS to state the obvious: Israel has the right to defend itself!

What were Israel's options? Do nothing? Accept any demand made by the terrorists while the hostages were still being held? No country in the world would tolerate this! The EU did not negotiate with ISIS, nor did the US negotiate with Al-Qaeda!

Just as the EU sees peace negotiations between Russia and Ukraine as dangerous and invalid without security guarantees for Ukraine, it should analyse the situation between Israel and Gaza in the same manner, as long as the hostages are not released. If not, let us admit that we are applying double standards in EU institutions.

If we truly want peace, we should start by standing with those fighting against terrorism, not by legitimizing those who practice it - including here, in Europe, in Brussels, Strasbourg, Paris, and many other places.

Which of our children will be the terrorists芒聙聶 next victim?

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Als n脙陇chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgen die Erkl脙陇rungen zur Abstimmung.

Aber ich sehe, dass Herr Panayiotou den Saal bereits verlassen hat, und somit gilt dieser Tagesordnungspunkt als behandelt und Herr Panayiotou kann dann seine Erkl脙陇rung in schriftlicher Form abgeben, wenn er dies w脙录nscht.
